C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\ast.rs:
    1|       |//! AST node types for D2 diagram language.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! These types represent the parsed syntax tree of a D2 document.
    4|       |//! The AST closely mirrors D2's Go AST, adapted for Rust idioms.
    5|       |//! Key types:
    6|       |//! - `D2Map`: top-level container of all declarations
    7|       |//! - `MapNode`: individual declaration (key assignment or comment)
    8|       |//! - `Key`: shape/edge/property declaration with optional value
    9|       |//! - `Edge`: connection between two key paths
   10|       |//! - `KeyPath`: dotted key path (e.g., "server.style.fill")
   11|       |
   12|       |use crate::graph::Range;
   13|       |
   14|       |/// Top-level map containing all declarations.
   15|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   16|       |pub struct D2Map {
   17|       |    pub nodes: Vec<MapNode>,
   18|       |    pub range: Range,
   19|       |}
   20|       |
   21|       |/// A node within a map.
   22|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   23|       |pub enum MapNode {
   24|       |    Comment(Comment),
   25|       |    Key(Key),
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// A key declaration (shape, edge, or property assignment).
   29|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   30|       |pub struct Key {
   31|       |    /// Dotted path: "a.b.c"
   32|       |    pub key: KeyPath,
   33|       |    /// Connection chain (empty if this is just a shape/property declaration)
   34|       |    pub edges: Vec<Edge>,
   35|       |    /// D2 syntax index for edge references: `(a -> b)[0]`
   36|       |    pub edge_index: Option<usize>,
   37|       |    /// Property on an edge reference
   38|       |    pub edge_key: Option<KeyPath>,
   39|       |    /// Label value (the primary scalar after `:`)
   40|       |    pub primary: Option<ScalarValue>,
   41|       |    /// Map, array, or scalar value
   42|       |    pub value: Option<Value>,
   43|       |    pub range: Range,
   44|       |}
   45|       |
   46|       |/// A string value with source position tracking.
   47|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   48|       |pub struct StringValue {
   49|       |    /// The resolved string value (after unquoting).
   50|       |    pub value: String,
   51|       |    /// Original source text (before unquoting).
   52|       |    pub raw: String,
   53|       |    pub range: Range,
   54|       |}
   55|       |
   56|       |/// A comment line.
   57|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   58|       |pub struct Comment {
   59|       |    pub value: String,
   60|       |    pub range: Range,
   61|       |}
   62|       |
   63|       |/// Dotted key path: "server.style.fill"
   64|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   65|       |pub struct KeyPath {
   66|       |    pub segments: Vec<StringValue>,
   67|       |}
   68|       |
   69|       |impl std::fmt::Display for KeyPath {
   70|     16|    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   71|     16|        let dotted = self
   72|     16|            .segments
   73|     16|            .iter()
   74|     16|            .map(|s| s.value.as_str())
   75|     16|            .collect::<Vec<_>>()
   76|     16|            .join(".");
   77|     16|        f.write_str(&dotted)
   78|     16|    }
   79|       |}
   80|       |
   81|       |/// An edge between two keys.
   82|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   83|       |pub struct Edge {
   84|       |    pub src: KeyPath,
   85|       |    pub dst: KeyPath,
   86|       |    /// `<-` (source has arrowhead)
   87|       |    pub src_arrow: bool,
   88|       |    /// `->` (destination has arrowhead)
   89|       |    pub dst_arrow: bool,
   90|       |    pub range: Range,
   91|       |}
   92|       |
   93|       |/// Values that can appear on the right side of `:`.
   94|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   95|       |pub enum Value {
   96|       |    Scalar(ScalarValue),
   97|       |    Map(D2Map),
   98|       |    Array(Vec<Value>),
   99|       |}
  100|       |
  101|       |/// Scalar value types.
  102|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
  103|       |pub enum ScalarValue {
  104|       |    Unquoted(String),
  105|       |    SingleQuoted(String),
  106|       |    DoubleQuoted(String),
  107|       |    Number(f64),
  108|       |    Boolean(bool),
  109|       |}
  110|       |
  111|       |impl ScalarValue {
  112|       |    /// Get the string representation of this scalar.
  113|    297|    pub fn as_str(&self) -> String {
  114|    297|        match self {
  115|    278|            ScalarValue::Unquoted(s)
  116|      0|            | ScalarValue::SingleQuoted(s)
  117|    296|            | ScalarValue::DoubleQuoted(s) => s.clone(),
                                                      ^18
  118|      0|            ScalarValue::Number(n) => n.to_string(),
  119|      1|            ScalarValue::Boolean(b) => b.to_string(),
  120|       |        }
  121|    297|    }
  122|       |}

C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\compiler.rs:
    1|       |//! AST ? D2Graph compilation.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! The compiler walks the AST and builds the D2Graph:
    4|       |//! 1. Collect declarations: walk all Key nodes, build object/edge lists
    5|       |//! 2. Resolve hierarchy: dotted keys create parent-child relationships
    6|       |//! 3. Apply reserved keywords: shape, label, style.*, direction, width, height
    7|       |//! 4. Resolve named colors to hex during style processing
    8|       |//! 5. Validate shape types, style values, connection endpoints
    9|       |//! 6. Set defaults: Rectangle shape, Style fields left as None
   10|       |//! 7. Collect warnings for recognized but deferred keywords
   11|       |
   12|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
   13|       |
   14|       |use petgraph::stable_graph::NodeIndex;
   15|       |
   16|       |use crate::ast::*;
   17|       |use crate::graph::*;
   18|       |use crate::keywords;
   19|       |
   20|       |/// Compilation failure.
   21|       |#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   22|       |#[error("{message}")]
   23|       |pub struct CompileError {
   24|       |    pub message: String,
   25|       |    pub range: Option<Range>,
   26|       |}
   27|       |

  873|       |        assert_eq!(graph.objects.len(), 1);

  877|       |        assert_eq!(obj.shape, ShapeType::Person);
  878|       |        assert!(
  879|       |            !obj.is_container,
  880|       |            "shape with inline property map should NOT be marked as container"
  881|       |        );
  882|       |    }

  888|       |        let obj = &graph.graph[graph.objects[0]];
  889|       |        assert_eq!(obj.shape, ShapeType::Diamond);
  890|       |        assert!(
  891|       |            !obj.is_container,

  898|       |        // A node with both children AND a shape IS a container
  899|       |        let graph = compile_ok("group: {\n  shape: hexagon\n  a\n  b\n}");
  900|       |        let group = &graph.graph[graph.objects[0]];
  901|       |        assert_eq!(group.shape, ShapeType::Hexagon);
  902|       |        assert!(
  903|       |            group.is_container,
  904|       |            "node with children should be a container even if it also has shape"
  905|       |        );
  906|       |        assert_eq!(group.children.len(), 2);
  907|       |    }

  117|       |/// Compute a port point on the boundary of a shape.
  118|       |/// `direction` is the exit/entry side; `port_cross` is the desired cross-axis

  120|    440|fn compute_port(shape: ShapeType, rect: &Rect, direction: Direction, port_cross: f64) -> Point {

  122|       |        ShapeType::Diamond | ShapeType::Hexagon | ShapeType::Circle | ShapeType::Oval => {

  124|     16|            let target = match direction {
  125|      4|                Direction::Right => Point::new(rect.right() + 100.0, port_cross),
  126|      0|                Direction::Left => Point::new(rect.x - 100.0, port_cross),
  127|      6|                Direction::Down => Point::new(port_cross, rect.bottom() + 100.0),
  128|      6|                Direction::Up => Point::new(port_cross, rect.y - 100.0),
  129|       |            };

  890|       |/// Compute a label candidate position at a given percentage along the route.
  891|       |/// Returns the label center point, offset perpendicular to the segment.
  892|       |/// `side` controls which side of the edge the label is placed:
  893|       |///   -1.0 = up/left (default), +1.0 = down/right.
  894|       |/// If the natural offset exceeds `MAX_LABEL_OFFSET`, the label falls back to
  895|       |/// on-edge placement (offset = 0) to avoid drifting too far from the route.
  896|    482|fn label_position_at(
  897|    482|    route: &[Point],
  898|    482|    seg_lengths: &[f64],
  899|    482|    total_length: f64,
  900|    482|    percentage: f64,
  901|    482|    label_width: f64,
  902|    482|    label_height: f64,
  903|    482|    side: f64,
  904|    482|) -> Option<Point> {
  905|    482|    if total_length < 1e-6 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (905:8): [True: 0, False: 198]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (905:8): [True: 0, False: 284]
  ------------------
  906|      0|        return Some(route[0]);
  907|    482|    }
  908|       |
  909|    482|    let target = total_length * percentage.clamp(0.0, 1.0);
  910|    482|    let mut accumulated = 0.0;
  911|       |
  912|    794|    for (i, &seg_len) in seg_lengths.iter().enumerate() {
                                       ^482        ^482   ^482
  913|    794|        if accumulated + seg_len >= target || i == seg_lengths.len() - 1 {
                                                            ^312
  ------------------
  |  Branch (913:12): [True: 198, False: 94]
  |  Branch (913:47): [True: 0, False: 94]
  ------------------

  970|       |/// How far a label's bounding rect extends beyond the bounding box of all nodes.

  979|    482|}

 1234|       |/// Generate a self-loop path as two cubic Bézier segments.
 1235|       |/// Exits from the top of the shape, arcs outward to the upper-right,
 1236|       |/// and re-enters from the right side.
 1237|       |/// Returns 7 points: [start, c1, c2, mid, c3, c4, end] forming two
 1238|       |/// cubic Bézier segments that the SVG renderer consumes as:
 1239|       |///   M start C c1 c2 mid C c3 c4 end
 1240|      5|fn self_loop_route(rect: &crate::geo::Rect) -> Vec<Point> {
 1241|      5|    let cx = rect.x + rect.width / 2.0;
 1242|      5|    let top = rect.y;
 1243|      5|    let right = rect.x + rect.width;
 1244|      5|    let loop_size = rect.width.max(rect.height) * 0.4;
 1245|       |
 1246|       |    // Segment 1: exit from top, arc up and to the right
 1247|      5|    let start = Point::new(cx + rect.width * 0.15, top);
 1248|      5|    let ctrl1 = Point::new(cx + rect.width * 0.15, top - loop_size);
 1249|      5|    let ctrl2 = Point::new(right + loop_size * 0.5, top - loop_size);
 1250|      5|    let mid = Point::new(right + loop_size * 0.5, top + rect.height * 0.25);
 1251|       |
 1252|       |    // Segment 2: arc down and re-enter from the right side
 1253|      5|    let ctrl3 = Point::new(right + loop_size * 0.5, top + rect.height * 0.6);
 1254|      5|    let ctrl4 = Point::new(right + loop_size * 0.1, top + rect.height * 0.35);
 1255|      5|    let end = Point::new(right, top + rect.height * 0.35);
 1256|       |
 1257|      5|    vec![start, ctrl1, ctrl2, mid, ctrl3, ctrl4, end]
 1258|      5|}
 1259|       |
 1260|       |/// Compute arrowhead triangle at the endpoint of an edge.
 1261|       |/// `tip` is the point where the edge meets the shape.
 1262|       |/// `from` is the previous control point (gives direction).
 1263|       |/// Returns three polygon vertices: (tip, left, right).
 1264|    143|pub fn arrowhead_polygon(tip: Point, from: Point, size: f64) -> (Point, Point, Point) {
 1265|    143|    let dx = tip.x - from.x;
 1266|    143|    let dy = tip.y - from.y;
 1267|    143|    let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
 1268|       |
 1269|    143|    if len < 1e-6 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1269:8): [True: 0, False: 124]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1269:8): [True: 1, False: 18]
  ------------------
 1270|       |        // Degenerate: tip ˜ from. Default to pointing downward.
 1271|      1|        return (
 1272|      1|            tip,
 1273|      1|            Point::new(tip.x - size * 0.4, tip.y - size),
 1274|      1|            Point::new(tip.x + size * 0.4, tip.y - size),
 1275|      1|        );
 1276|    142|    }
 1277|       |
 1278|    142|    let ux = dx / len;
 1279|    142|    let uy = dy / len;
 1280|    142|    let base_x = tip.x - ux * size;
 1281|    142|    let base_y = tip.y - uy * size;
 1282|    142|    let half_width = size * 0.4;
 1283|       |
 1284|    142|    let left = Point::new(base_x - uy * half_width, base_y + ux * half_width);
 1285|    142|    let right = Point::new(base_x + uy * half_width, base_y - ux * half_width);
 1286|       |
 1287|    142|    (tip, left, right)
 1288|    143|}
 1289|       |
 1290|       |/// Compute diamond arrowhead at the endpoint of an edge.
 1291|      0|pub fn diamond_arrowhead(tip: Point, from: Point, size: f64) -> [Point; 4] {
 1292|      0|    let dx = tip.x - from.x;
 1293|      0|    let dy = tip.y - from.y;
 1294|      0|    let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
 1295|       |
 1296|      0|    if len < 1e-6 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1296:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1296:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1297|      0|        return [tip; 4];
 1298|      0|    }
 1299|       |
 1300|      0|    let ux = dx / len;
 1301|      0|    let uy = dy / len;
 1302|      0|    let half = size * 0.5;
 1303|      0|    let quarter = size * 0.3;
 1304|       |
 1305|      0|    let mid = Point::new(tip.x - ux * half, tip.y - uy * half);
 1306|      0|    let back = Point::new(tip.x - ux * size, tip.y - uy * size);
 1307|      0|    let left = Point::new(mid.x - uy * quarter, mid.y + ux * quarter);
 1308|      0|    let right = Point::new(mid.x + uy * quarter, mid.y - ux * quarter);
 1309|       |
 1310|      0|    [tip, left, back, right]
 1311|      0|}

 1317|       |/// Collect all ancestors of `node` (inclusive) up to the root.
 1318|    223|fn ancestor_chain(graph: &D2Graph, node: NodeIndex) -> HashSet<NodeIndex> {
 1319|    223|    let mut ancestors = HashSet::new();

 1321|    223|    ancestors.insert(current);
 1322|    496|    while let Some(parent) = graph.graph[current].parent {
                                 ^273

  |  Branch (1322:15): [True: 144, False: 122]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1322:15): [True: 129, False: 101]
  ------------------
 1323|    273|        ancestors.insert(parent);
 1324|    273|        current = parent;
 1325|    273|    }
 1326|    223|    ancestors
 1327|    223|}

 1329|       |/// Find the lowest common ancestor of nodes `a` and `b`.
 1330|    223|fn find_lca(graph: &D2Graph, a: NodeIndex, b: NodeIndex) -> NodeIndex {
 1331|    223|    let a_ancestors = ancestor_chain(graph, a);
 1332|    223|    let mut current = b;
 1333|    223|    if a_ancestors.contains(&current) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1333:8): [True: 0, False: 122]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1333:8): [True: 0, False: 101]
  ------------------
 1334|      0|        return current;
 1335|    223|    }
 1336|    246|    while let Some(parent) = graph.graph[current].parent {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1336:15): [True: 132, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1336:15): [True: 114, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1337|    246|        if a_ancestors.contains(&parent) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1337:12): [True: 122, False: 10]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1337:12): [True: 101, False: 13]
  ------------------
 1338|    223|            return parent;
 1339|     23|        }
 1340|     23|        current = parent;
 1341|       |    }
 1342|      0|    graph.root
 1343|    223|}

 1345|       |/// Walk from `node` upward until we find the direct child of `ancestor`.
 1346|       |/// If `node == ancestor`, returns `node` (handles container-to-descendant edges).
 1347|    443|fn child_ancestor_of(graph: &D2Graph, node: NodeIndex, ancestor: NodeIndex) -> NodeIndex {
 1348|    443|    if node == ancestor {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1348:8): [True: 2, False: 242]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1348:8): [True: 2, False: 197]
  ------------------
 1349|      4|        return node;
 1350|    439|    }
 1351|    439|    let mut current = node;
 1352|    487|    while let Some(parent) = graph.graph[current].parent {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1352:15): [True: 262, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1352:15): [True: 225, False: 0]
  ------------------
 1353|    487|        if parent == ancestor {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1353:12): [True: 242, False: 20]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1353:12): [True: 197, False: 28]
  ------------------
 1354|    439|            return current;
 1355|     48|        }
 1356|     48|        current = parent;
 1357|       |    }
 1358|       |    // Fallback (shouldn't happen in well-formed graph)
 1359|      0|    node
 1360|    443|}
 1361|       |
 1362|       |/// Walk from `container` upward through its parent chain and return the
 1363|       |/// first explicit `direction` override found. If none is set on any
 1364|       |/// ancestor, fall back to `graph.direction`.
 1365|    223|fn effective_direction(graph: &D2Graph, container: NodeIndex) -> Direction {
 1366|    223|    let mut current = container;
 1367|       |    loop {
 1368|    250|        if let Some(dir) = graph.graph[current].direction {
                                  ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1368:16): [True: 0, False: 136]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1368:16): [True: 1, False: 113]
  ------------------
 1369|      1|            return dir;
 1370|    249|        }
 1371|    249|        match graph.graph[current].parent {
 1372|     27|            Some(parent) => current = parent,
 1373|    222|            None => return graph.direction,
 1374|       |        }
 1375|       |    }
 1376|    223|}

 1378|       |#[cfg(test)]

 1380|       |mod tests {
 1381|       |    use super::*;
 1382|       |    use crate::RenderOptions;
 1383|       |
 1384|       |    /// Helper: parse+compile+layout a D2 source string, return the graph.
 1385|       |    fn layout_ok(source: &str) -> crate::graph::D2Graph {
 1386|       |        let ast = crate::parse(source).ast;
 1387|       |        let mut graph = crate::compile(&ast).expect("compile should succeed");
 1388|       |        crate::layout(&mut graph, &RenderOptions::default()).expect("layout should succeed");
 1389|       |        graph
 1390|       |    }
 1391|       |
 1392|       |    /// Find a non-root object by label, return its NodeIndex.
 1393|       |    fn find_node_by_label(
 1394|       |        graph: &crate::graph::D2Graph,
 1395|       |        label: &str,
 1396|       |    ) -> petgraph::stable_graph::NodeIndex {
 1397|       |        for &idx in &graph.objects {
 1398|       |            if graph.graph[idx].label == label {
 1399|       |                return idx;
 1400|       |            }
 1401|       |        }
 1402|       |        panic!("no object with label '{label}' found");
 1403|       |    }
 1404|       |
 1405|       |    /// Find the edge between two nodes identified by label.
 1406|       |    fn find_edge_between<'a>(
 1407|       |        graph: &'a crate::graph::D2Graph,
 1408|       |        src_label: &str,
 1409|       |        dst_label: &str,
 1410|       |    ) -> &'a crate::graph::D2EdgeData {
 1411|       |        let src = find_node_by_label(graph, src_label);
 1412|       |        let dst = find_node_by_label(graph, dst_label);
 1413|       |        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
 1414|       |            if let Some((s, d)) = graph.graph.edge_endpoints(eidx) {
 1415|       |                if s == src && d == dst {
 1416|       |                    return &graph.graph[eidx];
 1417|       |                }
 1418|       |            }
 1419|       |        }
 1420|       |        panic!("no edge from '{src_label}' to '{dst_label}' found");
 1421|       |    }
 1422|       |

 1425|       |    #[test]
 1426|       |    fn test_label_between_nodes() {
 1427|       |        // Vertical layout (default direction=down): label should sit between
 1428|       |        // a's bottom edge and b's top edge.
 1429|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: hello");
 1430|       |
 1431|       |        let a_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "a")]
 1432|       |            .box_
 1433|       |            .expect("a should have a box");
 1434|       |        let b_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "b")]
 1435|       |            .box_
 1436|       |            .expect("b should have a box");
 1437|       |
 1438|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1439|       |        let label_pos = edge
 1440|       |            .label_position
 1441|       |            .expect("labeled edge should have a label_position");
 1442|       |
 1443|       |        let a_bottom = a_rect.bottom();
 1444|       |        let b_top = b_rect.y;
 1445|       |
 1446|       |        assert!(
 1447|       |            label_pos.y > a_bottom && label_pos.y < b_top,
 1448|       |            "label y ({}) should be between a.bottom ({}) and b.top ({})",
 1449|       |            label_pos.y,
 1450|       |            a_bottom,
 1451|       |            b_top,
 1452|       |        );
 1453|       |    }
 1454|       |

 1465|       |            .box_
 1466|       |            .expect("b should have a box");

 1468|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1469|       |        let label_pos = edge
 1470|       |            .label_position
 1471|       |            .expect("labeled edge should have a label_position");
 1472|       |
 1473|       |        let a_right = a_rect.right();
 1474|       |        let b_left = b_rect.x;
 1475|       |

 1477|       |            label_pos.x > a_right && label_pos.x < b_left,

 1500|       |    #[test]
 1501|       |    fn test_arrowhead_polygon() {
 1502|       |        let tip = Point::new(100.0, 50.0);
 1503|       |        let from = Point::new(50.0, 50.0);
 1504|       |        let (t, l, r) = arrowhead_polygon(tip, from, 10.0);
 1505|       |        assert!((t.x - 100.0).abs() < 0.01);
 1506|       |        // Left and right points should be offset perpendicular to the edge direction
 1507|       |        assert!((l.y - r.y).abs() > 1.0); // left and right are on opposite sides
 1508|       |        assert!(l.x < t.x); // both behind the tip
 1509|       |        assert!(r.x < t.x);
 1510|       |    }
 1511|       |
 1512|       |    #[test]
 1513|       |    fn test_arrowhead_degenerate() {
 1514|       |        let tip = Point::new(50.0, 50.0);
 1515|       |        let from = Point::new(50.0, 50.0);
 1516|       |        let (t, _l, _r) = arrowhead_polygon(tip, from, 10.0);
 1517|       |        assert_eq!(t.x, tip.x);
 1518|       |        assert_eq!(t.y, tip.y);
 1519|       |    }

 1523|       |    #[test]
 1524|       |    fn test_find_lca_root_level() {
 1525|       |        // x and y live in different top-level containers ? LCA is root
 1526|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a: { x }\nb: { y }\na.x -> b.y");
 1527|       |        let x = find_node_by_label(&graph, "x");
 1528|       |        let y = find_node_by_label(&graph, "y");
 1529|       |        let lca = find_lca(&graph, x, y);
 1530|       |        assert_eq!(
 1531|       |            lca, graph.root,
 1532|       |            "LCA of nodes in separate top-level containers should be root"
 1533|       |        );
 1534|       |    }
 1535|       |
 1536|       |    #[test]
 1537|       |    fn test_find_lca_siblings() {
 1538|       |        // a and b are siblings inside g ? LCA is g
 1539|       |        let graph = layout_ok("g: { a; b; a -> b }");
 1540|       |        let a = find_node_by_label(&graph, "a");
 1541|       |        let b = find_node_by_label(&graph, "b");
 1542|       |        let g = find_node_by_label(&graph, "g");
 1543|       |        let lca = find_lca(&graph, a, b);
 1544|       |        assert_eq!(lca, g, "LCA of siblings should be their parent container");
 1545|       |    }

 1548|       |    fn test_find_lca_container_to_descendant() {
 1549|       |        // g contains a ? LCA of g and a should be g
 1550|       |        let graph = layout_ok("g: { a }");
 1551|       |        let g = find_node_by_label(&graph, "g");
 1552|       |        let a = find_node_by_label(&graph, "a");
 1553|       |        let lca = find_lca(&graph, g, a);
 1554|       |        assert_eq!(
 1555|       |            lca, g,
 1556|       |            "LCA of container and its child should be the container"
 1557|       |        );
 1558|       |    }

 1561|       |    fn test_child_ancestor_of_direct() {
 1562|       |        // a is a direct child of g
 1563|       |        let graph = layout_ok("g: { a; b }");
 1564|       |        let a = find_node_by_label(&graph, "a");
 1565|       |        let g = find_node_by_label(&graph, "g");
 1566|       |        let result = child_ancestor_of(&graph, a, g);

 1568|       |    }

 1573|       |        let graph = layout_ok("g: { inner: { a } }");

 1575|       |        let g = find_node_by_label(&graph, "g");

 1579|       |            result, inner,
 1580|       |            "should return the immediate child of ancestor on the path to node"

 1633|       |    #[test]
 1634|       |    fn test_ortho_straight_horizontal() {
 1635|       |        // direction: right, a -> b — single forward edge should produce
 1636|       |        // a 2-point straight line with route_type == Orthogonal.
 1637|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b");
 1638|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1639|       |
 1640|       |        assert_eq!(
 1641|       |            edge.route_type,
 1642|       |            RouteType::Orthogonal,
 1643|       |            "non-self-loop edge should be Orthogonal"
 1644|       |        );
 1645|       |        assert_eq!(
 1646|       |            edge.route.len(),
 1647|       |            2,
 1648|       |            "straight horizontal edge should have 2 route points, got {}",
 1649|       |            edge.route.len()
 1650|       |        );
 1651|       |        // Destination x should be to the right of source x
 1652|       |        assert!(
 1653|       |            edge.route[1].x > edge.route[0].x,
 1654|       |            "dst.x ({}) should be right of src.x ({})",
 1655|       |            edge.route[1].x,
 1656|       |            edge.route[0].x,
 1657|       |        );
 1658|       |    }

 1724|       |    #[test]
 1725|       |    fn test_ortho_three_segment() {
 1726|       |        // direction: right, a -> c, b -> c — a and b are in different ranks,
 1727|       |        // the edge from the offset node should have >= 2 route points.
 1728|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c\nb -> c");
 1729|       |        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 1730|       |        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 1731|       |
 1732|       |        assert_eq!(edge_ac.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1733|       |        assert_eq!(edge_bc.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1734|       |
 1735|       |        // At least one of them should have >= 2 points (both should)
 1736|       |        assert!(
 1737|       |            edge_ac.route.len() >= 2,
 1738|       |            "a->c route should have >= 2 points, got {}",
 1739|       |            edge_ac.route.len()
 1740|       |        );
 1741|       |        assert!(
 1742|       |            edge_bc.route.len() >= 2,
 1743|       |            "b->c route should have >= 2 points, got {}",
 1744|       |            edge_bc.route.len()
 1745|       |        );
 1746|       |    }
 1747|       |
 1748|       |    #[test]
 1749|       |    fn test_ortho_same_rank_jog() {
 1750|       |        // Same-rank edges need a jog (detour) since both nodes are at the
 1751|       |        // same primary coordinate. We use two nodes at the same rank by
 1752|       |        // having them both connect to a third node.
 1753|       |        // However, to directly test same-rank we need nodes at the same
 1754|       |        // position. A simpler approach: two nodes with edges to each other
 1755|       |        // creates a forward + backward pair.
 1756|       |        // For a true same-rank test we need parallel edges in the same rank.
 1757|       |        // Use a -> c, b -> c pattern where a and b end up on the same rank.
 1758|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b\na -> c\nb -> c");
 1759|       |        // All edges should be non-empty and orthogonal
 1760|       |        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
 1761|       |            let edata = &graph.graph[eidx];
 1762|       |            assert_eq!(edata.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1763|       |            assert!(
 1764|       |                !edata.route.is_empty(),
 1765|       |                "every edge should have route points"
 1766|       |            );
 1767|       |        }
 1768|       |    }
 1769|       |
 1770|       |    #[test]
 1771|       |    fn test_ortho_backward_edge() {
 1772|       |        // b -> a is a backward edge when direction=right and a is before b.
 1773|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b\nb -> a");
 1774|       |
 1775|       |        // a -> b should be forward
 1776|       |        let edge_ab = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1777|       |        assert_eq!(edge_ab.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1778|       |        assert!(
 1779|       |            !edge_ab.route.is_empty(),
 1780|       |            "forward edge should have route points"
 1781|       |        );
 1782|       |
 1783|       |        // b -> a should be backward
 1784|       |        let edge_ba = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "a");
 1785|       |        assert_eq!(edge_ba.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 1786|       |        assert!(
 1787|       |            !edge_ba.route.is_empty(),
 1788|       |            "backward edge should have route points"
 1789|       |        );
 1790|       |    }
 1791|       |
 1792|       |    #[test]
 1793|       |    fn test_ortho_self_loop_unchanged() {
 1794|       |        // Self-loops should still use Bézier routing with 7 points.
 1795|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> a");
 1796|       |        let a = find_node_by_label(&graph, "a");
 1797|       |        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
 1798|       |            if let Some((s, d)) = graph.graph.edge_endpoints(eidx) {
 1799|       |                if s == a && d == a {
 1800|       |                    let edata = &graph.graph[eidx];
 1801|       |                    assert_eq!(
 1802|       |                        edata.route_type,
 1803|       |                        RouteType::Bezier,
 1804|       |                        "self-loop should use Bezier routing"
 1805|       |                    );
 1806|       |                    assert_eq!(
 1807|       |                        edata.route.len(),
 1808|       |                        7,
 1809|       |                        "self-loop should have 7 points, got {}",
 1810|       |                        edata.route.len()
 1811|       |                    );
 1812|       |                    return;
 1813|       |                }
 1814|       |            }
 1815|       |        }
 1816|       |        panic!("no self-loop edge found");
 1817|       |    }

 1820|       |    fn test_ortho_container_descendant() {

 1824|       |

 1827|       |            RouteType::Orthogonal,

 1833|       |        );
 1834|       |    }

 1836|       |    #[test]
 1837|       |    fn test_ortho_architecture_integration() {
 1838|       |        // Full architecture example — all edges should be routed without panics.
 1839|       |        let source = "\
 1840|       |direction: right
 1841|       |
 1842|       |network: {
 1843|       |  style.stroke: \"#4a90d9\"
 1844|       |  load_balancer.shape: diamond
 1845|       |  load_balancer.style.fill: \"#ffd700\"
 1846|       |  server1
 1847|       |  server2
 1848|       |  load_balancer -> server1: route
 1849|       |  load_balancer -> server2: route
 1850|       |}
 1851|       |
 1852|       |database.shape: cylinder
 1853|       |database.style.fill: \"#228b22\"
 1854|       |
 1855|       |network.server1 -> database: query
 1856|       |network.server2 -> database: query
 1857|       |";
 1858|       |        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 1859|       |
 1860|       |        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
 1861|       |            let edata = &graph.graph[eidx];
 1862|       |            if let Some((s, d)) = graph.graph.edge_endpoints(eidx) {
 1863|       |                if s == d {
 1864|       |                    continue; // skip self-loops
 1865|       |                }
 1866|       |            }
 1867|       |            assert!(
 1868|       |                !edata.route.is_empty(),
 1869|       |                "every non-self-loop edge should have route points"
 1870|       |            );
 1871|       |            assert_eq!(
 1872|       |                edata.route_type,
 1873|       |                RouteType::Orthogonal,
 1874|       |                "every non-self-loop edge should be Orthogonal"
 1875|       |            );
 1876|       |        }
 1877|       |    }
 1878|       |
 1879|       |    #[test]
 1880|       |    fn test_ortho_cross_container() {
 1881|       |        // Cross-container edges (between nodes in different containers).
 1882|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a: { x }\nb: { y }\na.x -> b.y");
 1883|       |
 1884|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "x", "y");
 1885|       |        assert_eq!(
 1886|       |            edge.route_type,
 1887|       |            RouteType::Orthogonal,
 1888|       |            "cross-container edge should be Orthogonal"
 1889|       |        );
 1890|       |        assert!(
 1891|       |            !edge.route.is_empty(),
 1892|       |            "cross-container edge should have route points"
 1893|       |        );
 1894|       |    }

 1896|       |    // --- Stage 4: label placement tests --------------------------------------
 1897|       |
 1898|       |    #[test]
 1899|       |    fn test_ortho_label_horizontal() {
 1900|       |        // Horizontal layout with labeled edge: label should be between nodes
 1901|       |        // and offset above the line (negative Y offset)
 1902|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b: hello");
 1903|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1904|       |        let label_pos = edge
 1905|       |            .label_position
 1906|       |            .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 1907|       |
 1908|       |        let a_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "a")].box_.unwrap();
 1909|       |        let b_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "b")].box_.unwrap();
 1910|       |
 1911|       |        // Label x should be between the two nodes
 1912|       |        assert!(
 1913|       |            label_pos.x > a_rect.right() && label_pos.x < b_rect.x,
 1914|       |            "label x ({}) should be between a.right ({}) and b.left ({})",
 1915|       |            label_pos.x,
 1916|       |            a_rect.right(),
 1917|       |            b_rect.x
 1918|       |        );
 1919|       |
 1920|       |        // For a 2-point straight horizontal route, the label should be offset
 1921|       |        // away from the line (either above or below).
 1922|       |        //
 1923|       |        // NOTE: The design doc (V4 §7.2) predicted side=-1 (above) would win
 1924|       |        // via the >= tie-breaker for symmetric layouts. In practice, the
 1925|       |        // overshoot scoring breaks the tie in favor of side=+1 (below) because
 1926|       |        // the edge route is not at the vertical center of the combined node
 1927|       |        // bounding box — the label overshoots less on the side with more room.
 1928|       |        assert_eq!(edge.route.len(), 2, "straight edge should have 2 points");
 1929|       |        let line_y = (edge.route[0].y + edge.route[1].y) / 2.0;
 1930|       |        assert!(
 1931|       |            (label_pos.y - line_y).abs() > 1.0,
 1932|       |            "label y ({}) should be offset from the line y ({})",
 1933|       |            label_pos.y,
 1934|       |            line_y
 1935|       |        );
 1936|       |    }
 1937|       |
 1938|       |    #[test]
 1939|       |    fn test_ortho_label_vertical() {
 1940|       |        // Vertical layout (default down) with labeled edge: label should be
 1941|       |        // offset away from the line (either left or right).
 1942|       |        //
 1943|       |        // NOTE: The design doc (V4 §7.2) predicted side=-1 (left) would win
 1944|       |        // via the >= tie-breaker for symmetric layouts. In practice, the
 1945|       |        // overshoot scoring breaks the tie in favor of side=+1 (right) because
 1946|       |        // the edge route is not at the horizontal center of the combined node
 1947|       |        // bounding box — the label overshoots less on the side with more room.
 1948|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: hello");
 1949|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1950|       |        let label_pos = edge
 1951|       |            .label_position
 1952|       |            .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 1953|       |
 1954|       |        let a_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "a")].box_.unwrap();
 1955|       |        let b_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "b")].box_.unwrap();
 1956|       |
 1957|       |        // Label y should be between the two nodes
 1958|       |        assert!(
 1959|       |            label_pos.y > a_rect.bottom() && label_pos.y < b_rect.y,
 1960|       |            "label y ({}) should be between a.bottom ({}) and b.top ({})",
 1961|       |            label_pos.y,
 1962|       |            a_rect.bottom(),
 1963|       |            b_rect.y
 1964|       |        );
 1965|       |
 1966|       |        // For a 2-point straight vertical route, the label should be offset
 1967|       |        // away from the line (either left or right)
 1968|       |        assert_eq!(edge.route.len(), 2, "straight edge should have 2 points");
 1969|       |        let line_x = (edge.route[0].x + edge.route[1].x) / 2.0;
 1970|       |        assert!(
 1971|       |            (label_pos.x - line_x).abs() > 1.0,
 1972|       |            "label x ({}) should be offset from the line x ({})",
 1973|       |            label_pos.x,
 1974|       |            line_x
 1975|       |        );
 1976|       |    }
 1977|       |
 1978|       |    #[test]
 1979|       |    fn test_ortho_label_unlabeled() {
 1980|       |        // Unlabeled edge should have no label position
 1981|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b");
 1982|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 1983|       |        assert!(
 1984|       |            edge.label_position.is_none(),
 1985|       |            "unlabeled edge should have no label position"
 1986|       |        );
 1987|       |    }
 1988|       |
 1989|       |    #[test]
 1990|       |    fn test_ortho_labels_spread() {
 1991|       |        // Multiple labeled edges in the same gap should have different label positions
 1992|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c: first\nb -> c: second");
 1993|       |        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 1994|       |        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 1995|       |
 1996|       |        let pos_ac = edge_ac.label_position.expect("a->c should have label");
 1997|       |        let pos_bc = edge_bc.label_position.expect("b->c should have label");
 1998|       |
 1999|       |        // Labels should not be at the exact same position
 2000|       |        let dist = ((pos_ac.x - pos_bc.x).powi(2) + (pos_ac.y - pos_bc.y).powi(2)).sqrt();
 2001|       |        assert!(
 2002|       |            dist > 1.0,
 2003|       |            "labels should be spread apart, got distance {}",
 2004|       |            dist
 2005|       |        );
 2006|       |    }
 2007|       |
 2008|       |    #[test]
 2009|       |    fn test_ortho_channel_spread() {
 2010|       |        // Two edges from different sources to nodes at the same rank should
 2011|       |        // get different channel positions (spread in the same gap).
 2012|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c\nb -> c");
 2013|       |        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 2014|       |        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 2015|       |
 2016|       |        assert_eq!(edge_ac.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 2017|       |        assert_eq!(edge_bc.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 2018|       |
 2019|       |        // Both edges should have been routed
 2020|       |        assert!(!edge_ac.route.is_empty());
 2021|       |        assert!(!edge_bc.route.is_empty());
 2022|       |
 2023|       |        // If both edges have 4 points (3-segment path), their channel positions
 2024|       |        // (the x coordinate of the middle segment) should differ.
 2025|       |        if edge_ac.route.len() == 4 && edge_bc.route.len() == 4 {
 2026|       |            // Channel segment is route[1].x to route[2].x for horizontal
 2027|       |            let channel_ac = edge_ac.route[1].x;
 2028|       |            let channel_bc = edge_bc.route[1].x;
 2029|       |            // They may be the same if both edges are straight lines
 2030|       |            // but if they use channels, the positions should be allocated
 2031|       |            // (this is a best-effort check)
 2032|       |            // At minimum, both should have valid positions
 2033|       |            assert!(channel_ac.is_finite(), "channel_ac should be finite");
 2034|       |            assert!(channel_bc.is_finite(), "channel_bc should be finite");
 2035|       |        }
 2036|       |    }
 2037|       |
 2038|       |    #[test]
 2039|       |    fn test_ortho_deep_nesting() {
 2040|       |        // 3-level nesting with cross-level edge.
 2041|       |        // outer -> inner -> leaf, with an edge from a different top-level node to the leaf.
 2042|       |        let graph = layout_ok("outer: { inner: { leaf } }\npeer\npeer -> outer.inner.leaf");
 2043|       |
 2044|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "peer", "leaf");
 2045|       |        assert_eq!(edge.route_type, RouteType::Orthogonal);
 2046|       |        assert!(
 2047|       |            !edge.route.is_empty(),
 2048|       |            "deep cross-level edge should have route points"
 2049|       |        );
 2050|       |        // The route should have at least 2 points
 2051|       |        assert!(
 2052|       |            edge.route.len() >= 2,
 2053|       |            "deep nesting edge should have >= 2 points, got {}",
 2054|       |            edge.route.len()
 2055|       |        );
 2056|       |    }
 2057|       |
 2058|       |    // --- label placement fix tests (V3 design doc, step 10) ------------------
 2059|       |
 2060|       |    #[test]
 2061|       |    fn test_label_at_route_midpoint() {
 2062|       |        // For a 4-point route, verify the label is at ~50% of total arc length,
 2063|       |        // NOT at the enter segment midpoint. Use a multi-node horizontal layout
 2064|       |        // where at least one edge has a 4-point route.
 2065|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c\nb -> c");
 2066|       |
 2067|       |        // Find an edge with a 4-point route (3-segment S-shape)
 2068|       |        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 2069|       |        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 2070|       |
 2071|       |        // Pick whichever edge has a 4-point route (the offset one)
 2072|       |        let (edge, src_label, dst_label) =
 2073|       |            if edge_ac.route.len() == 4 {
 2074|       |                (edge_ac, "a", "c")
 2075|       |            } else if edge_bc.route.len() == 4 {
 2076|       |                (edge_bc, "b", "c")
 2077|       |            } else {
 2078|       |                // Both edges are 2-point straight lines; this test needs a 4-point route.
 2079|       |                // Add a label and re-layout to force the geometry check on the labeled version.
 2080|       |                let graph2 = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c: label\nb -> c");
 2081|       |                let e = find_edge_between(&graph2, "a", "c");
 2082|       |                if e.route.len() == 4 {
 2083|       |                    let src_rect = graph2.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph2, "a")].box_.unwrap();
 2084|       |                    let dst_rect = graph2.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph2, "c")].box_.unwrap();
 2085|       |                    let label_pos = e.label_position.expect("labeled edge should have position");
 2086|       |                    let gap_mid_x = (src_rect.right() + dst_rect.x) / 2.0;
 2087|       |                    // Label x should be near the gap midpoint (within 40% of gap width)
 2088|       |                    let gap_width = (dst_rect.x - src_rect.right()).abs();
 2089|       |                    assert!(
 2090|       |                    (label_pos.x - gap_mid_x).abs() < gap_width * 0.4,
 2091|       |                    "label x ({}) should be near gap midpoint ({}) within 40% of gap width ({})",
 2092|       |                    label_pos.x, gap_mid_x, gap_width
 2093|       |                );
 2094|       |                    return;
 2095|       |                }
 2096|       |                // If still no 4-point route, the test passes vacuously
 2097|       |                return;
 2098|       |            };
 2099|       |
 2100|       |        // For the found 4-point edge, add a label and verify
 2101|       |        let labeled_src = format!(
 2102|       |            "direction: right\n{} -> {}: lbl\nb -> c",
 2103|       |            src_label, dst_label
 2104|       |        );
 2105|       |        let graph2 = layout_ok(&labeled_src);
 2106|       |        let edge2 = find_edge_between(&graph2, src_label, dst_label);
 2107|       |
 2108|       |        if edge2.route.len() >= 4 {
 2109|       |            let src_rect = graph2.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph2, src_label)]
 2110|       |                .box_
 2111|       |                .unwrap();
 2112|       |            let dst_rect = graph2.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph2, dst_label)]
 2113|       |                .box_
 2114|       |                .unwrap();
 2115|       |            let label_pos = edge2
 2116|       |                .label_position
 2117|       |                .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 2118|       |
 2119|       |            // The label x should be near the midpoint of the gap between source and dest
 2120|       |            let gap_mid_x = (src_rect.right() + dst_rect.x) / 2.0;
 2121|       |            let gap_width = (dst_rect.x - src_rect.right()).abs();
 2122|       |            assert!(
 2123|       |                (label_pos.x - gap_mid_x).abs() < gap_width * 0.4,
 2124|       |                "label x ({}) should be near gap midpoint ({}) within 40% of gap width ({})",
 2125|       |                label_pos.x,
 2126|       |                gap_mid_x,
 2127|       |                gap_width
 2128|       |            );
 2129|       |        }
 2130|       |        let _ = edge; // suppress unused warning
 2131|       |    }
 2132|       |
 2133|       |    #[test]
 2134|       |    fn test_label_offset_horizontal() {
 2135|       |        // For a horizontal 2-point route, verify the label is offset away from
 2136|       |        // the route line by at least label_height / 2 (on either side).
 2137|       |        //
 2138|       |        // NOTE: The design doc (V4 §7.2) predicted side=-1 (above) would win
 2139|       |        // for symmetric layouts. In practice, the overshoot scoring breaks the
 2140|       |        // tie in favor of the side with more room within the node bounding box.
 2141|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b: hello");
 2142|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2143|       |        let label_pos = edge
 2144|       |            .label_position
 2145|       |            .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 2146|       |
 2147|       |        assert_eq!(
 2148|       |            edge.route.len(),
 2149|       |            2,
 2150|       |            "straight horizontal edge should have 2 points"
 2151|       |        );
 2152|       |        let line_y = (edge.route[0].y + edge.route[1].y) / 2.0;
 2153|       |
 2154|       |        // Label should be offset from the line (either above or below)
 2155|       |        let offset = (label_pos.y - line_y).abs();
 2156|       |        assert!(
 2157|       |            offset >= edge.label_height / 2.0,
 2158|       |            "vertical offset ({}) should be >= label_height/2 ({})",
 2159|       |            offset,
 2160|       |            edge.label_height / 2.0
 2161|       |        );
 2162|       |    }
 2163|       |
 2164|       |    #[test]
 2165|       |    fn test_label_offset_vertical() {
 2166|       |        // For a vertical 2-point route, verify the label is offset away from
 2167|       |        // the route line by at least label_width / 2 (on either side).
 2168|       |        //
 2169|       |        // NOTE: The design doc (V4 §7.2) predicted side=-1 (left) would win
 2170|       |        // for symmetric layouts. In practice, the overshoot scoring breaks the
 2171|       |        // tie in favor of the side with more room within the node bounding box.
 2172|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: hello");
 2173|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2174|       |        let label_pos = edge
 2175|       |            .label_position
 2176|       |            .expect("labeled edge should have position");
 2177|       |
 2178|       |        assert_eq!(
 2179|       |            edge.route.len(),
 2180|       |            2,
 2181|       |            "straight vertical edge should have 2 points"
 2182|       |        );
 2183|       |        let line_x = (edge.route[0].x + edge.route[1].x) / 2.0;
 2184|       |
 2185|       |        // Label should be offset from the line (either left or right)
 2186|       |        let offset = (label_pos.x - line_x).abs();
 2187|       |        assert!(
 2188|       |            offset >= edge.label_width / 2.0,
 2189|       |            "horizontal offset ({}) should be >= label_width/2 ({})",
 2190|       |            offset,
 2191|       |            edge.label_width / 2.0
 2192|       |        );
 2193|       |    }
 2194|       |
 2195|       |    #[test]
 2196|       |    fn test_label_no_node_overlap() {
 2197|       |        // The bug test. Architecture diagram where labels frequently overlapped
 2198|       |        // destination nodes. Verify that no label bounding rect overlaps any
 2199|       |        // non-endpoint leaf node's bounding box.
 2200|       |        let source = "\
 2201|       |direction: right
 2202|       |lb: Load Balancer
 2203|       |gw: API Gateway
 2204|       |auth: Auth
 2205|       |redis: Redis
 2206|       |orders: Orders
 2207|       |pg: PostgreSQL
 2208|       |lb -> gw: HTTPS
 2209|       |gw -> auth: verify
 2210|       |auth -> redis: sessions
 2211|       |gw -> orders: route
 2212|       |orders -> pg: CRUD";
 2213|       |        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 2214|       |
 2215|       |        // Collect all leaf node rects (non-container, non-root)
 2216|       |        let leaf_rects: Vec<(NodeIndex, Rect)> = graph
 2217|       |            .objects
 2218|       |            .iter()
 2219|       |            .filter(|&&idx| idx != graph.root && !graph.graph[idx].is_container)
 2220|       |            .filter_map(|&idx| graph.graph[idx].box_.map(|r| (idx, r)))
 2221|       |            .collect();
 2222|       |
 2223|       |        // For each labeled edge, check label bounding rect vs non-endpoint leaf nodes
 2224|       |        let labeled_edges: Vec<(&str, &str)> = vec![
 2225|       |            ("Load Balancer", "API Gateway"),
 2226|       |            ("API Gateway", "Auth"),
 2227|       |            ("Auth", "Redis"),
 2228|       |            ("API Gateway", "Orders"),
 2229|       |            ("Orders", "PostgreSQL"),
 2230|       |        ];
 2231|       |
 2232|       |        for (src_label, dst_label) in &labeled_edges {
 2233|       |            let src_idx = find_node_by_label(&graph, src_label);
 2234|       |            let dst_idx = find_node_by_label(&graph, dst_label);
 2235|       |            let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, src_label, dst_label);
 2236|       |            let label_pos = edge.label_position.unwrap_or_else(|| {
 2237|       |                panic!(
 2238|       |                    "edge {} -> {} should have a label position",
 2239|       |                    src_label, dst_label
 2240|       |                )
 2241|       |            });
 2242|       |            let lw = edge.label_width;
 2243|       |            let lh = edge.label_height;
 2244|       |            assert!(
 2245|       |                lw > 0.0,
 2246|       |                "label_width should be > 0 for {} -> {}",
 2247|       |                src_label,
 2248|       |                dst_label
 2249|       |            );
 2250|       |            assert!(
 2251|       |                lh > 0.0,
 2252|       |                "label_height should be > 0 for {} -> {}",
 2253|       |                src_label,
 2254|       |                dst_label
 2255|       |            );
 2256|       |
 2257|       |            // Build label bounding rect with halo padding (matches nudge logic)
 2258|       |            let halo = LABEL_HALO_PADDING;
 2259|       |            let label_rect = Rect::new(
 2260|       |                label_pos.x - lw / 2.0 - halo,
 2261|       |                label_pos.y - lh / 2.0 - halo,
 2262|       |                lw + halo * 2.0,
 2263|       |                lh + halo * 2.0,
 2264|       |            );
 2265|       |
 2266|       |            // Check against all non-endpoint leaf nodes
 2267|       |            for &(nidx, nrect) in &leaf_rects {
 2268|       |                if nidx == src_idx || nidx == dst_idx {
 2269|       |                    continue; // skip endpoints — overlap with endpoints is acceptable
 2270|       |                }
 2271|       |                assert!(
 2272|       |                    !label_rect.intersects(&nrect),
 2273|       |                    "label for edge {} -> {} overlaps non-endpoint node {:?} \
 2274|       |                     (label_rect: x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1}, \
 2275|       |                      node_rect: x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1})",
 2276|       |                    src_label,
 2277|       |                    dst_label,
 2278|       |                    graph.graph[nidx].label,
 2279|       |                    label_rect.x,
 2280|       |                    label_rect.y,
 2281|       |                    label_rect.width,
 2282|       |                    label_rect.height,
 2283|       |                    nrect.x,
 2284|       |                    nrect.y,
 2285|       |                    nrect.width,
 2286|       |                    nrect.height,
 2287|       |                );
 2288|       |            }
 2289|       |        }
 2290|       |    }
 2291|       |
 2292|       |    #[test]
 2293|       |    fn test_label_dimensions_stored() {
 2294|       |        // After routing, verify label_width > 0 and label_height > 0
 2295|       |        // for every labeled edge.
 2296|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: foo\nb -> c: bar");
 2297|       |

 2305|       |            edge_ab.label_height > 0.0,

 2314|       |            edge_bc.label_width
 2315|       |        );
 2316|       |        assert!(
 2317|       |            edge_bc.label_height > 0.0,
 2318|       |            "b->c label_height should be > 0, got {}",

 2320|       |        );
 2321|       |    }

 2323|       |    #[test]
 2324|       |    fn test_self_loop_label_dimensions() {
 2325|       |        // Verify that self-loop edges also have label_width > 0 and label_height > 0.
 2326|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> a: loop");
 2327|       |
 2328|       |        let a = find_node_by_label(&graph, "a");
 2329|       |        let mut found = false;
 2330|       |        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
 2331|       |            if let Some((s, d)) = graph.graph.edge_endpoints(eidx) {
 2332|       |                if s == a && d == a {
 2333|       |                    let edata = &graph.graph[eidx];
 2334|       |                    assert!(
 2335|       |                        edata.label_width > 0.0,
 2336|       |                        "self-loop label_width should be > 0, got {}",
 2337|       |                        edata.label_width
 2338|       |                    );
 2339|       |                    assert!(
 2340|       |                        edata.label_height > 0.0,
 2341|       |                        "self-loop label_height should be > 0, got {}",
 2342|       |                        edata.label_height
 2343|       |                    );
 2344|       |                    found = true;
 2345|       |                    break;
 2346|       |                }
 2347|       |            }
 2348|       |        }
 2349|       |        assert!(found, "self-loop edge on 'a' should exist");
 2350|       |    }
 2351|       |
 2352|       |    #[test]
 2353|       |    fn test_nudge_finds_clear_position() {
 2354|       |        // Three-node chain where nodes are relatively close. The nudge mechanism
 2355|       |        // should ensure labels don't overlap any non-endpoint leaf node.
 2356|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b: first\nb -> c: second\na -> c: direct");
 2357|       |
 2358|       |        let node_rects: Vec<(String, crate::geo::Rect)> = graph
 2359|       |            .objects
 2360|       |            .iter()
 2361|       |            .filter(|&&idx| idx != graph.root && !graph.graph[idx].is_container)
 2362|       |            .filter_map(|&idx| {
 2363|       |                graph.graph[idx]
 2364|       |                    .box_
 2365|       |                    .map(|r| (graph.graph[idx].label.clone(), r))
 2366|       |            })
 2367|       |            .collect();
 2368|       |
 2369|       |        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
 2370|       |            let edge = &graph.graph[eidx];
 2371|       |            let pos = match edge.label_position {
 2372|       |                Some(p) => p,
 2373|       |                None => continue,
 2374|       |            };
 2375|       |            if edge.label_width < 1.0 || edge.label_height < 1.0 {
 2376|       |                continue;
 2377|       |            }
 2378|       |
 2379|       |            let (src, dst) = graph.graph.edge_endpoints(eidx).unwrap();
 2380|       |            let pad = LABEL_HALO_PADDING;
 2381|       |            let lr = crate::geo::Rect::new(
 2382|       |                pos.x - edge.label_width / 2.0 - pad,
 2383|       |                pos.y - edge.label_height / 2.0 - pad,
 2384|       |                edge.label_width + pad * 2.0,
 2385|       |                edge.label_height + pad * 2.0,
 2386|       |            );
 2387|       |
 2388|       |            for &(ref name, rect) in &node_rects {
 2389|       |                let node_idx = find_node_by_label(&graph, name);
 2390|       |                if node_idx == src || node_idx == dst {
 2391|       |                    continue;
 2392|       |                }
 2393|       |                assert!(
 2394|       |                    !lr.intersects(&rect),
 2395|       |                    "label at ({:.1},{:.1}) overlaps non-endpoint node '{}'",
 2396|       |                    pos.x,
 2397|       |                    pos.y,
 2398|       |                    name
 2399|       |                );
 2400|       |            }
 2401|       |        }
 2402|       |    }
 2403|       |
 2404|       |    #[test]
 2405|       |    fn test_parallel_labels_no_overlap() {
 2406|       |        // Two labeled edges sharing the same gap: their label rects should not overlap.
 2407|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> c: alpha\nb -> c: beta");
 2408|       |
 2409|       |        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 2410|       |        let edge_bc = find_edge_between(&graph, "b", "c");
 2411|       |
 2412|       |        let pos_ac = edge_ac.label_position.expect("a->c label");
 2413|       |        let pos_bc = edge_bc.label_position.expect("b->c label");
 2414|       |
 2415|       |        let pad = LABEL_HALO_PADDING;
 2416|       |        let lr_ac = crate::geo::Rect::new(
 2417|       |            pos_ac.x - edge_ac.label_width / 2.0 - pad,
 2418|       |            pos_ac.y - edge_ac.label_height / 2.0 - pad,
 2419|       |            edge_ac.label_width + pad * 2.0,
 2420|       |            edge_ac.label_height + pad * 2.0,
 2421|       |        );
 2422|       |        let lr_bc = crate::geo::Rect::new(
 2423|       |            pos_bc.x - edge_bc.label_width / 2.0 - pad,
 2424|       |            pos_bc.y - edge_bc.label_height / 2.0 - pad,
 2425|       |            edge_bc.label_width + pad * 2.0,
 2426|       |            edge_bc.label_height + pad * 2.0,
 2427|       |        );
 2428|       |
 2429|       |        assert!(!lr_ac.intersects(&lr_bc),
 2430|       |            "parallel labels should not overlap: ac at ({:.1},{:.1}) size {:.1}x{:.1}, bc at ({:.1},{:.1}) size {:.1}x{:.1}",
 2431|       |            pos_ac.x, pos_ac.y, edge_ac.label_width, edge_ac.label_height,
 2432|       |            pos_bc.x, pos_bc.y, edge_bc.label_width, edge_bc.label_height);
 2433|       |    }

 2436|       |
 2437|       |    #[test]
 2438|       |    fn test_detour_avoids_intermediate_node() {
 2439|       |        // 3-node vertical chain: a -> b, a -> c, b -> c
 2440|       |        // The a -> c edge should not pass through b's rect
 2441|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b\na -> c\nb -> c");
 2442|       |
 2443|       |        let b_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "b")].box_.unwrap();
 2444|       |        let edge_ac = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "c");
 2445|       |
 2446|       |        // Verify route has more than 2 points (detour was applied)
 2447|       |        assert!(
 2448|       |            edge_ac.route.len() > 2,
 2449|       |            "a->c should have a detour route (>2 points), got {}",
 2450|       |            edge_ac.route.len()
 2451|       |        );
 2452|       |
 2453|       |        // Check that no segment of a->c route intersects b's rect
 2454|       |        for i in 0..edge_ac.route.len().saturating_sub(1) {
 2455|       |            assert!(
 2456|       |                !ortho_segment_hits_rect(&edge_ac.route[i], &edge_ac.route[i + 1], &b_rect, 2.0),
 2457|       |                "a->c route segment {}-{} at ({:.1},{:.1})->({:.1},{:.1}) should not pass through b's rect \
 2458|       |                 (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1})",
 2459|       |                i, i + 1,
 2460|       |                edge_ac.route[i].x, edge_ac.route[i].y,
 2461|       |                edge_ac.route[i + 1].x, edge_ac.route[i + 1].y,
 2462|       |                b_rect.x, b_rect.y, b_rect.width, b_rect.height,
 2463|       |            );
 2464|       |        }
 2465|       |    }

 2494|       |    fn test_detour_no_false_positive() {

 2506|       |
 2507|       |    // --- V4 design doc tests (Section 7.1) -----------------------------------
 2508|       |
 2509|       |    /// Parse the viewBox attribute from an SVG string.
 2510|       |    /// Returns (x, y, width, height).
 2511|       |    fn parse_viewbox(svg: &str) -> (f64, f64, f64, f64) {
 2512|       |        let start = svg.find("viewBox=\"").expect("SVG should have viewBox") + 9;
 2513|       |        let end = svg[start..].find('"').expect("viewBox should close") + start;
 2514|       |        let parts: Vec<f64> = svg[start..end]
 2515|       |            .split_whitespace()
 2516|       |            .map(|s| {
 2517|       |                s.parse::<f64>()
 2518|       |                    .expect("viewBox component should be numeric")
 2519|       |            })
 2520|       |            .collect();
 2521|       |        assert_eq!(parts.len(), 4, "viewBox should have 4 components");
 2522|       |        (parts[0], parts[1], parts[2], parts[3])
 2523|       |    }
 2524|       |
 2525|       |    /// Build a viewBox Rect from parsed (x, y, w, h) tuple.
 2526|       |    fn viewbox_rect(vb: (f64, f64, f64, f64)) -> crate::geo::Rect {
 2527|       |        crate::geo::Rect::new(vb.0, vb.1, vb.2, vb.3)
 2528|       |    }
 2529|       |
 2530|       |    /// Build a label bounding rect (with halo padding) for an edge.
 2531|       |    fn edge_label_rect(edge: &crate::graph::D2EdgeData) -> crate::geo::Rect {
 2532|       |        let pos = edge
 2533|       |            .label_position
 2534|       |            .expect("edge should have label_position");
 2535|       |        let pad = LABEL_HALO_PADDING;
 2536|       |        crate::geo::Rect::new(
 2537|       |            pos.x - edge.label_width / 2.0 - pad,
 2538|       |            pos.y - edge.label_height / 2.0 - pad,
 2539|       |            edge.label_width + pad * 2.0,
 2540|       |            edge.label_height + pad * 2.0,
 2541|       |        )
 2542|       |    }
 2543|       |
 2544|       |    /// Check that rect `inner` is fully contained within rect `outer`.
 2545|       |    fn rect_contains(outer: &crate::geo::Rect, inner: &crate::geo::Rect) -> bool {
 2546|       |        inner.x >= outer.x
 2547|       |            && inner.y >= outer.y
 2548|       |            && inner.right() <= outer.right()
 2549|       |            && inner.bottom() <= outer.bottom()
 2550|       |    }
 2551|       |
 2552|       |    #[test]
 2553|       |    fn test_viewbox_includes_labels() {
 2554|       |        // Parse SVG output from a simple labeled-edge diagram, verify the viewBox
 2555|       |        // contains all edge label bounding rects.
 2556|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: hello");
 2557|       |        let svg = crate::svg_render::render(&graph, &RenderOptions::default());
 2558|       |
 2559|       |        let vb = parse_viewbox(&svg);
 2560|       |        let vb_rect = viewbox_rect(vb);
 2561|       |
 2562|       |        // Every labeled edge's bounding rect must fit within the viewBox
 2563|       |        for &eidx in &graph.edges {
 2564|       |            let edge = &graph.graph[eidx];
 2565|       |            if edge.label_position.is_none() || edge.label_width <= 0.0 {
 2566|       |                continue;
 2567|       |            }
 2568|       |            let lr = edge_label_rect(edge);
 2569|       |            assert!(
 2570|       |                rect_contains(&vb_rect, &lr),
 2571|       |                "label rect (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1}) should be contained in \
 2572|       |                 viewBox (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1})",
 2573|       |                lr.x,
 2574|       |                lr.y,
 2575|       |                lr.width,
 2576|       |                lr.height,
 2577|       |                vb_rect.x,
 2578|       |                vb_rect.y,
 2579|       |                vb_rect.width,
 2580|       |                vb_rect.height,
 2581|       |            );
 2582|       |        }
 2583|       |    }
 2584|       |
 2585|       |    #[test]
 2586|       |    fn test_label_not_clipped() {
 2587|       |        // The motivating example: 4-node vertical chain with labels on each edge.
 2588|       |        // Verify no label bounding rect extends beyond the SVG viewBox.
 2589|       |        let source = "\
 2590|       |client -> api: REST/JSON
 2591|       |api -> cache: get/set
 2592|       |cache -> db: SQL queries";
 2593|       |        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 2594|       |        let svg = crate::svg_render::render(&graph, &RenderOptions::default());
 2595|       |
 2596|       |        let vb = parse_viewbox(&svg);
 2597|       |        let vb_rect = viewbox_rect(vb);
 2598|       |
 2599|       |        let labeled_edges: Vec<(&str, &str)> =
 2600|       |            vec![("client", "api"), ("api", "cache"), ("cache", "db")];
 2601|       |
 2602|       |        for (src_label, dst_label) in &labeled_edges {
 2603|       |            let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, src_label, dst_label);
 2604|       |            assert!(
 2605|       |                edge.label_position.is_some(),
 2606|       |                "edge {} -> {} should have a label position",
 2607|       |                src_label,
 2608|       |                dst_label
 2609|       |            );
 2610|       |            let lr = edge_label_rect(edge);
 2611|       |            assert!(
 2612|       |                rect_contains(&vb_rect, &lr),
 2613|       |                "label for {} -> {} (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1}) extends beyond \
 2614|       |                 viewBox (x={:.1} y={:.1} w={:.1} h={:.1})",
 2615|       |                src_label,
 2616|       |                dst_label,
 2617|       |                lr.x,
 2618|       |                lr.y,
 2619|       |                lr.width,
 2620|       |                lr.height,
 2621|       |                vb_rect.x,
 2622|       |                vb_rect.y,
 2623|       |                vb_rect.width,
 2624|       |                vb_rect.height,
 2625|       |            );
 2626|       |        }
 2627|       |    }
 2628|       |
 2629|       |    /// Test that Fix B places labels on the side with better clearance when
 2630|       |    /// the layout is asymmetric (one side blocked by a node).
 2631|       |    #[test]
 2632|       |    fn test_label_side_asymmetric() {
 2633|       |        // Vertical layout: a at top, b at bottom, c to the right of the a->b edge.
 2634|       |        // The label on a->b should avoid c by going left (or at least not overlapping c).
 2635|       |        let source = "\
 2636|       |direction: down
 2637|       |a -> b: hi
 2638|       |a -> c
 2639|       |";
 2640|       |        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 2641|       |
 2642|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2643|       |        let label_pos = edge.label_position.expect("label position should exist");
 2644|       |
 2645|       |        // c is to the right of the a->b edge. The label should not overlap c.
 2646|       |        let c_rect = graph.graph[find_node_by_label(&graph, "c")]
 2647|       |            .box_
 2648|       |            .expect("c should have a box");
 2649|       |        let lr = edge_label_rect(edge);
 2650|       |        assert!(
 2651|       |            !lr.intersects(&c_rect),
 2652|       |            "label rect {:?} should not overlap node c rect {:?}",
 2653|       |            lr,
 2654|       |            c_rect
 2655|       |        );
 2656|       |
 2657|       |        // Verify the label is offset from the edge line (not zero — "hi" is 2 chars,
 2658|       |        // well below the 72px Fix C threshold)
 2659|       |        assert!(
 2660|       |            edge.label_width < 72.0,
 2661|       |            "label should be below Fix C threshold"
 2662|       |        );
 2663|       |        let line_x = (edge.route[0].x + edge.route.last().unwrap().x) / 2.0;
 2664|       |        let x_offset = (label_pos.x - line_x).abs();
 2665|       |        assert!(
 2666|       |            x_offset > 1.0,
 2667|       |            "label should be offset from the edge line, got offset {}",
 2668|       |            x_offset
 2669|       |        );
 2670|       |    }
 2671|       |
 2672|       |    #[test]
 2673|       |    fn test_wide_label_on_edge() {
 2674|       |        // "SQL queries" is 11 chars at default font (7.7px/char) = ~84.7px width.
 2675|       |        // Natural offset = 84.7/2 + 4 = 46.35 > MAX_LABEL_OFFSET (40).
 2676|       |        // Fix C should center it on the edge (offset = 0).
 2677|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b: SQL queries");
 2678|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2679|       |        let label_pos = edge
 2680|       |            .label_position
 2681|       |            .expect("labeled edge should have label_position");
 2682|       |
 2683|       |        // Default layout is vertical. For a vertical edge, the label is offset
 2684|       |        // in the x-direction. The route line x is at the midpoint of the route.
 2685|       |        assert_eq!(
 2686|       |            edge.route.len(),
 2687|       |            2,
 2688|       |            "straight vertical edge should have 2 points"
 2689|       |        );
 2690|       |        let line_x = (edge.route[0].x + edge.route[1].x) / 2.0;
 2691|       |
 2692|       |        // With Fix C, a wide label should have offset = 0 (centered on line).
 2693|       |        // Allow a small tolerance for floating-point and nudge adjustments.
 2694|       |        let x_offset = (label_pos.x - line_x).abs();
 2695|       |        assert!(
 2696|       |            x_offset < 5.0,
 2697|       |            "wide label ('SQL queries', width={:.1}) should be centered on edge \
 2698|       |             (line_x={:.1}, label_x={:.1}, offset={:.1}), expected offset < 5.0",
 2699|       |            edge.label_width,
 2700|       |            line_x,
 2701|       |            label_pos.x,
 2702|       |            x_offset,
 2703|       |        );
 2704|       |
 2705|       |        // Verify the label width is indeed > 72px (the threshold)
 2706|       |        assert!(
 2707|       |            edge.label_width > 72.0,
 2708|       |            "label width ({:.1}) should exceed the 72px threshold for on-edge placement",
 2709|       |            edge.label_width,
 2710|       |        );
 2711|       |    }
 2712|       |
 2713|       |    /// Test that Fix B's overshoot tie-breaking prefers placing labels toward
 2714|       |    /// the interior of the diagram (less overshoot beyond node bounding box).
 2715|       |    #[test]
 2716|       |    fn test_overshoot_prefers_inward() {
 2717|       |        // Horizontal layout: a -> b -> c -> d, with a->b at the left boundary.
 2718|       |        // The label on a->b should prefer the side closer to diagram center.
 2719|       |        let source = "\
 2720|       |direction: right
 2721|       |a -> b: tag
 2722|       |b -> c
 2723|       |c -> d
 2724|       |";
 2725|       |        let graph = layout_ok(source);
 2726|       |
 2727|       |        let edge = find_edge_between(&graph, "a", "b");
 2728|       |        let _label_pos = edge.label_position.expect("label position");
 2729|       |
 2730|       |        // Compute the node bounding box
 2731|       |        let node_bounds = {
 2732|       |            let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
 2733|       |            let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
 2734|       |            let mut max_x = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
 2735|       |            let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
 2736|       |            for &idx in &graph.objects {
 2737|       |                if idx == graph.root || graph.graph[idx].is_container {
 2738|       |                    continue;
 2739|       |                }
 2740|       |                if let Some(r) = graph.graph[idx].box_ {
 2741|       |                    min_x = min_x.min(r.x);
 2742|       |                    min_y = min_y.min(r.y);
 2743|       |                    max_x = max_x.max(r.x + r.width);
 2744|       |                    max_y = max_y.max(r.y + r.height);
 2745|       |                }
 2746|       |            }
 2747|       |            (min_x, min_y, max_x, max_y)
 2748|       |        };
 2749|       |
 2750|       |        // The a->b edge is horizontal, so the label offset is vertical (up or down).
 2751|       |        // Compute the label rect's overshoot in each vertical direction.
 2752|       |        let lr = edge_label_rect(edge);
 2753|       |        let top_overshoot = (node_bounds.1 - lr.y).max(0.0);
 2754|       |        let bottom_overshoot = (lr.y + lr.height - node_bounds.3).max(0.0);
 2755|       |
 2756|       |        // The total overshoot should be small — the label should stay near the
 2757|       |        // diagram's vertical extent. With 4 nodes in a horizontal row, the
 2758|       |        // vertical extent is just the node height, and the label should be
 2759|       |        // placed to minimize vertical overshoot.
 2760|       |        let total_overshoot = top_overshoot + bottom_overshoot;
 2761|       |        assert!(
 2762|       |            total_overshoot < edge.label_height + LABEL_HALO_PADDING * 2.0,
 2763|       |            "total vertical overshoot ({:.1}) should be less than one label height ({:.1})",
 2764|       |            total_overshoot,
 2765|       |            edge.label_height + LABEL_HALO_PADDING * 2.0
 2766|       |        );
 2767|       |    }
 2768|       |

    1|       |//! Geometry primitives for D2 layout and rendering.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! All coordinates use top-left origin convention:
    4|       |//! - x increases rightward
    5|       |//! - y increases downward
    6|       |//! - Rect.x/y is the top-left corner of the rectangle
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! This matches SVG's coordinate system, so no conversion is needed
    9|       |//! when generating SVG output.
   10|       |
   11|       |/// A 2D point.
   12|       |#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]
   13|       |pub struct Point {
   14|       |    pub x: f64,
   15|       |    pub y: f64,
   16|       |}
   17|       |
   18|       |impl Point {
   19|  1.97k|    pub fn new(x: f64, y: f64) -> Self {
   20|  1.97k|        Self { x, y }
   21|  1.97k|    }
   22|       |
   23|       |    /// Euclidean distance to another point.
   24|    214|    pub fn distance_to(&self, other: &Point) -> f64 {
   25|    214|        let dx = self.x - other.x;
   26|    214|        let dy = self.y - other.y;
   27|    214|        (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt()
   28|    214|    }
   29|       |}
   30|       |

  136|       |        let b = Rect::new(5.0, 5.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  137|       |        assert!(a.intersects(&b));
  138|       |        assert!(b.intersects(&a));
  139|       |

  141|       |        let c = Rect::new(10.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  142|       |        assert!(!a.intersects(&c), "touching rects should not intersect");
  143|       |        assert!(!c.intersects(&a));
  144|       |
