  146|       |        let d = Rect::new(20.0, 20.0, 5.0, 5.0);
  147|       |        assert!(!a.intersects(&d));
  148|       |        assert!(!d.intersects(&a));
  149|       |

  151|       |        let outer = Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, 100.0, 100.0);
  152|       |        let inner = Rect::new(10.0, 10.0, 20.0, 20.0);
  153|       |        assert!(outer.intersects(&inner));
  154|       |        assert!(inner.intersects(&outer));

  158|       |    fn test_rect_min_separation() {
  159|       |        // Positive: separated horizontally
  160|       |        let a = Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  161|       |        let b = Rect::new(15.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  162|       |        let sep = a.min_separation(&b);
  163|       |        assert!((sep - 5.0).abs() < 1e-10, "expected 5.0, got {}", sep);
  164|       |
  165|       |        // Zero: touching
  166|       |        let c = Rect::new(10.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  167|       |        let sep = a.min_separation(&c);
  168|       |        assert!(sep.abs() < 1e-10, "expected 0.0, got {}", sep);
  169|       |
  170|       |        // Negative: overlapping
  171|       |        let d = Rect::new(5.0, 5.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  172|       |        let sep = a.min_separation(&d);
  173|       |        assert!(sep < 0.0, "expected negative, got {}", sep);
  174|       |        // Overlap on x: min(10,15) - max(0,5) = 10-5 = 5, so sep_x = -5
  175|       |        // Overlap on y: min(10,15) - max(0,5) = 10-5 = 5, so sep_y = -5
  176|       |        // Both negative ? max(-5, -5) = -5
  177|       |        assert!((sep - (-5.0)).abs() < 1e-10, "expected -5.0, got {}", sep);
  178|       |
  179|       |        // Both axes separated: returns the tighter gap (min)
  180|       |        let e = Rect::new(15.0, 20.0, 10.0, 10.0);
  181|       |        // e is 5px right of a and 10px below a
  182|       |        // sep_x = 5.0, sep_y = 10.0 ? min_separation = 5.0
  183|       |        let sep = a.min_separation(&e);
  184|       |        assert!(
  185|       |            (sep - 5.0).abs() < 1e-10,
  186|       |            "both-axes-separated should return min gap, got {}",
  187|       |            sep
  188|       |        );

C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\graph.rs:
    1|       |//! Graph IR types for D2 diagram compilation and layout.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! The graph IR is the output of the compiler (Phase 2) and the input
    4|       |//! to the layout engine (Phase 4). It uses petgraph's `StableDiGraph`
    5|       |//! as the backbone, with `D2Object` for nodes and `D2EdgeData` for edges.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! Key types:
    8|       |//! - `D2Graph`: the complete diagram graph with hierarchy
    9|       |//! - `D2Object`: a shape or container
   10|       |//! - `D2EdgeData`: a connection/edge with routing data
   11|       |//! - `Style`: visual properties (fill, stroke, etc.)
   12|       |//! - `ShapeType`, `Direction`, `ArrowheadType`: enums
   13|       |
   14|       |use crate::geo::{Point, Rect};
   15|       |use petgraph::stable_graph::{EdgeIndex, NodeIndex, StableDiGraph};
   16|       |
   17|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   18|       |// Source position tracking
   19|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   20|       |
   21|       |/// Source position (0-indexed).
   22|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
   23|       |pub struct Position {
   24|       |    pub line: usize,
   25|       |    pub column: usize,
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Source range.
   29|       |#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
   30|       |pub struct Range {
   31|       |    pub start: Position,
   32|       |    pub end: Position,
   33|       |}
   34|       |
   35|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   36|       |// D2Graph
   37|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   38|       |
   39|       |/// The complete diagram graph.
   40|       |pub struct D2Graph {
   41|       |    /// petgraph backing store.
   42|       |    pub graph: StableDiGraph<D2Object, D2EdgeData>,
   43|       |
   44|       |    /// Root object (implicit top-level container).
   45|       |    /// The root is special: it has no shape, no label, no border.
   46|       |    /// It exists only as the parent of top-level objects.
   47|       |    /// Its `box_` is set to the bounding box of all children after layout.
   48|       |    /// The SVG renderer must NOT draw a shape or label for the root.
   49|       |    pub root: NodeIndex,
   50|       |
   51|       |    /// All objects in declaration order (preserved for deterministic iteration).
   52|       |    pub objects: Vec<NodeIndex>,
   53|       |
   54|       |    /// All edges in declaration order (preserved for deterministic rendering).
   55|       |    pub edges: Vec<EdgeIndex>,
   56|       |
   57|       |    /// Top-level layout direction (default: Down).
   58|       |    pub direction: Direction,
   59|       |
   60|       |    /// Non-fatal warnings accumulated during compilation and layout.
   61|       |    pub warnings: Vec<D2Warning>,
   62|       |}
   63|       |
   64|       |impl D2Graph {
   65|       |    /// Create a new empty graph with a root node.
   66|    189|    pub fn new() -> Self {
   67|    189|        let mut graph = StableDiGraph::new();
   68|    189|        let root = graph.add_node(D2Object {
   69|    189|            id: String::new(),
   70|    189|            label: String::new(),
   71|    189|            shape: ShapeType::Rectangle,
   72|    189|            style: Style::default(),
   73|    189|            parent: None,
   74|    189|            children: Vec::new(),
   75|    189|            direction: None,
   76|    189|            box_: None,
   77|    189|            label_dimensions: None,
   78|    189|            label_lines: Vec::new(),
   79|    189|            is_container: true,
   80|    189|        });
   81|    189|        D2Graph {
   82|    189|            graph,

   84|    189|            objects: Vec::new(),

   87|    189|            warnings: Vec::new(),
   88|    189|        }
   89|    189|    }
   90|       |}
   91|       |
   92|       |impl D2Graph {
   93|       |    /// Move a node to (new_x, new_y). If the node is a container,
   94|       |    /// shift all descendants by the same delta to preserve relative positions.
   95|    440|    pub fn reposition_node(&mut self, node: NodeIndex, new_x: f64, new_y: f64) {
   96|    440|        let (dx, dy) = if let Some(ref mut rect) = self.graph[node].box_ {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (96:31): [True: 200, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (96:31): [True: 240, False: 0]
  ------------------
   97|    440|            let dx = new_x - rect.x;
   98|    440|            let dy = new_y - rect.y;
   99|    440|            rect.x = new_x;
  100|    440|            rect.y = new_y;

  102|       |        } else {

  104|       |        };

  106|       |        // Skip subtree traversal for sub-pixel moves (floating-point drift)

  170|       |    pub children: Vec<NodeIndex>,
  171|       |

  173|       |    pub direction: Option<Direction>,
  174|       |
  175|       |    // Geometry (populated during layout)
  176|       |    /// Position + size after layout.
  177|       |    pub box_: Option<Rect>,
  178|       |    /// Measured text size (pre-layout).
  179|       |    pub label_dimensions: Option<(f64, f64)>,
  180|       |    /// Label text after wrapping (pre-layout).
  181|       |    pub label_lines: Vec<String>,
  182|       |
  183|       |    /// Whether this object is a container (has children).
  184|       |    pub is_container: bool,

  536|       |    /// Relative luminance per WCAG 2.0 (range 0.0–1.0).
  537|       |    /// Used to determine whether text should be dark or light on a given background.
  538|     11|    pub fn luminance(&self) -> f64 {
  539|     11|        let (r, g, b) = match *self {
  540|     11|            Color::Hex(r, g, b) | Color::HexAlpha(r, g, b, _) => (r, g, b),
                                                                ^0 ^0 ^0
  541|       |        };
  542|       |        // sRGB linearization
  543|     33|        fn lin(c: u8) -> f64 {
  544|     33|            let s = c as f64 / 255.0;
  545|     33|            if s <= 0.03928 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (545:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (545:16): [True: 8, False: 25]
  ------------------
  546|      8|                s / 12.92
  547|       |            } else {
  548|     25|                ((s + 0.055) / 1.055).powf(2.4)
  549|       |            }
  550|     33|        }
  551|     11|        0.2126 * lin(r) + 0.7152 * lin(g) + 0.0722 * lin(b)
  552|     11|    }

  559|       |        } else {
  560|      2|            Color::Hex(0xE0, 0xE0, 0xE0) // light text on dark bg
  561|       |        }
  562|      7|    }

    3|       |//! Coordinates the four layout sub-phases:

  147|     96|        if let Some(rect) = graph.graph[child].box_ {

  149|     96|            min_y = min_y.min(rect.y);
  150|     96|            max_x = max_x.max(rect.x + rect.width);
  151|     96|            max_y = max_y.max(rect.y + rect.height);
  152|     96|        }

  154|       |

  |  Branch (155:8): [True: 0, False: 22]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (155:8): [True: 0, False: 36]
  ------------------
  156|      0|        return;
  157|     58|    }
  158|       |
  159|       |    // Container bounds: children bbox + padding + label area at top
  160|     58|    let container_x = min_x - CONTAINER_PADDING;
  161|     58|    let container_y = min_y - CONTAINER_PADDING - label_height;
  162|     58|    let container_width = (max_x - min_x) + CONTAINER_PADDING * 2.0;
  163|     58|    let container_height = (max_y - min_y) + CONTAINER_PADDING * 2.0 + label_height;
  164|       |

  166|     58|        container_x,
  167|     58|        container_y,
  168|     58|        container_width,
  169|     58|        container_height,
  170|     58|    ));
  171|     58|}
  172|       |
  173|       |/// Recursively offset all nodes in a subtree by (dx, dy).
  174|     69|fn offset_subtree(graph: &mut D2Graph, nodes: &[NodeIndex], dx: f64, dy: f64) {
  175|    115|    for &node in nodes {
                               ^69
  176|    115|        if let Some(ref mut rect) = graph.graph[node].box_ {

  |  Branch (176:16): [True: 48, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (176:16): [True: 67, False: 0]

  178|    115|            rect.y += dy;
  179|    115|        }
                      ^0

  187|       |
  188|       |/// Compute the root node's bounding box from all objects.
  189|       |/// Non-root containers are already fitted by `layout_recursive`.
  190|    154|fn compute_root_bbox(graph: &mut D2Graph) {
  191|    154|    let all_objects: Vec<NodeIndex> = graph.objects.clone();
  192|    154|    let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
  193|    154|    let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
  194|    154|    let mut max_x = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
  195|    154|    let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
  196|       |
  197|    436|    for &idx in &all_objects {
                              ^154
  198|    436|        if let Some(rect) = graph.graph[idx].box_ {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (198:16): [True: 200, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (198:16): [True: 236, False: 0]
  ------------------
  199|    436|            min_x = min_x.min(rect.x);
  200|    436|            min_y = min_y.min(rect.y);
  201|    436|            max_x = max_x.max(rect.x + rect.width);
  202|    436|            max_y = max_y.max(rect.y + rect.height);
  203|    436|        }
                      ^0
  204|       |    }
  205|       |
  206|    154|    if min_x != f64::INFINITY {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (206:8): [True: 60, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (206:8): [True: 94, False: 0]
  ------------------
  207|    154|        graph.graph[graph.root].box_ = Some(Rect::new(min_x, min_y, max_x - min_x, max_y - min_y));
  208|    154|    }
                  ^0
  209|    154|}
  210|       |
  211|       |/// Normalize positions so the diagram starts near origin.
  212|    154|fn normalize_positions(graph: &mut D2Graph) {
  213|    154|    let objects: Vec<NodeIndex> = graph.objects.clone();
  214|       |
  215|    154|    let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
  216|    154|    let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
  217|       |
  218|    436|    for &idx in &objects {
                              ^154
  219|    436|        if let Some(rect) = graph.graph[idx].box_ {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (219:16): [True: 200, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (219:16): [True: 236, False: 0]
  ------------------
  220|    436|            min_x = min_x.min(rect.x);
  221|    436|            min_y = min_y.min(rect.y);
  222|    436|        }
                      ^0
  223|       |    }
  224|       |
  225|    154|    if min_x == f64::INFINITY {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (225:8): [True: 0, False: 60]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (225:8): [True: 0, False: 94]
  ------------------
  226|      0|        return;
  227|    154|    }
  228|       |
  229|       |    // Offset everything so top-left is near (0, 0)
  230|    154|    let offset_x = -min_x;
  231|    154|    let offset_y = -min_y;
  232|       |
  233|    154|    if offset_x.abs() < 0.01 && offset_y.abs() < 0.01 {
                                              ^87
  ------------------
  |  Branch (233:8): [True: 18, False: 42]
  |  Branch (233:33): [True: 12, False: 6]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (233:8): [True: 69, False: 25]
  |  Branch (233:33): [True: 46, False: 23]
  ------------------
  234|     58|        return;
  235|     96|    }
  236|       |
  237|    309|    for &idx in &objects {
                              ^96
  238|    309|        if let Some(ref mut rect) = graph.graph[idx].box_ {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (238:16): [True: 154, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (238:16): [True: 155, False: 0]
  ------------------
  239|    309|            rect.x += offset_x;
  240|    309|            rect.y += offset_y;
  241|    309|        }
                      ^0
  242|       |    }
  243|       |
  244|       |    // Also update root bounding box
  245|     96|    if let Some(ref mut rect) = graph.graph[graph.root].box_ {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (245:12): [True: 48, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (245:12): [True: 48, False: 0]
  ------------------
  246|     96|        rect.x += offset_x;
  247|     96|        rect.y += offset_y;
  248|     96|    }
                  ^0
  249|    154|}

  251|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  252|       |// Tests
  253|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  254|       |
  255|       |#[cfg(test)]
  256|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  257|       |mod tests {
  258|       |    use crate::{compile, layout, parse, RenderOptions};
  259|       |
  260|       |    fn opts() -> RenderOptions {
  261|       |        RenderOptions::default()
  262|       |    }
  263|       |
  264|       |    /// Helper: parse+compile+layout a D2 source string, return the graph.
  265|       |    fn layout_ok(source: &str) -> crate::graph::D2Graph {
  266|       |        let ast = parse(source).ast;
  267|       |        let mut graph = compile(&ast).expect("compile should succeed");
  268|       |        layout(&mut graph, &opts()).expect("layout should succeed");
  269|       |        graph
  270|       |    }
  271|       |
  272|       |    /// Find a non-root object by label.
  273|       |    fn find_by_label<'a>(
  274|       |        graph: &'a crate::graph::D2Graph,
  275|       |        label: &str,
  276|       |    ) -> &'a crate::graph::D2Object {
  277|       |        for &idx in &graph.objects {
  278|       |            let obj = &graph.graph[idx];
  279|       |            if obj.label == label {
  280|       |                return obj;
  281|       |            }
  282|       |        }
  283|       |        panic!("no object with label '{label}' found");
  284|       |    }
  285|       |
  286|       |    // --- test_layout_simple_graph --------------------------------------------
  287|       |
  288|       |    #[test]
  289|       |    fn test_layout_simple_graph() {
  290|       |        // a -> b: both should have non-zero positions, b below a (direction=down).
  291|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b");
  292|       |
  293|       |        let a = find_by_label(&graph, "a");
  294|       |        let b = find_by_label(&graph, "b");
  295|       |
  296|       |        let a_rect = a.box_.expect("a should have a box after layout");
  297|       |        let b_rect = b.box_.expect("b should have a box after layout");
  298|       |
  299|       |        // Both should have non-zero dimensions
  300|       |        assert!(a_rect.width > 0.0, "a should have non-zero width");
  301|       |        assert!(b_rect.width > 0.0, "b should have non-zero width");
  302|       |
  303|       |        // Default direction is Down, so b should be below a
  304|       |        assert!(
  305|       |            b_rect.y > a_rect.y,
  306|       |            "b (y={}) should be below a (y={}) in direction=down",
  307|       |            b_rect.y,
  308|       |            a_rect.y
  309|       |        );
  310|       |    }
  311|       |
  312|       |    // --- test_layout_container -----------------------------------------------
  313|       |
  314|       |    #[test]
  315|       |    fn test_layout_container() {
  316|       |        // Container with children: container bounds should enclose children.
  317|       |        let graph = layout_ok("group: {\n  child1\n  child2\n}");
  318|       |
  319|       |        let group = find_by_label(&graph, "group");
  320|       |        let child1 = find_by_label(&graph, "child1");
  321|       |        let child2 = find_by_label(&graph, "child2");
  322|       |
  323|       |        let g_rect = group.box_.expect("group should have a box");
  324|       |        let c1_rect = child1.box_.expect("child1 should have a box");
  325|       |        let c2_rect = child2.box_.expect("child2 should have a box");
  326|       |
  327|       |        // Container should enclose both children
  328|       |        assert!(
  329|       |            g_rect.x <= c1_rect.x,
  330|       |            "container left ({}) should be <= child1 left ({})",
  331|       |            g_rect.x,
  332|       |            c1_rect.x
  333|       |        );
  334|       |        assert!(
  335|       |            g_rect.y <= c1_rect.y,
  336|       |            "container top ({}) should be <= child1 top ({})",
  337|       |            g_rect.y,
  338|       |            c1_rect.y
  339|       |        );
  340|       |        assert!(
  341|       |            g_rect.x + g_rect.width >= c2_rect.x + c2_rect.width,
  342|       |            "container right ({}) should be >= child2 right ({})",
  343|       |            g_rect.x + g_rect.width,
  344|       |            c2_rect.x + c2_rect.width
  345|       |        );
  346|       |        assert!(
  347|       |            g_rect.y + g_rect.height >= c2_rect.y + c2_rect.height,
  348|       |            "container bottom ({}) should be >= child2 bottom ({})",
  349|       |            g_rect.y + g_rect.height,
  350|       |            c2_rect.y + c2_rect.height
  351|       |        );
  352|       |    }
  353|       |
  354|       |    // --- test_layout_direction_right -----------------------------------------
  355|       |
  356|       |    #[test]
  357|       |    fn test_layout_direction_right() {
  358|       |        // With direction=right, nodes should be left-to-right (increasing x).
  359|       |        let graph = layout_ok("direction: right\na -> b -> c");
  360|       |
  361|       |        let a = find_by_label(&graph, "a");
  362|       |        let b = find_by_label(&graph, "b");
  363|       |        let c = find_by_label(&graph, "c");
  364|       |
  365|       |        let a_rect = a.box_.expect("a should have a box");
  366|       |        let b_rect = b.box_.expect("b should have a box");
  367|       |        let c_rect = c.box_.expect("c should have a box");
  368|       |
  369|       |        assert!(
  370|       |            b_rect.x > a_rect.x,
  371|       |            "b (x={}) should be right of a (x={}) in direction=right",
  372|       |            b_rect.x,
  373|       |            a_rect.x
  374|       |        );
  375|       |        assert!(
  376|       |            c_rect.x > b_rect.x,
  377|       |            "c (x={}) should be right of b (x={}) in direction=right",
  378|       |            c_rect.x,
  379|       |            b_rect.x
  380|       |        );
  381|       |    }
  382|       |
  383|       |    // --- test_normalize_positions --------------------------------------------
  384|       |
  385|       |    #[test]
  386|       |    fn test_normalize_positions() {
  387|       |        // After layout, the top-left of all nodes should be near origin (>= 0).
  388|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> b -> c");
  389|       |
  390|       |        let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
  391|       |        let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
  392|       |
  393|       |        for &idx in &graph.objects {
  394|       |            if let Some(rect) = graph.graph[idx].box_ {
  395|       |                min_x = min_x.min(rect.x);
  396|       |                min_y = min_y.min(rect.y);
  397|       |            }
  398|       |        }
  399|       |
  400|       |        assert!(
  401|       |            min_x.abs() < 1.0,
  402|       |            "min_x ({min_x}) should be near 0 after normalization"
  403|       |        );
  404|       |        assert!(
  405|       |            min_y.abs() < 1.0,
  406|       |            "min_y ({min_y}) should be near 0 after normalization"
  407|       |        );
  408|       |    }
  409|       |
  410|       |    // --- helpers for container tests -----------------------------------------
  411|       |
  412|       |    /// Assert that a leaf rect is fully enclosed by a parent rect.
  413|       |    fn assert_inside(
  414|       |        leaf_label: &str,
  415|       |        leaf: crate::geo::Rect,
  416|       |        parent_label: &str,
  417|       |        parent: crate::geo::Rect,
  418|       |    ) {
  419|       |        assert!(
  420|       |            parent.x <= leaf.x,
  421|       |            "{leaf_label} left ({}) escapes {parent_label} left ({})",
  422|       |            leaf.x,
  423|       |            parent.x,
  424|       |        );
  425|       |        assert!(
  426|       |            parent.y <= leaf.y,
  427|       |            "{leaf_label} top ({}) escapes {parent_label} top ({})",
  428|       |            leaf.y,
  429|       |            parent.y,
  430|       |        );
  431|       |        assert!(
  432|       |            parent.x + parent.width >= leaf.x + leaf.width,
  433|       |            "{leaf_label} right ({}) escapes {parent_label} right ({})",
  434|       |            leaf.x + leaf.width,
  435|       |            parent.x + parent.width,
  436|       |        );
  437|       |        assert!(
  438|       |            parent.y + parent.height >= leaf.y + leaf.height,
  439|       |            "{leaf_label} bottom ({}) escapes {parent_label} bottom ({})",
  440|       |            leaf.y + leaf.height,
  441|       |            parent.y + parent.height,
  442|       |        );
  443|       |    }
  444|       |
  445|       |    /// Assert that no two leaf (non-container, non-root) nodes overlap.
  446|       |    fn assert_no_leaf_overlap(graph: &crate::graph::D2Graph) {
  447|       |        // Collect all non-root, non-container nodes that have boxes
  448|       |        let leaves: Vec<_> = graph
  449|       |            .objects
  450|       |            .iter()
  451|       |            .filter(|&&idx| idx != graph.root)
  452|       |            .filter(|&&idx| !graph.graph[idx].is_container)
  453|       |            .filter(|&&idx| graph.graph[idx].box_.is_some())
  454|       |            .map(|&idx| (graph.graph[idx].id.clone(), graph.graph[idx].box_.unwrap()))
  455|       |            .collect();
  456|       |
  457|       |        for i in 0..leaves.len() {
  458|       |            for j in (i + 1)..leaves.len() {
  459|       |                let (ref id_a, a) = leaves[i];
  460|       |                let (ref id_b, b) = leaves[j];
  461|       |
  462|       |                // Two rects overlap iff they overlap on BOTH axes
  463|       |                let overlap_x = a.x < b.x + b.width && b.x < a.x + a.width;
  464|       |                let overlap_y = a.y < b.y + b.height && b.y < a.y + a.height;
  465|       |
  466|       |                assert!(
  467|       |                    !(overlap_x && overlap_y),
  468|       |                    "leaf '{id_a}' ({a:?}) overlaps with leaf '{id_b}' ({b:?})"
  469|       |                );
  470|       |            }
  471|       |        }
  472|       |    }
  473|       |
  474|       |    // --- test_container_children_inside_parent --------------------------------
  475|       |
  476|       |    #[test]
  477|       |    fn test_container_children_inside_parent() {
  478|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a: { x; y }\nb: { p; q }\na.x -> b.p");
  479|       |
  480|       |        let a = find_by_label(&graph, "a")
  481|       |            .box_
  482|       |            .expect("a should have a box");
  483|       |        let b = find_by_label(&graph, "b")
  484|       |            .box_
  485|       |            .expect("b should have a box");
  486|       |
  487|       |        for (leaf_label, parent_label, parent_rect) in
  488|       |            [("x", "a", a), ("y", "a", a), ("p", "b", b), ("q", "b", b)]
  489|       |        {
  490|       |            let leaf = find_by_label(&graph, leaf_label)
  491|       |                .box_
  492|       |                .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("{leaf_label} should have a box"));
  493|       |            assert_inside(leaf_label, leaf, parent_label, parent_rect);
  494|       |        }
  495|       |    }
  496|       |

  514|       |                .box_
  515|       |                .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("{leaf_label} should have a box"));

  545|       |    #[test]
  546|       |    fn test_nested_container_no_overlap() {
  547|       |        let source = "\
  548|       |platform: {
  549|       |  frontend: {
  550|       |    app: React App
  551|       |    cdn: CDN
  552|       |  }
  553|       |  backend: {
  554|       |    api: API Server
  555|       |    svc: Service
  556|       |    api -> svc
  557|       |  }
  558|       |  frontend.app -> backend.api
  559|       |}";
  560|       |        let graph = layout_ok(source);
  561|       |        assert_no_leaf_overlap(&graph);

  564|       |    // --- test_container_delta_propagation -------------------------------------
  565|       |
  566|       |    #[test]
  567|       |    fn test_container_delta_propagation() {
  568|       |        let graph = layout_ok("outer: { mid: { inner } }");
  569|       |
  570|       |        let outer = find_by_label(&graph, "outer")
  571|       |            .box_
  572|       |            .expect("outer should have a box");
  573|       |        let mid = find_by_label(&graph, "mid")
  574|       |            .box_
  575|       |            .expect("mid should have a box");
  576|       |        let inner = find_by_label(&graph, "inner")
  577|       |            .box_
  578|       |            .expect("inner should have a box");
  579|       |
  580|       |        assert_inside("mid", mid, "outer", outer);
  581|       |        assert_inside("inner", inner, "mid", mid);
  582|       |    }
  583|       |
  584|       |    // --- test_arrange_in_line_containers -------------------------------------
  585|       |
  586|       |    #[test]
  587|       |    fn test_arrange_in_line_containers() {
  588|       |        let graph = layout_ok("g1: { a\n b }\ng2: { c\n d }");
  589|       |
  590|       |        let g1 = find_by_label(&graph, "g1")
  591|       |            .box_
  592|       |            .expect("g1 should have a box");
  593|       |        let g2 = find_by_label(&graph, "g2")
  594|       |            .box_
  595|       |            .expect("g2 should have a box");
  596|       |
  597|       |        assert_inside("a", find_by_label(&graph, "a").box_.unwrap(), "g1", g1);
  598|       |        assert_inside("b", find_by_label(&graph, "b").box_.unwrap(), "g1", g1);
  599|       |        assert_inside("c", find_by_label(&graph, "c").box_.unwrap(), "g2", g2);
  600|       |        assert_inside("d", find_by_label(&graph, "d").box_.unwrap(), "g2", g2);
  601|       |    }
  602|       |
  603|       |    // --- test_container_spacing_wider ----------------------------------------
  604|       |
  605|       |    /// Containers in the same rank should get at least CONTAINER_CROSS_SPACING
  606|       |    /// (60px) of horizontal gap between them, more than leaf NODE_SPACING_H.
  607|       |    #[test]
  608|       |    fn test_container_spacing_wider() {
  609|       |        // Two containers `a` and `b` in rank 0, connected to leaf `c` in rank 1.
  610|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a: { x }\nb: { y }\nc\na.x -> c\nb.y -> c");
  611|       |
  612|       |        let a = find_by_label(&graph, "a")
  613|       |            .box_
  614|       |            .expect("a should have a box");
  615|       |        let b = find_by_label(&graph, "b")
  616|       |            .box_
  617|       |            .expect("b should have a box");
  618|       |
  619|       |        // Determine which container is left and which is right
  620|       |        let (left, right) = if a.x < b.x { (a, b) } else { (b, a) };
  621|       |
  622|       |        let gap = right.x - (left.x + left.width);
  623|       |        assert!(
  624|       |            gap >= 60.0 - 0.01,
  625|       |            "gap between containers ({gap:.1}) should be >= CONTAINER_CROSS_SPACING (60.0)"
  626|       |        );
  627|       |    }
  628|       |
  629|       |    // --- test_leaf_spacing_unchanged -----------------------------------------
  630|       |
  631|       |    /// Leaf nodes (non-containers) in the same rank should use NODE_SPACING_H
  632|       |    /// (40px), not the wider container spacing.
  633|       |    #[test]
  634|       |    fn test_leaf_spacing_unchanged() {
  635|       |        // Two leaf nodes `a` and `b` in rank 0, connected to `c` in rank 1.
  636|       |        let graph = layout_ok("a -> c\nb -> c");
  637|       |
  638|       |        let a = find_by_label(&graph, "a")
  639|       |            .box_
  640|       |            .expect("a should have a box");
  641|       |        let b = find_by_label(&graph, "b")
  642|       |            .box_
  643|       |            .expect("b should have a box");
  644|       |
  645|       |        // Determine which is left and which is right
  646|       |        let (left, right) = if a.x < b.x { (a, b) } else { (b, a) };
  647|       |
  648|       |        let gap = right.x - (left.x + left.width);
  649|       |
  650|       |        // Gap should be NODE_SPACING_H (40.0), with a small tolerance
  651|       |        // for centering arithmetic.
  652|       |        assert!(
  653|       |            gap < 60.0,
  654|       |            "gap between leaf nodes ({gap:.1}) should be less than CONTAINER_CROSS_SPACING (60.0)"
  655|       |        );
  656|       |        assert!(
  657|       |            (gap - 40.0).abs() < 1.0,
  658|       |            "gap between leaf nodes ({gap:.1}) should be close to NODE_SPACING_H (40.0)"
  659|       |        );
  660|       |    }

    1|       |//! Sugiyama algorithm implementation for D2 layout.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Implements the four classic Sugiyama phases with node-size awareness:
    4|       |//! 1. Cycle removal (reverse back-edges)
    5|       |//! 2. Rank assignment (longest-path)
    6|       |//! 3. Crossing reduction (barycenter heuristic)
    7|       |//! 4. Coordinate assignment (simple center-aligned placement)
    8|       |//!
    9|       |//! This operates on a subset of the graph: the direct children of a
   10|       |//! container node, treating child containers as opaque nodes with
   11|       |//! pre-computed dimensions.
   12|       |
   13|       |use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet, VecDeque};
   14|       |
   15|       |use petgraph::stable_graph::{EdgeIndex, NodeIndex};
   16|       |
   17|       |use crate::graph::{D2Graph, Direction};
   18|       |use crate::layout::LayoutError;
   19|       |
   20|       |/// Default spacing between nodes (horizontal).
   21|       |const NODE_SPACING_H: f64 = 40.0;
   22|       |/// Default spacing between ranks (vertical).
   23|       |const NODE_SPACING_V: f64 = 60.0;
   24|       |
   25|       |/// Gap between disconnected components (pixels).
   26|       |const COMPONENT_GAP: f64 = 60.0;
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Minimum spacing between container nodes in the same rank.
   29|       |const CONTAINER_CROSS_SPACING: f64 = 60.0;
   30|       |/// Minimum spacing between ranks when either rank has a container.
   31|       |const CONTAINER_RANK_SPACING: f64 = 80.0;
   32|       |
   33|       |/// Number of barycenter crossing-reduction sweeps.
   34|       |/// Empirically sufficient for diagrams under ~200 nodes.
   35|       |const CROSSING_REDUCTION_ITERATIONS: usize = 12;

  384|       |/// Crossing reduction using barycenter heuristic.
  385|       |#[allow(clippy::needless_range_loop)]
  386|    119|fn reduce_crossings(
  387|    119|    ranks: &HashMap<usize, Vec<NodeIndex>>,
  388|    119|    edges: &[(NodeIndex, NodeIndex, EdgeIndex)],
  389|    119|) -> Vec<Vec<NodeIndex>> {
  390|    119|    let max_rank = ranks.keys().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);
  391|    119|    let mut ordered: Vec<Vec<NodeIndex>> = (0..=max_rank)
  392|    282|        .map(|r| ranks.get(&r).cloned().unwrap_or_default())
                       ^119
  393|    119|        .collect();
  394|       |
  395|       |    // Build position maps
  396|    119|    let mut node_positions: HashMap<NodeIndex, usize> = HashMap::new();
  397|    282|    for rank_nodes in &ordered {
                                    ^119
  398|    320|        for (i, &node) in rank_nodes.iter().enumerate() {
                                        ^282              ^282
  399|    320|            node_positions.insert(node, i);
  400|    320|        }
  401|       |    }
  402|       |
  403|  1.42k|    for _iter in 0..CROSSING_REDUCTION_ITERATIONS {
                               ^119
  404|       |        // Downward sweep
  405|  1.95k|        for r in 1..=max_rank {
                               ^1.42k
  406|  1.95k|            let curr_rank = &ordered[r];
  407|       |
  408|  1.95k|            let mut barycenters: Vec<(NodeIndex, f64)> = curr_rank
  409|  1.95k|                .iter()

                               ^1.95k
  411|  2.30k|                    let incoming: Vec<f64> = edges
  412|  2.30k|                        .iter()

                                       ^2.30k

                                       ^2.30k

                                       ^2.30k

  ------------------
  |  Branch (418:33): [True: 0, False: 1.32k]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (418:33): [True: 0, False: 984]
  ------------------
  419|      0|                        node_positions.get(&node).map(|&p| p as f64).unwrap_or(0.0)
  420|       |                    } else {
  421|  2.30k|                        incoming.iter().sum::<f64>() / incoming.len() as f64
  422|       |                    };
  423|  2.30k|                    (node, bc)
  424|  2.30k|                })
  425|  1.95k|                .collect();
  426|       |

                                                     ^348^348        ^348  ^348      ^348

  429|       |
  430|       |            // Update positions
  431|  2.30k|            for (i, &node) in ordered[r].iter().enumerate() {
                                            ^1.95k            ^1.95k
  432|  2.30k|                node_positions.insert(node, i);
  433|  2.30k|            }
  434|       |        }
  435|       |
  436|       |        // Upward sweep
  437|  1.95k|        for r in (0..max_rank).rev() {
                                ^1.42k       ^1.42k
  438|  1.95k|            let curr_rank = &ordered[r];
  439|       |
  440|  1.95k|            let mut barycenters: Vec<(NodeIndex, f64)> = curr_rank
  441|  1.95k|                .iter()

                               ^1.95k
  443|  2.11k|                    let outgoing: Vec<f64> = edges
  444|  2.11k|                        .iter()

                                       ^2.11k

                                       ^2.11k
  447|  2.59k|                        .map(|&pos| pos as f64)
                                       ^2.11k

  ------------------

  ------------------
  451|     12|                        node_positions.get(&node).map(|&p| p as f64).unwrap_or(0.0)
  452|       |                    } else {

  454|       |                    };

  456|  2.11k|                })

  474|       |
  475|       |#[cfg(test)]
  476|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  477|       |#[allow(clippy::items_after_test_module)]
  478|       |mod tests {
  479|       |    use super::*;
  480|       |    use crate::geo::Rect;
  481|       |    use crate::graph::{D2Graph, D2Object, ShapeType, Style};
  482|       |
  483|       |    /// Helper: build a minimal graph with N nodes as children of root,
  484|       |    /// each with a pre-set box_ so the layout can place them.
  485|       |    /// Returns (graph, vec_of_child_indices).
  486|       |    fn make_graph(labels: &[&str]) -> (D2Graph, Vec<NodeIndex>) {
  487|       |        let mut graph = D2Graph::new();
  488|       |        let root = graph.root;
  489|       |        let mut children = Vec::new();
  490|       |
  491|       |        for &label in labels {
  492|       |            let idx = graph.graph.add_node(D2Object {
  493|       |                id: label.to_string(),
  494|       |                label: label.to_string(),
  495|       |                shape: ShapeType::Rectangle,
  496|       |                style: Style::default(),
  497|       |                parent: Some(root),
  498|       |                children: Vec::new(),
  499|       |                direction: None,
  500|       |                box_: Some(Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, 60.0, 36.0)),
  501|       |                label_dimensions: Some((50.0, 26.0)),
  502|       |                label_lines: vec![label.to_string()],
  503|       |                is_container: false,
  504|       |            });
  505|       |            graph.graph[root].children.push(idx);
  506|       |            graph.objects.push(idx);
  507|       |            children.push(idx);
  508|       |        }
  509|       |
  510|       |        (graph, children)
  511|       |    }
  512|       |
  513|       |    /// Helper: add an edge between two children and register it.
  514|       |    fn add_edge(graph: &mut D2Graph, src: NodeIndex, dst: NodeIndex) -> EdgeIndex {
  515|       |        use crate::graph::{ArrowheadType, D2EdgeData};
  516|       |
  517|       |        let eidx = graph.graph.add_edge(
  518|       |            src,
  519|       |            dst,
  520|       |            D2EdgeData {
  521|       |                label: None,
  522|       |                src_arrow: ArrowheadType::None,
  523|       |                dst_arrow: ArrowheadType::Arrow,
  524|       |                style: Style::default(),
  525|       |                route_type: crate::graph::RouteType::Bezier,
  526|       |                route: Vec::new(),
  527|       |                label_position: None,
  528|       |                label_width: 0.0,
  529|       |                label_height: 0.0,
  530|       |            },
  531|       |        );
  532|       |        graph.edges.push(eidx);
  533|       |        eidx
  534|       |    }
  535|       |
  536|       |    // --- test_linear_chain_ranking -------------------------------------------
  537|       |
  538|       |    #[test]
  539|       |    fn test_linear_chain_ranking() {
  540|       |        // a -> b -> c should produce 3 distinct ranks: a=0, b=1, c=2.
  541|       |        let (mut graph, children) = make_graph(&["a", "b", "c"]);
  542|       |        let a = children[0];
  543|       |        let b = children[1];
  544|       |        let c = children[2];
  545|       |
  546|       |        let e1 = add_edge(&mut graph, a, b);
  547|       |        let e2 = add_edge(&mut graph, b, c);
  548|       |
  549|       |        let edges = vec![(a, b, e1), (b, c, e2)];
  550|       |        let (forward, _back) = remove_cycles(&children, &edges);
  551|       |        let ranks = assign_ranks(&children, &forward);
  552|       |
  553|       |        // There should be 3 distinct ranks: 0, 1, 2
  554|       |        assert_eq!(ranks.len(), 3, "expected 3 ranks, got {}", ranks.len());
  555|       |
  556|       |        // Build a node-to-rank lookup
  557|       |        let mut node_rank: HashMap<NodeIndex, usize> = HashMap::new();
  558|       |        for (&rank, nodes) in &ranks {
  559|       |            for &node in nodes {
  560|       |                node_rank.insert(node, rank);
  561|       |            }
  562|       |        }
  563|       |
  564|       |        let rank_a = node_rank[&a];
  565|       |        let rank_b = node_rank[&b];
  566|       |        let rank_c = node_rank[&c];
  567|       |
  568|       |        assert!(
  569|       |            rank_a < rank_b,
  570|       |            "a (rank {rank_a}) should be before b (rank {rank_b})"
  571|       |        );
  572|       |        assert!(
  573|       |            rank_b < rank_c,
  574|       |            "b (rank {rank_b}) should be before c (rank {rank_c})"
  575|       |        );
  576|       |    }
  577|       |
  578|       |    // --- test_cycle_removal --------------------------------------------------
  579|       |
  580|       |    #[test]
  581|       |    fn test_cycle_removal() {
  582|       |        // a -> b -> c -> a (cycle). Should not panic or infinite loop.
  583|       |        // Back-edges list should be non-empty (at least one edge reversed).
  584|       |        let (mut graph, children) = make_graph(&["a", "b", "c"]);
  585|       |        let a = children[0];
  586|       |        let b = children[1];
  587|       |        let c = children[2];
  588|       |
  589|       |        let e1 = add_edge(&mut graph, a, b);
  590|       |        let e2 = add_edge(&mut graph, b, c);
  591|       |        let e3 = add_edge(&mut graph, c, a);
  592|       |
  593|       |        let edges = vec![(a, b, e1), (b, c, e2), (c, a, e3)];
  594|       |        let (forward, back) = remove_cycles(&children, &edges);
  595|       |
  596|       |        // At least one edge should be classified as a back-edge
  597|       |        assert!(
  598|       |            !back.is_empty(),
  599|       |            "expected at least one back-edge in a cycle"
  600|       |        );
  601|       |
  602|       |        // All edges should appear in either forward or back (none lost)
  603|       |        assert_eq!(
  604|       |            forward.len() + back.len(),
  605|       |            edges.len(),
  606|       |            "forward + back should account for all edges"
  607|       |        );
  608|       |
  609|       |        // The forward edges should be usable for ranking without panic
  610|       |        let ranks = assign_ranks(&children, &forward);
  611|       |        assert!(
  612|       |            !ranks.is_empty(),
  613|       |            "ranking should succeed after cycle removal"
  614|       |        );
  615|       |    }
  616|       |
  617|       |    // --- test_crossing_reduction ---------------------------------------------
  618|       |
  619|       |    #[test]
  620|       |    fn test_crossing_reduction() {
  621|       |        // Minimal case: a -> c, b -> d. Two independent edges, no crossings.
  622|       |        // After crossing reduction, the order should not introduce crossings.
  623|       |        let (mut graph, children) = make_graph(&["a", "b", "c", "d"]);
  624|       |        let a = children[0];
  625|       |        let b = children[1];
  626|       |        let c = children[2];
  627|       |        let d = children[3];
  628|       |
  629|       |        let e1 = add_edge(&mut graph, a, c);
  630|       |        let e2 = add_edge(&mut graph, b, d);
  631|       |
  632|       |        let edges = vec![(a, c, e1), (b, d, e2)];
  633|       |        let (forward, _) = remove_cycles(&children, &edges);
  634|       |        let ranks = assign_ranks(&children, &forward);
  635|       |        let ordered = reduce_crossings(&ranks, &forward);
  636|       |
  637|       |        // Should produce 2 ranks
  638|       |        assert_eq!(ordered.len(), 2, "expected 2 ranks, got {}", ordered.len());
  639|       |
  640|       |        // Each rank should have 2 nodes
  641|       |        assert_eq!(ordered[0].len(), 2);
  642|       |        assert_eq!(ordered[1].len(), 2);
  643|       |
  644|       |        // Verify no crossing: if a is before b in rank 0, then c should be
  645|       |        // before d in rank 1 (or vice versa — the relative order should match).
  646|       |        let a_pos = ordered[0].iter().position(|&n| n == a).unwrap();
  647|       |        let b_pos = ordered[0].iter().position(|&n| n == b).unwrap();
  648|       |        let c_pos = ordered[1].iter().position(|&n| n == c).unwrap();
  649|       |        let d_pos = ordered[1].iter().position(|&n| n == d).unwrap();
  650|       |
  651|       |        // If a < b then c < d (no crossing), or if b < a then d < c
  652|       |        let same_order = (a_pos < b_pos && c_pos < d_pos) || (b_pos < a_pos && d_pos < c_pos);
  653|       |        assert!(
  654|       |            same_order,
  655|       |            "crossing reduction should not introduce crossings: \
  656|       |             a@{a_pos}, b@{b_pos}, c@{c_pos}, d@{d_pos}"
  657|       |        );
  658|       |    }
  659|       |
  660|       |    // --- test_single_node ----------------------------------------------------
  661|       |
  662|       |    #[test]
  663|       |    fn test_single_node() {
  664|       |        // A single node with no edges should not panic.
  665|       |        let (mut graph, children) = make_graph(&["alone"]);
  666|       |        let root = graph.root;
  667|       |
  668|       |        // layout_children should succeed without edges
  669|       |        let result = layout_children(&mut graph, root, Direction::Down);
  670|       |        assert!(result.is_ok(), "single node layout should succeed");
  671|       |
  672|       |        // The node should have a valid position
  673|       |        let rect = graph.graph[children[0]]
  674|       |            .box_
  675|       |            .expect("single node should have a box after layout");
  676|       |        assert!(
  677|       |            rect.width > 0.0 && rect.height > 0.0,
  678|       |            "node should have non-zero dimensions"
  679|       |        );
  680|       |    }
  681|       |
  682|       |    // --- test_disconnected_nodes ---------------------------------------------
  683|       |
  684|       |    #[test]
  685|       |    fn test_disconnected_nodes() {
  686|       |        // Multiple nodes with no edges: they should be arranged, not all
  687|       |        // at the same position.
  688|       |        let (mut graph, children) = make_graph(&["x", "y", "z"]);
  689|       |        let root = graph.root;
  690|       |
  691|       |        let result = layout_children(&mut graph, root, Direction::Down);
  692|       |        assert!(result.is_ok(), "disconnected nodes layout should succeed");
  693|       |
  694|       |        // Collect positions
  695|       |        let positions: Vec<(f64, f64)> = children
  696|       |            .iter()
  697|       |            .map(|&idx| {
  698|       |                let rect = graph.graph[idx]
  699|       |                    .box_
  700|       |                    .expect("node should have a box after layout");
  701|       |                (rect.x, rect.y)
  702|       |            })
  703|       |            .collect();
  704|       |
  705|       |        // Not all at the same position
  706|       |        let all_same = positions
  707|       |            .windows(2)
  708|       |            .all(|w| (w[0].0 - w[1].0).abs() < 0.01 && (w[0].1 - w[1].1).abs() < 0.01);
  709|       |        assert!(
  710|       |            !all_same,
  711|       |            "disconnected nodes should not all be at the same position: {positions:?}"
  712|       |        );
  713|       |
  714|       |        // For direction=Down, nodes should be stacked vertically (different y)
  715|       |        let ys: Vec<f64> = positions.iter().map(|p| p.1).collect();
  716|       |        assert!(
  717|       |            ys[0] < ys[1] && ys[1] < ys[2],
  718|       |            "nodes should be in increasing y order for direction=Down: {ys:?}"
  719|       |        );
  720|       |    }
  721|       |}
  722|       |
  723|       |/// Assign x,y coordinates to nodes based on rank and order.
  724|    118|fn assign_coordinates(graph: &mut D2Graph, ordered_ranks: &[Vec<NodeIndex>], direction: Direction) {
  725|    118|    if ordered_ranks.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (725:8): [True: 0, False: 60]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (725:8): [True: 0, False: 58]
  ------------------
  726|      0|        return;
  727|    118|    }
  728|       |
  729|    118|    let is_horizontal = direction.is_horizontal();
  730|       |
  731|       |    // Compute rank offsets (cumulative height/width of each rank)
  732|    118|    let mut rank_offset = 0.0;
  733|    118|    let mut rank_offsets: Vec<f64> = Vec::new();
  734|       |
  735|    280|    for rank_nodes in ordered_ranks {
                                    ^118
  736|    280|        rank_offsets.push(rank_offset);
  737|       |
  738|       |        // Max size in the rank direction
  739|    280|        let max_size = rank_nodes
  740|    280|            .iter()
  741|    316|            .filter_map(|&node| graph.graph[node].box_)
                           ^280
  742|    316|            .map(|rect| {
                           ^280
  743|    316|                if is_horizontal {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (743:20): [True: 22, False: 148]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (743:20): [True: 78, False: 68]
  ------------------
  744|    100|                    rect.width
  745|       |                } else {
  746|    216|                    rect.height
  747|       |                }
  748|    316|            })
  749|    280|            .fold(0.0f64, f64::max);
  750|       |
  751|    315|        let has_container = rank_nodes.iter().any(|&n| graph.graph[n].is_container);
                          ^280            ^280              ^280
  752|    280|        let rank_spacing = if has_container {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (752:31): [True: 12, False: 136]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (752:31): [True: 18, False: 114]
  ------------------
  753|     30|            CONTAINER_RANK_SPACING
  754|       |        } else {
  755|    250|            NODE_SPACING_V
  756|       |        };
  757|    280|        rank_offset += max_size + rank_spacing;
  758|       |    }
  759|       |
  760|       |    // Place nodes
  761|    280|    for (rank_idx, rank_nodes) in ordered_ranks.iter().enumerate() {
                                                ^118          ^118   ^118
  762|       |        // Compute total cross-rank size for centering (container-aware spacing).
  763|       |        // The gap after each node is determined by that node's is_container flag,
  764|       |        // matching the placement loop below.
  765|    280|        let total_cross: f64 = {
  766|    280|            let nodes_with_boxes: Vec<_> = rank_nodes
  767|    280|                .iter()
  768|    316|                .filter(|&&n| graph.graph[n].box_.is_some())
                               ^280
  769|    280|                .copied()
  770|    280|                .collect();
  771|    280|            let mut total = 0.0;
  772|    316|            for (i, &n) in nodes_with_boxes.iter().enumerate() {
                                         ^280                    ^280
  773|    316|                let rect = graph.graph[n].box_.unwrap();
  774|    316|                total += if is_horizontal {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (774:29): [True: 22, False: 148]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (774:29): [True: 78, False: 68]
  ------------------
  775|    100|                    rect.height
  776|       |                } else {
  777|    216|                    rect.width
  778|       |                };

    3|       |//! mdmdview-d2: Native D2 diagram renderer.
    4|       |//!
    5|       |//! This crate implements a subset of the D2 diagram language
    6|       |//! (https://d2lang.com/) as a pure-Rust library. It parses D2 source
    7|       |//! text, compiles it to an internal graph representation, performs
    8|       |//! automatic hierarchical layout via the Sugiyama algorithm, and
    9|       |//! renders the result to SVG.
   10|       |//!
   11|       |//! The primary entry point is [`render_d2_to_svg()`], which takes D2
   12|       |//! source text and rendering options and returns an SVG string.
   13|       |//! Individual pipeline phases ([`parse`], [`compile`], [`layout`],
   14|       |//! [`render_svg`]) are also exposed for testing and debugging.

C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\parser.rs:
    1|       |//! Recursive-descent parser for D2 diagram language.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Parses D2 source text into an AST (`D2Map`). The parser handles
    4|       |//! all v1 syntax features: shape declarations, connections, containers,
    5|       |//! styles, comments, semicolons, and quoted strings.
    6|       |//!
    7|       |//! The parser collects multiple errors and continues, returning a
    8|       |//! partial AST alongside diagnostics. This matches D2's behavior
    9|       |//! and supports future LSP integration.
   10|       |
   11|       |use crate::ast::*;
   12|       |use crate::graph::{Position, Range};
   13|       |
   14|       |/// A single parse diagnostic (position + message).
   15|       |#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   16|       |#[error("{range:?}: {message}")]
   17|       |pub struct ParseError {
   18|       |    pub message: String,
   19|       |    pub range: Range,
   20|       |}
   21|       |
   22|       |/// Result of parsing: always contains the AST, plus any errors.
   23|       |pub struct ParseResult {
   24|       |    pub ast: D2Map,
   25|       |    pub errors: Vec<ParseError>,
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Parse D2 source text into an AST.
   29|    216|pub fn parse(source: &str) -> ParseResult {
   30|    216|    let mut parser = Parser::new(source);
   31|    216|    let map = parser.parse_map(true);
   32|    216|    ParseResult {
   33|    216|        ast: map,
   34|    216|        errors: parser.errors,
   35|    216|    }
   36|    216|}
   37|       |
   38|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   39|       |// Parser internals
   40|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   41|       |
   42|       |struct Parser<'a> {
   43|       |    /// Source text (retained for future error reporting with source spans).
   44|       |    #[allow(dead_code)]
   45|       |    source: &'a str,
   46|       |    /// Character iterator with byte position tracking.
   47|       |    chars: Vec<(usize, char)>,
   48|       |    /// Current position in chars vec.
   49|       |    pos: usize,
   50|       |    /// Collected errors.
   51|       |    errors: Vec<ParseError>,
   52|       |    /// Current line (0-indexed).
   53|       |    line: usize,
   54|       |    /// Current column (0-indexed, byte offset).
   55|       |    col: usize,
   56|       |}
   57|       |
   58|       |impl<'a> Parser<'a> {
   59|    216|    fn new(source: &'a str) -> Self {
   60|    216|        let chars: Vec<(usize, char)> = source.char_indices().collect();
   61|    216|        Parser {
   62|    216|            source,
   63|    216|            chars,
   64|    216|            pos: 0,
   65|    216|            errors: Vec::new(),
   66|    216|            line: 0,
   67|    216|            col: 0,
   68|    216|        }
   69|    216|    }
   70|       |
   71|       |    // -- Utility methods --
   72|       |
   73|  7.66k|    fn at_end(&self) -> bool {
   74|  7.66k|        self.pos >= self.chars.len()
   75|  7.66k|    }
   76|       |
   77|  24.8k|    fn peek(&self) -> Option<char> {
   78|  24.8k|        self.chars.get(self.pos).map(|(_, c)| *c)
   79|  24.8k|    }
   80|       |
   81|    751|    fn peek_ahead(&self, offset: usize) -> Option<char> {
   82|    751|        self.chars.get(self.pos + offset).map(|(_, c)| *c)
   83|    751|    }
   84|       |
   85|  13.2k|    fn advance(&mut self) -> Option<char> {
   86|  13.2k|        if let Some(&(_, ch)) = self.chars.get(self.pos) {
                                       ^13.2k
  ------------------
  |  Branch (86:16): [True: 8.42k, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (86:16): [True: 4.86k, False: 1]
  ------------------
   87|  13.2k|            self.pos += 1;
   88|  13.2k|            if ch == '\n' {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (88:16): [True: 407, False: 8.01k]
  ------------------

  499|     28|                end: self.current_position(),
  500|     28|            },
  501|     28|        })
  502|     28|    }
  503|       |
  504|       |    /// Parse a single-quoted string (no escaping).
  505|      1|    fn parse_single_quoted_string(&mut self) -> Option<StringValue> {
  506|      1|        let start = self.current_position();
  507|      1|        self.advance(); // skip opening quote
  508|       |
  509|      1|        let mut value = String::new();
  510|      1|        let mut raw = String::from("'");
  511|       |
  512|       |        loop {
  513|     12|            match self.peek() {
  514|       |                None => {
  515|      0|                    self.emit_error(
  516|      0|                        "unterminated single-quoted string",
  517|      0|                        Range {
  518|      0|                            start,
  519|      0|                            end: self.current_position(),
  520|      0|                        },
  521|       |                    );
  522|      0|                    break;
  523|       |                }
  524|       |                Some('\'') => {
  525|      1|                    raw.push('\'');
  526|      1|                    self.advance();
  527|      1|                    break;
  528|       |                }
  529|     11|                Some(ch) => {
  530|     11|                    raw.push(ch);
  531|     11|                    value.push(ch);
  532|     11|                    self.advance();
  533|     11|                }
  534|       |            }
  535|       |        }
  536|       |
  537|      1|        Some(StringValue {
  538|      1|            value,
  539|      1|            raw,
  540|      1|            range: Range {
  541|      1|                start,
  542|      1|                end: self.current_position(),
  543|      1|            },
  544|      1|        })
  545|      1|    }
  546|       |
  547|       |    /// Parse an unquoted key string.
  548|       |    /// D2 allows spaces in unquoted strings. The string terminates at
  549|       |    /// reserved character sequences: ->, <-, --, <->, :, ., {, }, ;, #, \n
  550|    982|    fn parse_unquoted_key_string(&mut self) -> Option<StringValue> {
  551|    982|        let start = self.current_position();
  552|    982|        let mut value = String::new();
  553|       |
  554|       |        loop {
  555|  4.34k|            if self.at_end() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (555:16): [True: 7, False: 1.70k]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (555:16): [True: 56, False: 2.57k]
  ------------------
  556|     63|                break;
  557|  4.28k|            }
  558|       |
  559|  4.28k|            let ch = self.peek().unwrap();
  560|       |
  561|       |            // Terminators
  562|  4.28k|            match ch {
  563|    670|                ':' | '{' | '}' | ';' | '#' | '\n' | '.' | '[' | ']' | '(' | ')' => break,
  564|       |                '-' => {
  565|       |                    // Check for edge operators: --, ->
  566|    248|                    if matches!(self.peek_ahead(1), Some('-') | Some('>')) {
                                     ^4
  567|    244|                        break;
  568|      4|                    }
  569|      4|                    value.push(ch);
  570|      4|                    self.advance();
  571|       |                }
  572|       |                '<' => {
  573|       |                    // Check for edge operator: <-
  574|      5|                    if self.peek_ahead(1) == Some('-') {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (574:24): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (574:24): [True: 5, False: 0]
  ------------------
  575|      5|                        break;
  576|      0|                    }
  577|      0|                    value.push(ch);
  578|      0|                    self.advance();
  579|       |                }
  580|  3.35k|                _ => {
  581|  3.35k|                    value.push(ch);
  582|  3.35k|                    self.advance();
  583|  3.35k|                }
  584|       |            }
  585|       |        }
  586|       |
  587|       |        // Trim trailing whitespace
  588|    982|        let trimmed = value.trim_end().to_string();
  589|    982|        if trimmed.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (589:12): [True: 8, False: 388]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (589:12): [True: 3, False: 583]
  ------------------
  590|     11|            return None;
  591|    971|        }
  592|       |
  593|    971|        let raw = trimmed.clone();
  594|    971|        Some(StringValue {
  595|    971|            value: trimmed,
  596|    971|            raw,
  597|    971|            range: Range {
  598|    971|                start,
  599|    971|                end: self.current_position(),
  600|    971|            },
  601|    971|        })
  602|    982|    }
  603|       |
  604|       |    /// Parse a scalar value (after colon).
  605|    321|    fn parse_scalar_value(&mut self) -> Option<ScalarValue> {
  606|    321|        match self.peek()? {
                                       ^0
  607|       |            '"' => {
  608|     28|                let sv = self.parse_double_quoted_string()?;
                                                                        ^0
  609|     28|                Some(ScalarValue::DoubleQuoted(sv.value))
  610|       |            }
  611|       |            '\'' => {
  612|      1|                let sv = self.parse_single_quoted_string()?;
                                                                        ^0
  613|      1|                Some(ScalarValue::SingleQuoted(sv.value))
  614|       |            }
  615|       |            _ => {
  616|       |                // Unquoted value: read until newline, semicolon, #, {, }, ]
  617|    292|                let mut value = String::new();
  618|       |                loop {
  619|  2.28k|                    if self.at_end() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (619:24): [True: 0, False: 1.10k]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (619:24): [True: 49, False: 1.12k]
  ------------------
  620|     49|                        break;
  621|  2.23k|                    }
  622|  2.23k|                    let ch = self.peek().unwrap();
  623|  2.23k|                    match ch {
  624|    243|                        '\n' | ';' | '#' | '{' | '}' | ']' => break,
  625|  1.99k|                        _ => {
  626|  1.99k|                            value.push(ch);
  627|  1.99k|                            self.advance();
  628|  1.99k|                        }
  629|       |                    }
  630|       |                }
  631|       |
  632|    292|                let trimmed = value.trim().to_string();
  633|    292|                if trimmed.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (633:20): [True: 0, False: 130]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (633:20): [True: 0, False: 162]
  ------------------
  634|      0|                    return None;
  635|    292|                }
  636|       |
  637|       |                // Check for boolean
  638|    292|                match trimmed.as_str() {
  639|    292|                    "true" => return Some(ScalarValue::Boolean(true)),
                                                   ^2
  640|    290|                    "false" => return Some(ScalarValue::Boolean(false)),
                                                    ^0
  641|    290|                    _ => {}
  642|       |                }
  643|       |
  644|       |                // Check for number
  645|    290|                if let Ok(n) = trimmed.parse::<f64>() {
                                        ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (645:24): [True: 0, False: 130]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (645:24): [True: 2, False: 158]
  ------------------
  646|      2|                    if trimmed
  ------------------
  |  Branch (646:24): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (646:24): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  647|      2|                        .chars()
  648|      4|                        .all(|c| c.is_ascii_digit() || c == '.' || c == '-')
                                       ^2                            ^0          ^0
  ------------------
  |  Branch (648:34): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (648:56): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (648:34): [True: 4, False: 0]
  |  Branch (648:56): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  649|       |                    {
  650|      2|                        return Some(ScalarValue::Number(n));
  651|      0|                    }
  652|    288|                }
  653|       |
  654|    288|                Some(ScalarValue::Unquoted(trimmed))
  655|       |            }
  656|       |        }
  657|    321|    }
  658|       |
  659|       |    /// Parse a map value: { ... }
  660|     93|    fn parse_map_value(&mut self) -> Value {
  661|     93|        self.advance(); // skip '{'
  662|     93|        let map = self.parse_map(false);
  663|     93|        self.skip_whitespace();
  664|     93|        if self.peek() == Some('}') {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (664:12): [True: 32, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (664:12): [True: 60, False: 1]
  ------------------
  665|     92|            self.advance();
  666|     92|        } else {
  667|      1|            let pos = self.current_position();
  668|      1|            self.emit_error(
  669|      1|                "expected closing '}'",
  670|      1|                Range {
  671|      1|                    start: pos,
  672|      1|                    end: pos,
  673|      1|                },
  674|      1|            );
  675|      1|        }
  676|     93|        Value::Map(map)
  677|     93|    }
  678|       |
  679|       |    /// Parse an array value: [ ... ]
  680|      0|    fn parse_array_value(&mut self) -> Value {
  681|      0|        self.advance(); // skip '['
  682|      0|        let mut items = Vec::new();
  683|       |
  684|       |        loop {
  685|      0|            self.skip_whitespace();
  686|       |
  687|      0|            if self.at_end() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (687:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (687:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  688|      0|                break;
  689|      0|            }
  690|       |
  691|      0|            if self.peek() == Some(']') {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (691:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (691:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  692|      0|                self.advance();
  693|      0|                break;
  694|      0|            }
  695|       |
  696|      0|            if self.peek() == Some(';') {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (696:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (696:16): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  697|      0|                self.advance();
  698|      0|                continue;
  699|      0|            }
  700|       |
  701|      0|            if let Some(scalar) = self.parse_scalar_value() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (701:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (701:20): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  702|      0|                items.push(Value::Scalar(scalar));
  703|      0|            } else {
  704|      0|                break;
  705|       |            }
  706|       |        }
  707|       |
  708|      0|        Value::Array(items)
  709|      0|    }

  712|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  713|       |// Tests
  714|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  715|       |
  716|       |#[cfg(test)]
  717|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  718|       |mod tests {
  719|       |    use super::*;
  720|       |
  721|       |    fn parse_ok(source: &str) -> D2Map {
  722|       |        let result = parse(source);
  723|       |        assert!(
  724|       |            result.errors.is_empty(),
  725|       |            "unexpected parse errors: {:?}",
  726|       |            result.errors
  727|       |        );
  728|       |        result.ast
  729|       |    }
  730|       |
  731|       |    #[test]
  732|       |    fn test_empty_source() {
  733|       |        let ast = parse_ok("");
  734|       |        assert!(ast.nodes.is_empty());
  735|       |    }
  736|       |
  737|       |    #[test]
  738|       |    fn test_simple_shape() {
  739|       |        let ast = parse_ok("server");
  740|       |        assert_eq!(ast.nodes.len(), 1);
  741|       |        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  742|       |            assert_eq!(key.key.to_string(), "server");
  743|       |            assert!(key.edges.is_empty());
  744|       |        } else {
  745|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  746|       |        }
  747|       |    }
  748|       |
  749|       |    #[test]
  750|       |    fn test_shape_with_label() {
  751|       |        let ast = parse_ok("server: \"My Server\"");
  752|       |        assert_eq!(ast.nodes.len(), 1);
  753|       |        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  754|       |            assert_eq!(key.key.to_string(), "server");
  755|       |            assert!(matches!(
  756|       |                &key.primary,
  757|       |                Some(ScalarValue::DoubleQuoted(s)) if s == "My Server"
  758|       |            ));
  759|       |        } else {
  760|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  761|       |        }
  762|       |    }
  763|       |
  764|       |    #[test]
  765|       |    fn test_simple_connection() {
  766|       |        let ast = parse_ok("a -> b");
  767|       |        assert_eq!(ast.nodes.len(), 1);
  768|       |        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  769|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges.len(), 1);
  770|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges[0].src.to_string(), "a");
  771|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges[0].dst.to_string(), "b");
  772|       |            assert!(!key.edges[0].src_arrow);
  773|       |            assert!(key.edges[0].dst_arrow);
  774|       |        } else {
  775|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  776|       |        }
  777|       |    }
  778|       |
  779|       |    #[test]
  780|       |    fn test_bidirectional_connection() {
  781|       |        let ast = parse_ok("a <-> b");
  782|       |        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  783|       |            assert!(key.edges[0].src_arrow);
  784|       |            assert!(key.edges[0].dst_arrow);
  785|       |        } else {
  786|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  787|       |        }
  788|       |    }
  789|       |
  790|       |    #[test]
  791|       |    fn test_reverse_connection() {
  792|       |        let ast = parse_ok("a <- b");
  793|       |        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  794|       |            assert!(key.edges[0].src_arrow);
  795|       |            assert!(!key.edges[0].dst_arrow);
  796|       |        } else {
  797|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  798|       |        }
  799|       |    }
  800|       |
  801|       |    #[test]
  802|       |    fn test_undirected_connection() {
  803|       |        let ast = parse_ok("a -- b");
  804|       |        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  805|       |            assert!(!key.edges[0].src_arrow);
  806|       |            assert!(!key.edges[0].dst_arrow);
  807|       |        } else {
  808|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  809|       |        }
  810|       |    }
  811|       |
  812|       |    #[test]
  813|       |    fn test_connection_chain() {
  814|       |        let ast = parse_ok("a -> b -> c -> d");
  815|       |        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  816|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges.len(), 3);
  817|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges[0].src.to_string(), "a");
  818|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges[0].dst.to_string(), "b");
  819|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges[1].src.to_string(), "b");
  820|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges[1].dst.to_string(), "c");
  821|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges[2].src.to_string(), "c");
  822|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges[2].dst.to_string(), "d");
  823|       |        } else {
  824|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  825|       |        }
  826|       |    }
  827|       |
  828|       |    #[test]
  829|       |    fn test_connection_with_label() {
  830|       |        let ast = parse_ok("a -> b: sends data");
  831|       |        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  832|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges.len(), 1);
  833|       |            assert!(matches!(
  834|       |                &key.primary,
  835|       |                Some(ScalarValue::Unquoted(s)) if s == "sends data"
  836|       |            ));
  837|       |        } else {
  838|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  839|       |        }
  840|       |    }
  841|       |
  842|       |    #[test]
  843|       |    fn test_comment() {
  844|       |        let ast = parse_ok("# this is a comment\nserver");
  845|       |        assert_eq!(ast.nodes.len(), 2);
  846|       |        assert!(matches!(&ast.nodes[0], MapNode::Comment(_)));
  847|       |        assert!(matches!(&ast.nodes[1], MapNode::Key(_)));
  848|       |    }

  850|       |    #[test]
  851|       |    fn test_container() {
  852|       |        let ast = parse_ok("group: {\n  a\n  b\n}");

  854|       |            assert_eq!(key.key.to_string(), "group");
  855|       |            assert!(matches!(&key.value, Some(Value::Map(m)) if m.nodes.len() == 2));
  856|       |        } else {
  857|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  858|       |        }
  859|       |    }
  860|       |
  861|       |    #[test]

  865|       |            assert_eq!(key.key.to_string(), "group");

  868|       |                Some(ScalarValue::DoubleQuoted(s)) if s == "My Group"
  869|       |            ));
  870|       |            assert!(matches!(&key.value, Some(Value::Map(_))));
  871|       |        } else {
  872|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  873|       |        }
  874|       |    }
  875|       |
  876|       |    #[test]

  883|       |        } else {

  885|       |        }
  886|       |    }

  888|       |    #[test]

  890|       |        let ast = parse_ok("x.style.fill: \"#ff0000\"");

  892|       |            assert_eq!(key.key.segments.len(), 3);

  894|       |            assert_eq!(key.key.segments[1].value, "style");
  895|       |            assert_eq!(key.key.segments[2].value, "fill");
  896|       |        } else {
  897|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  898|       |        }
  899|       |    }
  900|       |
  901|       |    #[test]
  902|       |    fn test_semicolons() {
  903|       |        let ast = parse_ok("a; b; c");
  904|       |        let key_count = ast
  905|       |            .nodes
  906|       |            .iter()
  907|       |            .filter(|n| matches!(n, MapNode::Key(_)))
  908|       |            .count();
  909|       |        assert_eq!(key_count, 3);
  910|       |    }
  911|       |
  912|       |    #[test]

  916|       |            .nodes
  917|       |            .iter()
  918|       |            .filter(|n| matches!(n, MapNode::Key(_)))
  919|       |            .count();
  920|       |        assert_eq!(key_count, 3);
  921|       |    }

  923|       |    #[test]

  928|       |            assert_eq!(key.edges[0].dst.to_string(), "Your Server");

  938|       |            if let Some(Value::Map(outer_map)) = &key.value {
  939|       |                assert_eq!(outer_map.nodes.len(), 1);
  940|       |                if let MapNode::Key(inner_key) = &outer_map.nodes[0] {
  941|       |                    assert_eq!(inner_key.key.to_string(), "inner");
  942|       |                    assert!(matches!(&inner_key.value, Some(Value::Map(_))));
  943|       |                }
  944|       |            } else {
  945|       |                panic!("expected map value");
  946|       |            }
  947|       |        }
  948|       |    }

  977|       |        if let MapNode::Key(key) = &ast.nodes[0] {
  978|       |            assert!(
  979|       |                matches!(&key.primary, Some(ScalarValue::Number(n)) if (*n - 3.0).abs() < 0.001)
  980|       |            );
  981|       |        } else {
  982|       |            panic!("expected Key node");
  983|       |        }
  984|       |    }
