 1072|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1073|       |    // Error recovery tests
 1074|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1075|       |
 1076|       |    /// Parse input and return the full ParseResult (AST + errors).
 1077|       |    fn parse_with_errors(source: &str) -> ParseResult {
 1078|       |        parse(source)
 1079|       |    }
 1080|       |
 1081|       |    #[test]
 1082|       |    fn test_stray_closing_brace() {
 1083|       |        // A stray `}` at the file level is not valid syntax. The parser
 1084|       |        // should emit an error for the unexpected `}` but still parse the
 1085|       |        // surrounding declarations `a` and `b`.
 1086|       |        let result = parse_with_errors("a\n}\nb");
 1087|       |        assert!(
 1088|       |            !result.errors.is_empty(),
 1089|       |            "expected errors for stray `}}`, got none"
 1090|       |        );
 1091|       |        // Both `a` and `b` should still be parsed as key nodes.
 1092|       |        let key_count = result
 1093|       |            .ast
 1094|       |            .nodes
 1095|       |            .iter()
 1096|       |            .filter(|n| matches!(n, MapNode::Key(_)))
 1097|       |            .count();
 1098|       |        assert!(
 1099|       |            key_count >= 2,
 1100|       |            "expected at least 2 key nodes after recovery, got {}",
 1101|       |            key_count
 1102|       |        );
 1103|       |    }
 1104|       |
 1105|       |    #[test]
 1106|       |    fn test_unterminated_map() {
 1107|       |        // Opening a map with `{` but never closing it should produce an
 1108|       |        // error about the missing `}`.
 1109|       |        let result = parse_with_errors("a: { b");
 1110|       |        assert!(
 1111|       |            !result.errors.is_empty(),
 1112|       |            "expected error for missing `}}`, got none"
 1113|       |        );
 1114|       |        let has_brace_error = result.errors.iter().any(|e| e.message.contains('}'));
 1115|       |        assert!(
 1116|       |            has_brace_error,
 1117|       |            "expected an error mentioning `}}`, got: {:?}",
 1118|       |            result.errors
 1119|       |        );
 1120|       |    }
 1121|       |
 1122|       |    #[test]
 1123|       |    fn test_edge_missing_destination() {
 1124|       |        // `a -> ` has an edge operator but no destination. The parser
 1125|       |        // should handle this gracefully without panicking.
 1126|       |        let result = parse_with_errors("a -> ");
 1127|       |        // The parser consumed the edge operator but failed to find a
 1128|       |        // destination key. This may result in the entire key being
 1129|       |        // dropped (no nodes) or an error being emitted. Either outcome
 1130|       |        // is acceptable as long as the parser doesn't panic.
 1131|       |        let _ = result.ast.nodes.len();
 1132|       |        let _ = result.errors.len();
 1133|       |    }
 1134|       |
 1135|       |    #[test]
 1136|       |    fn test_unterminated_string() {
 1137|       |        // A double-quoted string that is never closed should produce a
 1138|       |        // parse error rather than hanging or panicking.
 1139|       |        let result = parse_with_errors("a: \"unterminated");
 1140|       |        assert!(
 1141|       |            !result.errors.is_empty(),
 1142|       |            "expected error for unterminated string, got none"
 1143|       |        );
 1144|       |        let has_unterminated = result
 1145|       |            .errors
 1146|       |            .iter()
 1147|       |            .any(|e| e.message.contains("unterminated"));
 1148|       |        assert!(
 1149|       |            has_unterminated,
 1150|       |            "expected 'unterminated' in error message, got: {:?}",
 1151|       |            result.errors
 1152|       |        );
 1153|       |    }
 1154|       |
 1155|       |    #[test]
 1156|       |    fn test_multiple_errors_collected() {
 1157|       |        // Several syntax problems in one input. The parser should collect
 1158|       |        // all of them rather than bailing out after the first.
 1159|       |        //
 1160|       |        // Line 1: `}`       — stray closing brace at file level
 1161|       |        // Line 2: `a`       — valid key (should still parse)
 1162|       |        // Line 3: `}`       — another stray closing brace
 1163|       |        // Line 4: `b`       — valid key (should still parse)
 1164|       |        //
 1165|       |        // Each stray `}` is an independent error that the parser can
 1166|       |        // recover from by skipping the unrecognized character.
 1167|       |        let result = parse_with_errors("}\na\n}\nb");
 1168|       |        assert!(
 1169|       |            result.errors.len() >= 2,
 1170|       |            "expected at least 2 errors for multiple syntax problems, got {}: {:?}",
 1171|       |            result.errors.len(),
 1172|       |            result.errors,
 1173|       |        );
 1174|       |        // Despite multiple errors, the parser should still produce some
 1175|       |        // AST nodes — both `a` and `b` should survive recovery.
 1176|       |        let key_count = result
 1177|       |            .ast
 1178|       |            .nodes
 1179|       |            .iter()
 1180|       |            .filter(|n| matches!(n, MapNode::Key(_)))
 1181|       |            .count();
 1182|       |        assert!(
 1183|       |            key_count >= 2,
 1184|       |            "expected at least 2 key nodes after recovery, got {}",
 1185|       |            key_count,
 1186|       |        );
 1187|       |    }
 1188|       |}

  189|       |        ShapeType::Cloud => {
  190|      1|            let x = rect.x;
  191|      1|            let y = rect.y;
  192|      1|            let w = rect.width;
  193|      1|            let h = rect.height;
  194|       |            // Cloud shape: a closed path of cubic Bézier arcs forming bumps.
  195|       |            // The path traces clockwise: bottom-left ? bottom-right (flat base),
  196|       |            // then up the right side, across the top with 3 bumps, down the left.
  197|      1|            let bx = x;
  198|      1|            let by = y + h * 0.75; // base y
  199|      1|            format!(
  200|       |                "<path d=\"M {bx} {by} \
  201|       |                 C {c1x} {c1y} {c2x} {c2y} {p1x} {p1y} \
  202|       |                 C {c3x} {c3y} {c4x} {c4y} {p2x} {p2y} \
  203|       |                 C {c5x} {c5y} {c6x} {c6y} {p3x} {p3y} \
  204|       |                 C {c7x} {c7y} {c8x} {c8y} {p4x} {p4y} \
  205|       |                 C {c9x} {c9y} {c10x} {c10y} {bx} {by} \
  206|       |                 Z\" \
  207|       |                 fill=\"{fill}\" stroke=\"{stroke}\" stroke-width=\"{sw}\"{opacity}{dash}/>",
  208|       |                bx = bx,
  209|       |                by = by,
  210|       |                // Bottom-right bump
  211|      1|                c1x = x + w * 0.15,
  212|      1|                c1y = y + h * 1.05,
  213|      1|                c2x = x + w * 0.85,
  214|      1|                c2y = y + h * 1.05,
  215|      1|                p1x = x + w,
  216|      1|                p1y = y + h * 0.65,
  217|       |                // Right bump up
  218|      1|                c3x = x + w * 1.05,
  219|      1|                c3y = y + h * 0.35,
  220|      1|                c4x = x + w * 0.9,
  221|      1|                c4y = y + h * 0.1,
  222|      1|                p2x = x + w * 0.7,
  223|      1|                p2y = y + h * 0.15,
  224|       |                // Top bump
  225|      1|                c5x = x + w * 0.6,
  226|      1|                c5y = y - h * 0.05,
  227|      1|                c6x = x + w * 0.4,
  228|      1|                c6y = y - h * 0.05,
  229|      1|                p3x = x + w * 0.3,
  230|      1|                p3y = y + h * 0.15,
  231|       |                // Left bump down
  232|      1|                c7x = x + w * 0.1,
  233|      1|                c7y = y + h * 0.1,
  234|      1|                c8x = x - w * 0.05,
  235|      1|                c8y = y + h * 0.35,
  236|       |                p4x = x,
  237|      1|                p4y = y + h * 0.65,
  238|       |                // Back to start
  239|      1|                c9x = x - w * 0.05,
  240|      1|                c9y = y + h * 0.85,
  241|      1|                c10x = x - w * 0.05,
  242|      1|                c10y = y + h * 0.95,
  243|       |                fill = fill,
  244|       |                stroke = stroke,
  245|       |                sw = stroke_width,
  246|       |                opacity = opacity_attr,
  247|       |                dash = dash_attr,
  248|       |            )
  249|       |        }
  250|       |        ShapeType::Parallelogram => {
  251|      1|            let x = rect.x;
  252|      1|            let y = rect.y;

  254|      1|            let h = rect.height;

  271|       |            )

  273|       |        ShapeType::Text => {
  274|       |            // Text shape: no border, no fill
  275|      2|            String::new()

  277|       |        ShapeType::Person => {
  278|       |            // Simplified person: circle head + rectangle body
  279|      6|            let head_r = rect.width.min(rect.height) * 0.2;
  280|      6|            let head_cx = rect.x + rect.width / 2.0;
  281|      6|            let head_cy = rect.y + head_r + 2.0;
  282|      6|            let body_x = rect.x + rect.width * 0.2;
  283|      6|            let body_y = head_cy + head_r + 4.0;
  284|      6|            let body_w = rect.width * 0.6;
  285|      6|            let body_h = rect.height - (body_y - rect.y) - 2.0;
  286|      6|            format!(
  287|       |                "<circle cx=\"{}\" cy=\"{}\" r=\"{}\" fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"{}\"/>\
  288|       |                 \n<rect x=\"{}\" y=\"{}\" width=\"{}\" height=\"{}\" rx=\"4\" \
  289|       |                 fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"{}\"/>",
  290|       |                head_cx, head_cy, head_r, fill, stroke, stroke_width, body_x, body_y, body_w,
  291|      6|                body_h.max(1.0),
  292|       |                fill, stroke, stroke_width,
  293|       |            )
  294|       |        }
  295|       |        ShapeType::Document => {
  296|      1|            let x = rect.x;
  297|      1|            let y = rect.y;
  298|      1|            let w = rect.width;
  299|      1|            let h = rect.height;
  300|      1|            let wave = h * 0.1;
  301|      1|            format!(
  302|       |                "<path d=\"M {x} {y} h {w} v {bh} \
  303|       |                 q {q1x} {q1y} {q2x} 0 \
  304|       |                 q {q3x} {q3y} {q4x} 0 \
  305|       |                 V {y} Z\" \
  306|       |                 fill=\"{fill}\" stroke=\"{stroke}\" stroke-width=\"{sw}\"{opacity}{dash}/>",
  307|       |                x = x,
  308|       |                y = y,
  309|       |                w = w,
  310|      1|                bh = h - wave,
  311|      1|                q1x = -w / 4.0,
  312|      1|                q1y = wave * 2.0,
  313|      1|                q2x = -w / 2.0,
  314|      1|                q3x = -w / 4.0,
  315|      1|                q3y = -wave * 2.0,
  316|      1|                q4x = -w / 2.0,
  317|       |                fill = fill,
  318|       |                stroke = stroke,
  319|       |                sw = stroke_width,
  320|       |                opacity = opacity_attr,
  321|       |                dash = dash_attr,
  322|       |            )
  323|       |        }
  324|       |        // These shapes fall back to rectangle for now.
  325|       |        // TODO(v2): custom SVG generators for each.
  326|       |        ShapeType::Package
  327|       |        | ShapeType::Queue
  328|       |        | ShapeType::Page
  329|       |        | ShapeType::Step
  330|       |        | ShapeType::Callout
  331|       |        | ShapeType::StoredData => {
  332|      6|            let rx = border_radius.unwrap_or(0.0);
  333|      6|            format!(
  334|       |                "<rect x=\"{}\" y=\"{}\" width=\"{}\" height=\"{}\" rx=\"{}\" \
  335|       |                 fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"{}\"{}{}/>",
  336|       |                rect.x,
  337|       |                rect.y,
  338|       |                rect.width,
  339|       |                rect.height,
  340|       |                rx,
  341|       |                fill,
  342|       |                stroke,
  343|       |                stroke_width,
  344|       |                opacity_attr,
  345|       |                dash_attr,
  346|       |            )
  347|       |        }
  348|       |    }

  351|       |/// Clip a line (from ? toward) to a shape boundary.
  352|       |/// Returns the point on the shape boundary.
  353|     16|pub fn clip_to_shape(shape: ShapeType, rect: &Rect, from: Point, toward: Point) -> Point {
  354|     16|    match shape {
  355|      2|        ShapeType::Circle | ShapeType::Oval => clip_to_ellipse(rect, from, toward),
  356|     14|        ShapeType::Diamond => clip_to_diamond(rect, from, toward),
  357|      0|        ShapeType::Hexagon => clip_to_hexagon(rect, from, toward),
  358|      0|        _ => clip_to_rect(rect, from, toward),
  359|       |    }
  360|     16|}

  370|      1|    let dy = toward.y - from.y;

  425|       |/// Clip to ellipse boundary.
  426|      3|fn clip_to_ellipse(rect: &Rect, _from: Point, toward: Point) -> Point {
  427|      3|    let cx = rect.x + rect.width / 2.0;
  428|      3|    let cy = rect.y + rect.height / 2.0;
  429|      3|    let rx = rect.width / 2.0;
  430|      3|    let ry = rect.height / 2.0;
  431|       |
  432|      3|    let dx = toward.x - cx;
  433|      3|    let dy = toward.y - cy;
  434|      3|    let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
  435|       |
  436|      3|    if len < 1e-10 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (436:8): [True: 0, False: 2]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (436:8): [True: 0, False: 1]
  ------------------
  437|      0|        return Point::new(cx, cy - ry);
  438|      3|    }
  439|       |
  440|      3|    let ux = dx / len;
  441|      3|    let uy = dy / len;
  442|       |
  443|       |    // Parametric intersection with ellipse
  444|      3|    let denom = ((ux * ux) / (rx * rx) + (uy * uy) / (ry * ry)).sqrt();
  445|      3|    let t = 1.0 / denom;
  446|       |
  447|      3|    Point::new(cx + ux * t, cy + uy * t)
  448|      3|}
  449|       |
  450|       |/// Clip to diamond boundary.
  451|     14|fn clip_to_diamond(rect: &Rect, _from: Point, toward: Point) -> Point {
  452|     14|    let cx = rect.x + rect.width / 2.0;
  453|     14|    let cy = rect.y + rect.height / 2.0;
  454|     14|    let hw = rect.width / 2.0;
  455|     14|    let hh = rect.height / 2.0;
  456|       |
  457|     14|    let dx = toward.x - cx;
  458|     14|    let dy = toward.y - cy;
  459|       |
  460|     14|    if dx.abs() < 1e-10 && dy.abs() < 1e-10 {
                                         ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (460:8): [True: 6, False: 4]
  |  Branch (460:28): [True: 0, False: 6]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (460:8): [True: 0, False: 4]
  |  Branch (460:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  461|      0|        return Point::new(cx, cy - hh);
  462|     14|    }
  463|       |
  464|       |    // Diamond edges: |x/hw| + |y/hh| = 1
  465|     14|    let scale = (dx.abs() / hw + dy.abs() / hh).max(1e-10);
  466|     14|    Point::new(cx + dx / scale, cy + dy / scale)
  467|     14|}
  468|       |
  469|       |/// Clip to hexagon boundary.
  470|       |/// Hexagon has 6 edges: top-left, top-right, right, bottom-right, bottom-left, left.
  471|      0|fn clip_to_hexagon(rect: &Rect, _from: Point, toward: Point) -> Point {
  472|      0|    let cx = rect.x + rect.width / 2.0;
  473|      0|    let cy = rect.y + rect.height / 2.0;
  474|      0|    let inset = rect.width * 0.25;
  475|       |
  476|       |    // Hexagon vertices (matching the SVG generator)
  477|      0|    let vertices = [
  478|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + inset, rect.y),              // top-left
  479|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + rect.width - inset, rect.y), // top-right
  480|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + rect.width, cy),             // right
  481|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + rect.width - inset, rect.y + rect.height), // bottom-right
  482|      0|        Point::new(rect.x + inset, rect.y + rect.height), // bottom-left
  483|      0|        Point::new(rect.x, cy),                          // left
  484|      0|    ];
  485|       |
  486|      0|    let dx = toward.x - cx;
  487|      0|    let dy = toward.y - cy;
  488|       |
  489|      0|    if dx.abs() < 1e-10 && dy.abs() < 1e-10 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (489:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (489:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (489:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (489:28): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  490|      0|        return vertices[0]; // default: top-left vertex
  491|      0|    }
  492|       |
  493|       |    // Test intersection with each of the 6 edges
  494|      0|    let mut best_t = f64::INFINITY;
  495|      0|    let mut best_point = Point::new(cx, cy);
  496|       |
  497|      0|    for i in 0..6 {
  498|      0|        let v0 = vertices[i];
  499|      0|        let v1 = vertices[(i + 1) % 6];
  500|       |
  501|       |        // Ray from center in direction (dx, dy): P = center + t * dir
  502|       |        // Edge from v0 to v1: Q = v0 + s * (v1 - v0)
  503|      0|        let ex = v1.x - v0.x;
  504|      0|        let ey = v1.y - v0.y;
  505|      0|        let det = dx * ey - dy * ex;
  506|       |
  507|      0|        if det.abs() < 1e-10 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (507:12): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (507:12): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  508|      0|            continue; // parallel
  509|      0|        }
  510|       |
  511|      0|        let ox = v0.x - cx;
  512|      0|        let oy = v0.y - cy;
  513|      0|        let t = (ox * ey - oy * ex) / det;
  514|      0|        let s = (ox * dy - oy * dx) / det;
  515|       |
  516|      0|        if t > 1e-10 && (0.0..=1.0).contains(&s) && t < best_t {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (516:12): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (516:25): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (516:53): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (516:12): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (516:25): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (516:53): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  517|      0|            best_t = t;
  518|      0|            best_point = Point::new(cx + dx * t, cy + dy * t);
  519|      0|        }
  520|       |    }
  521|       |
  522|      0|    best_point
  523|      0|}

  541|       |/// Generate SVG for 3D effect (extra parallelogram face on bottom-right).
  542|       |/// The face is filled with a slightly darker shade of the shape's fill color.
  543|      0|pub fn three_d_svg(
  544|      0|    shape: ShapeType,
  545|      0|    rect: &Rect,
  546|      0|    _fill: &str,
  547|      0|    stroke: &str,
  548|      0|    stroke_width: f64,
  549|      0|) -> String {
  550|      0|    let depth = 6.0;
  551|       |    // Darken the fill color for the 3D face. Use a fixed dark overlay.
  552|      0|    let face_fill = "#00000040"; // semi-transparent black overlay
  553|       |
  554|      0|    match shape {
  555|       |        ShapeType::Rectangle | ShapeType::Square | ShapeType::Code => {
  556|       |            // Right face: parallelogram on the right side
  557|      0|            let right_face = format!(
  558|       |                "<polygon points=\"{},{} {},{} {},{} {},{}\" fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"{}\"/>",
  559|      0|                rect.x + rect.width, rect.y,
  560|      0|                rect.x + rect.width + depth, rect.y - depth,
  561|      0|                rect.x + rect.width + depth, rect.y + rect.height - depth,
  562|      0|                rect.x + rect.width, rect.y + rect.height,
  563|      0|                face_fill, stroke, stroke_width * 0.5,
  564|       |            );
  565|       |            // Top face: parallelogram on the top
  566|      0|            let top_face = format!(
  567|       |                "<polygon points=\"{},{} {},{} {},{} {},{}\" fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"{}\"/>",
  568|       |                rect.x, rect.y,
  569|      0|                rect.x + depth, rect.y - depth,
  570|      0|                rect.x + rect.width + depth, rect.y - depth,
  571|      0|                rect.x + rect.width, rect.y,
  572|      0|                face_fill, stroke, stroke_width * 0.5,
  573|       |            );
  574|      0|            format!("{}\n{}", right_face, top_face)
  575|       |        }

  577|       |            // Simplified: just a right-side parallelogram

  580|      0|                rect.x + rect.width, rect.y + 10.0,
  581|      0|                rect.x + rect.width + depth, rect.y + 10.0 - depth,
  582|      0|                rect.x + rect.width + depth, rect.y + rect.height - depth,
  583|      0|                rect.x + rect.width, rect.y + rect.height,
  584|      0|                face_fill, stroke, stroke_width * 0.5,
  585|       |            );
  586|      0|            right_face
  587|       |        }
  588|       |        _ => {

  590|      0|            let right_face = format!(
  591|       |                "<polygon points=\"{},{} {},{} {},{} {},{}\" fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"{}\"/>",
  592|      0|                rect.x + rect.width, rect.y,
  593|      0|                rect.x + rect.width + depth, rect.y - depth,
  594|      0|                rect.x + rect.width + depth, rect.y + rect.height - depth,
  595|      0|                rect.x + rect.width, rect.y + rect.height,
  596|      0|                face_fill, stroke, stroke_width * 0.5,
  597|       |            );
  598|      0|            right_face
  599|       |        }
  600|       |    }

  603|       |/// Generate SVG for "multiple" effect (stacked copies behind main shape).
  604|      0|pub fn multiple_svg(
  605|      0|    shape: ShapeType,
  606|      0|    rect: &Rect,
  607|      0|    fill: &str,
  608|      0|    stroke: &str,
  609|      0|    stroke_width: f64,
  610|      0|    border_radius: Option<f64>,
  611|      0|) -> String {
  612|      0|    let mut svg = String::new();
  613|       |    // Two offset copies behind main shape
  614|      0|    for offset in &[8.0, 4.0] {
  615|      0|        let offset_rect = Rect::new(rect.x + offset, rect.y + offset, rect.width, rect.height);
  616|      0|        svg.push_str(&shape_svg(
  617|      0|            shape,
  618|      0|            &offset_rect,
  619|      0|            fill,
  620|      0|            stroke,
  621|      0|            stroke_width,
  622|      0|            border_radius,
  623|      0|            None,
  624|      0|            None,
  625|      0|            false,
  626|      0|        ));
  627|      0|        svg.push('\n');
  628|      0|    }
  629|      0|    svg

    1|       |//! SVG output generation for D2 diagrams.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Generates SVG strings from positioned D2Graph. The SVG uses a
    4|       |//! computed viewBox based on the bounding box of all elements plus padding.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! Z-ordering (back to front):
    7|       |//! 1. Background rectangle
    8|       |//! 2. Container fills (outermost first)
    9|       |//! 3. Shape fills + strokes
   10|       |//! 4. Edges + arrowheads
   11|       |//! 5. All labels (topmost — always readable)
   12|       |

   25|  1.91k|    (v * 100.0).round() / 100.0
   26|  1.91k|}

   28|       |/// Default bend radius for rounded corners on orthogonal polylines.
   29|       |const BEND_RADIUS: f64 = 5.0;
   30|       |
   31|       |/// Build an SVG path `d` attribute for an orthogonal polyline with rounded corners.
   32|       |///
   33|       |/// Each internal vertex gets a quadratic Bézier arc that rounds the corner.
   34|       |/// The radius is clamped so it never exceeds half the length of either adjacent
   35|       |/// segment, which handles very short segments gracefully.
   36|    143|fn polyline_with_rounded_corners(points: &[Point], radius: f64) -> String {
   37|    143|    if points.len() < 2 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (37:8): [True: 0, False: 122]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (37:8): [True: 2, False: 19]
  ------------------
   38|      2|        return String::new();
   39|    141|    }
   40|       |
   41|    141|    let mut d = String::new();
   42|    141|    d.push_str(&format!("M {} {}", c(points[0].x), c(points[0].y)));
   43|       |
   44|    141|    if points.len() == 2 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (44:8): [True: 86, False: 36]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (44:8): [True: 13, False: 6]
  ------------------
   45|     99|        d.push_str(&format!(" L {} {}", c(points[1].x), c(points[1].y)));
   46|     99|        return d;
   47|     42|    }
   48|       |
   49|     90|    for i in 1..points.len() - 1 {
                           ^42
   50|     90|        let prev = points[i - 1];
   51|     90|        let curr = points[i];
   52|     90|        let next = points[i + 1];
   53|       |
   54|     90|        let dx1 = prev.x - curr.x;
   55|     90|        let dy1 = prev.y - curr.y;
   56|     90|        let len1 = (dx1 * dx1 + dy1 * dy1).sqrt();
   57|       |
   58|     90|        let dx2 = next.x - curr.x;
   59|     90|        let dy2 = next.y - curr.y;
   60|     90|        let len2 = (dx2 * dx2 + dy2 * dy2).sqrt();
   61|       |
   62|     90|        if len1 < 1e-6 || len2 < 1e-6 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (62:12): [True: 0, False: 80]
  |  Branch (62:27): [True: 0, False: 80]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (62:12): [True: 0, False: 10]
  |  Branch (62:27): [True: 0, False: 10]
  ------------------
   63|       |            // Degenerate segment — just line to the vertex
   64|      0|            d.push_str(&format!(" L {} {}", c(curr.x), c(curr.y)));
   65|      0|            continue;
   66|     90|        }
   67|       |
   68|     90|        let r = radius.min(len1 / 2.0).min(len2 / 2.0);
   69|       |
   70|     90|        let arc_start_x = curr.x + dx1 / len1 * r;
   71|     90|        let arc_start_y = curr.y + dy1 / len1 * r;
   72|     90|        let arc_end_x = curr.x + dx2 / len2 * r;
   73|     90|        let arc_end_y = curr.y + dy2 / len2 * r;
   74|       |
   75|     90|        d.push_str(&format!(
   76|     90|            " L {} {} Q {} {} {} {}",
   77|     90|            c(arc_start_x),
   78|     90|            c(arc_start_y),
   79|     90|            c(curr.x),
   80|     90|            c(curr.y),
   81|     90|            c(arc_end_x),
   82|     90|            c(arc_end_y),
   83|     90|        ));
   84|       |    }
   85|       |
   86|       |    // Final point
   87|     42|    let last = points[points.len() - 1];
   88|     42|    d.push_str(&format!(" L {} {}", c(last.x), c(last.y)));
   89|     42|    d

  472|       |/// Render a single arrowhead.
  473|    141|fn render_arrowhead(
  474|    141|    svg: &mut String,
  475|    141|    arrow_type: &ArrowheadType,
  476|    141|    tip: Point,
  477|    141|    from: Point,
  478|    141|    color: &str,
  479|    141|    stroke_width: f64,
  480|    141|) {
  481|    141|    let size = stroke_width * 5.0;
  482|       |
  483|    141|    match arrow_type {
  484|    141|        ArrowheadType::Arrow | ArrowheadType::Triangle => {
  485|    141|            let (t, l, r) = arrowhead_polygon(tip, from, size);
  486|    141|            svg.push_str(&format!(
  487|    141|                "  <polygon points=\"{},{} {},{} {},{}\" fill=\"{}\"/>\n",
  488|    141|                t.x, t.y, l.x, l.y, r.x, r.y, color,
  489|    141|            ));
  490|    141|        }
  491|       |        ArrowheadType::Diamond | ArrowheadType::FilledDiamond => {
  492|      0|            let pts = diamond_arrowhead(tip, from, size);
  493|      0|            let fill = if *arrow_type == ArrowheadType::FilledDiamond {
  ------------------

  ------------------
  |  Branch (493:27): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  494|      0|                color
  495|       |            } else {
  496|      0|                "none"
  497|       |            };
  498|      0|            svg.push_str(&format!(
  499|      0|                "  <polygon points=\"{},{} {},{} {},{} {},{}\" fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"1\"/>\n",
  500|      0|                pts[0].x, pts[0].y, pts[1].x, pts[1].y, pts[2].x, pts[2].y, pts[3].x, pts[3].y,
  501|      0|                fill, color,
  502|      0|            ));
  503|       |        }
  504|       |        ArrowheadType::Circle | ArrowheadType::FilledCircle => {
  505|      0|            let r = size * 0.4;
  506|      0|            let dx = tip.x - from.x;
  507|      0|            let dy = tip.y - from.y;
  508|      0|            let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
  509|      0|            let (cx, cy) = if len > 1e-6 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (509:31): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (509:31): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  510|      0|                let ux = dx / len;
  511|      0|                let uy = dy / len;
  512|      0|                (tip.x - ux * r, tip.y - uy * r)
  513|       |            } else {
  514|      0|                (tip.x, tip.y - r)
  515|       |            };
  516|      0|            let fill = if *arrow_type == ArrowheadType::FilledCircle {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (516:27): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (516:27): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  517|      0|                color
  518|       |            } else {
  519|      0|                "none"
  520|       |            };
  521|      0|            svg.push_str(&format!(
  522|      0|                "  <circle cx=\"{}\" cy=\"{}\" r=\"{}\" fill=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"1\"/>\n",
  523|      0|                cx, cy, r, fill, color,
  524|      0|            ));
  525|       |        }
  526|       |        ArrowheadType::CfOne | ArrowheadType::CfOneRequired => {
  527|       |            // Crow's foot: single line perpendicular at tip
  528|      0|            let dx = tip.x - from.x;
  529|      0|            let dy = tip.y - from.y;
  530|      0|            let len = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();
  531|      0|            if len > 1e-6 {
  ------------------

  533|      0|                let uy = dy / len;

  535|      0|                svg.push_str(&format!(
  536|      0|                    "  <line x1=\"{}\" y1=\"{}\" x2=\"{}\" y2=\"{}\" stroke=\"{}\" stroke-width=\"{}\"/>\n",
  537|      0|                    tip.x - uy * w, tip.y + ux * w,
  538|      0|                    tip.x + uy * w, tip.y - ux * w,
  539|      0|                    color, stroke_width,
  540|      0|                ));
  541|      0|                if *arrow_type == ArrowheadType::CfOneRequired {
  ------------------

  554|       |        ArrowheadType::CfMany | ArrowheadType::CfManyRequired => {

  754|    234|                cy,
  755|    234|                font_family,
  756|    234|                font_size,
  757|    234|                font_color,
  758|    234|                font_weight,
  759|    234|                font_style,
  760|    234|                decoration,
  761|    234|                xml_escape(&obj.label),
  762|    234|            ));
  763|    234|        } else {
  764|       |            // Multi-line label using tspan
  765|      0|            let line_height = font_size * 1.4;
  766|      0|            let total_height = obj.label_lines.len() as f64 * line_height;
  767|      0|            let start_y = cy - total_height / 2.0 + line_height / 2.0;
  768|       |
  769|      0|            svg.push_str(&format!(
  770|      0|                "  <text x=\"{}\" font-family=\"{}\" font-size=\"{}\" \
  771|      0|                 fill=\"{}\" font-weight=\"{}\" font-style=\"{}\" \
  772|      0|                 text-anchor=\"middle\" dominant-baseline=\"central\"{}>\n",
  773|      0|                cx, font_family, font_size, font_color, font_weight, font_style, decoration,
  774|      0|            ));
  775|       |
  776|      0|            for (i, line) in obj.label_lines.iter().enumerate() {

  778|      0|                svg.push_str(&format!(
  779|      0|                    "    <tspan x=\"{}\" y=\"{}\">{}</tspan>\n",
  780|      0|                    cx,
  781|      0|                    y,
  782|      0|                    xml_escape(line),
  783|      0|                ));
  784|      0|            }
  785|       |
  786|      0|            svg.push_str("  </text>\n");
  787|       |        }
  788|       |    }

  961|       |    fn test_orthogonal_route_renders_polyline() {
  962|       |        // Full render of a graph with route_type=Orthogonal should produce
  963|       |        // L/Q commands in SVG, not C (cubic Bezier).
  964|       |        let source = "direction: right\na -> b";
  965|       |        let ast = crate::parse(source).ast;
  966|       |        let mut graph = crate::compile(&ast).expect("compile");
  967|       |        crate::layout(&mut graph, &crate::RenderOptions::default()).expect("layout");
  968|       |
  969|       |        let svg = render(&graph, &crate::RenderOptions::default());
  970|       |
  971|       |        // The edge path should contain L (line to) commands from the polyline
  972|       |        // and should NOT contain C (cubic Bezier) commands.
  973|       |        // Self-loops would have C, but this graph has no self-loops.
  974|       |        // The SVG has <path d="M ... L ... Q ..." for orthogonal edges.
  975|       |        // Note: We just need to verify the edge path uses L/Q, not C.
  976|       |        // The shape paths may contain other commands, so focus on the edge marker.
  977|       |
  978|       |        // At minimum, the SVG should contain the rendered edge
  979|       |        assert!(
  980|       |            svg.contains(" L ") || svg.contains(" Q "),
  981|       |            "orthogonal route SVG should contain L or Q commands"
  982|       |        );
  983|       |    }

  985|       |    /// Extract the y attribute from a `<text>` tag preceding the given marker text.
  986|       |    fn extract_text_y(svg: &str, marker_text: &str) -> f64 {
  987|       |        let marker = format!(">{marker_text}</text>");
  988|       |        let text_tag_end = svg
  989|       |            .find(&marker)
  990|       |            .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("should contain '{marker_text}' label"));
  991|       |        let text_tag_start = svg[..text_tag_end]
  992|       |            .rfind("<text ")
  993|       |            .expect("should find <text");
  994|       |        let text_tag = &svg[text_tag_start..text_tag_end];
  995|       |
  996|       |        let y_pos = text_tag.find(" y=\"").expect("text tag should have y attr");
  997|       |        let y_start = y_pos + 4;
  998|       |        let y_end = text_tag[y_start..].find('"').unwrap() + y_start;
  999|       |        text_tag[y_start..y_end]
 1000|       |            .parse()
 1001|       |            .unwrap_or_else(|_| panic!("y attr '{}' should be a number", &text_tag[y_start..y_end]))
 1002|       |    }

 1024|       |    fn test_cylinder_label_below_cap() {
 1025|       |        // Cylinder labels must sit in the rectangular body, fully below the
 1026|       |        // elliptical top cap.  We verify this against the actual SVG output:
 1027|       |        // the label text y must be below cap_bottom + half the font height.
 1028|       |        let graph = layout_ok("db: User {shape: cylinder}");
 1029|       |        let svg = render(&graph, &RenderOptions::default());
 1030|       |
 1031|       |        let label_y = extract_text_y(&svg, "User");
 1032|       |        let (cap_cy, cap_ry) = extract_first_ellipse_cy_ry(&svg);
 1033|       |        let cap_bottom = cap_cy + cap_ry;
 1034|       |
 1035|       |        // With dominant-baseline="central", the y value is the text center.
 1036|       |        // The top of the text glyph is roughly y - font_size/2.
 1037|       |        let font_size = 14.0;
 1038|       |        let text_top = label_y - font_size / 2.0;
 1039|       |
 1040|       |        let clearance = text_top - cap_bottom;
 1041|       |        assert!(
 1042|       |            clearance >= 4.0,
 1043|       |            "cylinder label top ({text_top:.1}) must be >= 4px below \
 1044|       |             cap bottom ({cap_bottom:.1}), got {clearance:.1}px clearance.\n\
 1045|       |             SVG:\n{svg}"
 1046|       |        );
 1047|       |    }

 1064|       |            .find(&needle)

 1066|       |        let start = pos + needle.len();
 1067|       |        let end = tag[start..].find('"').unwrap() + start;
 1068|       |        tag[start..end].parse().unwrap()
 1069|       |    }

C:\language\mdmdview\crates\d2\src\text.rs:
    1|       |//! Text measurement and wrapping for D2 label sizing.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Uses heuristic measurement based on average character widths for
    4|       |//! proportional fonts, with word-wrapping for long text. This doesn't
    5|       |//! need to be pixel-perfect — the layout just needs reasonable width/height
    6|       |//! estimates. The final text rendering is done by resvg with real font metrics.
    7|       |
    8|       |use crate::graph::ShapeType;
    9|       |
   10|       |/// Maximum label width before wrapping (pixels at default font size of 14px).
   11|       |const MAX_LABEL_WIDTH: f64 = 200.0;
   12|       |
   13|       |/// Average character width multiplier for proportional fonts.
   14|       |/// Arial at 14px: average ~7.5px per character ? 0.55 * font_size.
   15|       |const CHAR_WIDTH_FACTOR: f64 = 0.55;
   16|       |
   17|       |/// Line height multiplier.
   18|       |const LINE_HEIGHT_FACTOR: f64 = 1.4;
   19|       |
   20|       |/// Result of text measurement.
   21|       |pub struct TextMeasurement {
   22|       |    /// Wrapped lines of text.
   23|       |    pub lines: Vec<String>,
   24|       |    /// Total text width in pixels.
   25|       |    pub width: f64,
   26|       |    /// Total text height in pixels.
   27|       |    pub height: f64,
   28|       |}
   29|       |
   30|       |/// Measure and wrap a label, returning wrapped lines and total dimensions.
   31|    542|pub fn measure_and_wrap_label(text: &str, font_size: f64, _font_family: &str) -> TextMeasurement {
   32|    542|    if text.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (32:8): [True: 0, False: 256]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (32:8): [True: 1, False: 285]
  ------------------
   33|      1|        return TextMeasurement {
   34|      1|            lines: Vec::new(),
   35|      1|            width: 0.0,
   36|      1|            height: 0.0,
   37|      1|        };
   38|    541|    }
   39|       |
   40|    541|    let avg_char_width = font_size * CHAR_WIDTH_FACTOR;
   41|    541|    let line_height = font_size * LINE_HEIGHT_FACTOR;
   42|    541|    let max_width = MAX_LABEL_WIDTH * (font_size / 14.0);
   43|       |
   44|    541|    let mut lines = Vec::new();
   45|    541|    let mut current_line = String::new();
   46|    541|    let mut current_width = 0.0;
   47|       |
   48|    592|    for word in text.split_whitespace() {
                              ^541 ^541
   49|    592|        let word_width = word.chars().count() as f64 * avg_char_width;
   50|    592|        if current_width + word_width > max_width && !current_line.is_empty() {
                                                                   ^4
  ------------------
  |  Branch (50:12): [True: 2, False: 278]
  |  Branch (50:54): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (50:12): [True: 2, False: 310]
  |  Branch (50:54): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
   51|      4|            lines.push(current_line.clone());
   52|      4|            current_line.clear();
   53|      4|            current_width = 0.0;
   54|    588|        }
   55|    592|        if !current_line.is_empty() {

  |  Branch (55:12): [True: 22, False: 258]

  |  Branch (55:12): [True: 25, False: 287]

   56|     47|            current_line.push(' ');
   57|     47|            current_width += avg_char_width;
   58|    545|        }
   59|    592|        current_line.push_str(word);
   60|    592|        current_width += word_width;
   61|       |    }
   62|    541|    if !current_line.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (62:8): [True: 256, False: 0]
  ------------------
  |  Branch (62:8): [True: 285, False: 0]
  ------------------
   63|    541|        lines.push(current_line);
   64|    541|    }
                  ^0
   65|       |
   66|    541|    let width = lines
   67|    541|        .iter()
   68|    545|        .map(|l| l.chars().count() as f64 * avg_char_width)
                       ^541
   69|    541|        .fold(0.0f64, f64::max);
   70|    541|    let height = lines.len() as f64 * line_height;
   71|       |
   72|    541|    TextMeasurement {
   73|    541|        lines,
   74|    541|        width,
   75|    541|        height,
   76|    541|    }

   78|       |
   79|       |/// Shape padding: (horizontal, vertical) in pixels.
   80|    877|pub fn shape_padding(shape: ShapeType) -> (f64, f64) {
   81|    877|    match shape {
   82|     10|        ShapeType::Circle | ShapeType::Oval => (30.0, 20.0),
   83|     15|        ShapeType::Diamond => (40.0, 30.0),
   84|      2|        ShapeType::Hexagon => (25.0, 15.0),
   85|     34|        ShapeType::Cylinder => (20.0, 30.0),
   86|     12|        ShapeType::Person => (20.0, 30.0),
   87|      3|        ShapeType::Text => (0.0, 0.0),
   88|    801|        _ => (20.0, 12.0), // Rectangle/Square and all others
   89|       |    }
   90|    877|}
   91|       |
   92|       |/// Compute node dimensions from label + shape padding + optional user overrides.
   93|    439|pub fn compute_node_dimensions(
   94|    439|    label: &str,
   95|    439|    font_size: f64,
   96|    439|    font_family: &str,
   97|    439|    shape: ShapeType,
   98|    439|    user_width: Option<f64>,
   99|    439|    user_height: Option<f64>,
  100|    439|) -> TextMeasurement {
  101|    439|    let mut measurement = measure_and_wrap_label(label, font_size, font_family);
  102|    439|    let (h_pad, v_pad) = shape_padding(shape);
  103|       |

  932|       |    /// Normalize all line endings to Unix style (\n)
  933|       |    /// Handles Windows (\r\n), Unix (\n), and old Mac (\r) formats
  934|     32|    fn normalize_line_endings(s: &str) -> String {
  935|       |        // Order matters: replace \r\n first (Windows), then remaining \r (old Mac)
  936|     32|        let normalized = s.replace("\r\n", "\n").replace('\r', "\n");
  937|     32|        match normalized.strip_prefix('\u{FEFF}') {
  938|      1|            Some(stripped) => stripped.to_string(),
  939|     31|            None => normalized,
  940|       |        }
  941|     32|    }

 1014|       |    /// Calculate readability statistics for the given text
 1015|    276|    fn calculate_readability(text: &str) -> ReadabilityStats {
 1016|    276|        let words: Vec<&str> = text.split_whitespace().collect();
 1017|    276|        let word_count = words.len();
 1018|       |
 1019|    276|        if word_count == 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1019:12): [True: 2, False: 274]
  ------------------
 1020|      2|            return ReadabilityStats::default();
 1021|    274|        }
 1022|       |
 1023|       |        // Count sentences (periods, exclamation marks, question marks)
 1024|    274|        let sentence_count = text
 1025|    274|            .chars()
 1026|  4.45M|            .filter(|&c| c == '.' || c == '!' || c == '?')
                           ^274                    ^4.44M      ^4.44M
  ------------------
  |  Branch (1026:26): [True: 1.53k, False: 4.44M]
  |  Branch (1026:38): [True: 252, False: 4.44M]
  ------------------
 1027|    274|            .count()
 1028|    274|            .max(1); // At least 1 sentence
 1029|       |
 1030|       |        // Count syllables
 1031|  38.3k|        let syllable_count: usize = words.iter().map(|w| Self::count_syllables(w)).sum();
                          ^274            ^274    ^274         ^274                              ^274
 1032|       |
 1033|    274|        let words_f = word_count as f32;
 1034|    274|        let sentences_f = sentence_count as f32;
 1035|    274|        let syllables_f = syllable_count as f32;
 1036|       |
 1037|       |        // Flesch Reading Ease: 206.835 - 1.015*(words/sentences) - 84.6*(syllables/words)
 1038|    274|        let flesch_ease =
 1039|    274|            206.835 - 1.015 * (words_f / sentences_f) - 84.6 * (syllables_f / words_f);
 1040|       |
 1041|       |        // Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level: 0.39*(words/sentences) + 11.8*(syllables/words) - 15.59
 1042|    274|        let grade_level = 0.39 * (words_f / sentences_f) + 11.8 * (syllables_f / words_f) - 15.59;
 1043|       |
 1044|    274|        ReadabilityStats {
 1045|    274|            flesch_ease: flesch_ease.clamp(0.0, 100.0),
 1046|    274|            grade_level: grade_level.max(0.0),
 1047|    274|            word_count,
 1048|    274|            sentences: sentence_count,
 1049|    274|            syllables: syllable_count,
 1050|    274|        }
 1051|    276|    }

 2326|       |
 2327|      7|    fn take_chars(text: &str, count: usize) -> String {
 2328|      7|        text.chars().take(count).collect()
 2329|      7|    }
 2330|       |
 2331|      1|    fn take_last_chars(text: &str, count: usize) -> String {
 2332|      1|        let mut chars: Vec<char> = text.chars().rev().take(count).collect();
 2333|      1|        chars.reverse();
 2334|      1|        chars.into_iter().collect()
 2335|      1|    }
 2336|       |
 2337|     10|    fn middle_ellipsize(text: &str, max_chars: usize) -> String {
 2338|     10|        let total_chars = text.chars().count();
 2339|     10|        if total_chars <= max_chars {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2339:12): [True: 1, False: 9]
  ------------------
 2340|      1|            return text.to_string();
 2341|      9|        }
 2342|       |
 2343|      9|        if max_chars <= 3 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2343:12): [True: 2, False: 7]
  ------------------
 2344|      2|            return ".".repeat(max_chars);
 2345|      7|        }
 2346|       |
 2347|      7|        let file_name = text
 2348|      7|            .rsplit(['\\', '/'])
 2349|      7|            .next()
 2350|      7|            .filter(|segment| !segment.is_empty());
 2351|       |
 2352|      7|        if let Some(file_name) = file_name {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2352:16): [True: 7, False: 0]
  ------------------
 2353|      7|            let file_name_chars = file_name.chars().count();
 2354|      7|            if file_name_chars + 3 < max_chars {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2354:16): [True: 6, False: 1]
  ------------------
 2355|      6|                let prefix_chars = max_chars - file_name_chars - 3;
 2356|      6|                return format!("{}...{}", Self::take_chars(text, prefix_chars), file_name);
 2357|      1|            }
 2358|      0|        }
 2359|       |
 2360|      1|        let head_chars = (max_chars - 3) / 2;
 2361|      1|        let tail_chars = max_chars - 3 - head_chars;
 2362|      1|        format!(
 2363|       |            "{}...{}",
 2364|      1|            Self::take_chars(text, head_chars),
 2365|      1|            Self::take_last_chars(text, tail_chars)
 2366|       |        )
 2367|     10|    }
 2368|       |
 2369|      3|    fn fit_middle_ellipsized_label(
 2370|      3|        ui: &egui::Ui,
 2371|      3|        prefix: &str,
 2372|      3|        value: &str,
 2373|      3|        max_width: f32,
 2374|      3|    ) -> String {

 2723|     36|    fn update_impl(&mut self, ctx: &Context) {
 2724|       |        // Show window after a few frames to ensure dark theme is fully rendered
 2725|       |        // (avoids white flash on startup)
 2726|     36|        if self.startup_frame < 3 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2726:12): [True: 33, False: 3]
  ------------------

 2729|     33|            if self.startup_frame == 3 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2729:16): [True: 1, False: 32]

 2730|      1|                ctx.send_viewport_cmd(egui::ViewportCommand::Visible(true));
 2731|     32|            }
 2732|      3|        }

 2736|       |        // that bypasses eframe's minimize filter, giving us this transition frame.

 3515|     16|        let height = image.size[1] as u32;

 3523|     14|    fn json_escape(value: &str) -> String {
 3524|     14|        let mut out = String::with_capacity(value.len() + 8);
 3525|    665|        for ch in value.chars() {
                                ^14   ^14
 3526|    665|            match ch {
 3527|     86|                '\\' => out.push_str("\\\\"),
 3528|      1|                '"' => out.push_str("\\\""),
 3529|      1|                '\n' => out.push_str("\\n"),
 3530|      1|                '\r' => out.push_str("\\r"),
 3531|      1|                '\t' => out.push_str("\\t"),
 3532|    575|                _ => out.push(ch),
 3533|       |            }
 3534|       |        }
 3535|     14|        out
 3536|     14|    }

 3579|       |    fn env_lock() -> std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
 3580|       |        static ENV_LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
 3581|       |        ENV_LOCK
 3582|       |            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
 3583|       |            .lock()
 3584|       |            .expect("env lock")
 3585|       |    }
 3586|       |
 3587|       |    struct EnvGuard {
 3588|       |        key: &'static str,
 3589|       |        original: Option<String>,
 3590|       |    }
 3591|       |
 3592|       |    impl EnvGuard {
 3593|       |        fn set(key: &'static str, value: &str) -> Self {
 3594|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
 3595|       |            std::env::set_var(key, value);
 3596|       |            Self { key, original }
 3597|       |        }
 3598|       |    }
 3599|       |
 3600|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
 3601|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3602|       |            if let Some(value) = &self.original {
 3603|       |                std::env::set_var(self.key, value);
 3604|       |            } else {
 3605|       |                std::env::remove_var(self.key);
 3606|       |            }
 3607|       |        }
 3608|       |    }
 3609|       |
 3610|       |    struct CurrentDirGuard {
 3611|       |        _lock: std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()>,
 3612|       |        original: PathBuf,
 3613|       |    }
 3614|       |
 3615|       |    impl CurrentDirGuard {
 3616|       |        fn set(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
 3617|       |            let lock = env_lock();
 3618|       |            let original = std::env::current_dir()?;
 3619|       |            std::env::set_current_dir(path)?;
 3620|       |            Ok(Self {
 3621|       |                _lock: lock,
 3622|       |                original,
 3623|       |            })
 3624|       |        }
 3625|       |    }
 3626|       |
 3627|       |    impl Drop for CurrentDirGuard {
 3628|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3629|       |            let _ = std::env::set_current_dir(&self.original);
 3630|       |        }
 3631|       |    }
 3632|       |
 3633|       |    /// Helper to set platform-appropriate config env var and return expected config subdir.
 3634|       |    fn set_config_env(temp_path: &std::path::Path) -> (EnvGuard, std::path::PathBuf) {
 3635|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
 3636|       |        {
 3637|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("APPDATA", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
 3638|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("MarkdownView");
 3639|       |            (guard, subdir)
 3640|       |        }
 3641|       |        #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
 3642|       |        {
 3643|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
 3644|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("Library/Application Support/MarkdownView");
 3645|       |            (guard, subdir)
 3646|       |        }
 3647|       |        #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos")))]
 3648|       |        {
 3649|       |            let guard = EnvGuard::set("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", temp_path.to_string_lossy().as_ref());
 3650|       |            let subdir = temp_path.join("mdmdview");
 3651|       |            (guard, subdir)
 3652|       |        }
 3653|       |    }

 3693|       |    struct ForcedAppActions {
 3694|       |        actions: Vec<&'static str>,
 3695|       |    }
 3696|       |
 3697|       |    impl ForcedAppActions {
 3698|       |        fn new(actions: &[&'static str]) -> Self {
 3699|       |            FORCED_APP_ACTIONS.with(|set| {
 3700|       |                let mut set = set.borrow_mut();
 3701|       |                for action in actions {
 3702|       |                    set.insert(*action);
 3703|       |                }
 3704|       |            });
 3705|       |            Self {
 3706|       |                actions: actions.to_vec(),
 3707|       |            }
 3708|       |        }
 3709|       |    }
 3710|       |
 3711|       |    impl Drop for ForcedAppActions {
 3712|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3713|       |            FORCED_APP_ACTIONS.with(|set| {
 3714|       |                let mut set = set.borrow_mut();
 3715|       |                for action in &self.actions {
 3716|       |                    set.remove(action);
 3717|       |                }
 3718|       |            });
 3719|       |        }
 3720|       |    }

 3722|       |    struct ForcedDialogPaths;
 3723|       |
 3724|       |    impl ForcedDialogPaths {
 3725|       |        fn new(open: Option<PathBuf>, save: Option<PathBuf>) -> Self {
 3726|       |            FORCED_OPEN_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3727|       |                *slot.borrow_mut() = open;
 3728|       |            });
 3729|       |            FORCED_SAVE_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3730|       |                *slot.borrow_mut() = save;
 3731|       |            });
 3732|       |            Self
 3733|       |        }
 3734|       |    }
 3735|       |
 3736|       |    impl Drop for ForcedDialogPaths {
 3737|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3738|       |            FORCED_OPEN_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3739|       |                slot.borrow_mut().take();
 3740|       |            });
 3741|       |            FORCED_SAVE_PATH.with(|slot| {
 3742|       |                slot.borrow_mut().take();
 3743|       |            });
 3744|       |        }
 3745|       |    }

 3747|       |    /// RAII guard that sets a thread-local `RefCell<bool>` flag to `true` on creation
 3748|       |    /// and restores it to `false` on drop. Used to inject errors in test paths.
 3749|       |    struct ForcedFlag {
 3750|       |        flag: &'static std::thread::LocalKey<RefCell<bool>>,
 3751|       |    }
 3752|       |
 3753|       |    impl ForcedFlag {
 3754|       |        fn new(flag: &'static std::thread::LocalKey<RefCell<bool>>) -> Self {
 3755|       |            flag.with(|f| *f.borrow_mut() = true);
 3756|       |            Self { flag }
 3757|       |        }
 3758|       |    }
 3759|       |
 3760|       |    impl Drop for ForcedFlag {
 3761|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
 3762|       |            self.flag.with(|f| *f.borrow_mut() = false);
 3763|       |        }
 3764|       |    }
 3765|       |

 3824|       |    /// Helper to store a TextEditState with a given cursor position into the raw editor.
 3825|       |    fn store_raw_editor_cursor(ctx: &egui::Context, cursor_pos: usize) {
 3826|       |        let editor_id = egui::Id::new("raw_editor");
 3827|       |        let mut state = egui::text_edit::TextEditState::default();
 3828|       |        let cr = egui::text::CCursorRange::one(egui::text::CCursor::new(cursor_pos));
 3829|       |        state.cursor.set_char_range(Some(cr));
 3830|       |        state.store(ctx, editor_id);
 3831|       |    }
 3832|       |
 3833|       |    /// Helper to store a TextEditState with no cursor range into the raw editor.
 3834|       |    fn store_raw_editor_no_cursor(ctx: &egui::Context) {
 3835|       |        let editor_id = egui::Id::new("raw_editor");
 3836|       |        let mut state = egui::text_edit::TextEditState::default();
 3837|       |        state.cursor.set_char_range(None);
 3838|       |        state.store(ctx, editor_id);
 3839|       |    }

 3856|       |    #[test]
 3857|       |    fn test_normalize_line_endings() {
 3858|       |        // Windows style (\r\n)
 3859|       |        assert_eq!(
 3860|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Hello\r\nWorld"),
 3861|       |            "Hello\nWorld"
 3862|       |        );
 3863|       |
 3864|       |        // Unix style (\n) - no change
 3865|       |        assert_eq!(
 3866|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Hello\nWorld"),
 3867|       |            "Hello\nWorld"
 3868|       |        );
 3869|       |
 3870|       |        // Old Mac style (\r)
 3871|       |        assert_eq!(
 3872|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Hello\rWorld"),
 3873|       |            "Hello\nWorld"
 3874|       |        );
 3875|       |
 3876|       |        // Mixed line endings
 3877|       |        assert_eq!(
 3878|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("A\r\nB\nC\rD"),
 3879|       |            "A\nB\nC\nD"
 3880|       |        );
 3881|       |
 3882|       |        // Multiple blank lines with Windows endings
 3883|       |        assert_eq!(
 3884|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("A\r\n\r\nB"),
 3885|       |            "A\n\nB"
 3886|       |        );
 3887|       |
 3888|       |        // Empty string
 3889|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings(""), "");
 3890|       |
 3891|       |        // No line endings
 3892|       |        assert_eq!(
 3893|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::normalize_line_endings("Single line"),
 3894|       |            "Single line"
 3895|       |        );
 3896|       |    }

 3898|       |    #[test]
 3899|       |    fn test_load_file_with_windows_line_endings() -> Result<()> {
 3900|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3901|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 3902|       |
 3903|       |        // Write content with explicit Windows line endings
 3904|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"Line 1\r\nLine 2\r\n\r\nParagraph text")?;
 3905|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 3906|       |
 3907|       |        app.load_file(temp_file.path().to_path_buf(), true)?;
 3908|       |
 3909|       |        // Should not contain any \r characters after normalization
 3910|       |        assert!(!app.current_content.contains('\r'));
 3911|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("Line 1\nLine 2"));
 3912|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("\n\nParagraph"));
 3913|       |        Ok(())
 3914|       |    }
 3915|       |
 3916|       |    #[test]
 3917|       |    fn test_load_file_with_mixed_line_endings() -> Result<()> {
 3918|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3919|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 3920|       |
 3921|       |        // Mix of \r\n (Windows), \n (Unix), and \r (old Mac)
 3922|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"Line 1\r\nLine 2\nLine 3\rLine 4")?;
 3923|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 3924|       |
 3925|       |        app.load_file(temp_file.path().to_path_buf(), true)?;
 3926|       |
 3927|       |        // All should be normalized to \n
 3928|       |        assert!(!app.current_content.contains('\r'));
 3929|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_content.lines().count(), 4);
 3930|       |        let lines: Vec<&str> = app.current_content.lines().collect();
 3931|       |        assert_eq!(lines[0], "Line 1");
 3932|       |        assert_eq!(lines[1], "Line 2");
 3933|       |        assert_eq!(lines[2], "Line 3");
 3934|       |        assert_eq!(lines[3], "Line 4");
 3935|       |        Ok(())
 3936|       |    }

 3940|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 3941|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 3942|       |
 3943|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"\xEF\xBB\xBF# Heading\nContent")?;
 3944|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 3945|       |
 3946|       |        app.load_file(temp_file.path().to_path_buf(), true)?;
 3947|       |
 3948|       |        assert!(!app.current_content.starts_with('\u{FEFF}'));

 3950|       |        Ok(())
 3951|       |    }

 3999|       |    fn test_toggle_view_mode_with_state_without_cursor_range() {

 4001|       |        app.view_mode = ViewMode::Raw;
 4002|       |        app.write_enabled = true;
 4003|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 4004|       |        store_raw_editor_no_cursor(&ctx);
 4005|       |
 4006|       |        app.toggle_view_mode(&ctx);
 4007|       |
 4008|       |        assert!(matches!(app.view_mode, ViewMode::Rendered));
 4009|       |        assert!(app.raw_cursor.is_none());
 4010|       |    }

 4085|       |    #[test]
 4086|       |    fn test_spawn_file_loader_processes_request() -> Result<()> {
 4087|       |        let temp = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4088|       |        writeln!(&temp, "hello")?;
 4089|       |
 4090|       |        let (request_tx, result_rx) = MarkdownViewerApp::spawn_file_loader();
 4091|       |        request_tx
 4092|       |            .send(FileLoadRequest {
 4093|       |                id: 99,
 4094|       |                path: temp.path().to_path_buf(),
 4095|       |            })
 4096|       |            .expect("send");
 4097|       |        let result = result_rx
 4098|       |            .recv_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1))
 4099|       |            .expect("result");
 4100|       |        let (content, lossy) = result.content.expect("content");
 4101|       |        assert!(content.contains("hello"));
 4102|       |        assert!(!lossy);
 4103|       |        drop(request_tx);
 4104|       |        Ok(())
 4105|       |    }

 4111|       |

 4115|       |                id: 101,
 4116|       |                path: missing_path,
 4117|       |            })
 4118|       |            .expect("send");

 4120|       |            .recv_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1))

 4141|       |    fn test_load_file() -> Result<()> {
 4142|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4143|       |
 4144|       |        // Create a temporary markdown file
 4145|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4146|       |        let content = "# Temporary File\n\nThis is a test markdown file.";
 4147|       |        temp_file.write_all(content.as_bytes())?;
 4148|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4149|       |
 4150|       |        let path = temp_file.path().to_path_buf();
 4151|       |        app.load_file(path.clone(), true)?;
 4152|       |
 4153|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_content, content);
 4154|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(path));
 4155|       |        assert!(!app.parsed_elements.is_empty());
 4156|       |        assert!(app.error_message.is_none());
 4157|       |
 4158|       |        Ok(())
 4159|       |    }

 4214|       |    #[test]
 4215|       |    fn test_poll_file_loads_handles_mismatch_and_lossy() {
 4216|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4217|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = unbounded::<FileLoadResult>();
 4218|       |        app.file_load_rx = result_rx;
 4219|       |
 4220|       |        let temp = NamedTempFile::new().expect("temp");
 4221|       |        let path = temp.path().to_path_buf();
 4222|       |        app.pending_file_load = Some(PendingFileLoad {
 4223|       |            id: 1,
 4224|       |            path: path.clone(),
 4225|       |        });
 4226|       |
 4227|       |        result_tx
 4228|       |            .send(FileLoadResult {
 4229|       |                id: 2,
 4230|       |                content: Ok(("ignored".to_string(), false)),
 4231|       |            })
 4232|       |            .expect("send mismatch");
 4233|       |        app.poll_file_loads();
 4234|       |        assert!(app.pending_file_load.is_some());
 4235|       |
 4236|       |        result_tx
 4237|       |            .send(FileLoadResult {
 4238|       |                id: 1,
 4239|       |                content: Ok(("loaded".to_string(), true)),
 4240|       |            })
 4241|       |            .expect("send match");
 4242|       |        app.poll_file_loads();
 4243|       |        assert!(app.pending_file_load.is_none());
 4244|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(path));
 4245|       |    }

 4258|       |        });

 4271|       |    #[test]
 4272|       |    fn test_poll_file_loads_sets_error_on_failure() {
 4273|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4274|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = unbounded::<FileLoadResult>();
 4275|       |        app.file_load_rx = result_rx;
 4276|       |
 4277|       |        let temp = NamedTempFile::new().expect("temp");
 4278|       |        let path = temp.path().to_path_buf();
 4279|       |        app.pending_file_load = Some(PendingFileLoad { id: 1, path });
 4280|       |
 4281|       |        result_tx
 4282|       |            .send(FileLoadResult {
 4283|       |                id: 1,
 4284|       |                content: Err("boom".to_string()),
 4285|       |            })
 4286|       |            .expect("send match");
 4287|       |        app.poll_file_loads();
 4288|       |        assert!(app.pending_file_load.is_none());
 4289|       |        assert!(app.error_message.as_deref().unwrap_or("").contains("boom"));
 4290|       |    }

 4339|       |    #[test]
 4340|       |    fn test_reload_current_file() -> Result<()> {
 4341|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4342|       |
 4343|       |        // Create a temporary markdown file
 4344|       |        let mut temp_file = NamedTempFile::new()?;
 4345|       |        let content1 = "# Title\n\nVersion 1";
 4346|       |        temp_file.write_all(content1.as_bytes())?;
 4347|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4348|       |
 4349|       |        let path = temp_file.path().to_path_buf();
 4350|       |        app.load_file(path.clone(), true)?;
 4351|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("Version 1"));
 4352|       |
 4353|       |        // Update file content
 4354|       |        let content2 = "# Title\n\nVersion 2";
 4355|       |        temp_file.as_file_mut().set_len(0)?; // clear
 4356|       |        temp_file.write_all(content2.as_bytes())?;
 4357|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4358|       |
 4359|       |        // Reload and verify
 4360|       |        app.reload_current_file()?;
 4361|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains("Version 2"));
 4362|       |        Ok(())
 4363|       |    }

 4411|       |    #[test]
 4412|       |    fn test_window_state_change_detection() {
 4413|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4414|       |        app.last_window_pos = Some([10.0, 10.0]);
 4415|       |        app.last_window_size = Some([800.0, 600.0]);
 4416|       |        app.last_window_maximized = false;
 4417|       |
 4418|       |        let state = app.current_window_state().expect("state");
 4419|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&state));
 4420|       |        app.last_persisted_state = Some(state);
 4421|       |        assert!(!app.window_state_changed(&state));
 4422|       |
 4423|       |        let mut moved = state;
 4424|       |        moved.pos = [12.0, 10.0];
 4425|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&moved));
 4426|       |
 4427|       |        let mut resized = state;
 4428|       |        resized.size = [820.0, 610.0];
 4429|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&resized));
 4430|       |
 4431|       |        let mut maximized = state;
 4432|       |        maximized.maximized = true;
 4433|       |        assert!(app.window_state_changed(&maximized));
 4434|       |    }

 4470|       |    #[test]
 4471|       |    fn test_complex_markdown_loading() {
 4472|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4473|       |        let complex_content = r#"# Complex Document
 4474|       |
 4475|       |## With Multiple Sections
 4476|       |
 4477|       |This has **bold** and *italic* text.
 4478|       |
 4479|       |```rust
 4480|       |fn main() {
 4481|       |    println!("Hello, world!");
 4482|       |}
 4483|       |```
 4484|       |
 4485|       |- List item 1
 4486|       |- List item 2
 4487|       |
 4488|       |> A blockquote
 4489|       |
 4490|       |[A link](https://example.com)
 4491|       |
 4492|       |---
 4493|       |
 4494|       |The end.
 4495|       |"#;
 4496|       |
 4497|       |        app.load_content(complex_content, Some("Complex".to_string()));
 4498|       |
 4499|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_content, complex_content);
 4500|       |        assert!(app.title.contains("Complex"));
 4501|       |        assert!(!app.parsed_elements.is_empty());
 4502|       |        assert!(app.error_message.is_none());
 4503|       |
 4504|       |        // Should have parsed various element types
 4505|       |        assert!(app.parsed_elements.len() > 5); // Complex document should have many elements
 4506|       |    }
 4507|       |

 4618|       |
 4619|       |    #[test]
 4620|       |    fn test_page_navigation_calculations() {
 4621|       |        // Test the page size calculation logic used in navigation
 4622|       |        let viewport_height = 800.0f32;
 4623|       |        let page_size = viewport_height * 0.8;
 4624|       |        assert_eq!(page_size, 640.0);
 4625|       |
 4626|       |        // Test boundary conditions for Page Up
 4627|       |        let current_offset = 100.0f32;
 4628|       |        let new_offset_up = (current_offset - page_size).max(0.0);
 4629|       |        assert_eq!(new_offset_up, 0.0); // Should clamp to 0
 4630|       |
 4631|       |        // Test Page Down calculation
 4632|       |        let new_offset_down = current_offset + page_size;
 4633|       |        assert_eq!(new_offset_down, 740.0);
 4634|       |
 4635|       |        // Test that Page Up from near top goes to 0
 4636|       |        let near_top = 300.0f32;
 4637|       |        let from_near_top = (near_top - page_size).max(0.0);
 4638|       |        assert_eq!(from_near_top, 0.0);
 4639|       |    }
 4640|       |
 4641|       |    #[test]
 4642|       |    fn test_compute_window_adjustment_clamps_offscreen_window() {
 4643|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(1200.0, 900.0), egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0));
 4644|       |        let monitor = egui::vec2(1024.0, 768.0);
 4645|       |        let adjustment =
 4646|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(Some(outer), Some(outer), Some(monitor))
 4647|       |                .expect("should adjust window geometry");
 4648|       |        let pos = adjustment.pos.expect("expected position adjustment");
 4649|       |        let size = adjustment.size.unwrap_or_else(|| outer.size());
 4650|       |        assert!(pos.x <= monitor.x - size.x + 1.0);
 4651|       |        assert!(pos.y <= monitor.y - size.y + 1.0);
 4652|       |        assert!(adjustment.size.is_none());
 4653|       |    }
 4654|       |
 4655|       |    #[test]
 4656|       |    fn test_compute_window_adjustment_respects_min_size() {
 4657|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(-200.0, -100.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 100.0));
 4658|       |        let monitor = egui::vec2(1920.0, 1080.0);
 4659|       |        let adjustment =
 4660|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(Some(outer), Some(outer), Some(monitor))
 4661|       |                .expect("should enforce minimum window size");
 4662|       |        let pos = adjustment.pos.unwrap_or(outer.min);
 4663|       |        let size = adjustment.size.expect("expected size adjustment");
 4664|       |        assert!(size.x >= 600.0);
 4665|       |        assert!(size.y >= 400.0);
 4666|       |        assert!(pos.x >= 0.0);
 4667|       |        assert!(pos.y >= 0.0);
 4668|       |    }

 4670|       |    #[test]
 4671|       |    fn test_is_valid_markdown_file() {
 4672|       |        let app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4673|       |
 4674|       |        // Valid markdown extensions
 4675|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.md")));
 4676|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.markdown")));
 4677|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.mdown")));
 4678|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.mkd")));
 4679|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.txt")));
 4680|       |
 4681|       |        // Case insensitive
 4682|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.MD")));
 4683|       |        assert!(app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.Markdown")));
 4684|       |
 4685|       |        // Invalid extensions
 4686|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.pdf")));
 4687|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.docx")));
 4688|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test.html")));
 4689|       |
 4690|       |        // No extension
 4691|       |        assert!(!app.is_valid_markdown_file(Path::new("test")));
 4692|       |    }

 4694|       |    #[test]
 4695|       |    fn test_scan_directory() -> Result<()> {
 4696|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 4697|       |        let dir_path = temp_dir.path();
 4698|       |
 4699|       |        // Create test files
 4700|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("zebra.md"), "# Z")?;
 4701|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("alpha.md"), "# A")?;
 4702|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("image.png"), "fake")?;
 4703|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("beta.markdown"), "# B")?;
 4704|       |
 4705|       |        // Create subdirectory (should be ignored)
 4706|       |        std::fs::create_dir(dir_path.join("subdir"))?;
 4707|       |        std::fs::write(dir_path.join("subdir/nested.md"), "# N")?;
 4708|       |
 4709|       |        let app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4710|       |        let files = app.scan_directory(dir_path)?;
 4711|       |
 4712|       |        // Should find 3 markdown files (alpha, beta, zebra)
 4713|       |        // Should NOT find image.png or nested.md
 4714|       |        assert_eq!(files.len(), 3);
 4715|       |
 4716|       |        // Should be sorted alphabetically
 4717|       |        assert!(files[0].ends_with("alpha.md"));
 4718|       |        assert!(files[1].ends_with("beta.markdown"));
 4719|       |        assert!(files[2].ends_with("zebra.md"));
 4720|       |
 4721|       |        Ok(())
 4722|       |    }

 4853|       |    #[test]
 4854|       |    fn test_queue_navigation() -> Result<()> {
 4855|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4856|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 4857|       |
 4858|       |        let file1 = temp_dir.path().join("file1.md");
 4859|       |        let file2 = temp_dir.path().join("file2.md");
 4860|       |
 4861|       |        std::fs::write(&file1, "# File 1")?;
 4862|       |        std::fs::write(&file2, "# File 2")?;
 4863|       |
 4864|       |        // Load first file and queue second
 4865|       |        app.load_file(file1.clone(), true)?;
 4866|       |        app.pending_files.push_back(file2.clone());
 4867|       |
 4868|       |        assert!(app.can_navigate_forward());
 4869|       |        app.navigate_forward();
 4870|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(file2));
 4871|       |        assert!(app.pending_files.is_empty());
 4872|       |
 4873|       |        Ok(())
 4874|       |    }

 4913|       |    fn test_toggle_write_mode_tracks_cursor_and_focus() {
 4914|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4915|       |        app.view_mode = ViewMode::Raw;
 4916|       |        app.write_enabled = false;
 4917|       |        app.raw_focus_requested = false;
 4918|       |
 4919|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 4920|       |        store_raw_editor_cursor(&ctx, 3);

 4923|       |        assert!(app.write_enabled);
 4924|       |        assert!(app.raw_focus_requested);
 4925|       |
 4926|       |        app.toggle_write_mode(&ctx);
 4927|       |        assert!(!app.write_enabled);
 4928|       |        assert_eq!(app.raw_cursor, Some(3));

 4946|       |    fn test_find_next_and_previous_wraps() {
 4947|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4948|       |        app.load_content("Alpha\n\nBeta\n\nGamma", Some("Search".to_string()));
 4949|       |        app.search_query = "beta".to_string();
 4950|       |
 4951|       |        app.find_next();
 4952|       |        assert_eq!(app.last_match_index, Some(1));
 4953|       |        assert_eq!(app.pending_scroll_to_element, Some(1));
 4954|       |
