 4962|       |    #[test]
 4963|       |    fn test_update_impl_rendered_view_runs() -> Result<()> {
 4964|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 4965|       |        app.nav_request = Some(NavigationRequest::PageDown);
 4966|       |        app.toggle_fullscreen = true;
 4967|       |
 4968|       |        let mut temp_file = tempfile::Builder::new().suffix(".md").tempfile()?;
 4969|       |        temp_file.write_all(b"# Dropped\nContent")?;
 4970|       |        temp_file.flush()?;
 4971|       |
 4972|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 4973|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 4974|       |        let vp = input
 4975|       |            .viewports
 4976|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 4977|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 4978|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1024.0, 768.0));
 4979|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 4980|       |            egui::pos2(1200.0, 900.0),
 4981|       |            egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0),
 4982|       |        ));
 4983|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 4984|       |            egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 4985|       |            egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0),
 4986|       |        ));
 4987|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(false);
 4988|       |        vp.maximized = Some(false);
 4989|       |        input.hovered_files.push(egui::HoveredFile {
 4990|       |            path: Some(temp_file.path().to_path_buf()),
 4991|       |            ..Default::default()
 4992|       |        });
 4993|       |        input.dropped_files.push(egui::DroppedFile {
 4994|       |            path: Some(temp_file.path().to_path_buf()),
 4995|       |            ..Default::default()
 4996|       |        });
 4997|       |
 4998|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 4999|       |
 5000|       |        assert!(app.drag_hover);

 5002|       |        assert!(app.last_window_pos.is_some());
 5003|       |        assert!(app.last_window_size.is_some());
 5004|       |        Ok(())
 5005|       |    }

 5062|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5063|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5064|       |
 5065|       |        let mut minimized = default_input();

 5067|       |            .viewports
 5068|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 5069|       |            .expect("root viewport");

 5072|       |        assert!(app.gpu_textures_released);
 5073|       |
 5074|       |        let mut restored = default_input();

 5076|       |            .viewports
 5077|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 5078|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 5079|       |        restored_vp.minimized = Some(false);

 5081|       |        assert!(!app.gpu_textures_released);
 5082|       |    }

 5148|       |    fn test_save_screenshot_image_crops_and_writes_metadata() -> Result<()> {
 5149|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 5150|       |        let output_path = temp_dir.path().join("shot.png");
 5151|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 5152|       |            content_only: true,
 5153|       |            scroll_ratio: Some(0.5),
 5154|       |            font_source: Some("TestFont".to_string()),
 5155|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(output_path.clone())
 5156|       |        };
 5157|       |        let snapshot = ScreenshotSnapshot {
 5158|       |            content_rect: Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 5159|       |                egui::pos2(10.0, 5.0),
 5160|       |                egui::vec2(20.0, 10.0),
 5161|       |            )),
 5162|       |            stable_frames: 3,
 5163|       |            last_scroll_offset: Some(12.0),
 5164|       |            ..test_screenshot_snapshot(config)
 5165|       |        };
 5166|       |
 5167|       |        let mut image = egui::ColorImage::new([80, 60], Color32::BLACK);
 5168|       |        image.pixels[0] = Color32::from_rgb(10, 20, 30);
 5169|       |
 5170|       |        MarkdownViewerApp::save_screenshot_image(&image, &snapshot)?;
 5171|       |
 5172|       |        let saved = image::open(&output_path)?;
 5173|       |        assert_eq!(saved.width(), 20);
 5174|       |        assert_eq!(saved.height(), 10);
 5175|       |
 5176|       |        let metadata = std::fs::read_to_string(output_path.with_extension("json"))?;
 5177|       |        assert!(metadata.contains("\"content_only\": true"));
 5178|       |        assert!(metadata.contains("\"font_source\": \"TestFont\""));
 5179|       |        Ok(())
 5180|       |    }

 5182|       |    #[test]
 5183|       |    fn test_update_screenshot_state_requests_and_scrolls() {
 5184|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 5185|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 5186|       |            scroll_ratio: Some(0.5),
 5187|       |            theme: ScreenshotTheme::Dark,
 5188|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(temp_dir.path().join("shot.png"))
 5189|       |        };
 5190|       |
 5191|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5192|       |        app.set_screenshot_mode(config);
 5193|       |
 5194|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5195|       |        let snapshot = ScrollSnapshot {
 5196|       |            content_size: egui::vec2(100.0, 200.0),
 5197|       |            inner_rect: egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(100.0, 100.0)),
 5198|       |            offset_y: 0.0,
 5199|       |        };
 5200|       |        app.update_screenshot_state(&ctx, Some(1), Some(snapshot));
 5201|       |
 5202|       |        let target_offset = app
 5203|       |            .screenshot
 5204|       |            .as_ref()
 5205|       |            .and_then(|state| state.scroll_offset)
 5206|       |            .unwrap_or(0.0);
 5207|       |        let snapshot_ready = ScrollSnapshot {
 5208|       |            content_size: egui::vec2(100.0, 200.0),
 5209|       |            inner_rect: egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(100.0, 100.0)),
 5210|       |            offset_y: target_offset,
 5211|       |        };
 5212|       |        app.update_screenshot_state(&ctx, Some(1), Some(snapshot_ready));
 5213|       |
 5214|       |        let state = app.screenshot.as_ref().expect("screenshot state");
 5215|       |        assert!(state.viewport_adjusted);
 5216|       |        assert!(state.requested);
 5217|       |        assert!(state.scroll_ready());
 5218|       |    }

 5401|       |    fn test_save_screenshot_image_crop_condition_variants() -> Result<()> {
 5402|       |        let temp_dir = TempDir::new()?;
 5403|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::from_rgb(10, 20, 30));
 5404|       |
 5405|       |        let make_config = |name: &str| ScreenshotConfig {
 5406|       |            content_only: true,
 5407|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(temp_dir.path().join(name))

 5412|       |                egui::pos2(0.0, 2.0),

 5414|       |            )),
 5415|       |            ..test_screenshot_snapshot(make_config("shot_y.png"))
 5416|       |        };

 5418|       |

 5421|       |                egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 5422|       |                egui::pos2(6.0, 10.0),
 5423|       |            )),
 5424|       |            ..test_screenshot_snapshot(make_config("shot_w.png"))
 5425|       |        };

 5427|       |
 5428|       |        let snapshot_h = ScreenshotSnapshot {

 5430|       |                egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 5431|       |                egui::pos2(10.0, 6.0),
 5432|       |            )),
 5433|       |            ..test_screenshot_snapshot(make_config("shot_h.png"))

 5440|       |        Ok(())

 5443|       |    #[test]
 5444|       |    fn test_handle_screenshot_event_saves_file() -> Result<()> {
 5445|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 5446|       |        let output_path = temp_dir.path().join("shot.png");
 5447|       |        let config = test_screenshot_config(output_path.clone());
 5448|       |
 5449|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5450|       |        app.set_screenshot_mode(config);
 5451|       |
 5452|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 5453|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([40, 30], Color32::WHITE);
 5454|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::Screenshot {
 5455|       |            viewport_id: egui::ViewportId::ROOT,
 5456|       |            image: Arc::new(image),
 5457|       |        });
 5458|       |
 5459|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5460|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 5461|       |
 5462|       |        assert!(output_path.exists());
 5463|       |        assert!(output_path.with_extension("json").exists());
 5464|       |        assert!(app.screenshot.as_ref().is_some_and(|state| state.done));
 5465|       |        Ok(())
 5466|       |    }

 5505|       |    #[test]
 5506|       |    fn test_handle_shortcuts_navigation_keys() {
 5507|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5508|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5509|       |
 5510|       |        let nav_cases = [
 5511|       |            (egui::Key::PageUp, NavigationRequest::PageUp),
 5512|       |            (egui::Key::PageDown, NavigationRequest::PageDown),
 5513|       |            (egui::Key::Home, NavigationRequest::Top),
 5514|       |            (egui::Key::End, NavigationRequest::Bottom),
 5515|       |            (egui::Key::ArrowUp, NavigationRequest::ScrollUp),
 5516|       |            (egui::Key::ArrowDown, NavigationRequest::ScrollDown),
 5517|       |        ];
 5518|       |        for (key, expected) in nav_cases {
 5519|       |            app.nav_request = None;
 5520|       |            let mut input = default_input();
 5521|       |            input.events.push(key_event(key, egui::Modifiers::NONE));
 5522|       |            let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 5523|       |                app.handle_shortcuts(ctx);
 5524|       |            });
 5525|       |            assert_eq!(
 5526|       |                std::mem::discriminant(&app.nav_request),
 5527|       |                std::mem::discriminant(&Some(expected)),
 5528|       |                "failed for key {:?}",
 5529|       |                key,
 5530|       |            );
 5531|       |        }
 5532|       |    }

 5534|       |    #[test]
 5535|       |    fn test_handle_shortcuts_escape_clears_search() {
 5536|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5537|       |        app.show_search = true;
 5538|       |        app.search_query = "alpha".to_string();
 5539|       |        app.last_query = "alpha".to_string();
 5540|       |        app.last_match_index = Some(0);
 5541|       |        app.pending_scroll_to_element = Some(1);
 5542|       |
 5543|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 5544|       |        input
 5545|       |            .events
 5546|       |            .push(key_event(egui::Key::Escape, egui::Modifiers::NONE));
 5547|       |
 5548|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5549|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 5550|       |            app.handle_shortcuts(ctx);
 5551|       |        });
 5552|       |
 5553|       |        assert!(!app.show_search);
 5554|       |        assert!(app.search_query.is_empty());
 5555|       |        assert!(app.last_query.is_empty());
 5556|       |        assert!(app.last_match_index.is_none());
 5557|       |        assert!(app.pending_scroll_to_element.is_none());
 5558|       |    }

 5560|       |    #[test]
 5561|       |    fn test_update_search_results_updates_matches() {
 5562|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5563|       |        app.load_content("Alpha\n\nBeta\n\nGamma", Some("Search".to_string()));
 5564|       |
 5565|       |        app.search_query = "beta".to_string();
 5566|       |        app.update_search_results("");
 5567|       |        assert_eq!(app.last_query, "beta");
 5568|       |        assert_eq!(app.last_match_index, Some(1));
 5569|       |        assert_eq!(app.pending_scroll_to_element, Some(1));
 5570|       |
 5571|       |        app.search_query.clear();
 5572|       |        app.update_search_results("beta");
 5573|       |        assert!(app.last_query.is_empty());
 5574|       |    }

 5757|       |    fn test_restore_from_history_sets_state() {
 5758|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5759|       |        app.current_content = "first".to_string();
 5760|       |        app.current_file = Some(PathBuf::from("first.md"));
 5761|       |        app.title = "mdmdview - first".to_string();
 5762|       |        app.push_history();
 5763|       |
 5764|       |        app.current_content = "second".to_string();
 5765|       |        app.current_file = None;
 5766|       |        app.title = "mdmdview - second".to_string();
 5767|       |        app.push_history();
 5768|       |
 5769|       |        app.history_index = 0;
 5770|       |        app.restore_from_history();
 5771|       |
 5772|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(PathBuf::from("first.md")));
 5773|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_content, "first");
 5774|       |        assert_eq!(app.pending_scroll_to_element, Some(0));
 5775|       |    }

 5790|       |    fn test_handle_file_drop_mixed_files() -> Result<()> {
 5791|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5792|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
 5793|       |        let good1 = temp_dir.path().join("good1.md");
 5794|       |        let good2 = temp_dir.path().join("good2.md");
 5795|       |        let bad = temp_dir.path().join("bad.pdf");
 5796|       |        std::fs::write(&good1, "# Good1")?;
 5797|       |        std::fs::write(&good2, "# Good2")?;
 5798|       |        std::fs::write(&bad, "nope")?;
 5799|       |
 5800|       |        app.handle_file_drop(vec![bad, good1.clone(), good2.clone()]);
 5801|       |
 5802|       |        assert_eq!(app.current_file, Some(good1));
 5803|       |        assert!(app.pending_files.contains(&good2));
 5804|       |        assert!(app.error_message.is_some());
 5805|       |        Ok(())
 5806|       |    }

 5834|       |    #[test]
 5835|       |    fn test_move_raw_cursor_lines_up_and_down() {
 5836|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5837|       |        setup_raw_write_mode(&mut app, "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3");
 5838|       |
 5839|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5840|       |        store_raw_editor_cursor(&ctx, 0);
 5841|       |
 5842|       |        app.move_raw_cursor_lines(&ctx, 1);
 5843|       |        let down_idx = app.raw_cursor.unwrap_or(0);
 5844|       |        assert!(down_idx > 0);
 5845|       |
 5846|       |        app.move_raw_cursor_lines(&ctx, -1);
 5847|       |        let up_idx = app.raw_cursor.unwrap_or(0);
 5848|       |        assert!(up_idx <= down_idx);
 5849|       |    }

 5926|       |    #[test]
 5927|       |    fn test_move_raw_cursor_lines_down_at_end_no_loop() {
 5928|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 5929|       |        setup_raw_write_mode(&mut app, "Line 1\nLine 2");
 5930|       |
 5931|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 5932|       |        store_raw_editor_cursor(&ctx, app.raw_buffer.len());
 5933|       |
 5934|       |        app.move_raw_cursor_lines(&ctx, 1);
 5935|       |        assert_eq!(app.raw_cursor, Some(app.raw_buffer.len()));
 5936|       |    }

 6007|       |    fn test_compute_window_adjustment_clamps() {
 6008|       |        let outer = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(-100.0, -100.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 100.0));
 6009|       |        let monitor = egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0);
 6010|       |        let adjusted =
 6011|       |            MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(Some(outer), Some(outer), Some(monitor));
 6012|       |        assert!(adjusted.is_some());
 6013|       |
 6014|       |        let outer_ok = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(10.0, 10.0), egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0));
 6015|       |        let unchanged = MarkdownViewerApp::compute_window_adjustment(
 6016|       |            Some(outer_ok),
 6017|       |            Some(outer_ok),
 6018|       |            Some(monitor),
 6019|       |        );
 6020|       |        assert!(unchanged.is_none());
 6021|       |    }

 6129|       |

 6405|       |    #[test]
 6406|       |    fn test_middle_ellipsize_preserves_filename_when_possible() {
 6407|       |        let path = r"C:\language\mdmdview\wrk_docs\2026.02.26 - HLD - Munt.md";
 6408|       |
 6409|       |        let shortened = MarkdownViewerApp::middle_ellipsize(path, 40);
 6410|       |
 6411|       |        assert!(shortened.starts_with("C:\\language"));
 6412|       |        assert!(shortened.contains("..."));
 6413|       |        assert!(shortened.ends_with("2026.02.26 - HLD - Munt.md"));
 6414|       |    }

 6416|       |    #[test]
 6417|       |    fn test_middle_ellipsize_short_and_tiny_limits() {
 6418|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::middle_ellipsize("short", 10), "short");
 6419|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::middle_ellipsize("abcdef", 3), "...");
 6420|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::middle_ellipsize("abcdef", 2), "..");
 6421|       |    }

 6630|       |    fn test_update_impl_raw_edit_updates_content() {
 6631|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 6632|       |        app.view_mode = ViewMode::Raw;
 6633|       |        app.write_enabled = true;
 6634|       |        app.raw_focus_requested = true;
 6635|       |        app.raw_buffer = "Line".to_string();
 6636|       |        app.current_content = app.raw_buffer.clone();
 6637|       |
 6638|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 6639|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6640|       |        input.focused = true;
 6641|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 6642|       |
 6643|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6644|       |        input.focused = true;
 6645|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::Text("X".to_string()));
 6646|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 6647|       |
 6648|       |        assert!(app.current_content.contains('X'));
 6649|       |    }

 6694|       |        let nested = temp_dir.path().join("nested").join("shot.png");

 6696|       |            viewport_width: 40.0,
 6697|       |            viewport_height: 30.0,
 6698|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(nested.clone())

 6742|       |    #[test]
 6743|       |    fn test_handle_screenshot_events_branches() {
 6744|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 6745|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 6746|       |
 6747|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6748|       |        input
 6749|       |            .events
 6750|       |            .push(key_event(egui::Key::A, egui::Modifiers::NONE));
 6751|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 6752|       |            app.handle_screenshot_events(ctx);
 6753|       |        });
 6754|       |
 6755|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE);
 6756|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6757|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::Screenshot {
 6758|       |            viewport_id: egui::ViewportId::ROOT,
 6759|       |            image: std::sync::Arc::new(image),
 6760|       |        });
 6761|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 6762|       |            app.handle_screenshot_events(ctx);
 6763|       |        });
 6764|       |
 6765|       |        let temp_dir = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 6766|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 6767|       |            viewport_width: 40.0,
 6768|       |            viewport_height: 30.0,
 6769|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(temp_dir.path().to_path_buf())
 6770|       |        };
 6771|       |        app.screenshot = Some(ScreenshotState::new(config));
 6772|       |        let state = app.screenshot.as_mut().expect("screenshot state");
 6773|       |        state.done = true;
 6774|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE);
 6775|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 6776|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::Screenshot {
 6777|       |            viewport_id: egui::ViewportId::ROOT,
 6778|       |            image: std::sync::Arc::new(image),
 6779|       |        });
 6780|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 6781|       |            app.handle_screenshot_events(ctx);
 6782|       |        });
 6783|       |    }

 6785|       |    #[test]
 6786|       |    fn test_screenshot_state_helpers() {
 6787|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 6788|       |            viewport_width: 120.0,
 6789|       |            scroll_ratio: Some(0.5),
 6790|       |            wait_ms: 500,
 6791|       |            settle_frames: 2,
 6792|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(PathBuf::from("dummy.png"))
 6793|       |        };
 6794|       |        let mut state = ScreenshotState::new(config);
 6795|       |        assert!(!state.scroll_ready());
 6796|       |
 6797|       |        let snapshot = ScrollSnapshot {
 6798|       |            content_size: egui::vec2(200.0, 400.0),
 6799|       |            inner_rect: egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 200.0)),
 6800|       |            offset_y: 0.0,
 6801|       |        };
 6802|       |        assert!(state.record_scroll(snapshot));
 6803|       |        assert!(!state.record_scroll(snapshot));
 6804|       |
 6805|       |        state.update_stability(None, None);
 6806|       |        state.last_scroll_offset = Some(0.0);
 6807|       |        state.update_stability(Some(1), Some(10.0));
 6808|       |    }

 6811|       |    fn test_screenshot_is_stable_requires_no_pending() {
 6812|       |        assert!(MarkdownViewerApp::screenshot_is_stable(true, 2, 2, false));
 6813|       |        assert!(!MarkdownViewerApp::screenshot_is_stable(true, 2, 2, true));
 6814|       |        assert!(!MarkdownViewerApp::screenshot_is_stable(false, 2, 2, false));
 6815|       |        assert!(!MarkdownViewerApp::screenshot_is_stable(true, 1, 2, false));
 6816|       |    }

 6819|       |    fn test_record_scroll_without_ratio_returns_false() {
 6820|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 6821|       |            viewport_width: 120.0,
 6822|       |            wait_ms: 500,
 6823|       |            settle_frames: 2,
 6824|       |            ..test_screenshot_config(PathBuf::from("dummy.png"))
 6825|       |        };
 6826|       |        let mut state = ScreenshotState::new(config);
 6827|       |        let snapshot = ScrollSnapshot {
 6828|       |            content_size: egui::vec2(200.0, 400.0),
 6829|       |            inner_rect: egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 200.0)),
 6830|       |            offset_y: 0.0,
 6831|       |        };
 6832|       |        assert!(!state.record_scroll(snapshot));
 6833|       |        assert!(state.scroll_offset.is_none());
 6834|       |    }

 7318|       |    #[test]
 7319|       |    fn test_update_impl_persists_window_state_when_due() {
 7320|       |        let _lock = env_lock();
 7321|       |        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 7322|       |        let (_guard, config_dir) = set_config_env(temp_dir.path());
 7323|       |
 7324|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7325|       |        app.last_persist_instant = std::time::Instant::now() - std::time::Duration::from_secs(2);
 7326|       |
 7327|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7328|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 7329|       |        let vp = input
 7330|       |            .viewports
 7331|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 7332|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 7333|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1024.0, 768.0));
 7334|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7335|       |            egui::pos2(10.0, 10.0),
 7336|       |            egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0),
 7337|       |        ));
 7338|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7339|       |            egui::pos2(20.0, 20.0),
 7340|       |            egui::vec2(780.0, 580.0),
 7341|       |        ));
 7342|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(false);
 7343|       |        vp.maximized = Some(false);
 7344|       |
 7345|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 7346|       |
 7347|       |        let path = config_dir.join("window_state.txt");
 7348|       |        assert!(path.exists());
 7349|       |        assert!(app.last_persisted_state.is_some());
 7350|       |    }
 7351|       |
 7352|       |    #[test]
 7353|       |    fn test_update_impl_fullscreen_skips_size_persist() {
 7354|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7355|       |        app.last_window_size = None;
 7356|       |
 7357|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7358|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 7359|       |        let vp = input
 7360|       |            .viewports
 7361|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 7362|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 7363|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1024.0, 768.0));
 7364|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7365|       |            egui::pos2(10.0, 10.0),
 7366|       |            egui::vec2(800.0, 600.0),
 7367|       |        ));
 7368|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7369|       |            egui::pos2(20.0, 20.0),
 7370|       |            egui::vec2(780.0, 580.0),
 7371|       |        ));
 7372|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(true);
 7373|       |        vp.maximized = Some(false);
 7374|       |
 7375|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 7376|       |
 7377|       |        assert!(app.last_window_size.is_none());
 7378|       |    }
 7379|       |
 7380|       |    #[test]
 7381|       |    fn test_update_impl_maximized_skips_size_and_pos_tracking() {
 7382|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7383|       |        app.last_window_size = None;
 7384|       |        app.last_window_pos = None;
 7385|       |
 7386|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7387|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 7388|       |        let vp = input
 7389|       |            .viewports
 7390|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 7391|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 7392|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1920.0, 1080.0));
 7393|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7394|       |            egui::pos2(-8.0, -8.0),
 7395|       |            egui::vec2(1936.0, 1096.0),
 7396|       |        ));
 7397|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7398|       |            egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 7399|       |            egui::vec2(1920.0, 1048.0),
 7400|       |        ));
 7401|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(false);
 7402|       |        vp.maximized = Some(true);
 7403|       |
 7404|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 7405|       |
 7406|       |        // Pos and size should NOT be updated when maximized
 7407|       |        assert!(app.last_window_size.is_none());
 7408|       |        assert!(app.last_window_pos.is_none());
 7409|       |        // But maximized flag should be tracked
 7410|       |        assert!(app.last_window_maximized);
 7411|       |    }
 7412|       |
 7413|       |    #[test]
 7414|       |    fn test_update_impl_maximized_preserves_pre_maximize_geometry() {
 7415|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7416|       |        // Simulate pre-maximize state: window at (50,50) with size 900x700
 7417|       |        app.last_window_pos = Some([50.0, 50.0]);
 7418|       |        app.last_window_size = Some([900.0, 700.0]);
 7419|       |        app.last_window_maximized = false;
 7420|       |
 7421|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7422|       |        let mut input = default_input();
 7423|       |        let vp = input
 7424|       |            .viewports
 7425|       |            .get_mut(&egui::ViewportId::ROOT)
 7426|       |            .expect("root viewport");
 7427|       |        vp.monitor_size = Some(egui::vec2(1920.0, 1080.0));
 7428|       |        // Maximized window: outer extends beyond screen, inner fills it
 7429|       |        vp.outer_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7430|       |            egui::pos2(-8.0, -8.0),
 7431|       |            egui::vec2(1936.0, 1096.0),
 7432|       |        ));
 7433|       |        vp.inner_rect = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7434|       |            egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 7435|       |            egui::vec2(1920.0, 1048.0),
 7436|       |        ));
 7437|       |        vp.fullscreen = Some(false);
 7438|       |        vp.maximized = Some(true);
 7439|       |
 7440|       |        run_app_frame(&mut app, &ctx, input);
 7441|       |
 7442|       |        // Pre-maximize geometry must be preserved, not overwritten with maximized dimensions
 7443|       |        assert_eq!(app.last_window_pos, Some([50.0, 50.0]));
 7444|       |        assert_eq!(app.last_window_size, Some([900.0, 700.0]));
 7445|       |        assert!(app.last_window_maximized);
 7446|       |    }

 7463|       |    }

 7541|       |        let config = ScreenshotConfig {
 7542|       |            viewport_width: 200.0,
 7543|       |            viewport_height: 100.0,

 7546|       |        };

 7548|       |        state.requested = true;

 7573|       |    fn test_count_syllables_basic_words() {
 7574|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("hello"), 2);
 7575|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("world"), 1);
 7576|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("computer"), 3);
 7577|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("the"), 1);
 7578|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("a"), 1);
 7579|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables(""), 0);
 7580|       |        // Silent e
 7581|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("make"), 1);
 7582|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownViewerApp::count_syllables("time"), 1);
 7583|       |    }

 7615|       |    #[test]
 7616|       |    fn test_poll_readability_discards_stale_result() {
 7617|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7618|       |        // Set current request ID to 5
 7619|       |        app.readability_request_id = 5;
 7620|       |        // Send a stale result with ID 3 (mismatches)
 7621|       |        let _ = app.readability_tx.send(ReadabilityRequest {
 7622|       |            id: 3,
 7623|       |            content: "Stale content here.".to_string(),
 7624|       |        });
 7625|       |        // Give the background thread time to process
 7626|       |        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100));
 7627|       |        app.poll_readability();
 7628|       |        // Stale result should be discarded
 7629|       |        assert!(app.readability_stats.is_none());
 7630|       |
 7631|       |        // Now send a matching result
 7632|       |        let _ = app.readability_tx.send(ReadabilityRequest {
 7633|       |            id: 5,
 7634|       |            content: "Matching content here.".to_string(),
 7635|       |        });
 7636|       |        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100));
 7637|       |        app.poll_readability();
 7638|       |        // Matching result should be accepted
 7639|       |        assert!(app.readability_stats.is_some());
 7640|       |    }

 7701|       |            });
 7702|       |
 7703|       |            let _actions = ForcedAppActions::new(&["status_hover"]);
 7704|       |            let _ = ctx.run(default_input(), |ctx| {

 7813|       |    fn test_toggle_theme() {
 7814|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7815|       |        assert!(app.settings.dark_mode); // default is dark
 7816|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();

 7818|       |        assert!(!app.settings.dark_mode); // now light

 7820|       |        assert!(app.settings.dark_mode); // back to dark
 7821|       |    }

 7838|       |    #[test]
 7839|       |    fn test_apply_saved_theme_respects_setting() {
 7840|       |        let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
 7841|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7842|       |
 7843|       |        app.settings.dark_mode = false;
 7844|       |        app.apply_saved_theme(&ctx);
 7845|       |        assert!(!ctx.style().visuals.dark_mode);
 7846|       |
 7847|       |        app.settings.dark_mode = true;
 7848|       |        app.apply_saved_theme(&ctx);

   25|       |//! Full Test Runner for mdmdview
   26|       |//!
   27|       |//! Runs all quality checks, tests, Mermaid visual comparisons, D2 visual
   28|       |//! regression tests, and coverage in a single invocation, then generates
   29|       |//! a self-contained HTML report.
   30|       |//!
   31|       |//! Phases (run sequentially, fast-to-slow):
   32|       |//! 1. Code quality checks (cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy)
   33|       |//! 2. Unit + D2 tests (cargo test --release --lib --tests --workspace)
   34|       |//! 3. Mermaid visual tests (screenshot comparison against reference images)
   35|       |//!    3b. D2 visual tests (SVG rasterization comparison against d2 CLI)
   36|       |//! 4. Coverage analysis (cargo llvm-cov, optional)
   37|       |//!
   38|       |//! Usage:
   39|       |//!   cargo run --release --bin full_test
   40|       |//!
   41|       |//! Options:
   42|       |//!   --skip-quality           Skip fmt/clippy checks
   43|       |//!   --skip-coverage          Skip coverage analysis
   44|       |//!   --skip-mermaid           Skip Mermaid visual tests
   45|       |//!   --skip-d2-visual         Skip D2 visual regression tests
   46|       |//!   --update-d2-references   Regenerate D2 reference PNGs from d2 CLI
   47|       |//!   --quick                  Exclude ignored tests
   48|       |//!   --help, -h               Show this help message
   49|       |

   55|       |
   56|       |const VERSION: &str = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION");

  970|       |/// Rasterize an SVG string to a PNG file using usvg/resvg.
  971|       |/// Uses a shared fontdb to avoid rebuilding system fonts per fixture (~100ms each).
  972|      0|fn rasterize_svg_to_png(
  973|      0|    svg: &str,
  974|      0|    output: &Path,
  975|      0|    fontdb: &std::sync::Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  976|      0|) -> Result<(), String> {
  977|      0|    let opt = usvg::Options {
  978|      0|        fontdb: std::sync::Arc::clone(fontdb),
  979|      0|        ..Default::default()
  980|      0|    };
  981|      0|    let tree = usvg::Tree::from_data(svg.as_bytes(), &opt)
  982|      0|        .map_err(|e| format!("usvg parse failed: {e}"))?;
  983|       |
  984|      0|    let size = tree.size();
  985|      0|    let w = size.width().ceil() as u32;
  986|      0|    let h = size.height().ceil() as u32;
  987|      0|    if w == 0 || h == 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (987:8): [True: 0, False: 0]
  |  Branch (987:18): [True: 0, False: 0]
  ------------------
  988|      0|        return Err(format!("SVG has zero dimensions: {w}x{h}"));
  989|      0|    }
  990|       |
  991|      0|    let mut pixmap =
  992|      0|        tiny_skia::Pixmap::new(w, h).ok_or_else(|| format!("Failed to create pixmap {w}x{h}"))?;
  993|       |    // Fill with white background so transparent regions don't cause false diffs.
  994|      0|    pixmap.fill(tiny_skia::Color::WHITE);
  995|      0|    let mut pmut = pixmap.as_mut();
  996|      0|    resvg::render(&tree, tiny_skia::Transform::identity(), &mut pmut);
  997|       |
  998|       |    // Convert to image::RgbaImage and save as PNG.
  999|      0|    let rgba_data = pixmap.data().to_vec();
 1000|      0|    let img = image::RgbaImage::from_raw(w, h, rgba_data)
 1001|      0|        .ok_or_else(|| "Failed to create RgbaImage from pixmap".to_string())?;
 1002|      0|    img.save(output)
 1003|      0|        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to save PNG: {e}"))?;
 1004|       |
 1005|      0|    Ok(())
 1006|      0|}

 1458|       |/// Extract a number from nested JSON structure.
 1459|       |/// Looks for section_key, then within that section finds value_key and extracts the number.
 1460|     12|fn extract_json_number(json: &str, section_key: &str, value_key: &str) -> Option<usize> {
 1461|     12|    let section_start = json.find(section_key)?;
                      ^11                                   ^1
 1462|     11|    let section = &json[section_start..];
 1463|     11|    let brace_start = section.find('{')?;
                                                     ^0
 1464|     11|    let section_content = &section[brace_start..];
 1465|     11|    let brace_end = section_content.find('}')?;
                                                           ^0
 1466|     11|    let inner = &section_content[..brace_end];
 1467|       |
 1468|     11|    let value_start = inner.find(value_key)?;
                      ^10                                ^1
 1469|     10|    let after_key = &inner[value_start + value_key.len()..];
 1470|     10|    let after_colon = after_key.trim_start().strip_prefix(':')?;
                                                                            ^0
 1471|     10|    let num_str = after_colon.trim_start();
 1472|       |
 1473|     10|    let end = num_str
 1474|     34|        .find(|c: char| !c.is_ascii_digit())
                       ^10
 1475|     10|        .unwrap_or(num_str.len());
 1476|     10|    num_str[..end].parse().ok()

 2176|       |    fn make_result(name: &str, passed: bool) -> TestResult {
 2177|       |        TestResult {
 2178|       |            name: name.to_string(),
 2179|       |            passed,
 2180|       |            duration: Duration::ZERO,
 2181|       |            output: String::new(),
 2182|       |        }
 2183|       |    }
 2184|       |
 2185|       |    fn make_suite(
 2186|       |        passed: usize,
 2187|       |        failed: usize,
 2188|       |        skipped: usize,
 2189|       |        tests: Vec<TestResult>,
 2190|       |    ) -> SuiteResults {
 2191|       |        SuiteResults {
 2192|       |            mode: "tests".to_string(),
 2193|       |            total: passed + failed + skipped,
 2194|       |            passed,
 2195|       |            failed,
 2196|       |            skipped,
 2197|       |            duration: Duration::from_secs(5),
 2198|       |            tests,
 2199|       |        }
 2200|       |    }

 2204|       |    #[test]
 2205|       |    fn test_parse_options_defaults() {
 2206|       |        let args = vec!["full_test".to_string()];
 2207|       |        let options = parse_options(&args).unwrap();
 2208|       |        assert!(!options.quick);
 2209|       |        assert!(!options.skip_quality);
 2210|       |        assert!(!options.skip_coverage);
 2211|       |        assert!(!options.skip_mermaid);
 2212|       |        assert!(!options.skip_d2_visual);
 2213|       |        assert!(!options.update_d2_references);
 2214|       |    }

 2221|       |            "--skip-quality".to_string(),
 2222|       |            "--skip-coverage".to_string(),
 2223|       |            "--skip-mermaid".to_string(),
 2224|       |            "--skip-d2-visual".to_string(),
 2225|       |        ];

 2228|       |        assert!(options.skip_quality);
 2229|       |        assert!(options.skip_coverage);
 2230|       |        assert!(options.skip_mermaid);
 2231|       |        assert!(options.skip_d2_visual);
 2232|       |    }

 2238|       |

 2240|       |        assert!(parse_options(&args).is_none());
 2241|       |
 2242|       |        // Help takes precedence
 2243|       |        let args = vec![
 2244|       |            "full_test".to_string(),
 2245|       |            "--quick".to_string(),
 2246|       |            "--help".to_string(),
 2247|       |        ];
 2248|       |        assert!(parse_options(&args).is_none());
 2249|       |    }

 2268|       |    #[test]
 2269|       |    fn test_parse_test_output_counts_statuses() {
 2270|       |        let stdout = "\
 2271|       |test alpha ... ok\n\
 2272|       |test beta ... FAILED\n\
 2273|       |test gamma ... ignored\n\
 2274|       |not a test line\n";
 2275|       |        let duration = Duration::from_millis(3);
 2276|       |
 2277|       |        let result = parse_test_output("tests", stdout, "", duration);
 2278|       |
 2279|       |        assert_eq!(result.mode, "tests");
 2280|       |        assert_eq!(result.total, 3);
 2281|       |        assert_eq!(result.passed, 1);
 2282|       |        assert_eq!(result.failed, 1);
 2283|       |        assert_eq!(result.skipped, 1);
 2284|       |        assert_eq!(result.tests.len(), 2);
 2285|       |        assert_eq!(result.tests[0].name, "alpha");
 2286|       |        assert!(result.tests[0].passed);
 2287|       |        assert_eq!(result.tests[1].name, "beta");
 2288|       |        assert!(!result.tests[1].passed);
 2289|       |    }
 2290|       |
 2291|       |    #[test]
 2292|       |    fn test_parse_test_output_from_stderr() {
 2293|       |        let stdout = "test alpha ... ok\n";
 2294|       |        let stderr = "note: running tests\ntest delta ... ok\n";
 2295|       |
 2296|       |        let results = parse_test_output("tests", stdout, stderr, Duration::from_secs(1));
 2297|       |
 2298|       |        assert_eq!(results.total, 2);
 2299|       |        assert_eq!(results.passed, 2);
 2300|       |        assert!(results.tests.iter().any(|t| t.name == "alpha" && t.passed));
 2301|       |        assert!(results.tests.iter().any(|t| t.name == "delta" && t.passed));
 2302|       |    }
 2303|       |

 2346|       |    #[test]
 2347|       |    fn test_categorize_tests_groups_correctly() {
 2348|       |        let tests = vec![
 2349|       |            make_result("markdown_renderer::tests::test_heading", true),
 2350|       |            make_result("markdown_renderer::tests::test_paragraph", true),
 2351|       |            make_result("markdown_renderer::tests::test_code", false),
 2352|       |            make_result("app::tests::test_open", true),
 2353|       |            make_result("app::tests::test_close", false),
 2354|       |        ];
 2355|       |
 2356|       |        let categories = categorize_tests(&tests);
 2357|       |
 2358|       |        assert_eq!(categories.len(), 2);
 2359|       |
 2360|       |        // Failures first
 2361|       |        let first = &categories[0];
 2362|       |        assert!(first.failed > 0);
 2363|       |
 2364|       |        let mr_cat = categories
 2365|       |            .iter()
 2366|       |            .find(|c| c.name == "markdown_renderer::tests")
 2367|       |            .unwrap();
 2368|       |        assert_eq!(mr_cat.total, 3);
 2369|       |        assert_eq!(mr_cat.passed, 2);
 2370|       |        assert_eq!(mr_cat.failed, 1);
 2371|       |
 2372|       |        let app_cat = categories.iter().find(|c| c.name == "app::tests").unwrap();
 2373|       |        assert_eq!(app_cat.total, 2);
 2374|       |        assert_eq!(app_cat.passed, 1);
 2375|       |        assert_eq!(app_cat.failed, 1);
 2376|       |    }

 2415|       |
 2416|       |    #[test]
 2417|       |    fn test_parse_coverage_json_extracts_totals() {
 2418|       |        let json = r#"{"data":[{"totals":{"lines":{"count":1000,"covered":800},"functions":{"count":100,"covered":90},"branches":{"count":200,"covered":150},"regions":{"count":500,"covered":400}}}]}"#;
 2419|       |        let result = parse_coverage_json(json, Duration::from_secs(30)).unwrap();
 2420|       |        assert_eq!(result.lines_covered, 800);
 2421|       |        assert_eq!(result.lines_total, 1000);
 2422|       |        assert_eq!(result.functions_covered, 90);
 2423|       |        assert_eq!(result.functions_total, 100);
 2424|       |        assert_eq!(result.branches_covered, 150);
 2425|       |        assert_eq!(result.branches_total, 200);
 2426|       |        assert_eq!(result.regions_covered, 400);
 2427|       |        assert_eq!(result.regions_total, 500);
 2428|       |    }
 2429|       |
 2430|       |    #[test]
 2431|       |    fn test_parse_coverage_json_no_totals() {
 2432|       |        let json = r#"{"data":[]}"#;
 2433|       |        assert!(parse_coverage_json(json, Duration::ZERO).is_none());
 2434|       |    }

 2438|       |    #[test]
 2439|       |    fn test_html_escape_special_chars() {
 2440|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("hello"), "hello");
 2441|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("<script>"), "&lt;script&gt;");
 2442|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("a & b"), "a &amp; b");
 2443|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("\"quoted\""), "&quot;quoted&quot;");
 2444|       |        assert_eq!(html_escape("it's"), "it&#39;s");
 2445|       |    }

 2468|       |        let path = build_report_path(now);

 2473|       |    #[test]
 2474|       |    fn test_build_report_path_increments_on_collision() {
 2475|       |        let temp = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("temp dir");
 2476|       |        let dir = temp.path().join("tests").join("results");
 2477|       |        std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).unwrap();
 2478|       |
 2479|       |        // Create existing report
 2480|       |        std::fs::write(dir.join("2026.03.15 - full test report.html"), "r1").unwrap();
 2481|       |        std::fs::write(dir.join("2026.03.15 - full test report (2).html"), "r2").unwrap();
 2482|       |
 2483|       |        // Verify collision increment logic
 2484|       |        let three = dir.join("2026.03.15 - full test report (3).html");
 2485|       |        assert!(!three.exists());
 2486|       |    }

 2493|       |        let report_path = temp.path().join("reports").join("report.html");
 2494|       |
 2495|       |        write_report(&report_path, "<html>ok</html>").expect("write report");
 2496|       |
 2497|       |        assert!(report_path.exists());
 2498|       |        let contents = std::fs::read_to_string(&report_path).expect("read report");

 2510|       |
 2511|       |    // -- Image diff tests --------------------------------------------
 2512|       |
 2513|       |    #[test]
 2514|       |    fn test_diff_images_identical() {
 2515|       |        let img = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([128, 128, 128, 255]));
 2516|       |        let (diff_pixels, max_delta) = diff_images(&img, &img, 60);
 2517|       |        assert_eq!(diff_pixels, 0);
 2518|       |        assert_eq!(max_delta, 0);
 2519|       |    }
 2520|       |
 2521|       |    #[test]
 2522|       |    fn test_diff_images_within_tolerance() {
 2523|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([100, 100, 100, 255]));
 2524|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([150, 100, 100, 255]));
 2525|       |        let (diff_pixels, max_delta) = diff_images(&a, &b, 60);
 2526|       |        assert_eq!(diff_pixels, 0); // delta=50 is within tolerance=60
 2527|       |        assert_eq!(max_delta, 50);
 2528|       |    }
 2529|       |
 2530|       |    #[test]
 2531|       |    fn test_diff_images_exceeds_tolerance() {
 2532|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([0, 0, 0, 255]));
 2533|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([255, 255, 255, 255]));
 2534|       |        let (diff_pixels, max_delta) = diff_images(&a, &b, 60);
 2535|       |        assert_eq!(diff_pixels, 100); // all 10x10 pixels differ
 2536|       |        assert_eq!(max_delta, 255);
 2537|       |    }
 2538|       |
 2539|       |    #[test]
 2540|       |    fn test_diff_images_mixed() {
 2541|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(2, 2, image::Rgba([100, 100, 100, 255]));
 2542|       |        let mut b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(2, 2, image::Rgba([100, 100, 100, 255]));
 2543|       |        // Make one pixel different by more than tolerance
 2544|       |        b.put_pixel(0, 0, image::Rgba([200, 100, 100, 255]));
 2545|       |        let (diff_pixels, max_delta) = diff_images(&a, &b, 60);
 2546|       |        assert_eq!(diff_pixels, 1);
 2547|       |        assert_eq!(max_delta, 100);
 2548|       |    }
 2549|       |
 2550|       |    // -- Pad to common size tests ------------------------------------
 2551|       |
 2552|       |    #[test]
 2553|       |    fn test_pad_to_common_size_same_dimensions() {
 2554|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([128, 128, 128, 255]));
 2555|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 10, image::Rgba([64, 64, 64, 255]));
 2556|       |        let (pa, pb) = pad_to_common_size(&a, &b);
 2557|       |        assert_eq!(pa.dimensions(), (10, 10));
 2558|       |        assert_eq!(pb.dimensions(), (10, 10));
 2559|       |    }
 2560|       |
 2561|       |    #[test]
 2562|       |    fn test_pad_to_common_size_different_dimensions() {
 2563|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(10, 20, image::Rgba([128, 128, 128, 255]));
 2564|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(20, 10, image::Rgba([64, 64, 64, 255]));
 2565|       |        let (pa, pb) = pad_to_common_size(&a, &b);
 2566|       |        assert_eq!(pa.dimensions(), (20, 20));
 2567|       |        assert_eq!(pb.dimensions(), (20, 20));
 2568|       |    }
 2569|       |
 2570|       |    #[test]
 2571|       |    fn test_pad_to_common_size_preserves_content() {
 2572|       |        let a = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(2, 2, image::Rgba([100, 100, 100, 255]));
 2573|       |        let b = image::RgbaImage::from_pixel(4, 4, image::Rgba([200, 200, 200, 255]));
 2574|       |        let (pa, _pb) = pad_to_common_size(&a, &b);
 2575|       |        assert_eq!(pa.dimensions(), (4, 4));
 2576|       |        // Center pixel of padded a should be original color
 2577|       |        assert_eq!(pa.get_pixel(1, 1).0, [100, 100, 100, 255]);
 2578|       |        // Corner should be white (padding)
 2579|       |        assert_eq!(pa.get_pixel(0, 0).0, [255, 255, 255, 255]);
 2580|       |    }

 2628|       |    }

 2636|       |    // -- Run full test orchestration tests ---------------------------
 2637|       |
 2638|       |    #[test]
 2639|       |    fn test_run_full_test_calls_runner() {
 2640|       |        let options = FullTestOptions {
 2641|       |            quick: true,
 2642|       |            skip_quality: true,
 2643|       |            skip_coverage: true,
 2644|       |            skip_mermaid: true,
 2645|       |            skip_d2_visual: true,
 2646|       |            update_d2_references: false,
 2647|       |        };
 2648|       |        let mut calls = Vec::new();
 2649|       |        let runner = |include_ignored: bool| {
 2650|       |            calls.push(include_ignored);
 2651|       |            make_suite(1, 0, 0, vec![make_result("t", true)])
 2652|       |        };
 2653|       |
 2654|       |        let outcome = run_full_test(&options, runner, Local::now());
 2655|       |
 2656|       |        assert_eq!(calls, vec![false]); // quick mode = not include_ignored
 2657|       |        assert!(!outcome.any_failed);
 2658|       |    }
 2659|       |
 2660|       |    #[test]
 2661|       |    fn test_run_full_test_detects_test_failures() {
 2662|       |        let options = FullTestOptions {
 2663|       |            quick: true,
 2664|       |            skip_quality: true,
 2665|       |            skip_coverage: true,
 2666|       |            skip_mermaid: true,
 2667|       |            skip_d2_visual: true,
 2668|       |            update_d2_references: false,
 2669|       |        };
 2670|       |        let runner = |_include_ignored: bool| {
 2671|       |            make_suite(
 2672|       |                1,
 2673|       |                1,
 2674|       |                0,
 2675|       |                vec![make_result("t", true), make_result("f", false)],
 2676|       |            )
 2677|       |        };
 2678|       |
 2679|       |        let outcome = run_full_test(&options, runner, Local::now());
 2680|       |        assert!(outcome.any_failed);
 2681|       |    }
 2682|       |
 2683|       |    #[test]
 2684|       |    fn test_run_full_test_full_mode_includes_ignored() {
 2685|       |        let options = FullTestOptions {
 2686|       |            quick: false,
 2687|       |            skip_quality: true,
 2688|       |            skip_coverage: true,
 2689|       |            skip_mermaid: true,
 2690|       |            skip_d2_visual: true,
 2691|       |            update_d2_references: false,
 2692|       |        };
 2693|       |        let mut calls = Vec::new();
 2694|       |        let runner = |include_ignored: bool| {
 2695|       |            calls.push(include_ignored);
 2696|       |            make_suite(1, 0, 0, vec![make_result("t", true)])
 2697|       |        };
 2698|       |
 2699|       |        let _outcome = run_full_test(&options, runner, Local::now());
 2700|       |        assert_eq!(calls, vec![true]); // full mode = include_ignored
 2701|       |    }

 2712|       |                make_result("app::tests::test_a", true),

 2715|       |            ],
 2716|       |        );
 2717|       |

 2720|       |            &[],
 2721|       |            None,
 2722|       |            None,
 2723|       |            None,
 2724|       |            "abc123 (main)",

 2738|       |    }

 2895|       |    // -- D2 visual HTML report test -----------------------------------
 2896|       |
 2897|       |    #[test]
 2898|       |    fn test_generate_html_report_with_d2_visual() {
 2899|       |        let d2_visual = D2VisualResults {
 2900|       |            cases: vec![
 2901|       |                D2VisualCaseResult {
 2902|       |                    case_name: "001-simple-connection".to_string(),
 2903|       |                    passed: true,
 2904|       |                    message: "ok".to_string(),
 2905|       |                    diff_percent: 5.2,
 2906|       |                    diff_pixels: 800,
 2907|       |                    max_delta: 45,
 2908|       |                    duration: Duration::from_millis(200),
 2909|       |                },
 2910|       |                D2VisualCaseResult {
 2911|       |                    case_name: "020-simple-container".to_string(),
 2912|       |                    passed: false,
 2913|       |                    message: "diff_exceeds_threshold".to_string(),
 2914|       |                    diff_percent: 25.0,
 2915|       |                    diff_pixels: 90000,
 2916|       |                    max_delta: 180,
 2917|       |                    duration: Duration::from_millis(300),
 2918|       |                },
 2919|       |            ],
 2920|       |            d2_cli_available: true,
 2921|       |            duration: Duration::from_secs(5),
 2922|       |        };
 2923|       |
 2924|       |        let html = generate_html_report(
 2925|       |            None,
 2926|       |            &[],
 2927|       |            None,
 2928|       |            Some(&d2_visual),
 2929|       |            None,
 2930|       |            "abc (main)",
 2931|       |            Local::now(),
 2932|       |            "",
 2933|       |            Duration::from_secs(5),
 2934|       |        );
 2935|       |
 2936|       |        assert!(html.contains("D2 Visual Tests"));
 2937|       |        assert!(html.contains("001-simple-connection"));

C:\language\mdmdview\src\d2_renderer.rs:
    1|       |//! D2 diagram renderer.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Renders D2 diagram markup to SVG using the embedded mdmdview-d2 crate,
    4|       |//! then rasterizes SVG to egui textures via usvg/resvg. Heavy rendering
    5|       |//! work (D2 compilation + SVG rasterization) runs on a dedicated worker
    6|       |//! thread; the main thread only handles the cheap GPU texture upload.
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! Key components:
    9|       |//! - `D2Renderer` struct: holds font database, caches, and worker channels
   10|       |//! - `LruCache<K, V>`: generic LRU cache (shared module in lru_cache.rs)
   11|       |//! - `render_d2_to_svg()`: static method calling into the mdmdview-d2 crate
   12|       |//! - `rasterize_svg()`: pure function: SVG string -> RGBA bytes (runs on worker)
   13|       |//! - `poll_results()`: drains worker results, uploads textures to GPU (main thread)
   14|       |//!
   15|       |//! This module is conditionally compiled behind `#[cfg(feature = "d2")]`.
   16|       |//! It integrates with `MarkdownRenderer` following the same public API pattern
   17|       |//! as `PikchrRenderer` (render_block, begin_frame, has_pending, release_gpu_textures).
   18|       |
   19|       |use std::cell::RefCell;
   20|       |use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
   21|       |use std::sync::Arc;
   22|       |use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
   23|       |
   24|       |use crossbeam_channel::{Receiver, Sender, TrySendError};
   25|       |
   26|       |use crate::lru_cache::{hash_str, LruCache};
   27|       |use crate::ThemeColors;
   28|       |
   29|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   30|       |// Cache capacity constants
   31|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   32|       |
   33|       |const D2_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
   34|       |const D2_SVG_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
   35|       |const D2_ERROR_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 32;
   36|       |/// Trailing-edge debounce cooldown for resize. During window resize, suppress
   37|       |/// rasterization enqueue and show the last rendered texture scaled to fit.
   38|       |/// Only enqueue a new rasterization job once the width stabilizes for this
   39|       |/// duration.
   40|       |const RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS: u64 = 150;
   41|       |/// Maximum rasterized dimension (width or height) in pixels.
   42|       |/// Matches the cap used by pikchr_renderer and mermaid_renderer.
   43|       |const MAX_RASTER_SIDE: u32 = 4096;
   44|       |/// Rasterization supersample factor. D2 diagrams benefit from higher pixel
   45|       |/// density for crisp text and clean lines, same as pikchr_renderer.
   46|       |const D2_SUPERSAMPLE: f32 = 2.0;
   47|       |/// Bounded channel capacity for job and result channels. With debounce
   48|       |/// suppressing enqueue during resize, at most one job per diagram is
   49|       |/// enqueued after the debounce expires. A document with ~6-8 diagrams
   50|       |/// fits within 8 slots. The bounded channel provides natural backpressure
   51|       |/// if the worker falls behind.
   52|       |const CHANNEL_CAPACITY: usize = 8;
   53|       |

  164|       |/// D2 diagram renderer.
  165|       |///
  166|       |/// Renders D2 diagram markup to SVG using the embedded mdmdview-d2 crate,
  167|       |/// then rasterizes SVG to egui textures via usvg/resvg. Heavy rendering
  168|       |/// runs on a dedicated worker thread; the main thread only uploads the
  169|       |/// resulting RGBA data to the GPU (~0.1-1ms).
  170|       |pub(crate) struct D2Renderer {
  171|       |    /// LRU cache: texture_key (String) -> (TextureHandle, [width, height])
  172|       |    textures: RefCell<LruCache<String, D2TextureEntry>>,
  173|       |    /// SVG cache: svg_cache_key (u128: code_hash << 1 | dark_mode) -> SVG string
  174|       |    svg_cache: RefCell<LruCache<u128, String>>,
  175|       |    /// Error cache: code_hash (u64) -> error message.
  176|       |    /// Errors don't depend on dark_mode, only on source syntax.
  177|       |    errors: RefCell<LruCache<u64, String>>,
  178|       |    /// Per-diagram debounce state for resize suppression.
  179|       |    debounce: RefCell<HashMap<u64, DiagramDebounce>>,
  180|       |    /// Maps code_hash -> texture_key of the most recently rasterized texture.
  181|       |    /// Used to display a stale texture scaled to fit during debounce cooldown.
  182|       |    latest_texture_key: RefCell<HashMap<u64, String>>,
  183|       |    /// Job sender. Wrapped in Option so Drop can close the channel
  184|       |    /// before joining the worker thread (same pattern as Pikchr/Mermaid).
  185|       |    job_tx: Option<JobSender>,
  186|       |    /// Result receiver. Polled each frame in poll_results().
  187|       |    result_rx: ResultReceiver,
  188|       |    /// Worker thread handle. Joined on drop for clean shutdown.
  189|       |    worker_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
  190|       |    /// Set of code_hashes with in-flight jobs. Keyed by code_hash (not
  191|       |    /// texture_key) to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes
  192|       |    /// while a job is already pending for the same diagram.
  193|       |    pending: RefCell<HashSet<u64>>,
  194|       |    /// Tracks the currently desired width bucket per diagram (for stale discard).
  195|       |    wanted_bucket: RefCell<HashMap<u64, u32>>,

  199|       |    /// Create a new D2Renderer, loading system fonts once at startup.
  200|       |    /// Without system fonts, usvg's default empty fontdb silently drops all
  201|       |    /// text labels in rasterized SVGs. Takes ~50-200ms (one-time cost).
  202|       |    /// Spawns a dedicated worker thread for background rasterization.
  203|       |    /// Same pattern as pikchr_renderer.rs and mermaid_renderer.rs.
  204|    460|    pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {

  211|       |

  239|       |    /// Main loop for the worker thread. Receives jobs, processes them, and
  240|       |    /// sends results back. Uses `try_send` to avoid blocking if the result

  243|    464|    fn worker_loop(
  244|    464|        fontdb: Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  245|    464|        job_rx: JobReceiver,
  246|    464|        result_tx: ResultSender,
  247|    464|    ) {

                                   ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (248:19): [True: 6, False: 464]
  ------------------
  249|      6|            let result = Self::process_job(&fontdb, job);
  250|      6|            // Use try_send to avoid blocking if result channel is full.
  251|      6|            // This prevents deadlock during shutdown: if Drop joins workers
  252|      6|            // while they're blocked on send (because result_rx isn't being
  253|      6|            // polled), we'd hang forever. Dropping results is acceptable --
  254|      6|            // the main thread still has the code_hash in the pending set, so
  255|      6|            // render_block will show the stale texture or placeholder and the
  256|      6|            // pending entry will be leaked (harmless, cleared on next
  257|      6|            // release_gpu_textures or app restart).
  258|      6|            let _ = result_tx.try_send(result);
  259|      6|        }
  260|       |        // job_rx disconnected (main thread dropped job_tx) -- exit
  261|    464|    }

  320|       |    /// Rasterize an SVG string to RGBA pixels. Pure function -- no GPU access,
  321|       |    /// no egui context needed. Runs on the worker thread.
  322|       |    ///
  323|       |    /// Parses via usvg (using the shared fontdb with system fonts), and
  324|       |    /// rasterizes via resvg/tiny-skia. D2 SVGs include proper font references,
  325|       |    /// so no font-family injection is needed (unlike Pikchr).
  326|       |    /// Rasterizes at `D2_SUPERSAMPLE` x the logical display resolution
  327|       |    /// for crisp text and lines. Returns display dimensions in logical pixels
  328|       |    /// and raster dimensions in actual pixels.
  329|      9|    fn rasterize_svg(

  331|      9|        svg: &str,
  332|      9|        width_bucket: u32,
  333|      9|        scale_bucket: u32,
  334|      9|    ) -> Result<RasterOutput, String> {
  335|       |        // Parse SVG using the shared fontdb with system fonts loaded.
  336|      9|        let opt = usvg::Options {
  337|      9|            fontdb: Arc::clone(fontdb),
  338|      9|            ..Default::default()
  339|      9|        };

                          ^7
  341|      9|            .map_err(|e| format!("SVG parse error: {}", e))?;
                                       ^2                                ^2
  342|       |
  343|       |        // Compute dimensions from the parsed SVG tree
  344|      7|        let size = tree.size().to_int_size();

  346|       |
  347|       |        // Compute logical scale factor (user zoom, constrained by available width)
  348|      7|        let base_scale = scale_bucket as f32 / 100.0;
  349|      7|        let width_scale = if width_bucket > 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (349:30): [True: 6, False: 1]
  ------------------
  350|      6|            width_bucket as f32 / w.max(1) as f32
  351|       |        } else {
  352|      1|            base_scale
  353|       |        };
  354|      7|        let scale = base_scale.min(width_scale).clamp(0.1, 4.0);
  355|       |
  356|       |        // Display dimensions in logical pixels (what render_texture uses)
  357|      7|        let display_w = (w as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  358|      7|        let display_h = (h as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  359|       |
  360|       |        // Raster dimensions: supersample for crisp text and thin lines
  361|      7|        let raster_scale = scale * D2_SUPERSAMPLE;
  362|      7|        let mut raster_w = (w as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  363|      7|        let mut raster_h = (h as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  364|       |

  368|      1|                .min(MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_h as f32);
  369|      1|            raster_w = (raster_w as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  370|      1|            raster_h = (raster_h as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;

  380|      7|        Ok(RasterOutput {
  381|      7|            rgba: pixmap.data().to_vec(),
  382|      7|            raster_size: [raster_w as usize, raster_h as usize],
  383|      7|            display_size: [display_w, display_h],
  384|      7|        })
  385|      9|    }
  386|       |
  387|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  388|       |    // Main-thread polling
  389|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  390|       |
  391|       |    /// Drain completed results from the worker thread. Called once per frame
  392|       |    /// from `begin_frame`, NOT from `render_block`. This is critical:
  393|       |    /// `render_block` only runs for visible diagrams, so if diagrams scroll
  394|       |    /// off-screen while jobs are in-flight, results would pile up in the
  395|       |    /// bounded channel and eventually block the worker thread (deadlock).
  396|       |    /// Polling from `begin_frame` ensures results are always drained.
  397|     42|    fn poll_results(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) -> bool {
  398|     42|        let mut any_processed = false;
  399|       |
  400|       |        loop {
  401|     46|            match self.result_rx.try_recv() {
  402|      4|                Ok(result) => {
  403|      4|                    any_processed = true;
  404|       |
  405|       |                    // Remove from pending set
  406|      4|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().remove(&result.code_hash);
  407|       |

  409|      4|                    if let Some(ref svg) = result.svg {
                                              ^3
  ------------------
  |  Branch (409:28): [True: 3, False: 1]
  ------------------

  411|      3|                        self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(sk, svg.clone());
  412|      3|                    }
                                  ^1

  414|       |                    // Always cache errors (prevents infinite retry loops)

                                              ^1

  419|      1|                        continue; // No RGBA to upload

  ------------------

  ------------------
  429|      2|                        (result.rgba, result.raster_size, result.display_size)

  432|      2|                        let texture = ctx.load_texture(
  433|      2|                            result.texture_key.clone(),

  435|       |                            egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,

  438|      2|                            texture,
  439|      2|                            display_size,
  440|      2|                        };

  444|      2|                        self.latest_texture_key

  ------------------

  454|      1|                                    last_rasterized_bucket: width_bucket,
  455|      1|                                    last_seen_bucket: width_bucket,
  456|      1|                                    bucket_changed_at: None,
  457|      1|                                },
  458|      1|                            );
  459|      1|                        }
  460|      0|                    }
  461|       |                }
  462|     40|                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Empty) => break,
  463|       |                Err(crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {
  464|       |                    // Worker thread has died (panic or unexpected exit).
  465|       |                    // Clear all pending entries so render_block can detect the dead
  466|       |                    // worker via try_send(Disconnected) and show an error instead of
  467|       |                    // showing "Rendering..." forever.
  468|      2|                    self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();

  470|       |                }
  471|       |            }
  472|       |        }

  474|     42|        if any_processed {
  ------------------

  475|      4|            ctx.request_repaint();
  476|     38|        }
  477|     42|        any_processed
  478|     42|    }
