  484|       |    /// Called once at the start of each egui frame. Drains completed results
  485|       |    /// from the worker thread and requests repaint if jobs are still pending.
  486|     42|    pub(crate) fn begin_frame(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
  487|       |        // Drain completed results from worker thread. Must happen here (not in
  488|       |        // render_block) because render_block only runs for visible diagrams --
  489|       |        // off-screen results would pile up and block the worker.
  490|     42|        self.poll_results(ctx);
  491|       |
  492|       |        // If jobs are still in-flight, ensure we get another frame to poll again.
  493|     42|        if self.has_pending() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (493:12): [True: 1, False: 41]
  ------------------
  494|      1|            ctx.request_repaint_after(Duration::from_millis(16));
  495|     41|        }
  496|     42|    }
  497|       |
  498|       |    /// Returns true if any rasterization jobs are in-flight on the worker thread.
  499|     59|    pub(crate) fn has_pending(&self) -> bool {
  500|     59|        !self.pending.borrow().is_empty()
  501|     59|    }
  502|       |
  503|       |    /// Release GPU textures to reduce idle GPU usage (e.g., when minimized).
  504|       |    /// SVG and error caches are retained for fast texture rebuilds on next render.
  505|       |    /// Also clears pending, wanted_bucket, and debounce to allow clean re-enqueue
  506|       |    /// on restore. In-flight results will be detected as stale (wanted_bucket is
  507|       |    /// empty) and discarded in poll_results.
  508|      3|    pub(crate) fn release_gpu_textures(&self) {
  509|      3|        self.textures.borrow_mut().clear();
  510|      3|        self.debounce.borrow_mut().clear();
  511|      3|        self.latest_texture_key.borrow_mut().clear();
  512|      3|        self.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().clear();
  513|      3|        self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  514|      3|    }
  515|       |
  516|       |    /// Main entry point for rendering a D2 code block.
  517|       |    ///
  518|       |    /// Checks caches (error, texture, SVG) before enqueuing. On cache miss,
  519|       |    /// enqueues a job to the worker thread and shows a stale texture or
  520|       |    /// placeholder while waiting.
  521|       |    ///
  522|       |    /// Returns `true` when the block has produced UI output (always true).
  523|     10|    pub(crate) fn render_block(
  524|     10|        &self,
  525|     10|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  526|     10|        code: &str,
  527|     10|        ui_scale: f32,
  528|     10|        code_font_size: f32,
  529|     10|    ) -> bool {
  530|     10|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
  531|     10|        let dark_mode = ui.visuals().dark_mode;
  532|     10|        let svg_key = svg_cache_key(code_hash, dark_mode);
  533|       |
  534|       |        // 1. Check error cache (don't re-render known failures).
  535|       |        //    Errors depend only on syntax, not dark_mode, so keyed by code_hash.
  536|       |        //    Clone the error and drop the RefMut before calling render_error_block,
  537|       |        //    to avoid holding a borrow on self.errors across a &self method call.
  538|     10|        let cached_err = self.errors.borrow_mut().get(&code_hash);
  539|     10|        if let Some(err) = cached_err {
                                  ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (539:16): [True: 1, False: 9]
  ------------------
  540|      1|            self.render_error_block(ui, &err, code, code_font_size);
  541|      1|            return true;
  542|      9|        }
  543|       |
  544|       |        // 2. Compute texture cache key (incorporates width bucket + scale + theme)
  545|      9|        let available_width = ui.available_width().max(1.0);
  546|      9|        let width_bucket = bucket_width(available_width);
  547|      9|        let scale_bucket = bucket_scale(ui_scale);
  548|      9|        let texture_key = format!(
  549|       |            "d2:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
  550|      9|            code_hash, width_bucket, scale_bucket, dark_mode as u8
  551|       |        );
  552|       |
  553|       |        // 2b. Update wanted_bucket (for stale discard in poll_results)
  554|      9|        self.wanted_bucket
  555|      9|            .borrow_mut()
  556|      9|            .insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
  557|       |
  558|       |        // 3. Check texture cache.
  559|       |        {
  560|      9|            let mut cache = self.textures.borrow_mut();
  561|      9|            if let Some(entry) = cache.get(&texture_key) {
                                      ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (561:20): [True: 1, False: 8]
  ------------------
  562|      1|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  563|      1|                return true;
  564|      8|            }
  565|       |        }
  566|       |
  567|       |        // 3b. Debounce gate: suppress rasterization enqueue during resize.
  568|       |        //     Show the last rendered texture scaled to fit. Only enqueue
  569|       |        //     once the width stabilizes for RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS.
  570|      8|        let db_entry = self.debounce.borrow().get(&code_hash).map(|d| {
                                                                                    ^2
  571|      2|            (
  572|      2|                d.last_rasterized_bucket,
  573|      2|                d.last_seen_bucket,
  574|      2|                d.bucket_changed_at,
  575|      2|            )
  576|      2|        });
  577|      8|        if let Some((last_rasterized, last_seen, _changed_at)) = db_entry {
                                   ^2               ^2         ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (577:16): [True: 2, False: 6]
  ------------------
  578|      2|            if width_bucket != last_rasterized {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (578:16): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  579|       |                // Width changed since last rasterize -- apply trailing-edge debounce
  580|      2|                if width_bucket != last_seen {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (580:20): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  581|       |                    // New bucket since last frame -- reset timer
  582|      2|                    if let Some(d) = self.debounce.borrow_mut().get_mut(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (582:28): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  583|      2|                        d.last_seen_bucket = width_bucket;
  584|      2|                        d.bucket_changed_at = Some(Instant::now());
  585|      2|                    }
                                  ^0
  586|      0|                }
  587|      2|                let changed_at = self
  588|      2|                    .debounce
  589|      2|                    .borrow()
  590|      2|                    .get(&code_hash)
  591|      2|                    .and_then(|d| d.bucket_changed_at)
  592|      2|                    .unwrap_or_else(Instant::now);
  593|      2|                let elapsed = changed_at.elapsed();
  594|      2|                let debounce_dur = Duration::from_millis(RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS);
  595|      2|                if elapsed < debounce_dur {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (595:20): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  596|       |                    // Still in cooldown -- show stale texture scaled to fit
  597|      1|                    if let Some(stale_key) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (597:28): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  598|      2|                        self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned()
  599|       |                    {
  600|      1|                        if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (600:32): [True: 1, False: 0]
  ------------------
  601|      1|                            Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  602|      1|                            ui.ctx().request_repaint_after(debounce_dur - elapsed);
  603|      1|                            return true;
  604|      0|                        }
  605|      1|                    }
  606|       |                    // No stale texture available (first render) -- fall through to enqueue
  607|      0|                }
  608|       |                // Debounce expired -- fall through to enqueue
  609|      0|            }
  610|       |            // width == last_rasterized: texture was evicted -- fall through to enqueue
  611|      6|        }
  612|       |        // No debounce entry: first render -- fall through to enqueue
  613|       |
  614|       |        // 4. Check if already pending for this diagram (by code_hash, not texture_key,
  615|       |        //    to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes mid-flight)
  616|      7|        if self.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (616:12): [True: 1, False: 6]
  ------------------
  617|      1|            return self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width);
  618|      6|        }
  619|       |
  620|       |        // 5. Enqueue job
  621|      6|        let cached_svg = self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().get(&svg_key);
  622|      6|        let job = RasterJob {
  623|      6|            code_hash,
  624|      6|            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
  625|      6|            code: if cached_svg.is_none() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (625:22): [True: 5, False: 1]
  ------------------
  626|      5|                Some(code.to_string())
  627|       |            } else {
  628|      1|                None
  629|       |            },
  630|      6|            svg: cached_svg,
  631|      6|            dark_mode,
  632|      6|            width_bucket,
  633|      6|            scale_bucket,
  634|       |        };
  635|       |
  636|      6|        match self.job_tx.as_ref().unwrap().try_send(job) {
  637|      4|            Ok(()) => {
  638|      4|                self.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
  639|      4|            }
  640|      1|            Err(TrySendError::Full(_)) => {
  641|      1|                // Queue full -- will retry next frame
  642|      1|            }
  643|       |            Err(TrySendError::Disconnected(_)) => {
  644|       |                // Worker died -- show error
  645|      1|                self.errors
  646|      1|                    .borrow_mut()
  647|      1|                    .insert(code_hash, "Raster worker unavailable".to_string());
  648|      1|                self.render_error_block(ui, "Raster worker unavailable", code, code_font_size);
  649|      1|                return true;
  650|       |            }
  651|       |        }
  652|       |
  653|       |        // 6. Show stale texture or placeholder while waiting
  654|      5|        self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width)
  655|     10|    }
  656|       |
  657|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  658|       |    // Helpers
  659|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  660|       |
  661|       |    /// Show a stale texture (from a previous width) scaled to fit, or a
  662|       |    /// minimal-height placeholder if no stale texture is available.
  663|       |    /// Returns true (block always produces UI output).
  664|      8|    fn show_stale_or_placeholder(
  665|      8|        &self,
  666|      8|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  667|      8|        code_hash: u64,
  668|      8|        available_width: f32,
  669|      8|    ) -> bool {
  670|       |        // Try stale texture first (gives a good visual approximation)
  671|      8|        if let Some(stale_key) = self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned() {
                                  ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (671:16): [True: 2, False: 6]
  ------------------
  672|      2|            if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
                                      ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (672:20): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  673|      1|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  674|      1|                return true;
  675|      1|            }
  676|      6|        }
  677|       |        // No stale texture -- show minimal placeholder (first render).
  678|       |        // Use a small fixed height to reduce layout shift when the real texture arrives.
  679|      7|        let placeholder_height = 60.0;
  680|      7|        let (rect, _) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
  681|      7|            egui::vec2(available_width, placeholder_height),
  682|      7|            egui::Sense::hover(),
  683|      7|        );
  684|      7|        ui.painter().text(
  685|      7|            rect.center(),
  686|       |            egui::Align2::CENTER_CENTER,
  687|       |            "Rendering...",
  688|      7|            egui::FontId::proportional(12.0),
  689|      7|            ui.visuals().weak_text_color(),
  690|       |        );
  691|      7|        true
  692|      8|    }
  693|       |
  694|       |    /// Parse the width bucket value from a texture cache key.
  695|       |    /// Key format: `d2:<hex>:w<width>:s<scale>:dm<0|1>`
  696|     13|    fn parse_width_from_texture_key(key: &str) -> Option<u32> {
  697|     13|        let w_start = key.find(":w")? + 2;
                          ^9                      ^4
  698|      9|        let w_end = key[w_start..].find(':')? + w_start;
                                                          ^0
  699|      9|        key[w_start..w_end].parse().ok()
  700|     13|    }
  701|       |
  702|       |    /// Check whether a result's width bucket no longer matches what the main
  703|       |    /// thread currently wants for this diagram. Stale results have their SVG
  704|       |    /// cached but skip the expensive GPU upload.
  705|      7|    fn is_stale_result(&self, texture_key: &str, code_hash: u64) -> bool {
  706|      7|        let result_bucket = Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(texture_key);
  707|      7|        let wanted = self.wanted_bucket.borrow().get(&code_hash).copied();
  708|      7|        match (result_bucket, wanted) {
  709|      4|            (Some(rb), Some(wb)) => rb != wb, // Width mismatch -> stale
  710|      1|            (Some(_), None) => true,          // No wanted bucket (e.g. after
  711|       |            //                                   release_gpu_textures) -> stale
  712|      2|            _ => false, // Can't parse -> assume fresh
  713|       |        }
  714|      7|    }
  715|       |
  716|       |    /// Render D2 source code to an SVG string.
  717|       |    ///
  718|       |    /// Static associated function (no `&self`) -- pure input/output.
  719|       |    /// Calls into the mdmdview-d2 crate pipeline.
  720|     11|    fn render_d2_to_svg(code: &str, dark_mode: bool) -> Result<String, String> {
  721|     11|        let options = mdmdview_d2::RenderOptions {
  722|     11|            dark_mode,
  723|     11|            ..Default::default()
  724|     11|        };
  725|       |
  726|     11|        mdmdview_d2::render_d2_to_svg(code, &options)
  727|     11|            .map(|result| result.svg)
  728|     11|            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())
                                       ^2^2
  729|     11|    }
  730|       |
  731|       |    /// Display a cached texture in the UI, scaling down if it exceeds available width.
  732|      3|    fn render_texture(ui: &mut egui::Ui, entry: &D2TextureEntry, available_width: f32) {
  733|      3|        let (tw, th) = (entry.display_size[0] as f32, entry.display_size[1] as f32);
  734|      3|        let scale = if tw > available_width {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (734:24): [True: 2, False: 1]
  ------------------
  735|      2|            (available_width / tw).clamp(0.01, 4.0)
  736|       |        } else {
  737|      1|            1.0
  738|       |        };
  739|      3|        let size = egui::vec2((tw * scale).round(), (th * scale).round());
  740|      3|        ui.add(egui::Image::new(&entry.texture).fit_to_exact_size(size));
  741|      3|    }
  742|       |
  743|       |    /// Display a D2 error block with the error message and original source code.
  744|      2|    fn render_error_block(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, error: &str, code: &str, code_font_size: f32) {
  745|      2|        let tc = ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode);
  746|      2|        egui::Frame::none()
  747|      2|            .fill(tc.box_bg)
  748|      2|            .stroke(egui::Stroke::new(1.0, egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80)))
  749|      2|            .inner_margin(8.0)
  750|      2|            .show(ui, |ui| {
  751|      2|                ui.label(
  752|      2|                    egui::RichText::new("D2 rendering error:")
  753|      2|                        .color(egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80))
  754|      2|                        .size(code_font_size),
  755|       |                );
  756|      2|                ui.label(
  757|      2|                    egui::RichText::new(error)
  758|      2|                        .color(egui::Color32::LIGHT_GRAY)
  759|      2|                        .size(code_font_size)
  760|      2|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  761|       |                );
  762|      2|                ui.add_space(4.0);
  763|      2|                ui.label(
  764|      2|                    egui::RichText::new(code)
  765|      2|                        .size(code_font_size)
  766|      2|                        .color(tc.code_fallback_text)
  767|      2|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  768|       |                );
  769|      2|            });
  770|      2|    }
  771|       |}
  772|       |
  773|       |impl Drop for D2Renderer {
  774|    474|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  775|       |        // Close job channel -- worker will see Disconnected on recv() and exit.
  776|       |        // The worker uses try_send for results (never blocks), so it will
  777|       |        // drain remaining jobs and exit promptly once job_rx disconnects.
  778|    474|        self.job_tx.take();
  779|       |        // Join worker thread for clean shutdown
  780|    474|        if let Some(handle) = self.worker_handle.take() {
                                  ^460
  ------------------
  |  Branch (780:16): [True: 460, False: 14]
  ------------------
  781|    460|            let _ = handle.join();
  782|    460|        }
                      ^14
  783|    474|    }
  784|       |}

  787|       |// Unit tests
  788|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  789|       |
  790|       |#[cfg(test)]
  791|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  792|       |mod tests {
  793|       |    use super::*;
  794|       |
  795|       |    fn test_raw_input(width: f32, height: f32) -> egui::RawInput {
  796|       |        egui::RawInput {
  797|       |            screen_rect: Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
  798|       |                egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
  799|       |                egui::vec2(width, height),
  800|       |            )),
  801|       |            ..Default::default()
  802|       |        }
  803|       |    }
  804|       |
  805|       |    fn test_renderer_with_channels(
  806|       |        job_tx: Option<JobSender>,
  807|       |        result_rx: ResultReceiver,
  808|       |    ) -> D2Renderer {
  809|       |        D2Renderer {
  810|       |            textures: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(4)),
  811|       |            svg_cache: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(4)),
  812|       |            errors: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(4)),
  813|       |            debounce: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  814|       |            latest_texture_key: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  815|       |            job_tx,
  816|       |            result_rx,
  817|       |            worker_handle: None,
  818|       |            pending: RefCell::new(HashSet::new()),
  819|       |            wanted_bucket: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  820|       |        }
  821|       |    }
  822|       |
  823|       |    fn insert_test_texture(
  824|       |        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  825|       |        renderer: &D2Renderer,
  826|       |        key: &str,
  827|       |        display_size: [u32; 2],
  828|       |    ) {
  829|       |        let image = egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], egui::Color32::WHITE);
  830|       |        let texture =
  831|       |            ui.ctx()
  832|       |                .load_texture(key.to_string(), image, egui::TextureOptions::default());
  833|       |        renderer.textures.borrow_mut().insert(
  834|       |            key.to_string(),
  835|       |            D2TextureEntry {
  836|       |                texture,
  837|       |                display_size,
  838|       |            },
  839|       |        );
  840|       |    }
  841|       |
  842|       |    #[test]
  843|       |    fn test_render_simple_diagram() {
  844|       |        let svg = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("a -> b", false);
  845|       |        assert!(
  846|       |            svg.is_ok(),
  847|       |            "Simple D2 diagram should render: {:?}",
  848|       |            svg.err()
  849|       |        );
  850|       |        let svg = svg.unwrap();
  851|       |        assert!(svg.contains("<svg"));
  852|       |    }
  853|       |
  854|       |    #[test]
  855|       |    fn test_render_dark_mode_produces_different_svg() {
  856|       |        let light = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("a -> b", false).unwrap();
  857|       |        let dark = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("a -> b", true).unwrap();
  858|       |        assert_ne!(light, dark);
  859|       |    }
  860|       |
  861|       |    #[test]
  862|       |    fn test_render_invalid_syntax() {
  863|       |        // D2 should error on truly invalid syntax
  864|       |        let result = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("}{}{", false);
  865|       |        assert!(result.is_err());
  866|       |    }
  867|       |
  868|       |    #[test]
  869|       |    fn test_render_empty_input() {
  870|       |        let result = D2Renderer::render_d2_to_svg("", false);
  871|       |        // Empty input may produce an error or empty SVG depending on the crate
  872|       |        // Just verify it doesn't panic
  873|       |        let _ = result;
  874|       |    }
  875|       |
  876|       |    #[test]
  877|       |    fn test_svg_cache_key_differs_by_dark_mode() {
  878|       |        let key_light = svg_cache_key(12345, false);
  879|       |        let key_dark = svg_cache_key(12345, true);
  880|       |        assert_ne!(key_light, key_dark);
  881|       |    }
  882|       |
  883|       |    #[test]
  884|       |    fn test_svg_cache_key_differs_by_code_hash() {
  885|       |        let key1 = svg_cache_key(12345, false);
  886|       |        let key2 = svg_cache_key(67890, false);
  887|       |        assert_ne!(key1, key2);
  888|       |    }
  889|       |
  890|       |    #[test]
  891|       |    fn test_texture_key_includes_all_components() {
  892|       |        let key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}", 12345u64, 640, 100, 0);
  893|       |        assert!(key.contains("d2:"));
  894|       |        assert!(key.contains(":w640:"));
  895|       |        assert!(key.contains(":s100:"));
  896|       |        assert!(key.contains(":dm0"));
  897|       |    }
  898|       |
  899|       |    #[test]
  900|       |    fn test_width_bucketing() {
  901|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(641.0));
  902|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(655.0));
  903|       |        assert_ne!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(656.0));
  904|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), 640);
  905|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(672.0), 672);
  906|       |    }
  907|       |
  908|       |    #[test]
  909|       |    fn test_scale_bucketing() {
  910|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.0), 100);
  911|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.25), 125);
  912|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.5), 150);
  913|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(2.0), 200);
  914|       |    }
  915|       |
  916|       |    #[test]
  917|       |    fn test_renderer_new_creates_empty_caches() {
  918|       |        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
  919|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
  920|       |    }
  921|       |
  922|       |    #[test]
  923|       |    fn test_supersample_constant_is_reasonable() {
  924|       |        const { assert!(D2_SUPERSAMPLE >= 1.0, "Supersample must be >= 1.0") };
  925|       |        const {
  926|       |            assert!(
  927|       |                D2_SUPERSAMPLE <= 4.0,
  928|       |                "Supersample > 4.0 would waste memory"
  929|       |            )
  930|       |        };
  931|       |    }

  937|       |    #[test]
  938|       |    fn test_parse_width_from_texture_key() {
  939|       |        assert_eq!(
  940|       |            D2Renderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("d2:0000000000003039:w640:s100:dm0"),
  941|       |            Some(640)
  942|       |        );
  943|       |        assert_eq!(
  944|       |            D2Renderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("d2:abc:w1024:s150:dm1"),
  945|       |            Some(1024)
  946|       |        );
  947|       |        assert_eq!(
  948|       |            D2Renderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("d2:abc:w0:s100:dm0"),
  949|       |            Some(0)
  950|       |        );
  951|       |        // No :w prefix
  952|       |        assert_eq!(
  953|       |            D2Renderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("d2:abc:s100:dm0"),
  954|       |            None
  955|       |        );
  956|       |    }

  962|       |    /// Helper: create a fontdb with system fonts loaded (shared across worker tests).
  963|       |    fn make_fontdb() -> Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database> {
  964|       |        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
  965|       |        db.load_system_fonts();
  966|       |        Arc::new(db)
  967|       |    }

  969|       |    #[test]
  970|       |    fn test_render_block_basic_and_pending_placeholder() {
  971|       |        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
  972|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
  973|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
  974|       |        let code = "a -> b";
  975|       |        let mut rendered_first = false;
  976|       |        let mut rendered_second = false;
  977|       |
  978|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
  979|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
  980|       |                rendered_first = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
  981|       |                rendered_second = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
  982|       |            });
  983|       |        });
  984|       |
  985|       |        assert!(rendered_first);
  986|       |        assert!(rendered_second);
  987|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&hash_str(code)));
  988|       |    }

  990|       |    #[test]
  991|       |    fn test_render_block_reports_cached_error() {
  992|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
  993|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
  994|       |        let code = "a -> b";
  995|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
  996|       |        renderer
  997|       |            .errors
  998|       |            .borrow_mut()
  999|       |            .insert(code_hash, "boom".to_string());
 1000|       |
 1001|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1002|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1003|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1004|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1005|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1006|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1007|       |            });
 1008|       |        });
 1009|       |
 1010|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1011|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1012|       |    }

 1014|       |    #[test]
 1015|       |    fn test_render_block_enqueues_cached_svg() {
 1016|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1017|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1018|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1019|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1020|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1021|       |        renderer.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(
 1022|       |            svg_cache_key(code_hash, false),
 1023|       |            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"></svg>".to_string(),
 1024|       |        );
 1025|       |        renderer.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(
 1026|       |            svg_cache_key(code_hash, true),
 1027|       |            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"></svg>".to_string(),
 1028|       |        );
 1029|       |
 1030|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1031|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1032|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1033|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1034|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1035|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1036|       |            });
 1037|       |        });
 1038|       |
 1039|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1040|       |        let request = job_rx
 1041|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(1))
 1042|       |            .expect("request");
 1043|       |        assert!(request.svg.is_some());
 1044|       |        assert!(request.code.is_none());
 1045|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1046|       |    }

 1048|       |    #[test]
 1049|       |    fn test_render_block_queue_full_keeps_rendering() {
 1050|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1051|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1052|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx.clone()), result_rx);
 1053|       |        job_tx
 1054|       |            .send(RasterJob {
 1055|       |                code_hash: 1,
 1056|       |                texture_key: "d2:full:w224:s100:dm0".to_string(),
 1057|       |                code: Some("a -> b".to_string()),
 1058|       |                svg: None,
 1059|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1060|       |                width_bucket: 224,
 1061|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 1062|       |            })
 1063|       |            .expect("fill queue");
 1064|       |
 1065|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1066|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1067|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1068|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1069|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1070|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, "a -> b", 1.0, 14.0);
 1071|       |            });
 1072|       |        });
 1073|       |
 1074|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1075|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1076|       |        drop(job_rx);
 1077|       |    }

 1082|       |        drop(job_rx);

 1084|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);

 1086|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1087|       |
 1088|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();

 1092|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {

 1100|       |
 1101|       |    #[test]
 1102|       |    fn test_begin_frame_processes_result_and_uses_cached_texture() {
 1103|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(2);
 1104|       |        let (job_tx, _job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1105|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1106|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1107|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1108|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1109|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(999);
 1110|       |        let result_tx = RefCell::new(Some(result_tx));
 1111|       |        let texture_key = RefCell::new(String::new());
 1112|       |
 1113|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1114|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1115|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1116|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1117|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1118|       |                let width_bucket = bucket_width(ui.available_width().max(1.0));
 1119|       |                let key = format!(
 1120|       |                    "d2:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
 1121|       |                    code_hash,
 1122|       |                    width_bucket,
 1123|       |                    100,
 1124|       |                    ui.visuals().dark_mode as u8
 1125|       |                );
 1126|       |                *texture_key.borrow_mut() = key.clone();
 1127|       |                renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
 1128|       |                if let Some(tx) = result_tx.borrow_mut().take() {
 1129|       |                    tx.send(RasterResult {
 1130|       |                        code_hash,
 1131|       |                        texture_key: key,
 1132|       |                        dark_mode: ui.visuals().dark_mode,
 1133|       |                        svg: Some(
 1134|       |                            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 1 1\"><rect width=\"1\" height=\"1\" fill=\"red\"/></svg>"
 1135|       |                                .to_string(),
 1136|       |                        ),
 1137|       |                        rgba: Some(vec![255, 0, 0, 255]),
 1138|       |                        raster_size: Some([1, 1]),
 1139|       |                        display_size: Some([64, 32]),
 1140|       |                        error: None,
 1141|       |                    })
 1142|       |                    .expect("send result");
 1143|       |                }
 1144|       |                renderer.begin_frame(ctx);
 1145|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1146|       |            });
 1147|       |        });
 1148|       |
 1149|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1150|       |        let texture_key = texture_key.into_inner();
 1151|       |        assert!(renderer.textures.borrow_mut().get(&texture_key).is_some());
 1152|       |        assert!(renderer
 1153|       |            .svg_cache
 1154|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1155|       |            .get(&svg_cache_key(code_hash, ctx.style().visuals.dark_mode))
 1156|       |            .is_some());
 1157|       |        assert_eq!(
 1158|       |            renderer.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash),
 1159|       |            Some(&texture_key)
 1160|       |        );
 1161|       |        assert!(!renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1162|       |    }
 1163|       |

 1213|       |    #[test]
 1214|       |    fn test_begin_frame_discards_stale_result() {
 1215|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1216|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1217|       |        let code_hash = 11;

 1220|       |        result_tx
 1221|       |            .send(RasterResult {
 1222|       |                code_hash,
 1223|       |                texture_key: "d2:stale:w224:s100:dm0".to_string(),
 1224|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1225|       |                svg: Some(
 1226|       |                    "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 1 1\"><rect width=\"1\" height=\"1\" fill=\"red\"/></svg>"
 1227|       |                        .to_string(),
 1228|       |                ),
 1229|       |                rgba: Some(vec![255, 0, 0, 255]),
 1230|       |                raster_size: Some([1, 1]),
 1231|       |                display_size: Some([64, 32]),
 1232|       |                error: None,
 1233|       |            })
 1234|       |            .expect("send result");
 1235|       |
 1236|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1237|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1238|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1239|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |_ui| {
 1240|       |                renderer.begin_frame(ctx);
 1241|       |            });
 1242|       |        });

 1253|       |    #[test]
 1254|       |    fn test_begin_frame_processes_result_with_unparseable_texture_key() {
 1255|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1256|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1257|       |        let code_hash = 19;
 1258|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1259|       |        renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash, 128);
 1260|       |        result_tx
 1261|       |            .send(RasterResult {
 1262|       |                code_hash,
 1263|       |                texture_key: "d2:not-a-valid-width-key".to_string(),
 1264|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1265|       |                svg: Some(
 1266|       |                    "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 1 1\"><rect width=\"1\" height=\"1\" fill=\"red\"/></svg>"
 1267|       |                        .to_string(),
 1268|       |                ),
 1269|       |                rgba: Some(vec![255, 0, 0, 255]),
 1270|       |                raster_size: Some([1, 1]),
 1271|       |                display_size: Some([64, 32]),
 1272|       |                error: None,
 1273|       |            })
 1274|       |            .expect("send result");
 1275|       |
 1276|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1277|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1278|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1279|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |_ui| {
 1280|       |                renderer.begin_frame(ctx);
 1281|       |            });
 1282|       |        });

 1284|       |        assert!(renderer
 1285|       |            .textures

 1291|       |    }

 1312|       |    fn test_render_block_debounce_without_stale_texture_enqueues_job() {
 1313|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1314|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1315|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1316|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1317|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1318|       |        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 1319|       |            code_hash,
 1320|       |            DiagramDebounce {
 1321|       |                last_rasterized_bucket: 224,
 1322|       |                last_seen_bucket: 224,
 1323|       |                bucket_changed_at: Some(Instant::now()),
 1324|       |            },
 1325|       |        );
 1326|       |
 1327|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1328|       |        let input = test_raw_input(96.0, 160.0);
 1329|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1330|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1331|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1332|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1333|       |            });
 1334|       |        });
 1335|       |
 1336|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1337|       |        assert_eq!(job_rx.len(), 1);
 1338|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1339|       |        assert_eq!(
 1340|       |            renderer
 1341|       |                .debounce
 1342|       |                .borrow()
 1343|       |                .get(&code_hash)
 1344|       |                .map(|d| d.last_seen_bucket),
 1345|       |            Some(bucket_width(96.0))
 1346|       |        );
 1347|       |    }

 1350|       |    fn test_render_block_uses_stale_texture_during_debounce() {
 1351|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1352|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1353|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1354|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1355|       |        let stale_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w224:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1356|       |        renderer
 1357|       |            .latest_texture_key
 1358|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1359|       |            .insert(code_hash, stale_key.clone());
 1360|       |        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 1361|       |            code_hash,
 1362|       |            DiagramDebounce {
 1363|       |                last_rasterized_bucket: 224,
 1364|       |                last_seen_bucket: 224,
 1365|       |                bucket_changed_at: Some(Instant::now()),
 1366|       |            },
 1367|       |        );
 1368|       |
 1369|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1370|       |        let input = test_raw_input(96.0, 160.0);
 1371|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1372|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1373|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1374|       |                insert_test_texture(ui, &renderer, &stale_key, [320, 160]);

 1376|       |            });
 1377|       |        });
 1378|       |

 1380|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1381|       |        assert_eq!(
 1382|       |            renderer
 1383|       |                .debounce
 1384|       |                .borrow()
 1385|       |                .get(&code_hash)
 1386|       |                .map(|d| d.last_seen_bucket),
 1387|       |            Some(bucket_width(96.0))

 1483|       |    #[test]
 1484|       |    fn test_worker_processes_job() {
 1485|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1486|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1487|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 1488|       |
 1489|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1490|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1491|       |        let texture_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1492|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 1493|       |            code_hash,
 1494|       |            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
 1495|       |            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1496|       |            svg: None,
 1497|       |            dark_mode: false,
 1498|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 1499|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 1500|       |        };
 1501|       |
 1502|       |        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 1503|       |        // Drop sender so worker_loop exits after processing the one job.
 1504|       |        drop(job_tx);
 1505|       |
 1506|       |        D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1507|       |
 1508|       |        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).unwrap();
 1509|       |        assert!(result.rgba.is_some(), "Worker should produce RGBA data");
 1510|       |        assert!(
 1511|       |            result.raster_size.is_some(),
 1512|       |            "Worker should produce raster_size"
 1513|       |        );
 1514|       |        assert!(
 1515|       |            result.display_size.is_some(),
 1516|       |            "Worker should produce display_size"
 1517|       |        );
 1518|       |        assert!(result.error.is_none(), "Worker should not produce an error");
 1519|       |        assert!(result.svg.is_some(), "Worker should produce SVG on success");
 1520|       |    }
 1521|       |
 1522|       |    #[test]
 1523|       |    fn test_worker_caches_svg_on_success() {
 1524|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1525|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1526|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 1527|       |
 1528|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1529|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1530|       |        let texture_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1531|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 1532|       |            code_hash,
 1533|       |            texture_key,
 1534|       |            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1535|       |            svg: None,
 1536|       |            dark_mode: false,
 1537|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 1538|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 1539|       |        };
 1540|       |
 1541|       |        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 1542|       |        drop(job_tx);
 1543|       |
 1544|       |        D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1545|       |
 1546|       |        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).unwrap();
 1547|       |        assert!(result.svg.is_some(), "SVG should be cached on success");
 1548|       |        let svg = result.svg.unwrap();
 1549|       |        assert!(
 1550|       |            svg.contains("<svg"),
 1551|       |            "Cached SVG should contain <svg tag, got: {}",
 1552|       |            &svg[..svg.len().min(200)]
 1553|       |        );
 1554|       |    }

 1556|       |    #[test]
 1557|       |    fn test_worker_caches_error() {
 1558|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1559|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1560|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(4);
 1561|       |
 1562|       |        let code = "}{}{";
 1563|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1564|       |        let texture_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1565|       |        let job = RasterJob {
 1566|       |            code_hash,
 1567|       |            texture_key,
 1568|       |            code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1569|       |            svg: None,
 1570|       |            dark_mode: false,
 1571|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 1572|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 1573|       |        };
 1574|       |
 1575|       |        job_tx.send(job).unwrap();
 1576|       |        drop(job_tx);
 1577|       |
 1578|       |        D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1579|       |
 1580|       |        let result = result_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).unwrap();
 1581|       |        assert!(
 1582|       |            result.error.is_some(),
 1583|       |            "Worker should produce an error for invalid code"
 1584|       |        );
 1585|       |        assert!(
 1586|       |            result.rgba.is_none(),
 1587|       |            "Worker should not produce RGBA for invalid code"
 1588|       |        );
 1589|       |        assert!(
 1590|       |            result.svg.is_none(),
 1591|       |            "Worker should not produce SVG for invalid code"
 1592|       |        );
 1593|       |    }

 1595|       |    #[test]
 1596|       |    fn test_is_stale_result_width_mismatch() {
 1597|       |        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
 1598|       |        let code_hash = 12345u64;
 1599|       |
 1600|       |        // Insert wanted_bucket: code_hash -> width 640
 1601|       |        renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash, 640);
 1602|       |
 1603|       |        // Texture key with w1024 should be stale (mismatch)
 1604|       |        let stale_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w1024:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1605|       |        assert!(
 1606|       |            renderer.is_stale_result(&stale_key, code_hash),
 1607|       |            "Width 1024 vs wanted 640 should be stale"
 1608|       |        );
 1609|       |
 1610|       |        // Texture key with w640 should be fresh (match)
 1611|       |        let fresh_key = format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash);
 1612|       |        assert!(
 1613|       |            !renderer.is_stale_result(&fresh_key, code_hash),
 1614|       |            "Width 640 vs wanted 640 should be fresh"
 1615|       |        );
 1616|       |    }

 1619|       |    fn test_is_stale_result_when_wanted_is_none() {

 1623|       |        assert!(
 1624|       |            renderer.is_stale_result(&texture_key, 99999),
 1625|       |            "No wanted_bucket entry should be treated as stale"
 1626|       |        );

 1642|       |    fn test_pending_set_basics() {
 1643|       |        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
 1644|       |
 1645|       |        assert!(
 1646|       |            !renderer.has_pending(),
 1647|       |            "Pending should be empty on new renderer"
 1648|       |        );
 1649|       |
 1650|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(42);
 1651|       |        assert!(
 1652|       |            renderer.has_pending(),
 1653|       |            "Pending should be non-empty after insert"
 1654|       |        );
 1655|       |
 1656|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().remove(&42);
 1657|       |        assert!(
 1658|       |            !renderer.has_pending(),
 1659|       |            "Pending should be empty after remove"
 1660|       |        );
 1661|       |    }

 1663|       |    #[test]
 1664|       |    fn test_release_gpu_clears_pending_and_wanted() {
 1665|       |        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
 1666|       |
 1667|       |        // Populate all four collections
 1668|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(1);
 1669|       |        renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(1, 640);
 1670|       |        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 1671|       |            1,
 1672|       |            DiagramDebounce {
 1673|       |                last_rasterized_bucket: 640,
 1674|       |                last_seen_bucket: 640,
 1675|       |                bucket_changed_at: None,
 1676|       |            },
 1677|       |        );
 1678|       |        renderer
 1679|       |            .latest_texture_key
 1680|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1681|       |            .insert(1, "d2:key".to_string());
 1682|       |
 1683|       |        renderer.release_gpu_textures();
 1684|       |
 1685|       |        assert!(
 1686|       |            renderer.pending.borrow().is_empty(),
 1687|       |            "pending should be empty after release_gpu_textures"
 1688|       |        );
 1689|       |        assert!(
 1690|       |            renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow().is_empty(),
 1691|       |            "wanted_bucket should be empty after release_gpu_textures"
 1692|       |        );
 1693|       |        assert!(
 1694|       |            renderer.debounce.borrow().is_empty(),
 1695|       |            "debounce should be empty after release_gpu_textures"
 1696|       |        );
 1697|       |        assert!(
 1698|       |            renderer.latest_texture_key.borrow().is_empty(),
 1699|       |            "latest_texture_key should be empty after release_gpu_textures"
 1700|       |        );
 1701|       |    }
 1702|       |
 1703|       |    #[test]
 1704|       |    fn test_queue_full_does_not_block() {
 1705|       |        let (tx, _rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(2);
 1706|       |
 1707|       |        // Fill the channel with 2 dummy jobs
 1708|       |        for i in 0..2 {
 1709|       |            tx.send(RasterJob {
 1710|       |                code_hash: i,
 1711|       |                texture_key: format!("d2:dummy:w640:s100:dm0:{}", i),
 1712|       |                code: Some("a -> b".to_string()),
 1713|       |                svg: None,
 1714|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1715|       |                width_bucket: 640,
 1716|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 1717|       |            })
 1718|       |            .unwrap();
 1719|       |        }
 1720|       |
 1721|       |        // Third send should fail with Full, not block
 1722|       |        let result = tx.try_send(RasterJob {
 1723|       |            code_hash: 99,
 1724|       |            texture_key: "d2:dummy:w640:s100:dm0:99".to_string(),
 1725|       |            code: Some("a -> b".to_string()),
 1726|       |            svg: None,
 1727|       |            dark_mode: false,
 1728|       |            width_bucket: 640,
 1729|       |            scale_bucket: 100,
 1730|       |        });
 1731|       |        assert!(
 1732|       |            matches!(result, Err(TrySendError::Full(_))),
 1733|       |            "try_send on full channel should return Err(Full), got {:?}",
 1734|       |            result.err()
 1735|       |        );
 1736|       |    }
 1737|       |
 1738|       |    #[test]
 1739|       |    fn test_shutdown_joins_worker() {
 1740|       |        let start = Instant::now();
 1741|       |        {
 1742|       |            let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
 1743|       |            // Drop renderer — should close channels and join worker
 1744|       |            drop(renderer);
 1745|       |        }
 1746|       |        let elapsed = start.elapsed();
 1747|       |        assert!(
 1748|       |            elapsed < Duration::from_secs(5),
 1749|       |            "Drop should join worker within 5 seconds, took {:?}",
 1750|       |            elapsed
 1751|       |        );
 1752|       |    }
 1753|       |
 1754|       |    #[test]
 1755|       |    fn test_pending_dedup_prevents_double_enqueue() {
 1756|       |        let renderer = D2Renderer::new();
 1757|       |        let code_hash = 42u64;
 1758|       |
 1759|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1760|       |
 1761|       |        // The dedup check in render_block uses contains()
 1762|       |        assert!(
 1763|       |            renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash),
 1764|       |            "Pending set should contain the inserted code_hash"
 1765|       |        );
 1766|       |
 1767|       |        // Inserting again is a no-op on HashSet
 1768|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1769|       |        assert_eq!(
 1770|       |            renderer.pending.borrow().len(),
 1771|       |            1,
 1772|       |            "HashSet dedup: inserting same hash twice should keep len at 1"
 1773|       |        );
 1774|       |    }
 1775|       |
 1776|       |    #[test]
 1777|       |    fn test_worker_try_send_full_does_not_block() {
 1778|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1779|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterJob>(4);
 1780|       |        // Result channel with capacity 1
 1781|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded::<RasterResult>(1);
 1782|       |
 1783|       |        // Fill the result channel with a dummy result
 1784|       |        result_tx
 1785|       |            .send(RasterResult {
 1786|       |                code_hash: 0,
 1787|       |                texture_key: "dummy".to_string(),
 1788|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1789|       |                svg: None,
 1790|       |                rgba: None,
 1791|       |                raster_size: None,
 1792|       |                display_size: None,
 1793|       |                error: None,
 1794|       |            })
 1795|       |            .unwrap();
 1796|       |
 1797|       |        // Submit a job that will produce a result the worker can't send (channel full)
 1798|       |        let code = "a -> b";
 1799|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1800|       |        job_tx
 1801|       |            .send(RasterJob {
 1802|       |                code_hash,
 1803|       |                texture_key: format!("d2:{:016x}:w640:s100:dm0", code_hash),
 1804|       |                code: Some(code.to_string()),
 1805|       |                svg: None,
 1806|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1807|       |                width_bucket: 640,
 1808|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 1809|       |            })
 1810|       |            .unwrap();
 1811|       |        // Drop job_tx so worker_loop exits after processing the one job
 1812|       |        drop(job_tx);
 1813|       |
 1814|       |        // Run worker_loop on a spawned thread with a timeout to detect hangs
 1815|       |        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {
 1816|       |            D2Renderer::worker_loop(fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
 1817|       |        });
 1818|       |
 1819|       |        // Wait up to 10 seconds for the worker to finish (D2 can be slower than pikchr)
 1820|       |        let start = Instant::now();
 1821|       |        loop {
 1822|       |            if handle.is_finished() {
 1823|       |                break;
 1824|       |            }
 1825|       |            if start.elapsed() > Duration::from_secs(10) {
 1826|       |                panic!("worker_loop blocked for >10s — try_send likely blocked instead of dropping the result");
 1827|       |            }
 1828|       |            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50));
 1829|       |        }
 1830|       |        handle.join().unwrap();
 1831|       |
 1832|       |        // The result channel should still contain the original dummy result
 1833|       |        let dummy = result_rx.try_recv().unwrap();
 1834|       |        assert_eq!(
 1835|       |            dummy.code_hash, 0,
 1836|       |            "Original dummy result should still be in the channel"
 1837|       |        );
 1838|       |    }
 1839|       |
 1840|       |    #[test]
 1841|       |    fn test_process_job_with_cached_svg() {
 1842|       |        let fontdb = make_fontdb();
 1843|       |        let valid_svg = r#"<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 100 50"><rect width="100" height="50" fill="red"/></svg>"#;
 1844|       |        let code_hash = hash_str("cached_diagram");

 1887|       |            "svg_cache_key (dark) should be deterministic"
 1888|       |        );
 1889|       |

 1891|       |        assert_ne!(
 1892|       |            key_a, key_dark_a,
 1893|       |            "Different dark_mode should produce different keys"
 1894|       |        );
 1895|       |    }

    1|       |// emoji_assets.rs -- Procedural vector fallback icons for a small subset of emoji.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// When a Twemoji PNG is unavailable (see emoji_catalog.rs), this module can
    4|       |// generate simple rasterised shapes for a handful of commonly used emoji.
    5|       |// The fallback set is intentionally small; most emoji are served as embedded
    6|       |// PNGs from the catalog.
    7|       |//
    8|       |// Public API:
    9|       |//   make_image(emoji, size) -> Option<ColorImage>  -- render a fallback icon
   10|       |//   has_fallback(emoji)     -> bool                 -- cheap membership check
   11|       |
   12|       |use egui::Color32 as C;
   13|       |
   14|       |/// Emoji codepoints that have procedural vector fallbacks.
   15|       |/// Both `make_image` and `has_fallback` derive from this list, keeping them
   16|       |/// automatically in sync.
   17|       |const FALLBACK_EMOJI: &[&str] = &[
   18|       |    "\u{2705}",  // white check mark
   19|       |    "\u{1f389}", // tada / party popper
   20|       |    "\u{1f680}", // rocket
   21|       |    "\u{2764}",  // red heart
   22|       |    "\u{1f496}", // sparkling heart (shares heart renderer)
   23|       |    "\u{2b50}",  // star
   24|       |    "\u{1f525}", // fire
   25|       |];
   26|       |
   27|       |/// Draw a simple vector fallback icon for a supported emoji.
   28|       |///
   29|       |/// Returns `None` for emoji not in `FALLBACK_EMOJI`.
   30|     13|pub fn make_image(emoji: &str, size: usize) -> Option<egui::ColorImage> {
   31|     13|    let mut img = egui::ColorImage::new([size, size], C::TRANSPARENT);
   32|     13|    match emoji {
   33|     13|        "\u{2705}" => {
   34|      1|            draw_circle(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(34, 139, 34));
   35|      1|            draw_check(&mut img, size, C::WHITE);
   36|      1|        }
   37|     12|        "\u{1f389}" => {
   38|      1|            draw_circle(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(255, 215, 0));
   39|      1|            confetti(&mut img, size);
   40|      1|        }
   41|     11|        "\u{1f680}" => {
   42|      2|            draw_circle(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(60, 60, 170));
   43|      2|            rocket(
   44|      2|                &mut img,
   45|      2|                size,
   46|      2|                C::from_rgb(230, 230, 230),
   47|      2|                C::from_rgb(255, 110, 60),
   48|      2|            );
   49|      2|        }
   50|      9|        "\u{2764}" | "\u{1f496}" => {
                                   ^8
   51|      2|            heart(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(220, 20, 60));
   52|      2|        }
   53|      7|        "\u{2b50}" => {
   54|      1|            star(&mut img, size, C::from_rgb(255, 215, 0));
   55|      1|        }
   56|      6|        "\u{1f525}" => {
   57|      1|            flame(&mut img, size);
   58|      1|        }
   59|      5|        _ => return None,
   60|       |    }
   61|      8|    Some(img)
   62|     13|}

                           ^1
  175|    576|        for x in 0..s {

  177|    576|            let dy = (y - s) as f32 / (s as f32 / 1.5);
  178|    576|            let v = (dx * dx + dy * dy).sqrt();

C:\language\mdmdview\src\emoji_catalog.rs:
    1|       |// emoji_catalog.rs -- Embedded Twemoji PNG lookup and shortcode-to-emoji mapping.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// The catalog embeds 80 Twemoji PNG files at compile time via `include_bytes!`.
    4|       |// Two lookup paths are provided:
    5|       |//   image_bytes_for(emoji)       -> Option<&[u8]>   -- codepoint to PNG bytes
    6|       |//   emoji_for_shortcode(code)    -> Option<&str>     -- shortcode to codepoint
    7|       |//   shortcode_map()              -> &HashMap         -- full shortcode table
    8|       |//
    9|       |// Adding a new emoji requires:
   10|       |//   1. Place the PNG in assets/emoji/<hex>.png
   11|       |//   2. Add the codepoint to the `emoji_png!` invocation in `image_bytes_for`
   12|       |//   3. Add the shortcode mapping in `shortcode_map`
   13|       |//   4. Add the codepoint to `ALL_EMOJI_CODEPOINTS` in tests
   14|       |
   15|       |use std::collections::HashMap;
   16|       |
   17|       |/// Generates a match expression that maps Unicode literals to embedded PNG bytes.
   18|       |/// Eliminates the repetitive `Some(include_bytes!(concat!(...)))` boilerplate.
   19|       |macro_rules! emoji_png {
   20|       |    ( $input:expr; $( $unicode:literal => $file:literal ),+ $(,)? ) => {
   21|       |        match $input {
   22|       |            $( $unicode => Some(include_bytes!(concat!(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"), "/assets/emoji/", $file))), )+
   23|       |            _ => None,
   24|       |        }
   25|       |    };
   26|       |}
   27|       |
   28|       |/// Returns the embedded PNG bytes for a known emoji character.
   29|       |///
   30|       |/// Uses pattern matching only -- cannot panic regardless of input.
   31|       |/// Unknown emoji characters return `None`.
   32|  7.98k|pub fn image_bytes_for(emoji: &str) -> Option<&'static [u8]> {
   33|  7.98k|    emoji_png! { emoji;
   34|       |        // --- Faces (4) ---
   35|       |        "\u{1f642}" => "1f642.png",
   36|       |        "\u{1f600}" => "1f600.png",
   37|       |        "\u{1f609}" => "1f609.png",
   38|       |        "\u{1f914}" => "1f914.png",
   39|       |        // --- Status/Indicators (11) ---
   40|       |        "\u{2705}" => "2705.png",
   41|       |        "\u{26a0}" => "26a0.png",
   42|       |        "\u{274c}" => "274c.png",
   43|       |        "\u{2728}" => "2728.png",
   44|       |        "\u{1f4af}" => "1f4af.png",
   45|       |        "\u{23f3}" => "23f3.png",
   46|       |        "\u{1f504}" => "1f504.png",
   47|       |        "\u{2714}" => "2714.png",
   48|       |        "\u{274e}" => "274e.png",
   49|       |        "\u{1f6ab}" => "1f6ab.png",
   50|       |        "\u{2611}" => "2611.png",
   51|       |        // --- Objects/Tools (16) ---
   52|       |        "\u{1f4a1}" => "1f4a1.png",
   53|       |        "\u{1f4dd}" => "1f4dd.png",
   54|       |        "\u{1f4e6}" => "1f4e6.png",
   55|       |        "\u{1f527}" => "1f527.png",
   56|       |        "\u{1f517}" => "1f517.png",

   63|       |        "\u{1f50d}" => "1f50d.png",

   65|       |        "\u{1f528}" => "1f528.png",
   66|       |        "\u{270f}" => "270f.png",
   67|       |        "\u{1f9ea}" => "1f9ea.png",
   68|       |        // --- Charts (3) ---
   69|       |        "\u{1f4ca}" => "1f4ca.png",
   70|       |        "\u{1f4c8}" => "1f4c8.png",
   71|       |        "\u{1f4c9}" => "1f4c9.png",
   72|       |        // --- Arrows (4) ---

   77|       |        // --- Communication (3) ---
   78|       |        "\u{1f4ac}" => "1f4ac.png",
   79|       |        "\u{1f4e2}" => "1f4e2.png",
   80|       |        "\u{1f4e7}" => "1f4e7.png",
   81|       |        // --- Nature/Symbols (10) ---
   82|       |        "\u{2b50}" => "2b50.png",

   93|       |        "\u{1f44d}" => "1f44d.png",

   95|       |        "\u{1f440}" => "1f440.png",

  370|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":eyes:"), Some(&"\u{1f440}"));
  371|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":trophy:"), Some(&"\u{1f3c6}"));

  375|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":target:"), Some(&"\u{1f3af}"));
  376|       |        assert_eq!(map.get(":open_book:"), Some(&"\u{1f4d6}"));
  377|       |        // Unknown

  379|       |    }

  407|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(":tada:"), Some("\u{1f389}"));
  408|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_for_shortcode(":fire:"), Some("\u{1f525}"));

  411|       |

    1|       |// Image decoding module for raster (PNG, JPEG, etc.) and SVG images.
    2|       |//
    3|       |// Provides functions to decode image bytes into egui `ColorImage` values
    4|       |// with optional background blending. SVG images are rendered via usvg/resvg
    5|       |// and automatically scaled to fit within MAX_IMAGE_SIDE limits. Raster images
    6|       |// are validated against size and pixel count limits before decoding.
    7|       |//
    8|       |// The primary entry point is `bytes_to_color_image_guess`, which tries raster
    9|       |// decoding first and falls back to SVG.
   10|       |

  273|       |        let mut bytes = Vec::new();

    1|       |#![cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, feature(coverage_attribute))]
    2|       |//! Standalone markdown viewer for Windows built with Rust and egui.
    3|       |//!
    4|       |//! This library provides markdown parsing, rendering, and GUI application logic
    5|       |//! as a single-binary viewer with syntax highlighting, embedded samples, Mermaid
    6|       |//! diagram support, and keyboard-driven navigation.
    7|       |

    9|       |pub mod emoji_assets;
   10|       |pub mod emoji_catalog;

   23|       |#[cfg(feature = "pikchr")]
   24|       |pub(crate) mod pikchr_renderer;
   25|       |
   26|       |// Automatically lower the test binary's process priority on Windows.
   27|       |// The full test suite (~986 tests, ~8 minutes) saturates all CPU cores,
   28|       |// making remote desktop sessions unresponsive. Setting BELOW_NORMAL priority
   29|       |// lets tests use idle CPU without starving interactive applications.
   30|       |// This runs via the C runtime .CRT$XCU initializer, before main().
   31|       |#[cfg(all(test, windows))]
   32|       |mod test_priority {
   33|       |    extern "system" {
   34|       |        fn GetCurrentProcess() -> isize;
   35|       |        fn SetPriorityClass(hProcess: isize, dwPriorityClass: u32) -> i32;
   36|       |        fn GetPriorityClass(hProcess: isize) -> u32;
   37|       |    }
   38|       |    const BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS: u32 = 0x00004000;
   39|       |
   40|       |    #[used]
   41|       |    #[link_section = ".CRT$XCU"]
   42|       |    static SET_BELOW_NORMAL: unsafe extern "C" fn() = {
   43|      1|        unsafe extern "C" fn init() {
   44|      1|            SetPriorityClass(GetCurrentProcess(), BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS);
   45|      1|        }
   46|       |        init
   47|       |    };
   48|       |
   49|       |    #[test]
   50|      1|    fn test_process_priority_is_below_normal() {
   51|      1|        let priority = unsafe { GetPriorityClass(GetCurrentProcess()) };
   52|      1|        assert_eq!(
   53|       |            priority, BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS,
   54|       |            "Test process should be running at BELOW_NORMAL priority, got 0x{:08X}",
   55|       |            priority
   56|       |        );
   57|      1|    }
   58|       |}
   59|       |
   60|       |pub use app::{MarkdownViewerApp, APP_TITLE_PREFIX};
   61|       |pub use markdown_renderer::{MarkdownElement, MarkdownRenderer};
   62|       |pub use sample_files::{SampleFile, SAMPLE_FILES};
   63|       |pub use theme::{apply_dark_mode_visuals, ThemeColors};
   64|       |pub use window_state::{
   65|       |    load_app_settings, load_window_state, sanitize_window_state, save_app_settings,
   66|       |    save_window_state, AppSettings, MermaidTheme, WindowState,
   67|       |};

C:\language\mdmdview\src\lru_cache.rs:
    1|       |//! Shared LRU cache and hashing utilities.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Provides a generic `LruCache<K, V>` with true LRU access-order tracking
    4|       |//! and a `hash_str` helper for non-cryptographic string hashing.
    5|       |//!
    6|       |//! Used by `pikchr_renderer`, `mermaid_renderer`, and `markdown_renderer`.
    7|       |

  178|       |        let mut counter = 1usize;

  180|       |            name = format!("{base}-{counter}");

  192|       |        } else {
  193|       |            prop_names.push(name);
  194|       |        }

  224|       |
  225|       |    let cli = match parse_cli_args() {
  226|       |        Ok(opts) => opts,
  227|       |        Err(err) => {
  228|       |            eprintln!("{err}");
  229|       |            return Ok(());
  230|       |        }
  231|       |    };
  232|       |
  233|       |    let screenshot_enabled = cli.screenshot;
  234|       |    let resolved_theme = if screenshot_enabled {
  235|       |        Some(cli.theme.unwrap_or(ThemeChoice::Light))
  236|       |    } else {
  237|       |        cli.theme
  238|       |    };
  239|       |    let screenshot_zoom = cli.zoom.unwrap_or(1.0);
  240|       |
  241|       |    let default_width = if screenshot_enabled { 1280.0 } else { 1000.0 };
  242|       |    let default_height = if screenshot_enabled { 720.0 } else { 700.0 };
  243|       |    let window_width = cli.width.unwrap_or(default_width);
  244|       |    let window_height = cli.height.unwrap_or(default_height);
  245|       |
  246|       |    let mut viewport = egui::ViewportBuilder::default()
  247|       |        .with_title(APP_TITLE_PREFIX.to_string())
  248|       |        .with_inner_size(egui::Vec2::new(window_width, window_height))
  249|       |        .with_min_inner_size(egui::Vec2::new(
  250|       |            if screenshot_enabled { 100.0 } else { 600.0 },
  251|       |            if screenshot_enabled { 100.0 } else { 400.0 },
  252|       |        ))
  253|       |        .with_icon(create_app_icon())
  254|       |        .with_resizable(!screenshot_enabled)
  255|       |        .with_maximize_button(!screenshot_enabled)
  256|       |        .with_minimize_button(!screenshot_enabled)
  257|       |        .with_drag_and_drop(!screenshot_enabled)
  258|       |        // Start hidden to avoid white flash before dark theme is applied
  259|       |        .with_visible(screenshot_enabled);
  260|       |
  261|       |    if !screenshot_enabled {
  262|       |        if let Some(ws) = load_window_state() {
  263|       |            if let Some(ws) = sanitize_window_state(ws) {
  264|       |                viewport = viewport
  265|       |                    .with_inner_size(egui::Vec2::new(ws.size[0], ws.size[1]))
  266|       |                    .with_position(egui::pos2(ws.pos[0], ws.pos[1]))
  267|       |                    .with_maximized(ws.maximized);
  268|       |            }
  269|       |        }
  270|       |    }
  271|       |
  272|       |    let native_options = eframe::NativeOptions {
  273|       |        viewport,
  274|       |        // Disable eframe's built-in window persistence - we handle it ourselves via
  275|       |        // load_window_state/save_window_state to avoid conflicts
  276|       |        persist_window: false,
  277|       |        ..Default::default()
  278|       |    };
  279|       |
  280|       |    let initial_file = cli.initial_file.clone();
  281|       |    let test_fonts = cli.test_fonts.clone();
  282|       |    let screenshot_config = if screenshot_enabled {
  283|       |        let theme = match resolved_theme.unwrap_or(ThemeChoice::Light) {
  284|       |            ThemeChoice::Light => ScreenshotTheme::Light,
  285|       |            ThemeChoice::Dark => ScreenshotTheme::Dark,
  286|       |        };
  287|       |        Some(ScreenshotConfig {
  288|       |            output_path: cli
  289|       |                .screenshot_output
  290|       |                .clone()
  291|       |                .expect("screenshot output required"),
  292|       |            viewport_width: window_width,
  293|       |            viewport_height: window_height,
  294|       |            content_only: cli.content_only,
  295|       |            scroll_ratio: cli.scroll,
  296|       |            wait_ms: cli.wait_ms.unwrap_or(2000),
  297|       |            settle_frames: cli.settle_frames.unwrap_or(3),
  298|       |            zoom: screenshot_zoom,
  299|       |            theme,
  300|       |            font_source: test_fonts
  301|       |                .as_ref()
  302|       |                .map(|path| path.to_string_lossy().to_string()),
  303|       |        })
  304|       |    } else {
  305|       |        None
  306|       |    };
  307|       |
  308|       |    eframe::run_native(
  309|       |        APP_TITLE_PREFIX,
  310|       |        native_options,
  311|       |        Box::new(move |cc| {
  312|       |            if let Some(theme) = resolved_theme {
  313|       |                let visuals = match theme {
  314|       |                    ThemeChoice::Light => egui::Visuals::light(),
  315|       |                    ThemeChoice::Dark => egui::Visuals::dark(),
  316|       |                };
  317|       |                cc.egui_ctx.set_visuals(visuals);
  318|       |            }
  319|       |
  320|       |            configure_egui_style(&cc.egui_ctx);
  321|       |            if let Some(font_dir) = test_fonts.as_ref() {
  322|       |                if let Err(err) = load_fonts_from_dir(&cc.egui_ctx, font_dir) {
  323|       |                    eprintln!("{err}");
  324|       |                }
  325|       |            }
  326|       |
  327|       |            let mut app = MarkdownViewerApp::new();
  328|       |            // Apply saved theme ONLY when no explicit --theme flag was passed
  329|       |            if resolved_theme.is_none() {
  330|       |                app.apply_saved_theme(&cc.egui_ctx);
  331|       |            }
  332|       |            if screenshot_zoom != 1.0 {
  333|       |                app.set_zoom_scale(screenshot_zoom);
  334|       |            }
  335|       |            if let Some(config) = screenshot_config {
  336|       |                app.set_screenshot_mode(config);
  337|       |            }
  338|       |
  339|       |            if let Some(file_path) = initial_file {
  340|       |                if file_path.exists() && file_path.is_file() {
  341|       |                    if let Err(e) = app.load_file(file_path, true) {
  342|       |                        eprintln!("Failed to load file: {e}");
  343|       |                    }
  344|       |                } else {
  345|       |                    eprintln!("File not found: {}", file_path.display());
  346|       |                }
  347|       |            }
  348|       |
  349|       |            Box::new(app)
  350|       |        }),
  351|       |    )
  352|       |}
  353|       |
  354|       |#[cfg(test)]
  355|      1|fn main() {}
  356|       |
