  428|       |/// Configure egui styling for optimal markdown display
  429|      2|fn configure_egui_style(ctx: &egui::Context) {
  430|      2|    let mut style = (*ctx.style()).clone();
  431|       |
  432|      2|    style.spacing.item_spacing = egui::Vec2::new(8.0, 8.0);
  433|      2|    style.spacing.window_margin = egui::Margin::same(8.0);
  434|      2|    style.spacing.menu_margin = egui::Margin::same(6.0);
  435|       |
  436|      2|    style.interaction.resize_grab_radius_side = 8.0;
  437|      2|    style.interaction.resize_grab_radius_corner = 12.0;
  438|       |
  439|      2|    style.visuals.window_rounding = egui::Rounding::same(4.0);
  440|      2|    style.visuals.menu_rounding = egui::Rounding::same(4.0);
  441|       |
  442|       |    // True black background for maximum contrast in dark mode
  443|      2|    if style.visuals.dark_mode {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (443:8): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  444|      1|        style.visuals.window_fill = egui::Color32::BLACK;
  445|      1|        style.visuals.panel_fill = egui::Color32::BLACK;
  446|      1|        style.visuals.faint_bg_color = egui::Color32::from_gray(20);
  447|      1|        style.visuals.extreme_bg_color = egui::Color32::BLACK;
  448|      1|    }
  449|       |
  450|      2|    ctx.set_style(style);
  451|      2|    configure_font_fallbacks(ctx);
  452|      2|}
  453|       |
  454|       |/// Configure font fallbacks for better symbol coverage.
  455|       |/// Adds platform-specific symbol fonts as fallbacks so characters like ? ? ?
  456|       |/// render instead of showing tofu boxes.
  457|      2|fn configure_font_fallbacks(ctx: &egui::Context) {
  458|      2|    let mut fonts = egui::FontDefinitions::default();
  459|       |
  460|       |    // Platform-specific symbol font paths
  461|       |    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
  462|      2|    let symbol_font_paths = &[
  463|      2|        "C:\\Windows\\Fonts\\seguisym.ttf", // Segoe UI Symbol
  464|      2|        "C:\\Windows\\Fonts\\segoeui.ttf",  // Segoe UI (backup)
  465|      2|    ];
  466|       |
  467|       |    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
  468|       |    let symbol_font_paths = &[
  469|       |        "/System/Library/Fonts/Apple Symbols.ttf",
  470|       |        "/System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/Arial Unicode.ttf",
  471|       |    ];
  472|       |
  473|       |    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
  474|       |    let symbol_font_paths = &[
  475|       |        "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu/DejaVuSans.ttf",
  476|       |        "/usr/share/fonts/truetype/freefont/FreeSans.ttf",
  477|       |    ];
  478|       |
  479|       |    #[cfg(not(any(target_os = "windows", target_os = "macos", target_os = "linux")))]
  480|       |    let symbol_font_paths: &[&str] = &[];
  481|       |
  482|       |    // Load the first available symbol font as fallback
  483|      2|    let font_data = symbol_font_paths.iter().find_map(|p| std::fs::read(p).ok());
  484|      2|    if let Some(font_data) = font_data {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (484:12): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  485|      2|        let font_name = "symbol_fallback".to_string();
  486|      2|        fonts
  487|      2|            .font_data
  488|      2|            .insert(font_name.clone(), egui::FontData::from_owned(font_data));
  489|       |
  490|      2|        if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Proportional) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (490:16): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  491|      2|            family.push(font_name.clone());
  492|      2|        }
                      ^0
  493|      2|        if let Some(family) = fonts.families.get_mut(&egui::FontFamily::Monospace) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (493:16): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  494|      2|            family.push(font_name);
  495|      2|        }
                      ^0
  496|      0|    }
  497|       |
  498|      2|    ctx.set_fonts(fonts);
  499|      2|}
  500|       |
  501|       |#[cfg(test)]
  502|       |#[cfg_attr(coverage_nightly, coverage(off))]
  503|       |mod tests {
  504|       |    use super::*;
  505|       |    use std::sync::{Mutex, OnceLock};
  506|       |
  507|       |    fn env_lock() -> std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()> {
  508|       |        static ENV_LOCK: OnceLock<Mutex<()>> = OnceLock::new();
  509|       |        ENV_LOCK
  510|       |            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
  511|       |            .lock()
  512|       |            .expect("env lock")
  513|       |    }
  514|       |
  515|       |    struct EnvGuard {
  516|       |        key: &'static str,
  517|       |        original: Option<String>,
  518|       |        _lock: std::sync::MutexGuard<'static, ()>,
  519|       |    }
  520|       |
  521|       |    impl EnvGuard {
  522|       |        fn unset(key: &'static str) -> Self {
  523|       |            let lock = env_lock();
  524|       |            let original = std::env::var(key).ok();
  525|       |            std::env::remove_var(key);
  526|       |            Self {
  527|       |                key,
  528|       |                original,
  529|       |                _lock: lock,
  530|       |            }
  531|       |        }
  532|       |    }
  533|       |
  534|       |    impl Drop for EnvGuard {
  535|       |        fn drop(&mut self) {
  536|       |            if let Some(prev) = &self.original {
  537|       |                std::env::set_var(self.key, prev);
  538|       |            } else {
  539|       |                std::env::remove_var(self.key);
  540|       |            }
  541|       |        }
  542|       |    }
  543|       |
  544|       |    #[test]
  545|       |    fn test_app_icon_creation() {
  546|       |        let icon = create_app_icon();
  547|       |        assert_eq!(icon.width, 32);
  548|       |        assert_eq!(icon.height, 32);
  549|       |        assert_eq!(icon.rgba.len(), 32 * 32 * 4); // RGBA format
  550|       |    }
  551|       |
  552|       |    #[test]
  553|       |    fn test_configure_egui_style_dark_mode() {
  554|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
  555|       |        let mut style = (*ctx.style()).clone();
  556|       |        style.visuals.dark_mode = true;
  557|       |        ctx.set_style(style);
  558|       |
  559|       |        configure_egui_style(&ctx);
  560|       |
  561|       |        let style = ctx.style();
  562|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.item_spacing, egui::Vec2::new(8.0, 8.0));
  563|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.window_margin, egui::Margin::same(8.0));
  564|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.menu_margin, egui::Margin::same(6.0));
  565|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.window_rounding, egui::Rounding::same(4.0));
  566|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.menu_rounding, egui::Rounding::same(4.0));
  567|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.window_fill, egui::Color32::BLACK);
  568|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.panel_fill, egui::Color32::BLACK);
  569|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.faint_bg_color, egui::Color32::from_gray(20));
  570|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.extreme_bg_color, egui::Color32::BLACK);
  571|       |    }
  572|       |
  573|       |    #[test]
  574|       |    fn test_configure_egui_style_light_mode() {
  575|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
  576|       |        let mut style = (*ctx.style()).clone();
  577|       |        style.visuals.dark_mode = false;
  578|       |        style.visuals.window_fill = egui::Color32::from_rgb(1, 2, 3);
  579|       |        style.visuals.panel_fill = egui::Color32::from_rgb(4, 5, 6);
  580|       |        style.visuals.faint_bg_color = egui::Color32::from_rgb(7, 8, 9);
  581|       |        style.visuals.extreme_bg_color = egui::Color32::from_rgb(10, 11, 12);
  582|       |        ctx.set_style(style);
  583|       |
  584|       |        configure_egui_style(&ctx);
  585|       |
  586|       |        let style = ctx.style();
  587|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.item_spacing, egui::Vec2::new(8.0, 8.0));
  588|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.window_margin, egui::Margin::same(8.0));
  589|       |        assert_eq!(style.spacing.menu_margin, egui::Margin::same(6.0));
  590|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.window_rounding, egui::Rounding::same(4.0));
  591|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.menu_rounding, egui::Rounding::same(4.0));
  592|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.window_fill, egui::Color32::from_rgb(1, 2, 3));
  593|       |        assert_eq!(style.visuals.panel_fill, egui::Color32::from_rgb(4, 5, 6));
  594|       |        assert_eq!(
  595|       |            style.visuals.faint_bg_color,
  596|       |            egui::Color32::from_rgb(7, 8, 9)
  597|       |        );
  598|       |        assert_eq!(
  599|       |            style.visuals.extreme_bg_color,
  600|       |            egui::Color32::from_rgb(10, 11, 12)
  601|       |        );
  602|       |    }
  603|       |
  604|       |    #[test]
  605|       |    fn test_parse_cli_sets_initial_file_from_arg() {
  606|       |        let opts = parse_cli_from(vec!["readme.md".to_string()]).expect("parse");
  607|       |        assert_eq!(opts.initial_file, Some(PathBuf::from("readme.md")));
  608|       |    }
  609|       |
  610|       |    #[test]
  611|       |    fn test_parse_cli_screenshot_args() {
  612|       |        let args = vec![
  613|       |            "--screenshot".to_string(),
  614|       |            "doc.md".to_string(),
  615|       |            "--output".to_string(),
  616|       |            "out.png".to_string(),
  617|       |            "--width".to_string(),
  618|       |            "800".to_string(),
  619|       |            "--height".to_string(),
  620|       |            "600".to_string(),
  621|       |            "--theme".to_string(),
  622|       |            "dark".to_string(),
  623|       |            "--scroll".to_string(),
  624|       |            "0.5".to_string(),
  625|       |        ];
  626|       |        let opts = parse_cli_from(args).expect("parse");
  627|       |        assert!(opts.screenshot);
  628|       |        assert_eq!(opts.initial_file, Some(PathBuf::from("doc.md")));
  629|       |        assert_eq!(opts.screenshot_output, Some(PathBuf::from("out.png")));
  630|       |        assert_eq!(opts.width, Some(800.0));
  631|       |        assert_eq!(opts.height, Some(600.0));
  632|       |        assert_eq!(opts.theme, Some(ThemeChoice::Dark));
  633|       |        assert_eq!(opts.scroll, Some(0.5));

  216|       |
  217|       |/// Generic LRU (Least Recently Used) cache with configurable capacity.
  218|       |///
  219|       |/// This cache evicts the least recently accessed entries when capacity is exceeded.
  220|       |/// Used for `emoji_textures` and `image_failures` to prevent unbounded memory growth.
  221|       |///
  222|       |/// # Performance Note
  223|       |///
  224|       |/// The `touch()`, `insert()` (for existing keys), and `remove()` operations use
  225|       |/// `VecDeque::retain()` which is O(n) where n is the cache capacity. This is acceptable
  226|       |/// because:
  227|       |/// - Cache capacities are small and bounded (256-512 entries max)
  228|       |/// - These operations are infrequent compared to `get()` which is O(1)
  229|       |/// - For these cache sizes, the constant factors of a more complex O(1) data structure
  230|       |///   (e.g., intrusive linked list with HashMap) would likely outweigh the O(n) cost
  231|       |struct LruCache<K, V> {
  232|       |    entries: HashMap<K, V>,
  233|       |    order: VecDeque<K>,
  234|       |    capacity: usize,
  235|       |}
  236|       |
  237|       |impl<K, V> LruCache<K, V>
  238|       |where
  239|       |    K: Eq + std::hash::Hash + Clone,
  240|       |    V: Clone,
  241|       |{
  242|       |    /// Creates a new LRU cache with the specified capacity.
  243|    911|    fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
  244|    911|        Self {
  245|    911|            entries: HashMap::new(),
  246|    911|            order: VecDeque::new(),
  247|    911|            capacity,
  248|    911|        }
  249|    911|    }
  250|       |
  251|       |    /// Gets a value by key, returning a clone if found.
  252|       |    /// Does not update access order (use `touch` for that).
  253|     64|    fn get(&self, key: &K) -> Option<V> {
  254|     64|        self.entries.get(key).cloned()
  255|     64|    }
  256|       |
  257|       |    /// Checks if the cache contains the given key.
  258|       |    #[allow(dead_code)] // Used in tests
  259|     26|    fn contains_key(&self, key: &K) -> bool {
  260|     26|        self.entries.contains_key(key)
  261|     26|    }
  262|       |
  263|       |    /// Inserts a key-value pair, evicting oldest entries if at capacity.
  264|     71|    fn insert(&mut self, key: K, value: V) {
  265|       |        // Zero capacity means no storage
  266|     71|        if self.capacity == 0 {

  |  Branch (266:12): [True: 0, False: 24]

  |  Branch (266:12): [True: 1, False: 29]

  267|      1|            return;

  269|       |
  270|       |        // If key already exists, update value and move to back of order
  271|     70|        if self.entries.contains_key(&key) {

  |  Branch (271:12): [True: 0, False: 24]

  |  Branch (271:12): [True: 1, False: 28]

  274|      1|            self.order.push_back(key);

  277|       |
  278|       |        // Evict oldest entries if at capacity
  279|     79|        while self.entries.len() >= self.capacity {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (279:15): [True: 0, False: 17]

  438|       |struct ImageCacheEntry {
  439|       |    texture: egui::TextureHandle,
  440|       |    size: [u32; 2],
  441|       |    modified: Option<SystemTime>,
  442|       |    /// Approximate memory size in bytes (width * height * 4 for RGBA).

  444|       |    /// True when the texture has been replaced with a 2x2 placeholder to save memory.
  445|       |    degraded: bool,
  446|       |}
  447|       |
  448|       |impl ImageCacheEntry {
  449|       |    /// Estimate texture memory size: width * height * 4 bytes (RGBA).
  450|     70|    fn estimate_bytes(size: [u32; 2]) -> usize {
  451|     70|        (size[0] as usize) * (size[1] as usize) * 4
  452|     70|    }
  453|       |}
  454|       |
  455|       |/// Cache for image textures with entry count and memory size limits.
  456|       |///
  457|       |/// Entry count is capped at `capacity` (safety valve).  Memory budget is
  458|       |/// enforced separately by `enforce_budget()`, which degrades far-from-viewport
  459|       |/// entries to tiny placeholders instead of evicting them.
  460|       |///
  461|       |/// # Performance Note
  462|       |///
  463|       |/// The `remove()` operation uses `VecDeque::retain()` which is O(n).
  464|       |/// This is acceptable because the cache capacity is bounded (256 entries max)
  465|       |/// and these operations are infrequent compared to `get()` which is O(1).
  466|       |struct ImageCache {
  467|       |    entries: HashMap<String, ImageCacheEntry>,
  468|       |    order: VecDeque<String>,
  469|       |    capacity: usize,
  470|       |    /// Total approximate bytes of all cached textures.
  471|       |    total_bytes: usize,
  472|       |    /// Maximum total bytes before degradation triggers.
  473|       |    max_bytes: usize,
  474|       |    /// Distance of each cached image from the viewport center (in pixels).
  475|       |    /// Updated each frame by the renderer.  When non-empty, enforce_budget()
  476|       |    /// degrades the entry with the greatest distance first.
  477|       |    viewport_distances: HashMap<String, f32>,
  478|       |    /// Estimated bytes of all in-flight restore reloads (enqueued but not yet
  479|       |    /// completed).  Used by `would_restore_fit` to prevent over-committing the
  480|       |    /// budget when multiple degraded images are enqueued in the same frame.
  481|       |    pending_restore_bytes: usize,
  482|       |}
  483|       |
  484|       |impl ImageCache {
  485|    475|    fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
  486|    475|        Self {
  487|    475|            entries: HashMap::new(),
  488|    475|            order: VecDeque::new(),
  489|    475|            capacity: capacity.max(1),
  490|    475|            total_bytes: 0,
  491|    475|            max_bytes: MAX_IMAGE_TEXTURE_CACHE_BYTES,
  492|    475|            viewport_distances: HashMap::new(),
  493|    475|            pending_restore_bytes: 0,
  494|    475|        }
  495|    475|    }
  496|       |
  497|     64|    fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<ImageCacheEntry> {
  498|     64|        self.entries.get(key).cloned()
  499|     64|    }
  500|       |

  502|     68|        let new_bytes = entry.byte_size;
  503|       |

                                  ^1
  ------------------

  ------------------

  507|      1|            self.entries.insert(key, entry);
  508|      1|            self.total_bytes += new_bytes;
  509|      1|            return;
  510|     67|        }
  511|       |
  512|       |        // Entry-count safety valve: if at capacity, remove the oldest entry.
  513|     67|        if self.entries.len() >= self.capacity {
  ------------------

  ------------------

                                      ^3

  519|      3|                }
                              ^0
  520|      1|            }
  521|     63|        }
  522|       |
  523|     67|        self.order.push_back(key.clone());
  524|     67|        self.total_bytes += new_bytes;
  525|     67|        self.entries.insert(key, entry);
  526|     68|    }
  527|       |
  528|       |    /// Replace viewport distances wholesale.  Called once per frame by the
  529|       |    /// renderer after it has collected all image Y positions.

  553|       |    /// Degrade an entry to a 2x2 neutral grey placeholder texture.
  554|       |    /// Preserves `size` (layout dimensions) and `modified` timestamp.
  555|       |    /// No-op if the key is not found or already degraded.
  556|     19|    fn degrade(&mut self, key: &str, ctx: &egui::Context) {
  557|     19|        let entry = match self.entries.get_mut(key) {
                          ^18
  558|     18|            Some(e) => e,
  559|      1|            None => return,
  560|       |        };
  561|     18|        if entry.degraded {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (561:12): [True: 1, False: 17]
  ------------------
  562|      1|            return;
  563|     17|        }
  564|     17|        let old_bytes = entry.byte_size;
  565|     17|        let placeholder = egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::from_rgb(128, 128, 128));
  566|     17|        entry.texture = ctx.load_texture(
  567|     17|            format!("degraded:{key}"),
  568|     17|            placeholder,
  569|       |            egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
  570|       |        );
  571|     17|        entry.byte_size = 2 * 2 * 4;
  572|     17|        entry.degraded = true;
  573|     17|        self.total_bytes = self.total_bytes.saturating_sub(old_bytes) + entry.byte_size;
  574|     19|    }
  575|       |
  576|       |    /// Degrade far-from-viewport entries to keep memory within budget and
  577|       |    /// create headroom for near-viewport degraded entries to restore.
  578|       |    ///
  579|       |    /// Phase 1: Degrade orphan entries (no viewport distance, from old documents).
  580|       |    /// Phase 2: Degrade current-document entries furthest-first until under budget.
  581|       |    /// Phase 3: Proactively degrade far non-degraded entries to make room for
  582|       |    ///   near-viewport degraded entries. Without this, enforce_budget and
  583|       |    ///   would_restore_fit deadlock: enforce_budget stops because under budget,
  584|       |    ///   but would_restore_fit blocks restores because there's no headroom.
  585|      7|    fn enforce_budget(&mut self, ctx: &egui::Context, viewport_height: f32) {
  586|       |        // Phase 1: Degrade orphan entries (no viewport distance) first.
  587|       |        // These are leftovers from a previous document — safe to degrade before
  588|       |        // touching current-document entries.
  589|      7|        if self.total_bytes > self.max_bytes {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (589:12): [True: 4, False: 3]
  ------------------
  590|      4|            let orphans: Vec<String> = self
  591|      4|                .entries
  592|      4|                .iter()
  593|     13|                .filter(|(_, e)| !e.degraded)
                               ^4
  594|     13|                .filter(|(k, _)| !self.viewport_distances.contains_key(k.as_str()))
                               ^4
  595|      4|                .map(|(k, _)| k.clone())
                                            ^2^2
  596|      4|                .collect();
  597|      4|            for key in &orphans {
                              ^2
  598|      2|                self.degrade(key, ctx);
  599|      2|                if self.total_bytes <= self.max_bytes {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (599:20): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  600|      1|                    break;
  601|      1|                }
  602|       |            }
  603|      3|        }
  604|       |
  605|       |        // Build sorted candidate list (non-degraded entries with distances,
  606|       |        // sorted furthest-first). Used by both Phase 2 and Phase 3.
  607|      7|        let mut candidates: Vec<(String, f32)> = self
  608|      7|            .entries
  609|      7|            .iter()
  610|     17|            .filter(|(_, e)| !e.degraded)
                           ^7
  611|     14|            .filter_map(|(k, _)| self.viewport_distances.get(k).map(|&d| (k.clone(), d)))
                           ^7
  612|      7|            .collect();
  613|     13|        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));
                      ^7         ^7
  614|       |
  615|       |        // Phase 2: Degrade current-document entries furthest-first until under budget.
  616|      7|        if self.total_bytes > self.max_bytes {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (616:12): [True: 3, False: 4]
  ------------------
  617|      7|            for (key, _) in &candidates {
                                          ^3
  618|     24|                let non_degraded_count = self.entries.values().filter(|e| !e.degraded).count();
                                  ^7                   ^7           ^7       ^7                      ^7
  619|      7|                if non_degraded_count <= 1 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (619:20): [True: 0, False: 7]
  ------------------
  620|      0|                    break;
  621|      7|                }
  622|      7|                self.degrade(key, ctx);
  623|      7|                if self.total_bytes <= self.max_bytes {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (623:20): [True: 3, False: 4]
  ------------------
  624|      3|                    break;
  625|      4|                }
  626|       |            }
  627|      4|        }
  628|       |
  629|       |        // Phase 3: Proactive headroom — degrade far non-degraded entries to make
  630|       |        // room for near-viewport degraded entries that want to restore.
  631|       |        // Compute how many bytes near-viewport degraded entries would need.
  632|      7|        let reload_zone = viewport_height * 2.0;
  633|      7|        let needed: usize = self
  634|      7|            .entries
  635|      7|            .iter()
  636|      7|            .filter(|(_, e)| e.degraded)
  637|     10|            .filter(|(k, _)| {
                           ^7
  638|     10|                self.viewport_distances
  639|     10|                    .get(k.as_str())
  640|     10|                    .is_some_and(|&d| d < reload_zone)
                                                    ^8  ^8
  641|     10|            })
  642|      7|            .map(|(_, e)| ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes(e.size).saturating_sub(e.byte_size))
                                        ^1                              ^1      ^1             ^1
  643|      7|            .sum();
  644|       |
  645|      7|        if needed > 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (645:12): [True: 1, False: 6]
  ------------------
  646|      1|            let target = self.max_bytes.saturating_sub(needed);
  647|       |            // Re-filter candidates (some may have been degraded in Phase 2).
  648|      1|            let far_candidates: Vec<(String, f32)> = candidates
  649|      1|                .iter()
  650|      2|                .filter(|(k, _)| self.entries.get(k).is_some_and(|e| !e.degraded))
                               ^1
  651|      2|                .filter(|(_, d)| *d >= reload_zone)
                               ^1
  652|      1|                .cloned()
  653|      1|                .collect();
  654|       |
  655|      2|            for (key, _) in &far_candidates {
                                          ^1
  656|      2|                if self.total_bytes <= target {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (656:20): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  657|      1|                    break;
  658|      1|                }
  659|      3|                let non_degraded_count = self.entries.values().filter(|e| !e.degraded).count();
                                  ^1                   ^1           ^1       ^1                      ^1
  660|      1|                if non_degraded_count <= 1 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (660:20): [True: 0, False: 1]
  ------------------
  661|      0|                    break;
  662|      1|                }
  663|      1|                self.degrade(key, ctx);
  664|       |            }
  665|      6|        }
  666|      7|    }
  667|       |
  668|       |    /// Returns true if restoring a degraded entry (replacing its 2x2 texture
  669|       |    /// with full-resolution) would keep `total_bytes` within `max_bytes`.
  670|     11|    fn would_restore_fit(&self, key: &str) -> bool {
  671|     11|        let entry = match self.entries.get(key) {
                          ^10
  672|     10|            Some(e) => e,
  673|      1|            None => return false,
  674|       |        };
  675|     10|        if !entry.degraded {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (675:12): [True: 1, False: 9]
  ------------------
  676|      1|            return false;
  677|      9|        }
  678|      9|        let full_bytes = ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes(entry.size);
  679|      9|        self.total_bytes.saturating_sub(entry.byte_size) + full_bytes + self.pending_restore_bytes
  680|      9|            <= self.max_bytes
  681|     11|    }
  682|       |
  683|    278|    fn clear(&mut self) {
  684|    278|        self.entries.clear();
  685|    278|        self.order.clear();
  686|    278|        self.total_bytes = 0;
  687|    278|        self.viewport_distances.clear();
  688|    278|        self.pending_restore_bytes = 0;
  689|    278|    }

  692|       |#[derive(Clone)]
  693|       |struct ImageFailure {
  694|       |    last_attempt: Instant,
  695|       |}
  696|       |
  697|       |enum ImageLoadSource {
  698|       |    Embedded(&'static [u8]),
  699|       |    File(PathBuf),
  700|       |    Remote(String),
  701|       |}
  702|       |
  703|       |struct ImageLoadRequest {
  704|       |    key: String,
  705|       |    source: ImageLoadSource,
  706|       |    /// Generation counter at the time this request was enqueued.
  707|       |    /// Results from older generations are discarded in poll_image_results.
  708|       |    generation: u64,
  709|       |}
  710|       |
  711|       |enum ImageLoadResult {
  712|       |    Loaded {
  713|       |        key: String,
  714|       |        image: egui::ColorImage,
  715|       |        size: [u32; 2],
  716|       |        modified: Option<SystemTime>,
  717|       |        generation: u64,
  718|       |    },
  719|       |    Failed {
  720|       |        key: String,
  721|       |        generation: u64,
  722|       |    },
  723|       |}
  724|       |
  725|       |/// Markdown renderer with proper inline element handling
  726|       |pub struct MarkdownRenderer {
  727|       |    font_sizes: FontSizes,
  728|       |    syntax_set: SyntaxSet,
  729|       |    theme_set: ThemeSet,
  730|       |    emoji_textures: RefCell<LruCache<String, egui::TextureHandle>>,
  731|       |    image_textures: RefCell<ImageCache>,
  732|       |    image_pending: RefCell<HashSet<String>>,
  733|       |    image_failures: RefCell<LruCache<String, ImageFailure>>,
  734|       |    image_job_tx: Sender<ImageLoadRequest>,
  735|       |    image_result_rx: Receiver<ImageLoadResult>,
  736|       |    /// Generation counter incremented on each document load.  Image load results
  737|       |    /// from a previous generation are silently discarded so that stale background
  738|       |    /// loads do not pollute the cache after a document switch.
  739|       |    image_generation: Cell<u64>,
  740|       |    // Per-frame Y position of each image (keyed by resolved path) for viewport
  741|       |    // distance calculation.  Populated during rendering, consumed at frame end
  742|       |    // to update the cache's degradation distances.
  743|       |    image_doc_y: RefCell<HashMap<String, f32>>,
  744|       |    // Mapping of header id -> last rendered rect (for in-document navigation)
  745|       |    header_rects: RefCell<HashMap<String, egui::Rect>>,
  746|       |    // Last clicked internal anchor (e.g., "getting-started") for the app to consume
  747|       |    pending_anchor: RefCell<Option<String>>,
  748|       |    // Unique counter to avoid egui Id collisions for repeated links
  749|       |    link_counter: RefCell<u64>,
  750|       |    // Per-frame table counter to build stable table ids in render order
  751|       |    table_counter: RefCell<u64>,
  752|       |    // Per-frame rect for each top-level element in render order
  753|       |    element_rects: RefCell<Vec<egui::Rect>>,
  754|       |    // Optional highlight phrase (lowercased) for in-text highlighting
  755|       |    highlight_phrase: RefCell<Option<String>>,
  756|       |    // Cache for image/diagram textures
  757|       |    // Base directory used to resolve relative image paths
  758|       |    base_dir: RefCell<Option<PathBuf>>,
  759|       |    mermaid: MermaidRenderer,
  760|       |    /// Current resolved Mermaid theme (Auto resolved at set time).
  761|       |    mermaid_theme: Cell<MermaidTheme>,
  762|       |    #[cfg(feature = "pikchr")]
  763|       |    pikchr: pikchr_renderer::PikchrRenderer,
  764|       |    #[cfg(feature = "d2")]
  765|       |    d2: d2_renderer::D2Renderer,
  766|       |    table_layout_cache: RefCell<CellLayoutCache>,
  767|       |    table_metrics: RefCell<TableMetrics>,
  768|       |    column_stats_cache: RefCell<HashMap<u64, ColumnStatsCacheEntry>>,
  769|       |    /// Allow loading images from remote URLs (http/https)
  770|       |    allow_remote_images: Cell<bool>,
  771|       |    /// Current theme mode — true for dark, false for light.
  772|       |    /// Used by syntect highlighting at parse time.

 2072|       |        };
 2073|  1.08k|        *count += 1;

 2939|     36|    pub fn render_to_ui(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, elements: &[MarkdownElement]) {
 2940|     36|        self.poll_image_results(ui.ctx());
 2941|     36|        self.mermaid.begin_frame();
 2942|       |        #[cfg(feature = "pikchr")]
 2943|     36|        self.pikchr.begin_frame(ui.ctx());
 2944|       |        #[cfg(feature = "d2")]
 2945|     36|        self.d2.begin_frame(ui.ctx());
 2946|       |        // Clear header rects before rendering a new frame
 2947|     36|        self.header_rects.borrow_mut().clear();

 2949|     36|        *self.link_counter.borrow_mut() = 0;
 2950|       |        // Reset per-frame table counter
 2951|     36|        *self.table_counter.borrow_mut() = 0;
 2952|       |        // Reset per-frame element rects
 2953|     36|        self.element_rects.borrow_mut().clear();
 2954|       |        // Clear per-frame image Y positions
 2955|     36|        self.image_doc_y.borrow_mut().clear();
 2956|    132|        for element in elements.iter() {
                                     ^36      ^36
 2957|       |            // Wrap each element in a no-op frame to capture its rect
 2958|    132|            let ir = egui::Frame::none().show(ui, |ui| {
 2959|    132|                self.render_element_body(ui, element);
 2960|    132|            });
 2961|    132|            self.element_rects.borrow_mut().push(ir.response.rect);
 2962|       |        }
 2963|       |
 2964|       |        // Update viewport distances for image cache degradation.
 2965|       |        // `clip_rect()` gives the visible portion of the scroll area.
 2966|     36|        let viewport_height = ui.clip_rect().height();
 2967|     36|        self.last_viewport_height.set(viewport_height);
 2968|     36|        let viewport_center_y = ui.clip_rect().center().y;
 2969|     36|        let distances: HashMap<String, f32> = self
 2970|     36|            .image_doc_y
 2971|     36|            .borrow()
 2972|     36|            .iter()
 2973|     36|            .map(|(key, &y)| (key.clone(), (y - viewport_center_y).abs()))
                                            ^4  ^4       ^4                      ^4
 2974|     36|            .collect();
 2975|     36|        if !distances.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (2975:12): [True: 1, False: 35]
  ------------------
 2976|      1|            let mut cache = self.image_textures.borrow_mut();
 2977|      1|            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 2978|      1|            cache.enforce_budget(ui.ctx(), self.last_viewport_height.get());
 2979|     35|        }
 2980|       |
 2981|       |        // Add a little extra breathing room at the end so
 2982|       |        // the final line doesn't sit flush under the status bar.
 2983|     36|        ui.add_space(16.0);
 2984|     36|    }

 3879|     23|    fn generate_emoji_image(&self, emoji: &str, size: usize) -> egui::ColorImage {
 3880|       |        // Simple procedural placeholder icons to keep binary small and avoid external assets
 3881|       |        use egui::Color32 as C;
 3882|     23|        let mut img = egui::ColorImage::new([size, size], C::TRANSPARENT);

 4930|       |    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
 4931|     26|    fn resolve_table_rects(
 4932|     26|        &self,
 4933|     26|        header_rect: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4934|     26|        body_rect: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4935|     26|        layout_rect: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4936|     26|        header_clip_rect: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4937|     26|        body_clip: Option<egui::Rect>,
 4938|     26|        widths: &[f32],
 4939|     26|        column_spacing: f32,
 4940|     26|    ) -> (egui::Rect, egui::Rect) {
 4941|       |        // Combine header and body clip rects to ensure dividers respect scroll bounds.
 4942|     26|        let clip_rect = match (header_clip_rect, body_clip) {
 4943|     19|            (Some(h), Some(b)) => Some(h.union(b)),
 4944|      7|            (h, b) => h.or(b),
 4945|       |        };
 4946|       |
 4947|       |        // Calculate accurate table width from column widths.
 4948|       |        // This is more accurate than union of cell min_rects which may be smaller
 4949|       |        // than the allocated column space.
 4950|     26|        let calculated_width: f32 =
 4951|     26|            widths.iter().sum::<f32>() + column_spacing * widths.len().saturating_sub(1) as f32;
 4952|       |
 4953|       |        // Combine header/body bounds with layout bounds for accurate borders,
 4954|       |        // especially when the first column is centered or right-aligned.
 4955|     26|        let left = layout_rect
 4956|     26|            .map(|rect| rect.left())
                                      ^22  ^22
 4957|     26|            .or_else(|| header_rect.map(|rect| rect.left()))
                                      ^4          ^4         ^1   ^1
 4958|     26|            .or_else(|| body_rect.map(|rect| rect.left()));
                                      ^3        ^3         ^1   ^1
 4959|     26|        let right = left.map(|value| value + calculated_width);
                                                   ^24     ^24
 4960|     26|        let top = header_rect
 4961|     26|            .map(|rect| rect.top())
                                      ^22  ^22
 4962|     26|            .or_else(|| body_rect.map(|rect| rect.top()))
                                      ^4        ^4         ^1   ^1
 4963|     26|            .or_else(|| layout_rect.map(|rect| rect.top()));
                                      ^3          ^3         ^1   ^1
 4964|     26|        let bottom = body_rect
 4965|     26|            .map(|rect| rect.bottom())
                                      ^21  ^21
 4966|     26|            .or_else(|| header_rect.map(|rect| rect.bottom()))
                                      ^5          ^5         ^2   ^2
 4967|     26|            .or_else(|| layout_rect.map(|rect| rect.bottom()));
                                      ^3          ^3         ^1   ^1
 4968|     26|        let table_rect = match (left, right, top, bottom) {
 4969|     24|            (Some(left), Some(right), Some(top), Some(bottom)) => Some(egui::Rect::from_min_max(
 4970|     24|                egui::pos2(left, top),
 4971|     24|                egui::pos2(right, bottom),
 4972|     24|            )),
 4973|      2|            _ => None,
 4974|       |        };
 4975|       |
 4976|     26|        let zero_rect = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 4977|     26|        let rect = table_rect.or(layout_rect).unwrap_or(zero_rect);
 4978|     26|        let clip_rect = clip_rect.unwrap_or(rect);
 4979|     26|        (rect, clip_rect)
 4980|     26|    }
 4981|       |
 4982|     27|    fn table_min_column_width(&self) -> f32 {
 4983|     27|        (self.font_sizes.body * 6.0).max(48.0)
 4984|     27|    }
 4985|       |
 4986|     23|    fn resolve_table_column_widths(
 4987|     23|        &self,
 4988|     23|        table_id: u64,
 4989|     23|        column_specs: &[ColumnSpec],
 4990|     23|        min_floor: f32,
 4991|     23|    ) -> Vec<f32> {
 4992|     23|        if column_specs.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (4992:12): [True: 1, False: 22]
  ------------------
 4993|      1|            return Vec::new();
 4994|     22|        }
 4995|       |
 4996|     22|        let stored_widths = {
 4997|     22|            let metrics = self.table_metrics.borrow();
 4998|     22|            metrics
 4999|     22|                .entry(table_id)
 5000|     22|                .map(|entry| entry.current_widths().to_vec())
                                           ^21                    ^21
 5001|     22|                .unwrap_or_default()
 5002|       |        };
 5003|       |
 5004|     22|        column_specs
 5005|     22|            .iter()
 5006|     22|            .enumerate()
 5007|     61|            .map(|(idx, spec)| {
                           ^22
 5008|     61|                let stored = stored_widths
 5009|     61|                    .get(idx)
 5010|     61|                    .copied()
 5011|     61|                    .filter(|width| width.is_finite() && *width > 0.0);
                                                  ^7    ^7             ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5011:37): [True: 6, False: 1]
  ------------------
 5012|     61|                let width = match spec.policy {
 5013|     17|                    ColumnPolicy::Fixed { width, .. } => width,
 5014|     23|                    ColumnPolicy::Resizable { min, preferred, .. } => {
 5015|     23|                        stored.unwrap_or(preferred.max(min)).max(min)
 5016|       |                    }
 5017|     17|                    ColumnPolicy::Remainder { .. } => min_floor,
 5018|      4|                    ColumnPolicy::Auto => stored.unwrap_or(min_floor).max(min_floor),
 5019|       |                };
 5020|     61|                width.max(1.0)
 5021|     61|            })
 5022|     22|            .collect()
 5023|     23|    }

 5025|       |    #[cfg(test)]

 5027|      5|        &self,
 5028|      5|        column_specs: &[ColumnSpec],

 5125|       |
 5126|       |    /// Estimate per-column natural widths from content statistics and
 5127|       |    /// policy-resolved widths (which include any user-persisted widths).
 5128|       |    ///
 5129|       |    /// For each column, takes the maximum of:
 5130|       |    /// - Content-based estimate (grapheme count * char_width_factor + cell_padding)
 5131|       |    /// - Policy-resolved width (from resolve_table_column_widths)
 5132|       |    ///
 5133|       |    /// Each column width is clamped to [MIN_COL_WIDTH, MAX_COL_WIDTH].
 5134|     29|    fn estimate_natural_column_widths(
 5135|     29|        &self,
 5136|     29|        column_stats: &[ColumnStat],
 5137|     29|        headers: &[Vec<InlineSpan>],
 5138|     29|        resolved_widths: &[f32],
 5139|     29|        column_count: usize,
 5140|     29|    ) -> Vec<f32> {
 5141|     29|        let body = self.font_sizes.body;
 5142|     29|        let char_width_factor = body * 0.62;
 5143|     29|        let cell_padding = body * 1.8;
 5144|     29|        let min_col_width = body * 4.0;
 5145|     29|        let max_col_width = body * 35.0;
 5146|       |
 5147|     29|        (0..column_count)
 5148|     77|            .map(|i| {
                           ^29
 5149|       |                // Extract header text and compute its display width
 5150|     77|                let header_width = headers
 5151|     77|                    .get(i)
 5152|     77|                    .map(|spans| {
                                               ^72
 5153|     72|                        let text = spans_to_text(spans);
 5154|     72|                        UnicodeWidthStr::width(text.as_str())
 5155|     72|                    })
 5156|     77|                    .unwrap_or(0);
 5157|       |
 5158|       |                // Content width from stats (already unicode-width-based)
 5159|     77|                let content_graphemes = column_stats.get(i).map(|s| s.max_graphemes).unwrap_or(0);
 5160|       |
 5161|     77|                let display_graphemes = header_width.max(content_graphemes);
 5162|     77|                let content_width = display_graphemes as f32 * char_width_factor + cell_padding;
 5163|     77|                let policy_width = resolved_widths.get(i).copied().unwrap_or(0.0);
 5164|     77|                let natural = content_width.max(policy_width);
 5165|     77|                natural.clamp(min_col_width, max_col_width)
 5166|     77|            })
 5167|     29|            .collect()
 5168|     29|    }
 5169|       |
 5170|       |    /// Compute the total natural table width from per-column widths and inter-column spacing.
 5171|     25|    fn natural_table_width(natural_widths: &[f32], column_spacing: f32) -> f32 {
 5172|     25|        let spacing = column_spacing * natural_widths.len().saturating_sub(1) as f32;
 5173|     25|        natural_widths.iter().sum::<f32>() + spacing
 5174|     25|    }

 5176|     29|    fn estimate_table_row_height(

 5182|     29|        widths: &[f32],

 5199|     72|            max_height = max_height.max(height);
 5200|       |        }
 5201|     29|        max_height
 5202|     29|    }

 5354|       |
 5355|       |    /// Paint vertical dividers between table columns, a horizontal header separator,
 5356|       |    /// and an outer border.
 5357|       |    ///
 5358|       |    /// # Arguments
 5359|       |    /// * `widths` - The actual allocated column widths from `TableBody::widths()`.
 5360|       |    ///   IMPORTANT: These must NOT be cell content widths from `ui.min_rect()`,
 5361|       |    ///   which would cause dividers to be misaligned.
 5362|       |    /// * `header_height` - The height of the header row. A horizontal separator is
 5363|       |    ///   drawn at this y-offset to visually separate header from body rows.
 5364|       |    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
 5365|     26|    fn paint_table_dividers(
 5366|     26|        &self,
 5367|     26|        painter: &Painter,
 5368|     26|        visuals: &Visuals,
 5369|     26|        rect: egui::Rect,
 5370|     26|        clip_rect: egui::Rect,

 5372|     26|        header_height: f32,
 5373|     26|        column_spacing: f32,
 5374|     26|        row_spacing: f32,
 5375|     26|        draw_dividers: bool,
 5376|     26|    ) {
 5377|     26|        if widths.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5377:12): [True: 1, False: 25]
  ------------------
 5378|      1|            return;
 5379|     25|        }
 5380|     25|        let separator_color = visuals
 5381|     25|            .widgets
 5382|     25|            .noninteractive
 5383|     25|            .bg_stroke
 5384|     25|            .color
 5385|     25|            .gamma_multiply(0.9);
 5386|     25|        let separator_stroke = Stroke::new(1.0, separator_color);
 5387|     25|        let border_stroke = visuals.window_stroke();
 5388|       |        // Expand border rect by half the column/row spacing to match the visual
 5389|       |        // extent of egui's striped row backgrounds (which expand by
 5390|       |        // item_spacing/2 on each side). This eliminates the double-line effect
 5391|       |        // at table edges and provides padding between the border and cell text.
 5392|     25|        let half_col_spacing = column_spacing * 0.5;
 5393|     25|        let half_row_spacing = row_spacing * 0.5;
 5394|     25|        let border_rect = egui::Rect::from_min_max(
 5395|     25|            egui::pos2(
 5396|     25|                rect.left() - half_col_spacing,
 5397|     25|                rect.top() - half_row_spacing,
 5398|       |            ),
 5399|     25|            egui::pos2(
 5400|     25|                rect.right() + half_col_spacing,
 5401|     25|                rect.bottom() + half_row_spacing,
 5402|       |            ),
 5403|       |        );
 5404|       |        // Expand clip_rect to include the full border rect so borders aren't clipped.
 5405|       |        // The cell clip_rect may not include the outer border area.
 5406|     25|        let expanded_clip = clip_rect.union(border_rect);
 5407|     25|        let painter = painter.with_clip_rect(expanded_clip);

 5409|       |        // Draw vertical dividers between columns

 5412|     19|            for (idx, width) in widths
                                              ^10

 5415|     10|                .enumerate()
 5416|     19|            {
 5417|     19|                x += *width;
 5418|     19|                let divider_x = x + column_spacing * (idx as f32 + 0.5);
 5419|     19|                let x_pos = (divider_x.round() + 0.5).clamp(rect.left(), rect.right());
 5420|     19|                painter.vline(x_pos, border_rect.y_range(), separator_stroke);
 5421|     19|            }
 5422|     15|        }
 5423|       |
 5424|       |        // Draw horizontal separator below header row

 5610|       |    #[cfg(test)]
 5611|      8|    fn resolve_table_widths(available: f32, mins: &[f32], desired: &[f32]) -> Vec<f32> {
 5612|      8|        debug_assert_eq!(mins.len(), desired.len());
 5613|      8|        if mins.is_empty() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5613:12): [True: 1, False: 7]
  ------------------
 5614|      1|            return Vec::new();
 5615|      7|        }
 5616|      7|        let clamped_available = available.max(1.0);
 5617|      7|        let desired_total: f32 = desired.iter().sum();
 5618|      7|        let min_total: f32 = mins.iter().sum();
 5619|       |
 5620|      7|        let widths = if min_total >= clamped_available {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5620:25): [True: 2, False: 5]
  ------------------
 5621|      2|            mins.iter()
 5622|      6|                .map(|m| m * (clamped_available / min_total))
                               ^2
 5623|      2|                .collect()
 5624|      5|        } else if desired_total <= clamped_available {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5624:19): [True: 3, False: 2]

 5625|      3|            let slack = desired_total - min_total;
 5626|      3|            if slack <= f32::EPSILON {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (5626:16): [True: 2, False: 1]

 5628|      4|                mins.iter().map(|m| m + bonus).collect()
                              ^2   ^2     ^2                 ^2
 5629|       |            } else {
 5630|      1|                desired.to_vec()
 5631|       |            }
 5632|       |        } else {
 5633|      2|            let mut widths = mins.to_vec();
 5634|      2|            let extra = clamped_available - min_total;
 5635|      2|            let slack: Vec<f32> = desired
 5636|      2|                .iter()
 5637|      2|                .zip(mins.iter())
 5638|      6|                .map(|(d, m)| (d - m).max(0.0))
                               ^2
 5639|      2|                .collect();

 5641|      6|            for (w, s) in widths.iter_mut().zip(slack.iter()) {
                                        ^2                ^2  ^2
 5642|      6|                *w += extra * (*s / total_slack);
 5643|      6|            }
 5644|      2|            widths
 5645|       |        };
 5646|       |
 5647|      7|        widths
 5648|      8|    }

 5792|       |    /// Get texture cache statistics for debugging.
 5793|       |    ///
 5794|       |    /// Returns (emoji_cache_len, image_cache_len).
 5795|       |    ///
 5796|       |    /// # Texture Memory Management (Stage 7 Research)
 5797|       |    ///
 5798|       |    /// egui's TextureHandle uses reference counting via `Arc<RwLock<TextureManager>>`.
 5799|       |    /// When all TextureHandles for a texture are dropped, egui automatically frees
 5800|       |    /// the GPU texture. Our LruCache eviction correctly drops TextureHandles,
 5801|       |    /// triggering this cleanup.

 5803|       |    /// Note: `ctx.forget_image(uri)` is for URI-based image loaders, not for
 5804|       |    /// TextureHandle-based textures created via `ctx.load_texture()`. No additional
 5805|       |    /// explicit cleanup API is needed for our use case.

 5811|      1|        )
 5812|      1|    }

  ------------------
  |  Branch (6227:16): [True: 1, False: 1]

 6252|     24|    fn image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 6253|     24|        cached_modified: Option<SystemTime>,
 6254|     24|        current: Option<SystemTime>,
 6255|     24|    ) -> bool {
 6256|     24|        match (cached_modified, current) {
 6257|     14|            (Some(a), Some(b)) => {
 6258|       |                // Treat timestamps within the tolerance window as identical.
 6259|     14|                let delta = if a > b {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (6259:32): [True: 2, False: 12]
  ------------------
 6260|      2|                    a.duration_since(b)
 6261|       |                } else {
 6262|     12|                    b.duration_since(a)
 6263|       |                };
 6264|     14|                match delta {
 6265|     14|                    Ok(d) => d > IMAGE_TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE,
 6266|      0|                    Err(_) => true, // clock went backwards — treat as stale
 6267|       |                }
 6268|       |            }
 6269|       |            // Had a valid cache but metadata now fails (file locked by AV,
 6270|       |            // transient 9P error, etc.) — keep the cached texture rather than
 6271|       |            // flashing "Loading image…" every frame.
 6272|      2|            (Some(_), None) => false,
 6273|      6|            (None, None) => false,
 6274|       |            // File appeared after being missing ? stale, pick it up.
 6275|      2|            (None, Some(_)) => true,
 6276|       |        }
 6277|     24|    }
 6278|       |
 6279|     16|    fn image_source_stale(cached_modified: Option<SystemTime>, path: &Path) -> bool {
 6280|     16|        let current = Self::disk_image_timestamp(path);
 6281|     16|        Self::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(cached_modified, current)
 6282|     16|    }

 6458|       |/// is stripped before decoding (some generators wrap long base64 lines).
 6459|     21|fn decode_data_uri(uri: &str) -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
 6460|       |    // Must start with "data:"
 6461|     21|    let rest = uri.strip_prefix("data:")?;

 6506|       |/// Compute a fixed-size cache key for a data URI string.
 6507|       |///
 6508|       |/// Uses `DefaultHasher` (SipHash-1-3) to produce a 16-hex-digit hash,
 6509|       |/// prefixed with `data-uri:`. The resulting key is always 25 characters
 6510|       |/// regardless of payload size, preventing megabyte-sized URI strings from
 6511|       |/// bloating the `ImageCache` key storage.
 6512|     21|fn data_uri_cache_key(uri: &str) -> String {
 6513|     21|    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();
 6514|     21|    uri.hash(&mut hasher);
 6515|     21|    format!("data-uri:{:016x}", hasher.finish())
 6516|     21|}
 6517|       |
 6518|       |/// Downscale a `ColorImage` if it exceeds `max_w` or `max_h`, preserving
 6519|       |/// aspect ratio.  Returns the (possibly new) image and its pixel dimensions.
 6520|       |///
 6521|       |/// Uses nearest-neighbor sampling directly on the `Color32` pixel buffer so
 6522|       |/// there is **no intermediate byte-copy** — peak memory is just the original
 6523|       |/// plus the (smaller) destination.
 6524|     10|fn downscale_color_image(
 6525|     10|    image: egui::ColorImage,
 6526|     10|    max_w: u32,
 6527|     10|    max_h: u32,
 6528|     10|) -> (egui::ColorImage, u32, u32) {
 6529|     10|    let (src_w, src_h) = (image.size[0] as u32, image.size[1] as u32);
 6530|     10|    if src_w <= max_w && src_h <= max_h {
                                       ^8
  ------------------
  |  Branch (6530:8): [True: 8, False: 2]
  |  Branch (6530:26): [True: 8, False: 0]
  ------------------
 6531|      8|        return (image, src_w, src_h);
 6532|      2|    }
 6533|       |    // Determine uniform scale factor
 6534|      2|    let scale_x = max_w as f64 / src_w as f64;
 6535|      2|    let scale_y = max_h as f64 / src_h as f64;
 6536|      2|    let scale = scale_x.min(scale_y);
 6537|      2|    let dst_w = ((src_w as f64 * scale).round() as u32).max(1);
 6538|      2|    let dst_h = ((src_h as f64 * scale).round() as u32).max(1);
 6539|       |
 6540|       |    // Nearest-neighbor downscale — reads directly from the source pixel
 6541|       |    // buffer with no intermediate Vec<u8> copy.
 6542|      2|    let src_stride = src_w as usize;
 6543|      2|    let mut dst_pixels = Vec::with_capacity((dst_w * dst_h) as usize);
 6544|    200|    for y in 0..dst_h {
                           ^2
 6545|    200|        let src_y = ((y as f64 / scale).min((src_h - 1) as f64)) as usize;
 6546|  60.0k|        for x in 0..dst_w {
                               ^200
 6547|  60.0k|            let src_x = ((x as f64 / scale).min((src_w - 1) as f64)) as usize;
 6548|  60.0k|            dst_pixels.push(image.pixels[src_y * src_stride + src_x]);
 6549|  60.0k|        }
 6550|       |    }
 6551|      2|    let out = egui::ColorImage {
 6552|      2|        size: [dst_w as usize, dst_h as usize],
 6553|      2|        pixels: dst_pixels,
 6554|      2|    };
 6555|      2|    (out, dst_w, dst_h)
 6556|     10|}

 6682|       |            None
 6683|       |        }
 6684|       |    }
 6685|       |
 6686|       |    fn emoji_fragment_key<'a>(fragment: &'a CellFragment<'a>) -> Option<&'a str> {
 6687|       |        if let CellFragment::Emoji(key) = fragment {
 6688|       |            Some(key.as_str())
 6689|       |        } else {
 6690|       |            None
 6691|       |        }
 6692|       |    }
 6693|       |
 6694|       |    fn with_test_ui<F>(f: F)
 6695|       |    where
 6696|       |        F: FnOnce(&egui::Context, &mut egui::Ui),
 6697|       |    {
 6698|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 6699|       |        ctx.begin_frame(screen_input(1024.0, 768.0));
 6700|       |        egui::CentralPanel::default().show(&ctx, |ui| {
 6701|       |            f(&ctx, ui);
 6702|       |        });
 6703|       |        let _ = ctx.end_frame();
 6704|       |    }
 6705|       |
 6706|       |    fn wait_for_image(
 6707|       |        renderer: &MarkdownRenderer,
 6708|       |        ctx: &egui::Context,
 6709|       |        ui: &egui::Ui,
 6710|       |        path: &str,
 6711|       |    ) -> Option<(egui::TextureHandle, u32, u32)> {
 6712|       |        // Allow extra time for async image loading during tests (up to 10s total).
 6713|       |        wait_for_image_with_limit(renderer, ctx, ui, path, 200)
 6714|       |    }
 6715|       |
 6716|       |    fn wait_for_image_with_limit(
 6717|       |        renderer: &MarkdownRenderer,
 6718|       |        ctx: &egui::Context,
 6719|       |        ui: &egui::Ui,
 6720|       |        path: &str,
 6721|       |        max_iters: usize,
 6722|       |    ) -> Option<(egui::TextureHandle, u32, u32)> {
 6723|       |        for _ in 0..max_iters {
 6724|       |            if let Some(loaded) = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, path) {
 6725|       |                return Some(loaded);
 6726|       |            }
 6727|       |            renderer.poll_image_results(ctx);
 6728|       |            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50));
 6729|       |        }
 6730|       |        renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, path)
 6731|       |    }
 6732|       |
 6733|       |    fn run_frame_with_input<F>(ctx: &egui::Context, input: egui::RawInput, f: F)
 6734|       |    where
 6735|       |        F: FnOnce(&egui::Context, &mut egui::Ui),
 6736|       |    {
 6737|       |        ctx.begin_frame(input);
 6738|       |        egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 6739|       |            f(ctx, ui);
 6740|       |        });
 6741|       |        let _ = ctx.end_frame();
 6742|       |    }
 6743|       |
 6744|       |    fn input_with_click(pos: egui::Pos2, button: egui::PointerButton) -> egui::RawInput {
 6745|       |        let mut input = screen_input(320.0, 240.0);
 6746|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(pos));
 6747|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::PointerButton {
 6748|       |            pos,
 6749|       |            button,
 6750|       |            pressed: true,
 6751|       |            modifiers: egui::Modifiers::default(),
 6752|       |        });
 6753|       |        input.events.push(egui::Event::PointerButton {
 6754|       |            pos,
 6755|       |            button,
 6756|       |            pressed: false,
 6757|       |            modifiers: egui::Modifiers::default(),
 6758|       |        });
 6759|       |        input
 6760|       |    }
 6761|       |
 6762|       |    /// Create a solid-color PNG of the given dimensions.  Solid PNGs compress
 6763|       |    /// extremely well (a few hundred bytes on disk) but decode to
 6764|       |    /// `width * height * 4` bytes of RGBA — ideal for cache-pressure tests.
 6765|       |    fn solid_png_bytes(width: u32, height: u32) -> Vec<u8> {
 6766|       |        let pixels = vec![255u8; (width * height * 4) as usize];
 6767|       |        let mut out = Vec::new();
 6768|       |        let encoder = PngEncoder::new(&mut out);
 6769|       |        encoder
 6770|       |            .write_image(&pixels, width, height, ColorType::Rgba8)
 6771|       |            .expect("encode png");
 6772|       |        out
 6773|       |    }
 6774|       |
 6775|       |    fn tiny_png_bytes() -> Vec<u8> {
 6776|       |        let width = 2u32;
 6777|       |        let height = 2u32;
 6778|       |        let pixels = vec![255u8; (width * height * 4) as usize];
 6779|       |        let mut out = Vec::new();
 6780|       |        let encoder = PngEncoder::new(&mut out);
 6781|       |        encoder
 6782|       |            .write_image(&pixels, width, height, ColorType::Rgba8)
 6783|       |            .expect("encode png");
 6784|       |        out
 6785|       |    }
 6786|       |
 6787|       |    fn spawn_http_response_server(
 6788|       |        status_line: &str,
 6789|       |        content_type: &str,
 6790|       |        body: Vec<u8>,
 6791|       |    ) -> (String, std::thread::JoinHandle<()>) {
 6792|       |        let listener = std::net::TcpListener::bind(("127.0.0.1", 0)).expect("bind");
 6793|       |        let addr = listener.local_addr().expect("local addr");
 6794|       |        let status_line = status_line.to_string();
 6795|       |        let content_type = content_type.to_string();
 6796|       |        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {
 6797|       |            let (mut stream, _) = listener.accept().expect("accept");
 6798|       |            let mut request_buf = [0u8; 1024];
 6799|       |            let _ = std::io::Read::read(&mut stream, &mut request_buf);
 6800|       |            let headers = format!(
 6801|       |                "{status_line}\r\nContent-Length: {}\r\nContent-Type: {content_type}\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n",
 6802|       |                body.len()
 6803|       |            );
 6804|       |            std::io::Write::write_all(&mut stream, headers.as_bytes()).expect("write headers");
 6805|       |            std::io::Write::write_all(&mut stream, &body).expect("write body");
 6806|       |        });
 6807|       |        (format!("http://{addr}/image.png"), handle)
 6808|       |    }
 6809|       |
 6810|       |    fn unused_local_url() -> String {
 6811|       |        let listener = std::net::TcpListener::bind(("127.0.0.1", 0)).expect("bind");
 6812|       |        let addr = listener.local_addr().expect("local addr");
 6813|       |        drop(listener);
 6814|       |        format!("http://{addr}/missing.png")
 6815|       |    }
 6816|       |
 6817|       |    /// Creates a test ImageCacheEntry with the given pixel size inside a with_test_ui context.
 6818|       |    fn make_test_cache_entry(size: [usize; 2]) -> ImageCacheEntry {
 6819|       |        let mut entry_slot = None;
 6820|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 6821|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 6822|       |                "test_entry",
 6823|       |                egui::ColorImage::new(size, Color32::WHITE),
 6824|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 6825|       |            );
 6826|       |            entry_slot = Some(ImageCacheEntry {
 6827|       |                texture: tex,
 6828|       |                size: [size[0] as u32, size[1] as u32],
 6829|       |                modified: None,
 6830|       |                byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([size[0] as u32, size[1] as u32]),
 6831|       |                degraded: false,
 6832|       |            });
 6833|       |        });
 6834|       |        entry_slot.expect("texture")
 6835|       |    }
 6836|       |
 6837|       |    /// Creates an egui::RawInput with the specified screen dimensions.
 6838|       |    fn screen_input(width: f32, height: f32) -> egui::RawInput {
 6839|       |        egui::RawInput {
 6840|       |            screen_rect: Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 6841|       |                egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 6842|       |                egui::vec2(width, height),
 6843|       |            )),
 6844|       |            ..Default::default()
 6845|       |        }
 6846|       |    }

 6848|       |    /// Returns the rocket emoji character from the shortcode map.
 6849|       |    fn rocket_emoji() -> &'static str {
 6850|       |        crate::emoji_catalog::shortcode_map()
 6851|       |            .get(":rocket:")
 6852|       |            .expect("rocket shortcode")
 6853|       |    }

 6855|       |    /// Asserts that inline code pipes were escaped (sentinel is present).
 6856|       |    fn assert_pipes_escaped(prepared: &str) {
 6857|       |        let expected = format!("`a{}b{}c`", PIPE_SENTINEL, PIPE_SENTINEL);
 6858|       |        assert!(
 6859|       |            prepared.contains(&expected),
 6860|       |            "expected pipe sentinel in: {}",
 6861|       |            prepared
 6862|       |        );
 6863|       |    }
 6864|       |
 6865|       |    /// Asserts that inline code pipes were NOT escaped (original backtick content preserved).
 6866|       |    fn assert_pipes_preserved(prepared: &str) {
 6867|       |        assert!(
 6868|       |            prepared.contains("`a|b|c`"),
 6869|       |            "expected preserved pipes in: {}",
 6870|       |            prepared
 6871|       |        );
 6872|       |        assert!(
 6873|       |            !prepared.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL),
 6874|       |            "unexpected pipe sentinel in: {}",
 6875|       |            prepared
 6876|       |        );
 6877|       |    }

 6879|       |    /// Creates a ColumnSpec with Resizable policy for test use.
 6880|       |    fn resizable_spec(col: usize, name: &str, min: f32, preferred: f32) -> ColumnSpec {
 6881|       |        ColumnSpec::new(
 6882|       |            col,
 6883|       |            name,
 6884|       |            ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
 6885|       |                min,
 6886|       |                preferred,
 6887|       |                clip: false,
 6888|       |            },
 6889|       |            None,
 6890|       |        )
 6891|       |    }

 6894|       |    fn fixed_spec(col: usize, name: &str, width: f32) -> ColumnSpec {
 6895|       |        ColumnSpec::new(col, name, ColumnPolicy::Fixed { width, clip: false }, None)
 6896|       |    }

 6899|       |    fn test_image_cache_get_missing_and_insert_empty_order() {
 6900|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(1);
 6901|       |        assert!(cache.get("missing").is_none());
 6902|       |
 6903|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([1, 1]);
 6904|       |        cache.entries.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 6905|       |        cache.order.clear();
 6906|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry);
 6907|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));

 6910|       |    #[test]
 6911|       |    fn test_image_cache_insert_existing_key_updates_entry() {
 6912|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(2);
 6913|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([1, 1]);
 6914|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry);
 6915|       |
 6916|       |        let entry2 = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]);
 6917|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry2);
 6918|       |
 6919|       |        let stored = cache.get("a").expect("stored");
 6920|       |        assert_eq!(stored.size, [2, 2]);
 6921|       |    }
