 6923|       |    #[test]
 6924|       |    fn test_image_cache_evicts_oldest_entry() {
 6925|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(1);
 6926|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([1, 1]);
 6927|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 6928|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry);
 6929|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 6930|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("a"));
 6931|       |    }
 6932|       |
 6933|       |    #[test]
 6934|       |    fn test_image_cache_tracks_memory_without_byte_eviction() {
 6935|       |        // insert() no longer evicts based on bytes — only enforce_budget() does.
 6936|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 6937|       |        cache.max_bytes = 32; // 8 pixels worth (2x2 RGBA = 16 bytes each)
 6938|       |
 6939|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]);
 6940|       |
 6941|       |        // Insert first entry (16 bytes)
 6942|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 6943|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 16);
 6944|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 6945|       |
 6946|       |        // Insert second entry (16 bytes) — fits within 32 byte limit
 6947|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry.clone());
 6948|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 32);
 6949|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 6950|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 6951|       |
 6952|       |        // Insert third entry — does NOT evict despite exceeding max_bytes
 6953|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), entry);
 6954|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 48);
 6955|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 6956|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 6957|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("c"));
 6958|       |    }
 6959|       |
 6960|       |    #[test]
 6961|       |    fn test_image_cache_remove_decrements_bytes() {
 6962|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(10);
 6963|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([4, 4]);
 6964|       |        cache.insert("x".to_string(), entry);
 6965|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 64);
 6966|       |
 6967|       |        cache.remove("x");
 6968|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 0);
 6969|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("x"));
 6970|       |    }
 6971|       |
 6972|       |    #[test]
 6973|       |    fn test_image_cache_clear() {
 6974|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(10);
 6975|       |        let entry1 = make_test_cache_entry([4, 4]);
 6976|       |        let entry2 = make_test_cache_entry([8, 8]);
 6977|       |        let entry3 = make_test_cache_entry([16, 16]);
 6978|       |
 6979|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry1);
 6980|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry2);
 6981|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), entry3);
 6982|       |
 6983|       |        // Verify entries exist and bytes are tracked
 6984|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 6985|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 6986|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("c"));
 6987|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 64 + 256 + 1024); // 4*4*4 + 8*8*4 + 16*16*4
 6988|       |
 6989|       |        cache.clear();
 6990|       |
 6991|       |        // Verify all entries removed and bytes zeroed
 6992|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("a"));
 6993|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("b"));
 6994|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("c"));
 6995|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 0);
 6996|       |    }
 6997|       |
 6998|       |    // --- Viewport-aware degradation tests ---
 6999|       |
 7000|       |    #[test]
 7001|       |    fn test_image_cache_enforce_budget_degrades_furthest() {
 7002|       |        // Each 10x10 entry is 400 bytes. Budget fits 2 full entries (800 bytes).
 7003|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7004|       |        cache.max_bytes = 800;
 7005|       |
 7006|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7007|       |            let make = |name: &str| {
 7008|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7009|       |                    name,
 7010|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7011|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7012|       |                );
 7013|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7014|       |                    texture: tex,
 7015|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7016|       |                    modified: None,
 7017|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7018|       |                    degraded: false,
 7019|       |                }
 7020|       |            };
 7021|       |
 7022|       |            cache.insert("near".to_string(), make("near"));
 7023|       |            cache.insert("mid".to_string(), make("mid"));
 7024|       |            cache.insert("far".to_string(), make("far"));
 7025|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 1200); // over budget of 800
 7026|       |
 7027|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 7028|       |            distances.insert("near".to_string(), 100.0);
 7029|       |            distances.insert("mid".to_string(), 500.0);
 7030|       |            distances.insert("far".to_string(), 5000.0);
 7031|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7032|       |
 7033|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
 7034|       |
 7035|       |            // "far" should be degraded (furthest), others kept
 7036|       |            assert!(
 7037|       |                cache.entries.get("far").unwrap().degraded,
 7038|       |                "far should be degraded"
 7039|       |            );
 7040|       |            assert!(
 7041|       |                !cache.entries.get("near").unwrap().degraded,
 7042|       |                "near should survive"
 7043|       |            );
 7044|       |            // total_bytes should be back under budget (800 - 400 + 16 = 416 = 800)
 7045|       |            assert!(cache.current_bytes() <= 800, "should be under budget");
 7046|       |        });
 7047|       |    }
 7048|       |
 7049|       |    #[test]
 7050|       |    fn test_image_cache_enforce_budget_noop_under_budget() {
 7051|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7052|       |        cache.max_bytes = 500;
 7053|       |
 7054|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7055|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7056|       |                "small",
 7057|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7058|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7059|       |            );
 7060|       |            cache.insert(
 7061|       |                "a".to_string(),
 7062|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7063|       |                    texture: tex,
 7064|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7065|       |                    modified: None,
 7066|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7067|       |                    degraded: false,
 7068|       |                },
 7069|       |            );
 7070|       |
 7071|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 7072|       |            distances.insert("a".to_string(), 100.0);
 7073|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7074|       |
 7075|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
 7076|       |            assert!(!cache.entries.get("a").unwrap().degraded);
 7077|       |        });
 7078|       |    }
 7079|       |
 7080|       |    #[test]
 7081|       |    fn test_image_cache_enforce_budget_stops_when_under_budget() {
 7082|       |        // With 3 entries over budget, only enough should be degraded to get under.
 7083|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7084|       |        // Each 10x10 entry is 400 bytes. Budget fits 2 full entries (800 bytes).
 7085|       |        cache.max_bytes = 800;
 7086|       |
 7087|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7088|       |            let make = |name: &str| {
 7089|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7090|       |                    name,
 7091|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7092|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7093|       |                );
 7094|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7095|       |                    texture: tex,
 7096|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7097|       |                    modified: None,
 7098|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7099|       |                    degraded: false,
 7100|       |                }
 7101|       |            };
 7102|       |
 7103|       |            cache.insert("a".to_string(), make("a"));
 7104|       |            cache.insert("b".to_string(), make("b"));
 7105|       |            cache.insert("c".to_string(), make("c"));
 7106|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 1200);
 7107|       |
 7108|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 7109|       |            distances.insert("a".to_string(), 100.0); // nearest
 7110|       |            distances.insert("b".to_string(), 300.0);
 7111|       |            distances.insert("c".to_string(), 500.0); // furthest
 7112|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7113|       |
 7114|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
 7115|       |
 7116|       |            // 1200 - 400(c) + 16 = 816 > 800 ? "c" degraded, still over budget.
 7117|       |            // 816 - 400(b) + 16 = 432 = 800 ? "b" also degraded, now under budget.
 7118|       |            assert!(
 7119|       |                cache.entries.get("c").unwrap().degraded,
 7120|       |                "c (furthest) should be degraded"
 7121|       |            );
 7122|       |            assert!(
 7123|       |                cache.entries.get("b").unwrap().degraded,
 7124|       |                "b (mid) should also be degraded"
 7125|       |            );
 7126|       |            assert!(
 7127|       |                !cache.entries.get("a").unwrap().degraded,
 7128|       |                "a (nearest) should survive"
 7129|       |            );
 7130|       |            assert!(cache.current_bytes() <= 800);
 7131|       |        });
 7132|       |    }
 7133|       |
 7134|       |    #[test]
 7135|       |    fn test_enforce_budget_degrades_orphan_entries_without_distances() {
 7136|       |        // Entries from a previous document have no viewport distances.
 7137|       |        // enforce_budget should degrade them as a fallback when still over budget.
 7138|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7139|       |        cache.max_bytes = 600; // fits 1 full entry (400 bytes) + margin
 7140|       |
 7141|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7142|       |            let make = |name: &str| {
 7143|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7144|       |                    name,
 7145|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7146|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7147|       |                );
 7148|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7149|       |                    texture: tex,
 7150|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7151|       |                    modified: None,
 7152|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]),
 7153|       |                    degraded: false,
 7154|       |                }
 7155|       |            };
 7156|       |
 7157|       |            // "old_a" and "old_b" are from a previous document (no viewport distances).
 7158|       |            cache.insert("old_a".to_string(), make("old_a"));
 7159|       |            cache.insert("old_b".to_string(), make("old_b"));
 7160|       |            // "current" is from the active document.
 7161|       |            cache.insert("current".to_string(), make("current"));
 7162|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 1200);
 7163|       |
 7164|       |            // Only "current" has a viewport distance.
 7165|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 7166|       |            distances.insert("current".to_string(), 50.0);
 7167|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7168|       |
 7169|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
 7170|       |
 7171|       |            // "current" should survive (it's the only entry with a distance and
 7172|       |            // we keep at least one non-degraded). The orphans should be degraded.
 7173|       |            assert!(
 7174|       |                !cache.entries.get("current").unwrap().degraded,
 7175|       |                "current doc entry should survive"
 7176|       |            );
 7177|       |            // At least one orphan must be degraded to get under budget.
 7178|       |            let orphan_degraded = cache.entries.get("old_a").unwrap().degraded
 7179|       |                || cache.entries.get("old_b").unwrap().degraded;
 7180|       |            assert!(orphan_degraded, "at least one orphan should be degraded");
 7181|       |            assert!(cache.current_bytes() <= 600);
 7182|       |        });
 7183|       |    }
 7184|       |
 7185|       |    #[test]
 7186|       |    fn test_enforce_budget_phase3_degrades_far_to_make_room_for_near_degraded() {
 7187|       |        // Scenario: cache is under budget, but near-viewport degraded entries
 7188|       |        // can't restore because far non-degraded entries use the space.
 7189|       |        // Phase 3 should degrade the far entries to create headroom.
 7190|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(256);
 7191|       |        cache.max_bytes = 1000;
 7192|       |
 7193|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7194|       |            let make = |name: &str| {
 7195|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7196|       |                    name,
 7197|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7198|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7199|       |                );
 7200|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7201|       |                    texture: tex,
 7202|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7203|       |                    modified: None,
 7204|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7205|       |                    degraded: false,
 7206|       |                }
 7207|       |            };
 7208|       |
 7209|       |            // "near": degraded entry near viewport (distance=50, in reload zone).
 7210|       |            let mut near_entry = make("near");
 7211|       |            near_entry.byte_size = 16;
 7212|       |            near_entry.degraded = true;
 7213|       |            cache.insert("near".to_string(), near_entry);
 7214|       |
 7215|       |            // "far1" and "far2": full-res entries far from viewport.
 7216|       |            cache.insert("far1".to_string(), make("far1"));
 7217|       |            cache.insert("far2".to_string(), make("far2"));
 7218|       |
 7219|       |            // total_bytes = 16 + 400 + 400 = 816. Under max_bytes (1000).
 7220|       |            // But restoring "near" would need 816 - 16 + 400 = 1200 > 1000.
 7221|       |            assert!(
 7222|       |                !cache.would_restore_fit("near"),
 7223|       |                "near shouldn't fit before phase 3"
 7224|       |            );
 7225|       |
 7226|       |            let mut distances = HashMap::new();
 7227|       |            distances.insert("near".to_string(), 50.0);
 7228|       |            distances.insert("far1".to_string(), 5000.0);
 7229|       |            distances.insert("far2".to_string(), 6000.0);
 7230|       |            cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
 7231|       |
 7232|       |            // viewport_height=600 ? reload_zone=1200. "near" (50) in zone.
 7233|       |            // "far1" (5000) and "far2" (6000) outside zone.
 7234|       |            cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 600.0);
 7235|       |
 7236|       |            // Phase 3 should degrade far entries to make room for "near".
 7237|       |            assert!(
 7238|       |                cache.entries.get("far1").unwrap().degraded
 7239|       |                    || cache.entries.get("far2").unwrap().degraded,
 7240|       |                "at least one far entry should be degraded to make room"
 7241|       |            );
 7242|       |            assert!(
 7243|       |                cache.would_restore_fit("near"),
 7244|       |                "near should fit after phase 3"
 7245|       |            );
 7246|       |        });
 7247|       |    }
 7248|       |

 7280|       |    fn test_degrade_reduces_bytes() {
 7281|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7282|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7283|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7284|       |                "big",
 7285|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7286|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7287|       |            );
 7288|       |            cache.insert(
 7289|       |                "img".to_string(),
 7290|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7291|       |                    texture: tex,
 7292|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7293|       |                    modified: None,
 7294|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7295|       |                    degraded: false,
 7296|       |                },
 7297|       |            );
 7298|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 400);
 7299|       |
 7300|       |            cache.degrade("img", ctx);
 7301|       |
 7302|       |            let entry = cache.entries.get("img").unwrap();
 7303|       |            assert!(entry.degraded);
 7304|       |            assert_eq!(entry.byte_size, 16); // 2*2*4
 7305|       |            assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 16);
 7306|       |        });
 7307|       |    }

 7381|       |    #[test]
 7382|       |    fn test_insert_no_byte_eviction() {
 7383|       |        // Verify that inserting when over max_bytes does NOT evict.
 7384|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7385|       |        cache.max_bytes = 16;
 7386|       |
 7387|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]); // 16 bytes
 7388|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7389|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry);
 7390|       |        // Both entries should coexist even though total (32) > max_bytes (16)
 7391|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("a"));
 7392|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 7393|       |        assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 32);
 7394|       |    }

 7396|       |    #[test]
 7397|       |    fn test_insert_count_limit_still_works() {
 7398|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(2);
 7399|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]);
 7400|       |
 7401|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7402|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7403|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), entry);
 7404|       |
 7405|       |        // "a" should be evicted (oldest by insertion order)
 7406|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("a"));
 7407|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 7408|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("c"));
 7409|       |        assert_eq!(cache.entries.len(), 2);
 7410|       |    }
 7411|       |
 7412|       |    #[test]
 7413|       |    fn test_would_restore_fit() {
 7414|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7415|       |        // Budget of 400 bytes
 7416|       |        cache.max_bytes = 400;
 7417|       |
 7418|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7419|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7420|       |                "restorable",
 7421|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7422|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7423|       |            );
 7424|       |            cache.insert(
 7425|       |                "img".to_string(),
 7426|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7427|       |                    texture: tex,
 7428|       |                    size: [10, 10],
 7429|       |                    modified: None,
 7430|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7431|       |                    degraded: false,
 7432|       |                },
 7433|       |            );
 7434|       |            cache.degrade("img", ctx);
 7435|       |            // Now 16 bytes used. Restoring would use 400. Budget is 400. Should fit.
 7436|       |            assert!(cache.would_restore_fit("img"));
 7437|       |
 7438|       |            // Shrink budget so restore wouldn't fit
 7439|       |            cache.max_bytes = 200;
 7440|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("img"));
 7441|       |
 7442|       |            // Non-degraded entry returns false
 7443|       |            let tex2 = ctx.load_texture(
 7444|       |                "full",
 7445|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
 7446|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7447|       |            );
 7448|       |            cache.insert(
 7449|       |                "full".to_string(),
 7450|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7451|       |                    texture: tex2,
 7452|       |                    size: [2, 2],
 7453|       |                    modified: None,
 7454|       |                    byte_size: 16,
 7455|       |                    degraded: false,
 7456|       |                },
 7457|       |            );
 7458|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("full"));
 7459|       |
 7460|       |            // Missing key returns false
 7461|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("missing"));
 7462|       |        });
 7463|       |    }
 7464|       |
 7465|       |    #[test]
 7466|       |    fn test_would_restore_fit_accounts_for_pending_bytes() {
 7467|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7468|       |        // Budget of 800 bytes. Two 10x10 images (400 bytes each).
 7469|       |        cache.max_bytes = 800;
 7470|       |
 7471|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
 7472|       |            // Insert and degrade two images.
 7473|       |            for name in &["img_a", "img_b"] {
 7474|       |                let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7475|       |                    *name,
 7476|       |                    egui::ColorImage::new([10, 10], Color32::WHITE),
 7477|       |                    egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7478|       |                );
 7479|       |                cache.insert(
 7480|       |                    name.to_string(),
 7481|       |                    ImageCacheEntry {
 7482|       |                        texture: tex,
 7483|       |                        size: [10, 10],
 7484|       |                        modified: None,
 7485|       |                        byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]), // 400
 7486|       |                        degraded: false,
 7487|       |                    },
 7488|       |                );
 7489|       |                cache.degrade(name, ctx);
 7490|       |            }
 7491|       |            // Both degraded: total_bytes = 32 (two 2x2 placeholders).
 7492|       |            // Restoring either one: 32 - 16 + 400 = 416 = 800. Fits.
 7493|       |            assert!(cache.would_restore_fit("img_a"));
 7494|       |            assert!(cache.would_restore_fit("img_b"));
 7495|       |
 7496|       |            // Simulate enqueuing img_a's restore: pending_restore_bytes = 400.
 7497|       |            cache.pending_restore_bytes = ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([10, 10]);
 7498|       |            // Now img_b: 32 - 16 + 400 + 400 = 816 > 800. Doesn't fit.
 7499|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("img_b"));
 7500|       |            // img_a still individually fits (its own pending is already counted,
 7501|       |            // but that's correct — the check is conservative).
 7502|       |            // 32 - 16 + 400 + 400 = 816 > 800. Also blocked.
 7503|       |            assert!(!cache.would_restore_fit("img_a"));
 7504|       |
 7505|       |            // Increase budget so both fit even with pending.
 7506|       |            cache.max_bytes = 900;
 7507|       |            // 32 - 16 + 400 + 400 = 816 = 900. Fits.
 7508|       |            assert!(cache.would_restore_fit("img_b"));
 7509|       |        });
 7510|       |    }
 7511|       |
 7512|       |    #[test]
 7513|       |    fn test_pending_restore_bytes_cleared_by_clear() {
 7514|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(100);
 7515|       |        cache.pending_restore_bytes = 12345;
 7516|       |        cache.clear();
 7517|       |        assert_eq!(cache.pending_restore_bytes, 0);
 7518|       |    }

 7534|       |
 7535|       |    #[test]
 7536|       |    fn test_get_no_touch() {
 7537|       |        // Verify get() doesn't reorder `order` — it's now a pure lookup.
 7538|       |        let mut cache = ImageCache::new(3);
 7539|       |        let entry = make_test_cache_entry([2, 2]);
 7540|       |
 7541|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7542|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7543|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), entry.clone());
 7544|       |
 7545|       |        // Order should be [a, b, c]
 7546|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[0], "a");
 7547|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[1], "b");
 7548|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[2], "c");
 7549|       |
 7550|       |        // get("a") should NOT move "a" to the back
 7551|       |        let _ = cache.get("a");
 7552|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[0], "a", "get() must not reorder");
 7553|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[1], "b");
 7554|       |        assert_eq!(cache.order[2], "c");
 7555|       |
 7556|       |        // Insert "d" — should evict "a" (front of order), proving order wasn't touched
 7557|       |        cache.insert("d".to_string(), entry);
 7558|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key("a"), "a should be evicted (oldest)");
 7559|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("b"));
 7560|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("c"));
 7561|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key("d"));
 7562|       |    }
 7563|       |
 7564|       |    // --- Degraded get_or_load tests (LOD Stage 2) ---
 7565|       |
 7566|       |    #[test]
 7567|       |    fn test_get_or_load_returns_degraded_texture_with_original_size() {
 7568|       |        // A degraded entry should still return Some with the original display
 7569|       |        // dimensions, even though the actual texture is 2x2.
 7570|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 7571|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
 7572|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7573|       |                "full-res",
 7574|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([100, 80], Color32::WHITE),
 7575|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7576|       |            );
 7577|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
 7578|       |                "assets/emoji/1f600.png".to_string(),
 7579|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7580|       |                    texture: tex,
 7581|       |                    size: [100, 80],
 7582|       |                    modified: None,
 7583|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([100, 80]),
 7584|       |                    degraded: false,
 7585|       |                },
 7586|       |            );
 7587|       |            // Degrade the entry — texture becomes 2x2 but size stays [100, 80].
 7588|       |            renderer
 7589|       |                .image_textures
 7590|       |                .borrow_mut()
 7591|       |                .degrade("assets/emoji/1f600.png", ctx);
 7592|       |
 7593|       |            let result = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "assets/emoji/1f600.png");
 7594|       |            assert!(result.is_some(), "degraded entry should still return Some");
 7595|       |            let (_tex, w, h) = result.unwrap();
 7596|       |            assert_eq!(w, 100, "width should be original display width");
 7597|       |            assert_eq!(h, 80, "height should be original display height");
 7598|       |        });
 7599|       |    }
 7600|       |
 7601|       |    #[test]
 7602|       |    fn test_degraded_entry_not_removed_when_stale() -> Result<()> {
 7603|       |        // When a degraded entry's source file changes on disk (stale), the
 7604|       |        // entry must NOT be removed — it stays in the cache as a layout
 7605|       |        // placeholder while a reload is enqueued.
 7606|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 7607|       |        let temp = tempdir()?;
 7608|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("image.png");
 7609|       |        std::fs::write(&image_path, tiny_png_bytes())?;
 7610|       |        let resolved = image_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
 7611|       |
 7612|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
 7613|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
 7614|       |                "degraded-stale",
 7615|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::from_rgb(128, 128, 128)),
 7616|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
 7617|       |            );
 7618|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
 7619|       |                resolved.clone(),
 7620|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
 7621|       |                    texture: tex,
 7622|       |                    size: [50, 40],
 7623|       |                    // Use UNIX_EPOCH to ensure it's detected as stale
 7624|       |                    modified: Some(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH),
 7625|       |                    byte_size: 2 * 2 * 4,
 7626|       |                    degraded: true,
 7627|       |                },
 7628|       |            );
 7629|       |
 7630|       |            let result = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &resolved);
 7631|       |            // Should still return the degraded texture (layout preservation).
 7632|       |            assert!(result.is_some(), "degraded stale entry should return Some");
 7633|       |            let (_tex, w, h) = result.unwrap();
 7634|       |            assert_eq!(w, 50);
 7635|       |            assert_eq!(h, 40);
 7636|       |            // Entry must NOT have been removed from the cache.
 7637|       |            assert!(
 7638|       |                renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&resolved),
 7639|       |                "degraded stale entry must not be removed from cache"
 7640|       |            );
 7641|       |        });
 7642|       |        Ok(())
 7643|       |    }
 7644|       |
 7645|       |    // --- Generation token tests ---
 7646|       |
 7647|       |    #[test]
 7648|       |    fn test_stale_generation_result_is_discarded() {
 7649|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 7650|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded::<ImageLoadResult>();
 7651|       |        renderer.image_result_rx = result_rx;
 7652|       |
 7653|       |        // Simulate: gen 0 enqueued an image, then document was switched (gen ? 1)
 7654|       |        renderer
 7655|       |            .image_pending
 7656|       |            .borrow_mut()
 7657|       |            .insert("old.png".to_string());
 7658|       |        renderer.reset_image_state(); // bumps to gen 1
 7659|       |
 7660|       |        // Stale result from gen 0 arrives
 7661|       |        result_tx
 7662|       |            .send(ImageLoadResult::Failed {
 7663|       |                key: "old.png".to_string(),
 7664|       |                generation: 0,
 7665|       |            })
 7666|       |            .expect("send");
 7667|       |
 7668|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7669|       |        renderer.poll_image_results(&ctx);
 7670|       |
 7671|       |        // The stale result should NOT register as a failure in the current gen
 7672|       |        assert!(
 7673|       |            !renderer
 7674|       |                .image_failures
 7675|       |                .borrow()
 7676|       |                .contains_key(&"old.png".to_string()),
 7677|       |            "stale generation result should be discarded"
 7678|       |        );
 7679|       |    }
 7680|       |
 7681|       |    #[test]
 7682|       |    fn test_current_generation_result_is_accepted() {
 7683|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 7684|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded::<ImageLoadResult>();
 7685|       |        renderer.image_result_rx = result_rx;
 7686|       |
 7687|       |        renderer
 7688|       |            .image_pending
 7689|       |            .borrow_mut()
 7690|       |            .insert("current.png".to_string());
 7691|       |
 7692|       |        // Result from current gen (0)
 7693|       |        result_tx
 7694|       |            .send(ImageLoadResult::Failed {
 7695|       |                key: "current.png".to_string(),
 7696|       |                generation: 0,
 7697|       |            })
 7698|       |            .expect("send");
 7699|       |
 7700|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 7701|       |        renderer.poll_image_results(&ctx);
 7702|       |
 7703|       |        assert!(
 7704|       |            renderer
 7705|       |                .image_failures
 7706|       |                .borrow()
 7707|       |                .contains_key(&"current.png".to_string()),
 7708|       |            "current generation result should be processed"
 7709|       |        );
 7710|       |    }
 7711|       |
 7712|       |    // --- Image downscale tests ---
 7713|       |
 7714|       |    #[test]
 7715|       |    fn test_downscale_color_image_no_op_when_within_limits() {
 7716|       |        let img = egui::ColorImage::new([100, 80], egui::Color32::RED);
 7717|       |        let (out, w, h) = downscale_color_image(img, 200, 200);
 7718|       |        assert_eq!(w, 100);
 7719|       |        assert_eq!(h, 80);
 7720|       |        assert_eq!(out.size, [100, 80]);
 7721|       |    }
 7722|       |
 7723|       |    #[test]
 7724|       |    fn test_downscale_color_image_shrinks_oversized() {
 7725|       |        let img = egui::ColorImage::new([1000, 500], egui::Color32::BLUE);
 7726|       |        let (out, w, h) = downscale_color_image(img, 200, 200);
 7727|       |        // Scale factor: min(200/1000, 200/500) = 0.2
 7728|       |        // 1000 * 0.2 = 200, 500 * 0.2 = 100
 7729|       |        assert_eq!(w, 200);
 7730|       |        assert_eq!(h, 100);
 7731|       |        assert_eq!(out.size, [200, 100]);
 7732|       |    }
 7733|       |
 7734|       |    #[test]
 7735|       |    fn test_downscale_color_image_preserves_aspect_ratio() {
 7736|       |        let img = egui::ColorImage::new([800, 200], egui::Color32::GREEN);
 7737|       |        let (_, w, h) = downscale_color_image(img, 400, 400);
 7738|       |        // Scale = min(400/800, 400/200) = 0.5
 7739|       |        assert_eq!(w, 400);
 7740|       |        assert_eq!(h, 100);
 7741|       |    }
 7742|       |
 7743|       |    #[test]
 7744|       |    fn test_downscale_color_image_exact_boundary() {
 7745|       |        let img = egui::ColorImage::new([200, 200], egui::Color32::WHITE);
 7746|       |        let (_, w, h) = downscale_color_image(img, 200, 200);
 7747|       |        assert_eq!(w, 200);
 7748|       |        assert_eq!(h, 200);
 7749|       |    }

 7751|       |    // --- LruCache Tests ---
 7752|       |
 7753|       |    #[test]
 7754|       |    fn test_lru_cache_basic_insert_and_get() {
 7755|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(10);
 7756|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7757|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7758|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7759|       |
 7760|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"a".to_string()), Some(1));
 7761|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"b".to_string()), Some(2));
 7762|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"c".to_string()), Some(3));
 7763|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"d".to_string()), None);
 7764|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 3);
 7765|       |    }
 7766|       |
 7767|       |    #[test]
 7768|       |    fn test_lru_cache_evicts_oldest_entry() {
 7769|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(2);
 7770|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7771|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7772|       |        // Cache is now full at capacity 2
 7773|       |
 7774|       |        // Insert third entry, should evict "a" (oldest)
 7775|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7776|       |
 7777|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string())); // "a" evicted
 7778|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string()));
 7779|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"c".to_string()));
 7780|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 2);
 7781|       |    }
 7782|       |
 7783|       |    #[test]
 7784|       |    fn test_lru_cache_touch_updates_order() {
 7785|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(2);
 7786|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7787|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7788|       |
 7789|       |        // Touch "a" to make it recently used
 7790|       |        cache.touch(&"a".to_string());
 7791|       |
 7792|       |        // Insert third entry, should evict "b" now (oldest after touch)
 7793|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7794|       |
 7795|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string())); // "a" preserved by touch
 7796|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string())); // "b" evicted
 7797|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"c".to_string()));
 7798|       |    }
 7799|       |
 7800|       |    #[test]
 7801|       |    fn test_lru_cache_insert_existing_updates_value_and_order() {
 7802|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(2);
 7803|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7804|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7805|       |
 7806|       |        // Re-insert "a" with new value
 7807|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 100);
 7808|       |
 7809|       |        assert_eq!(cache.get(&"a".to_string()), Some(100));
 7810|       |
 7811|       |        // Insert third entry, should evict "b" (oldest after "a" was updated)
 7812|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7813|       |
 7814|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7815|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string())); // "b" evicted
 7816|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"c".to_string()));
 7817|       |    }
 7818|       |
 7819|       |    #[test]
 7820|       |    fn test_lru_cache_remove() {
 7821|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(10);
 7822|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7823|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7824|       |
 7825|       |        cache.remove(&"a".to_string());
 7826|       |
 7827|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7828|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string()));
 7829|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 1);
 7830|       |
 7831|       |        // Removing non-existent key should be safe
 7832|       |        cache.remove(&"nonexistent".to_string());
 7833|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 1);
 7834|       |    }
 7835|       |
 7836|       |    #[test]
 7837|       |    fn test_lru_cache_clear() {
 7838|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(10);
 7839|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7840|       |        cache.insert("b".to_string(), 2);
 7841|       |        cache.insert("c".to_string(), 3);
 7842|       |
 7843|       |        cache.clear();
 7844|       |
 7845|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 0);
 7846|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7847|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"b".to_string()));
 7848|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"c".to_string()));
 7849|       |    }
 7850|       |
 7851|       |    #[test]
 7852|       |    fn test_lru_cache_capacity_enforcement() {
 7853|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(3);
 7854|       |        for i in 0..10 {
 7855|       |            cache.insert(format!("key{}", i), i);
 7856|       |        }
 7857|       |
 7858|       |        // Only last 3 entries should remain
 7859|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 3);
 7860|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"key7".to_string()));
 7861|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"key8".to_string()));
 7862|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"key9".to_string()));
 7863|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"key0".to_string()));
 7864|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"key6".to_string()));
 7865|       |    }
 7866|       |
 7867|       |    #[test]
 7868|       |    fn test_lru_cache_touch_nonexistent_key() {
 7869|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(10);
 7870|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7871|       |
 7872|       |        // Touching non-existent key should be safe (no-op)
 7873|       |        cache.touch(&"nonexistent".to_string());
 7874|       |
 7875|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 1);
 7876|       |        assert!(cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7877|       |    }
 7878|       |
 7879|       |    #[test]
 7880|       |    fn test_lru_cache_zero_capacity() {
 7881|       |        let mut cache: LruCache<String, i32> = LruCache::new(0);
 7882|       |        cache.insert("a".to_string(), 1);
 7883|       |
 7884|       |        // With zero capacity, nothing should be stored
 7885|       |        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 0);
 7886|       |        assert!(!cache.contains_key(&"a".to_string()));
 7887|       |    }

 7962|       |    #[test]
 7963|       |    fn test_extend_table_rect_handles_nan() {
 7964|       |        let mut target = Some(egui::Rect::from_min_size(
 7965|       |            egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0),
 7966|       |            egui::vec2(10.0, 10.0),
 7967|       |        ));
 7968|       |        let nan_rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(f32::NAN, 0.0), egui::vec2(1.0, 1.0));
 7969|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, nan_rect);
 7970|       |        let stored = target.expect("target");
 7971|       |        assert!(!stored.min.x.is_nan());
 7972|       |
 7973|       |        let mut target_none = None;
 7974|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(
 7975|       |            &mut target_none,
 7976|       |            egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(5.0, 6.0), egui::vec2(2.0, 2.0)),
 7977|       |        );
 7978|       |        assert!(target_none.is_some());
 7979|       |    }
 7980|       |
 7981|       |    #[test]
 7982|       |    fn test_extend_table_rect_rejects_nan_components() {
 7983|       |        let base = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::vec2(3.0, 4.0));
 7984|       |        let mut target = Some(base);
 7985|       |        let nan_min_y = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, f32::NAN), egui::vec2(1.0, 1.0));
 7986|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, nan_min_y);
 7987|       |        assert_eq!(target, Some(base));
 7988|       |
 7989|       |        let mut target = Some(base);
 7990|       |        let nan_max_x = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(f32::NAN, 1.0));
 7991|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, nan_max_x);
 7992|       |        assert_eq!(target, Some(base));
 7993|       |
 7994|       |        let mut target = Some(base);
 7995|       |        let nan_max_y = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(1.0, f32::NAN));
 7996|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, nan_max_y);
 7997|       |        assert_eq!(target, Some(base));
 7998|       |    }

 8017|       |    fn test_link_at_pointer_outside_rect_returns_none() {
 8018|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8019|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
 8020|       |            text: "Link".to_string(),
 8021|       |            url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
 8022|       |        }];
 8023|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 8024|       |        let mut input = screen_input(320.0, 240.0);
 8025|       |        input
 8026|       |            .events
 8027|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(egui::pos2(200.0, 200.0)));
 8028|       |        ctx.begin_frame(input);
 8029|       |        let mut found = false;
 8030|       |        egui::CentralPanel::default().show(&ctx, |ui| {
 8031|       |            let response = ui.label("anchor");
 8032|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 200.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 8033|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
 8034|       |            found = renderer
 8035|       |                .link_at_pointer(&response, &galley, &build, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0))
 8036|       |                .is_some();
 8037|       |        });
 8038|       |        let _ = ctx.end_frame();
 8039|       |        assert!(!found);
 8040|       |    }

 8042|       |    #[test]
 8043|       |    fn test_spawn_image_loader_missing_file_reports_failed() {
 8044|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(1);
 8045|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = bounded(1);
 8046|       |        MarkdownRenderer::spawn_image_loader(job_rx, result_tx);
 8047|       |
 8048|       |        let dir = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
 8049|       |        let path = dir.path().join("missing.png");
 8050|       |        job_tx
 8051|       |            .send(ImageLoadRequest {
 8052|       |                key: "missing".to_string(),
 8053|       |                source: ImageLoadSource::File(path),
 8054|       |                generation: 0,
 8055|       |            })
 8056|       |            .expect("send");
 8057|       |        let result = result_rx
 8058|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(3))
 8059|       |            .expect("result");
 8060|       |        assert!(matches!(
 8061|       |            result,
 8062|       |            ImageLoadResult::Failed { ref key, .. } if key == "missing"
 8063|       |        ));
 8064|       |    }

 8066|       |    #[test]
 8067|       |    fn test_spawn_image_loader_embedded_invalid_bytes_reports_failed() {
 8068|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(1);
 8069|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = bounded(1);
 8070|       |        MarkdownRenderer::spawn_image_loader(job_rx, result_tx);
 8071|       |
 8072|       |        job_tx
 8073|       |            .send(ImageLoadRequest {
 8074|       |                key: "bad-embedded".to_string(),
 8075|       |                source: ImageLoadSource::Embedded(b"not an image"),
 8076|       |                generation: 0,
 8077|       |            })
 8078|       |            .expect("send");
 8079|       |        let result = result_rx
 8080|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(8))
 8081|       |            .expect("result");
 8082|       |        assert!(matches!(
 8083|       |            result,
 8084|       |            ImageLoadResult::Failed { ref key, .. } if key == "bad-embedded"
 8085|       |        ));
 8086|       |    }

 8088|       |    #[test]
 8089|       |    fn test_spawn_image_loader_file_invalid_bytes_reports_failed() -> Result<()> {
 8090|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(1);
 8091|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = bounded(1);
 8092|       |        MarkdownRenderer::spawn_image_loader(job_rx, result_tx);
 8093|       |
 8094|       |        let dir = tempdir()?;
 8095|       |        let path = dir.path().join("bad.png");
 8096|       |        std::fs::write(&path, b"not an image")?;
 8097|       |        job_tx
 8098|       |            .send(ImageLoadRequest {
 8099|       |                key: "bad-file".to_string(),
 8100|       |                source: ImageLoadSource::File(path),
 8101|       |                generation: 0,
 8102|       |            })
 8103|       |            .expect("send");
 8104|       |        let result = result_rx
 8105|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(3))
 8106|       |            .expect("result");
 8107|       |        assert!(matches!(
 8108|       |            result,
 8109|       |            ImageLoadResult::Failed { ref key, .. } if key == "bad-file"
 8110|       |        ));
 8111|       |        Ok(())
 8112|       |    }

 8114|       |    #[test]
 8115|       |    fn test_spawn_image_loader_file_read_error_reports_failed() -> Result<()> {
 8116|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(1);
 8117|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = bounded(1);
 8118|       |        MarkdownRenderer::spawn_image_loader(job_rx, result_tx);
 8119|       |
 8120|       |        let dir = tempdir()?;
 8121|       |        let bad_path = dir.path().join("read_error_dir");
 8122|       |        std::fs::create_dir_all(&bad_path)?;
 8123|       |        job_tx
 8124|       |            .send(ImageLoadRequest {
 8125|       |                key: "read-error".to_string(),
 8126|       |                source: ImageLoadSource::File(bad_path),
 8127|       |                generation: 0,
 8128|       |            })
 8129|       |            .expect("send");
 8130|       |        let result = result_rx
 8131|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(1))
 8132|       |            .expect("result");
 8133|       |        assert!(matches!(
 8134|       |            result,
 8135|       |            ImageLoadResult::Failed { ref key, .. } if key == "read-error"
 8136|       |        ));
 8137|       |        Ok(())
 8138|       |    }
 8139|       |
 8140|       |    #[test]
 8141|       |    fn test_spawn_image_loader_remote_success_reports_loaded() {
 8142|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(1);
 8143|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = bounded(1);
 8144|       |        MarkdownRenderer::spawn_image_loader(job_rx, result_tx);
 8145|       |
 8146|       |        let (url, handle) =
 8147|       |            spawn_http_response_server("HTTP/1.1 200 OK", "image/png", tiny_png_bytes());
 8148|       |        job_tx
 8149|       |            .send(ImageLoadRequest {
 8150|       |                key: "remote-ok".to_string(),
 8151|       |                source: ImageLoadSource::Remote(url),
 8152|       |                generation: 7,
 8153|       |            })
 8154|       |            .expect("send");
 8155|       |
 8156|       |        let result = result_rx
 8157|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))
 8158|       |            .expect("result");
 8159|       |        handle.join().expect("server join");
 8160|       |
 8161|       |        match result {
 8162|       |            ImageLoadResult::Loaded {
 8163|       |                key,
 8164|       |                size,
 8165|       |                modified,
 8166|       |                generation,
 8167|       |                ..
 8168|       |            } => {
 8169|       |                assert_eq!(key, "remote-ok");
 8170|       |                assert_eq!(size, [2, 2]);
 8171|       |                assert_eq!(generation, 7);
 8172|       |                assert!(modified.is_none());
 8173|       |            }
 8174|       |            _ => panic!("expected loaded result"),
 8175|       |        }
 8176|       |    }
 8177|       |

 8248|       |    fn test_fence_helpers_cover_branches() {
 8249|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownRenderer::fence_start("```"), Some(('`', 3)));
 8250|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownRenderer::fence_start("``"), None);
 8251|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownRenderer::fence_start("~~~"), Some(('~', 3)));
 8252|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::is_fence_end("```", '`', 3));
 8253|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::is_fence_end("``", '`', 3));
 8254|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::is_fence_end("   ```", '`', 3));
 8255|       |    }

 8304|       |            "recent".to_string(),
 8305|       |            ImageFailure {
 8306|       |                last_attempt: std::time::Instant::now(),
 8307|       |            },
 8308|       |        );
 8309|       |        assert!(!renderer.should_retry_image("recent"));
 8310|       |

 8312|       |            "old".to_string(),

 8314|       |                last_attempt: std::time::Instant::now()
 8315|       |                    - IMAGE_FAILURE_BACKOFF

 8317|       |            },

 8323|       |    fn test_extend_table_rect_accepts_valid_rect() {
 8324|       |        let mut target = None;
 8325|       |        let rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::vec2(3.0, 4.0));
 8326|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, rect);
 8327|       |        assert_eq!(target, Some(rect));
 8328|       |

 8330|       |        MarkdownRenderer::extend_table_rect(&mut target, rect2);
 8331|       |        let merged = target.expect("merged rect");
 8332|       |        assert!(merged.contains(rect.min));
 8333|       |        assert!(merged.contains(rect2.max));
 8334|       |    }

 8381|       |    #[test]
 8382|       |    fn test_superscript_expansion_single_caret() {
 8383|       |        // Test that single carets (not paired) are left as-is
 8384|       |        let s = "2^32 = 4,294,967,296";
 8385|       |        let out = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(s);
 8386|       |        assert_eq!(out, "2^32 = 4,294,967,296");
 8387|       |
 8388|       |        // Test the problematic line from the bug report
 8389|       |        let problematic = "A 32-bit address bus would provide 2^32 = 4,294,967,296 bytes (4 GB) of addressable memory.";
 8390|       |        let fixed = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(problematic);
 8391|       |        assert_eq!(fixed, problematic);
 8392|       |
 8393|       |        // Test mixed cases
 8394|       |        let mixed = "Use 2^32 for math and 5^th^ for ordinal";
 8395|       |        let result = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(mixed);
 8396|       |        assert!(result.contains("2^32"));
 8397|       |        assert!(result.contains("5??"));
 8398|       |    }

 8404|       |        assert_eq!(out, s);
 8405|       |        let no_close = "10^abc";
 8406|       |        let out = MarkdownRenderer::expand_superscripts(no_close);
 8407|       |        assert_eq!(out, no_close);

 8419|       |    fn test_zoom_functionality() {
 8420|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8421|       |        let original_body = renderer.font_sizes.body;
 8422|       |
 8423|       |        renderer.zoom_in();
 8424|       |        assert!(renderer.font_sizes.body > original_body);
 8425|       |
 8426|       |        renderer.zoom_out();
 8427|       |        assert!(renderer.font_sizes.body < original_body * 1.1);
 8428|       |
 8429|       |        renderer.reset_zoom();
 8430|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.font_sizes.body, original_body);

 8434|       |    fn test_set_zoom_scale() {

 8438|       |        assert!((renderer.font_sizes.body - 21.0).abs() < 0.1);
 8439|       |
 8440|       |        renderer.set_zoom_scale(10.0);
 8441|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.font_sizes.body, 32.0);

 8446|       |
 8447|       |    #[test]
 8448|       |    fn font_size_change_does_not_repersist_stale_widths() {
 8449|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8450|       |        let table_id = 7u64;
 8451|       |        let specs = vec![resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0)];
 8452|       |        let widths = vec![100.0f32];
 8453|       |
 8454|       |        // Initial persist at default font size.
 8455|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
 8456|       |        let policy_hash = specs[0].policy_hash;
 8457|       |        {
 8458|       |            let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8459|       |            let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
 8460|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), Some(100.0));
 8461|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(renderer.font_sizes.body));
 8462|       |        }
 8463|       |
 8464|       |        // Simulate zoom: font size changes but widths captured are still the old ones.
 8465|       |        renderer.font_sizes.body = 16.0;
 8466|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
 8467|       |        {
 8468|       |            let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8469|       |            let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry exists");
 8470|       |            // Cleared and NOT re-saved in the same frame.
 8471|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), None);
 8472|       |            assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(16.0));
 8473|       |        }
 8474|       |
 8475|       |        // Next frame with new layout widths should persist again.
 8476|       |        let new_widths = vec![80.0f32];
 8477|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &new_widths);
 8478|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8479|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry exists");
 8480|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), Some(80.0));
 8481|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_font_size, Some(16.0));
 8482|       |    }
 8483|       |
 8484|       |    #[test]
 8485|       |    fn highlight_phrase_keeps_persisted_table_widths() {
 8486|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8487|       |        let table_id = 11u64;
 8488|       |        let specs = vec![resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0)];
 8489|       |        let widths = vec![120.0f32];
 8490|       |        let policy_hash = specs[0].policy_hash;
 8491|       |
 8492|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
 8493|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("alpha"));
 8494|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("beta"));
 8495|       |
 8496|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
 8497|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry exists");
 8498|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(policy_hash), Some(120.0));
 8499|       |    }
 8500|       |
 8501|       |    #[test]
 8502|       |    fn test_tight_list_inline_code_and_styles() {
 8503|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8504|       |        let md = "Intro\n\n- Use `code` and **bold** and *italic* and ~~strike~~\n";
 8505|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8506|       |        let (ordered, items) = parsed
 8507|       |            .iter()
 8508|       |            .find_map(|el| {
 8509|       |                if let MarkdownElement::List { ordered, items } = el {
 8510|       |                    Some((*ordered, items))
 8511|       |                } else {
 8512|       |                    None
 8513|       |                }
 8514|       |            })
 8515|       |            .expect("list element");
 8516|       |        assert!(!ordered);
 8517|       |        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
 8518|       |        let mut blocks = Vec::new();
 8519|       |        blocks.push(MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule);
 8520|       |        blocks.extend(items[0].blocks.clone());
 8521|       |        let spans = blocks
 8522|       |            .iter()
 8523|       |            .find_map(|block| {
 8524|       |                if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = block {
 8525|       |                    Some(spans)
 8526|       |                } else {
 8527|       |                    None
 8528|       |                }
 8529|       |            })
 8530|       |            .expect("paragraph block");
 8531|       |        let mut saw_code = false;
 8532|       |        let mut saw_strong = false;
 8533|       |        let mut saw_emphasis = false;
 8534|       |        let mut saw_strike = false;
 8535|       |        let mut saw_other = false;
 8536|       |        for span in spans {
 8537|       |            match span {
 8538|       |                InlineSpan::Code(_) => saw_code = true,
 8539|       |                InlineSpan::Strong(_) => saw_strong = true,
 8540|       |                InlineSpan::Emphasis(_) => saw_emphasis = true,
 8541|       |                InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_) => saw_strike = true,
 8542|       |                _ => saw_other = true,
 8543|       |            }
 8544|       |        }
 8545|       |        assert!(saw_code);
 8546|       |        assert!(saw_strong);
 8547|       |        assert!(saw_emphasis);
 8548|       |        assert!(saw_strike);
 8549|       |        assert!(saw_other);
 8550|       |    }

 8553|       |    fn test_expand_shortcodes_basic() {

 8561|       |
 8562|       |        assert_eq!(
 8563|       |            MarkdownRenderer::expand_shortcodes("Hello :tada:!"),
 8564|       |            format!("Hello {}!", tada)
 8565|       |        );
 8566|       |    }

 8575|       |    #[test]
 8576|       |    fn test_fix_unicode_chars_normalizes_basic_cases() {
 8577|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8578|       |        let input = "A\u{00A0}B\u{2013}C";
 8579|       |        let normalized = renderer.normalize_text_for_test(input);
 8580|       |        assert_eq!(normalized, "A B-C");
 8581|       |
 8582|       |        let arrows = renderer.normalize_text_for_test("? ? ? ?");
 8583|       |        assert_eq!(arrows, "<- -> ^ v");
 8584|       |
 8585|       |        let untouched = renderer.normalize_text_for_test("Plain text");
 8586|       |        assert_eq!(untouched, "Plain text");
 8587|       |    }

 8590|       |    fn test_elements_to_plain_text_basic() {

 8592|       |            MarkdownElement::Header {
 8593|       |                level: 1,
 8594|       |                spans: vec![InlineSpan::Text("Test Header".to_string())],
 8595|       |                id: "test-header".to_string(),
 8596|       |            },
 8597|       |            MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![
 8598|       |                InlineSpan::Text("This is a ".to_string()),
 8599|       |                InlineSpan::Strong("bold".to_string()),
 8600|       |                InlineSpan::Text(" paragraph.".to_string()),
 8601|       |            ]),
 8602|       |        ];
 8603|       |

 8609|       |    #[test]
 8610|       |    fn test_elements_to_plain_text_code_block() {
 8611|       |        let elements = vec![MarkdownElement::CodeBlock {
 8612|       |            language: Some("rust".to_string()),
 8613|       |            text: "fn main() {\n    println!(\"Hello\");\n}".to_string(),
 8614|       |            highlighted: None,
 8615|       |        }];
 8616|       |
 8617|       |        let plain_text = MarkdownRenderer::elements_to_plain_text(&elements);
 8618|       |        assert_eq!(plain_text, "fn main() {\n    println!(\"Hello\");\n}");
 8619|       |    }

 8647|       |    fn test_elements_to_plain_text_mixed_blocks_adds_newlines() {
 8648|       |        let elements = vec![
 8649|       |            MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("First".to_string())]),
 8650|       |            MarkdownElement::List {
 8651|       |                ordered: false,
 8652|       |                items: vec![ListItem {
 8653|       |                    blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
 8654|       |                        "List".to_string(),
 8655|       |                    )])],
 8656|       |                }],
 8657|       |            },
 8658|       |            MarkdownElement::Quote {
 8659|       |                depth: 1,
 8660|       |                blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
 8661|       |                    "Quote".to_string(),
 8662|       |                )])],
 8663|       |            },
 8664|       |            MarkdownElement::Table {
 8665|       |                headers: vec![
 8666|       |                    vec![InlineSpan::Text("H1".to_string())],
 8667|       |                    vec![InlineSpan::Text("H2".to_string())],
 8668|       |                ],
 8669|       |                rows: vec![vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("R1".to_string())]]],
 8670|       |                alignments: vec![Alignment::Left, Alignment::Left],
 8671|       |            },
 8672|       |            MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule,
 8673|       |        ];
 8674|       |        let plain_text = MarkdownRenderer::elements_to_plain_text(&elements);
 8675|       |        assert_eq!(plain_text, "First\nList\nQuote\nH1\nH2\nR1\n---\n");
 8676|       |    }

 8758|       |
 8759|       |    #[test]
 8760|       |    fn test_image_source_stale_detects_file_changes() {
 8761|       |        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().expect("temp dir");
 8762|       |        let file_path = dir.path().join("image.bin");
 8763|       |        std::fs::write(&file_path, [1u8, 2, 3, 4]).expect("write image");
 8764|       |        let initial = MarkdownRenderer::disk_image_timestamp(&file_path);
 8765|       |
 8766|       |        // Unchanged file ? not stale
 8767|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(initial, &file_path));
 8768|       |
 8769|       |        // Deleted file ? NOT stale (keep cached texture; avoids flashing when
 8770|       |        // antivirus or filesystem transiently locks the file)
 8771|       |        std::fs::remove_file(&file_path).expect("remove image");
 8772|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(initial, &file_path));
 8773|       |
 8774|       |        // File without cached timestamp appears ? stale (pick it up)
 8775|       |        std::fs::write(&file_path, [4u8, 3, 2, 1]).expect("rewrite image");
 8776|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(None, &file_path));
 8777|       |
 8778|       |        // A timestamp far in the past ? stale (beyond tolerance)
 8779|       |        let old = Some(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH);
 8780|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale(old, &file_path));
 8781|       |    }
 8782|       |
 8783|       |    #[test]
 8784|       |    fn test_disk_image_timestamp_missing_returns_none() {
 8785|       |        let missing = std::path::Path::new("missing_image_timestamp.bin");
 8786|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::disk_image_timestamp(missing).is_none());
 8787|       |    }
 8788|       |
 8789|       |    #[test]
 8790|       |    fn test_image_source_stale_with_timestamp_combinations() {
 8791|       |        let stamp = SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH;
 8792|       |        // Had cache, metadata now fails ? NOT stale (transient failure)
 8793|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8794|       |            Some(stamp),
 8795|       |            None
 8796|       |        ));
 8797|       |        // No cache, file appeared ? stale (pick it up)
 8798|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8799|       |            None,
 8800|       |            Some(stamp)
 8801|       |        ));
 8802|       |        // Both None ? not stale
 8803|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8804|       |            None, None
 8805|       |        ));
 8806|       |        // Same timestamp ? not stale
 8807|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8808|       |            Some(stamp),
 8809|       |            Some(stamp)
 8810|       |        ));
 8811|       |        // Within tolerance ? not stale
 8812|       |        let nearby = stamp + Duration::from_secs(5);
 8813|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8814|       |            Some(stamp),
 8815|       |            Some(nearby)
 8816|       |        ));
 8817|       |        assert!(!MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8818|       |            Some(nearby),
 8819|       |            Some(stamp)
 8820|       |        ));
 8821|       |        // Beyond tolerance ? stale
 8822|       |        let far = stamp + IMAGE_TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE + Duration::from_secs(1);
 8823|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8824|       |            Some(stamp),
 8825|       |            Some(far)
 8826|       |        ));
 8827|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::image_source_stale_with_timestamp(
 8828|       |            Some(far),
 8829|       |            Some(stamp)
 8830|       |        ));
 8831|       |    }
 8832|       |
 8833|       |    #[test]
 8834|       |    fn test_inline_code_preserves_whitespace() {
 8835|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8836|       |        let md = "Start `code` end";
 8837|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8838|       |        let non_paragraph = MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule;
 8839|       |        let mut saw_paragraph = false;
 8840|       |        for element in [&parsed[0], &non_paragraph] {
 8841|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
 8842|       |                let code_span = spans.iter().find_map(|span| match span {
 8843|       |                    InlineSpan::Code(t) => Some(t),
 8844|       |                    _ => None,
 8845|       |                });
 8846|       |                assert_eq!(code_span, Some(&"code".to_string()));
 8847|       |                saw_paragraph = true;
 8848|       |            }
 8849|       |        }
 8850|       |        assert!(saw_paragraph);
 8851|       |    }

 8854|       |    fn test_footnote_markers_render_as_visible_text() {
 8855|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8856|       |        let md = "Paragraph with footnote[^1].\n\n[^1]: footnote body.";
 8857|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8858|       |        assert_eq!(parsed.len(), 2);
 8859|       |        let first_plain = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&parsed[0]);
 8860|       |        assert!(first_plain.contains("footnote[^1]"));
 8861|       |        let second_plain = MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&parsed[1]);
 8862|       |        assert!(second_plain.contains("[^1]: footnote body."));
 8863|       |    }

 8865|       |    #[test]
 8866|       |    fn test_parse_headers_assign_ids_and_dedupe() {
 8867|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8868|       |        let md = "# Getting Started\n\nParagraph text.\n\n## Getting Started\n\n### API & Usage\n\n## API & Usage\n";
 8869|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8870|       |
 8871|       |        let mut ids = vec![];
 8872|       |        for el in parsed {
 8873|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Header { id, .. } = el {
 8874|       |                ids.push(id);
 8875|       |            }
 8876|       |        }
 8877|       |        assert_eq!(ids.len(), 4);
 8878|       |        assert_eq!(ids[0], "getting-started");
 8879|       |        assert_eq!(ids[1], "getting-started-1");
 8880|       |        assert_eq!(ids[2], "api-usage");
 8881|       |        assert_eq!(ids[3], "api-usage-1");
 8882|       |    }

 8884|       |    #[test]
 8885|       |    fn test_formatting_sample_contains_expected_header_ids() {
 8886|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8887|       |        let formatting = SAMPLE_FILES
 8888|       |            .iter()
 8889|       |            .find(|f| f.name == "formatting.md")
 8890|       |            .expect("formatting sample present");
 8891|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(formatting.content).expect("parse ok");
 8892|       |        let ids: Vec<String> = parsed
 8893|       |            .into_iter()
 8894|       |            .filter_map(|el| match el {
 8895|       |                MarkdownElement::Header { id, .. } => Some(id),
 8896|       |                _ => None,
 8897|       |            })
 8898|       |            .collect();
 8899|       |
 8900|       |        for expected in [
 8901|       |            "markdown-formatting-guide",
 8902|       |            "table-of-contents",
 8903|       |            "text-formatting",
 8904|       |            "headers",
 8905|       |            "lists",
 8906|       |            "links-and-images",
 8907|       |            "emojis",
 8908|       |            "blockquotes",
 8909|       |            "horizontal-rules",
 8910|       |            "tables",
 8911|       |        ] {
 8912|       |            assert!(ids.iter().any(|id| id == expected));
 8913|       |        }
 8914|       |    }

 8916|       |    #[test]
 8917|       |    fn test_inline_image_parsing() {
 8918|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8919|       |        let md = "Here is an image: ![Alt text](images/pic.webp \"Title\") end.";
 8920|       |        let parsed = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 8921|       |        assert_eq!(parsed.len(), 1);
 8922|       |        assert!(matches!(&parsed[0], MarkdownElement::Paragraph(_)));
 8923|       |        let non_paragraph = MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule;
 8924|       |        let mut saw_paragraph = false;
 8925|       |        for element in [&parsed[0], &non_paragraph] {
 8926|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
 8927|       |                let img = spans.iter().find(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Image { .. }));
 8928|       |                assert!(img.is_some());
 8929|       |                let (src, alt, title) = image_span_fields(img.unwrap()).expect("image span");
 8930|       |                assert_eq!(src, "images/pic.webp");
 8931|       |                assert_eq!(alt, "Alt text");
 8932|       |                assert_eq!(title, Some("Title"));
 8933|       |                saw_paragraph = true;
 8934|       |            }
 8935|       |        }
 8936|       |        assert!(saw_paragraph);
 8937|       |        assert!(image_span_fields(&InlineSpan::Text("nope".into())).is_none());
 8938|       |    }

 8972|       |    #[test]
 8973|       |    fn test_cell_fragments_split_text_and_images() {
 8974|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 8975|       |        let spans = vec![
 8976|       |            InlineSpan::Text("alpha".into()),
 8977|       |            InlineSpan::Strong("beta".into()),
 8978|       |            InlineSpan::Image {
 8979|       |                src: "img.png".into(),
 8980|       |                alt: "img".into(),
 8981|       |                title: None,
 8982|       |            },
 8983|       |            InlineSpan::Text("gamma".into()),
 8984|       |        ];
 8985|       |        let fragments = renderer.cell_fragments(&spans);
 8986|       |        assert_eq!(fragments.len(), 3);
 8987|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[0], CellFragment::Text(_)));
 8988|       |        let mut saw_first_text = false;
 8989|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[0], &fragments[1]] {
 8990|       |            if let CellFragment::Text(slice) = fragment {
 8991|       |                assert_eq!(slice.len(), 2);
 8992|       |                saw_first_text = true;
 8993|       |            }
 8994|       |        }
 8995|       |        assert!(saw_first_text);
 8996|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[1], CellFragment::Image(_)));
 8997|       |        let mut saw_image = false;
 8998|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[1], &fragments[0]] {
 8999|       |            if let CellFragment::Image(span) = fragment {
 9000|       |                let (src, _alt, _title) = image_span_fields(span).expect("image span");
 9001|       |                assert_eq!(src, "img.png");
 9002|       |                saw_image = true;
 9003|       |            }
 9004|       |        }
 9005|       |        assert!(saw_image);
 9006|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[2], CellFragment::Text(_)));
 9007|       |        let mut saw_last_text = false;
 9008|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[2], &fragments[1]] {
 9009|       |            if let CellFragment::Text(slice) = fragment {
 9010|       |                assert_eq!(slice.len(), 1);
 9011|       |                let content = text_span_content(&slice[0]).expect("text span");
 9012|       |                assert_eq!(content, "gamma");
 9013|       |                saw_last_text = true;
 9014|       |            }
 9015|       |        }
 9016|       |        assert!(saw_last_text);
 9017|       |        assert!(text_span_content(&InlineSpan::Image {
 9018|       |            src: "img.png".into(),
 9019|       |            alt: "img".into(),
 9020|       |            title: None,
 9021|       |        })
 9022|       |        .is_none());
 9023|       |    }

 9093|       |    #[test]

 9097|       |        force_emoji_decode_error_once();

 9100|       |        });
 9101|       |    }
