 9103|       |    #[test]
 9104|       |    fn test_get_or_make_emoji_texture_generates_on_decode_error() {
 9105|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9106|       |        let fallback = [
 9107|       |            "\u{2705}",
 9108|       |            "\u{1f389}",
 9109|       |            "\u{1f680}",
 9110|       |            "\u{2764}",
 9111|       |            "\u{1f496}",
 9112|       |            "\u{2b50}",
 9113|       |            "\u{1f525}",
 9114|       |        ];
 9115|       |        let emoji = crate::emoji_catalog::shortcode_map()
 9116|       |            .values()
 9117|       |            .find(|emoji| {
 9118|       |                !fallback.contains(emoji) && emoji_catalog::image_bytes_for(emoji).is_some()
 9119|       |            })
 9120|       |            .expect("emoji outside fallback");
 9121|       |        assert!(emoji_assets::make_image(emoji, 64).is_none());
 9122|       |        force_emoji_decode_error_once();
 9123|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
 9124|       |            let _ = renderer.get_or_make_emoji_texture(ui, emoji);
 9125|       |        });
 9126|       |    }

 9128|       |    #[test]
 9129|       |    fn test_cell_fragments_inline_emoji_stays_text() {
 9130|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9131|       |        let rocket = rocket_emoji();
 9132|       |        let spans = vec![
 9133|       |            InlineSpan::Text((*rocket).to_string()),
 9134|       |            InlineSpan::Text("tail".into()),
 9135|       |        ];
 9136|       |        let fragments = renderer.cell_fragments(&spans);
 9137|       |        assert_eq!(fragments.len(), 1);
 9138|       |        assert!(matches!(fragments[0], CellFragment::Text(_)));
 9139|       |        let dummy_span = InlineSpan::Image {
 9140|       |            src: "dummy.png".into(),
 9141|       |            alt: "dummy".into(),
 9142|       |            title: None,
 9143|       |        };
 9144|       |        let dummy_fragment = CellFragment::Image(&dummy_span);
 9145|       |        let mut saw_text = false;
 9146|       |        for fragment in [&fragments[0], &dummy_fragment] {
 9147|       |            if let CellFragment::Text(slice) = fragment {
 9148|       |                assert_eq!(slice.len(), 2);
 9149|       |                saw_text = true;
 9150|       |            }
 9151|       |        }
 9152|       |        assert!(saw_text);
 9153|       |    }

 9208|       |    fn test_layout_job_builder_highlights_matches() {
 9209|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9210|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("wrap"));
 9211|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("wrap me, wrap me again".into())];
 9212|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
 9213|       |        let build = renderer.build_layout_job(&style, &spans, 200.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 9214|       |        let highlight_bg = style.visuals.selection.bg_fill;
 9215|       |        assert!(build
 9216|       |            .job

 9222|       |    #[test]
 9223|       |    fn test_layout_job_builder_tracks_link_ranges() {
 9224|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9225|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
 9226|       |            text: "Docs".into(),
 9227|       |            url: "https://example.org/docs".into(),
 9228|       |        }];
 9229|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
 9230|       |        let build = renderer.build_layout_job(&style, &spans, 220.0, false, Align::LEFT);
 9231|       |        assert_eq!(build.link_ranges.len(), 1);
 9232|       |        let link = &build.link_ranges[0];
 9233|       |        assert_eq!(link.url, "https://example.org/docs");
 9234|       |        let char_len = link.char_range.end - link.char_range.start;
 9235|       |        let linked_text: String = build
 9236|       |            .job
 9237|       |            .text
 9238|       |            .chars()
 9239|       |            .skip(link.char_range.start)
 9240|       |            .take(char_len)
 9241|       |            .collect();
 9242|       |        assert_eq!(linked_text, "Docs");
 9243|       |    }

 9272|       |    fn test_append_spans_empty_and_code_light() {

 9275|       |        let mut job = LayoutJob::default();

 9279|       |            renderer.append_plain_span(
 9280|       |                &style,
 9281|       |                &mut job,
 9282|       |                &mut plain_text,

 9285|       |                None
 9286|       |            ),
 9287|       |            0
 9288|       |        );

 9290|       |            renderer.append_code_span(&style, &mut job, &mut plain_text, ""),
 9291|       |            0
 9292|       |        );

 9295|       |        light_style.visuals.dark_mode = false;
 9296|       |        assert!(renderer.append_code_span(&light_style, &mut job, &mut plain_text, "code") > 0);
 9297|       |

 9301|       |            renderer.append_text_sections(
 9302|       |                &style,
 9303|       |                &mut empty_job,
 9304|       |                &mut empty_plain,
 9305|       |                "",
 9306|       |                12.0,
 9307|       |                InlineStyle::default(),
 9308|       |                None
 9309|       |            ),
 9310|       |            0
 9311|       |        );
 9312|       |    }

 9367|       |    #[test]
 9368|       |    fn test_table_width_solver_keeps_short_columns_readable() {
 9369|       |        let mins = vec![90.0, 90.0, 90.0, 90.0];
 9370|       |        let desired = vec![120.0, 120.0, 360.0, 160.0];
 9371|       |        let widths = MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(420.0, &mins, &desired);
 9372|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 4);
 9373|       |        // Narrow columns should stay near their minimums even when a wide column exists
 9374|       |        assert!(widths[0] >= 85.0);
 9375|       |        assert!(widths[2] > widths[0]);
 9376|       |        let sum: f32 = widths.iter().sum();
 9377|       |        assert!((sum - 420.0).abs() < 0.5);
 9378|       |    }
 9379|       |
 9380|       |    #[test]
 9381|       |    fn test_table_width_solver_handles_constrained_space() {
 9382|       |        let mins = vec![100.0, 100.0, 100.0, 100.0];
 9383|       |        let desired = vec![200.0, 240.0, 360.0, 160.0];
 9384|       |        let widths = MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(260.0, &mins, &desired);
 9385|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 4);
 9386|       |        assert!(widths.iter().all(|w| *w > 0.0));
 9387|       |        let sum: f32 = widths.iter().sum();
 9388|       |        assert!((sum - 260.0).abs() < 0.5);
 9389|       |    }

 9391|       |    #[test]
 9392|       |    fn test_resolve_table_rects_clip_rect_fallbacks() {
 9393|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9394|       |        let widths = vec![12.0];
 9395|       |        let body_clip = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::pos2(3.0, 4.0));
 9396|       |        let (rect, clip) =
 9397|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, None, None, None, Some(body_clip), &widths, 4.0);
 9398|       |        assert_eq!(clip, body_clip);
 9399|       |
 9400|       |        let (rect2, clip2) =
 9401|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, None, None, None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9402|       |        assert_eq!(clip2, rect2);
 9403|       |        assert_eq!(rect2.min, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 9404|       |        assert_eq!(rect2.max, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 9405|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min, egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0));
 9406|       |    }

 9408|       |    #[test]
 9409|       |    fn test_resolve_table_rects_prefers_layout_then_header_then_body() {
 9410|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9411|       |        let widths = vec![10.0, 20.0];
 9412|       |        let layout = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(5.0, 6.0), egui::pos2(25.0, 26.0));
 9413|       |        let header = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(1.0, 2.0), egui::pos2(9.0, 12.0));
 9414|       |        let body = egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(2.0, 3.0), egui::pos2(14.0, 18.0));
 9415|       |
 9416|       |        let (rect, _) = renderer.resolve_table_rects(
 9417|       |            Some(header),
 9418|       |            Some(body),
 9419|       |            Some(layout),
 9420|       |            None,
 9421|       |            None,
 9422|       |            &widths,
 9423|       |            4.0,
 9424|       |        );
 9425|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.x, layout.min.x);
 9426|       |
 9427|       |        let (rect, _) =
 9428|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(Some(header), None, None, None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9429|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.x, header.min.x);
 9430|       |
 9431|       |        let (rect, _) =
 9432|       |            renderer.resolve_table_rects(None, Some(body), None, None, None, &widths, 4.0);
 9433|       |        assert_eq!(rect.min.x, body.min.x);
 9434|       |    }

 9447|       |    #[test]
 9448|       |    fn table_cells_keep_images_and_formatting() {
 9449|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9450|       |        let md = "\
 9451|       |Intro
 9452|       |
 9453|       || H1 | H2 |
 9454|       || --- | --- |
 9455|       || text **extra** ![Alt](img.png) | **bold** and [link](https://example.com) |";
 9456|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9457|       |        let table = elements
 9458|       |            .iter()
 9459|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9460|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
 9461|       |                    headers: _,
 9462|       |                    rows,
 9463|       |                    alignments: _,
 9464|       |                } => Some(rows),
 9465|       |                _ => None,
 9466|       |            })
 9467|       |            .expect("table present");
 9468|       |        assert_eq!(table.len(), 1);
 9469|       |        let row = &table[0];
 9470|       |        let mut saw_image = false;
 9471|       |        let mut saw_text = false;
 9472|       |        for span in &row[0] {
 9473|       |            match span {
 9474|       |                InlineSpan::Image { .. } => saw_image = true,
 9475|       |                InlineSpan::Text(_) => saw_text = true,
 9476|       |                _ => {}
 9477|       |            }
 9478|       |        }
 9479|       |        assert!(saw_image);
 9480|       |        assert!(saw_text);
 9481|       |        let mut saw_strong = false;
 9482|       |        let mut saw_link = false;
 9483|       |        let mut saw_other = false;
 9484|       |        for span in &row[1] {
 9485|       |            match span {
 9486|       |                InlineSpan::Strong(_) => saw_strong = true,
 9487|       |                InlineSpan::Link { .. } => saw_link = true,
 9488|       |                _ => saw_other = true,
 9489|       |            }
 9490|       |        }
 9491|       |        assert!(saw_strong);
 9492|       |        assert!(saw_link);
 9493|       |        assert!(saw_other);
 9494|       |    }
 9495|       |
 9496|       |    #[test]
 9497|       |    fn table_inline_code_keeps_pipes() {
 9498|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9499|       |        let md = "\
 9500|       |Intro
 9501|       |
 9502|       || Col | Notes |
 9503|       || --- | --- |
 9504|       || code | see `a|b|c` |";
 9505|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9506|       |        let rows = elements
 9507|       |            .iter()
 9508|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9509|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
 9510|       |                    headers: _,
 9511|       |                    rows,
 9512|       |                    alignments: _,
 9513|       |                } => Some(rows),
 9514|       |                _ => None,
 9515|       |            })
 9516|       |            .expect("table present");
 9517|       |        let code_text = rows[0][1]
 9518|       |            .iter()
 9519|       |            .find_map(|span| match span {
 9520|       |                InlineSpan::Code(text) => Some(text.as_str()),
 9521|       |                _ => None,
 9522|       |            })
 9523|       |            .expect("code span");
 9524|       |        assert_eq!(code_text, "a|b|c");
 9525|       |    }

 9554|       |    #[test]
 9555|       |    fn blockquote_table_after_paragraph_parses() -> Result<()> {
 9556|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9557|       |        let md = "\
 9558|       |Prelude
 9559|       |
 9560|       |> Intro line
 9561|       |> | Col | Notes |
 9562|       |> | --- | --- |
 9563|       |> | A | B |";
 9564|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md)?;
 9565|       |        let blocks = elements
 9566|       |            .iter()
 9567|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9568|       |                MarkdownElement::Quote { blocks, .. } => Some(blocks),
 9569|       |                _ => None,
 9570|       |            })
 9571|       |            .expect("quote block");
 9572|       |        assert!(blocks
 9573|       |            .iter()
 9574|       |            .any(|block| matches!(block, MarkdownElement::Table { .. })));
 9575|       |        Ok(())
 9576|       |    }
 9577|       |
 9578|       |    #[test]
 9579|       |    fn blockquote_list_table_after_paragraph_parses() -> Result<()> {
 9580|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9581|       |        let md = "\
 9582|       |Prelude
 9583|       |
 9584|       |> Note before list
 9585|       |> - Item:
 9586|       |>   | Key | Val |
 9587|       |>   | --- | --- |
 9588|       |>   | A | 1 |";
 9589|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md)?;
 9590|       |        let blocks = elements
 9591|       |            .iter()
 9592|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
 9593|       |                MarkdownElement::Quote { blocks, .. } => Some(blocks),
 9594|       |                _ => None,
 9595|       |            })
 9596|       |            .expect("quote block");
 9597|       |        let list = blocks
 9598|       |            .iter()
 9599|       |            .find_map(|block| match block {
 9600|       |                MarkdownElement::List { items, .. } => Some(items),
 9601|       |                _ => None,
 9602|       |            })
 9603|       |            .expect("list block");
 9604|       |        let first = list.first().expect("list item");
 9605|       |        assert!(first
 9606|       |            .blocks
 9607|       |            .iter()
 9608|       |            .any(|block| matches!(block, MarkdownElement::Table { .. })));
 9609|       |        Ok(())
 9610|       |    }
 9611|       |

 9969|       |    #[test]
 9970|       |    fn table_ids_are_unique_per_position() {
 9971|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
 9972|       |        let md = "\
 9973|       |Intro
 9974|       |
 9975|       || H |
 9976|       || - |
 9977|       || a |
 9978|       |
 9979|       || H |
 9980|       || - |
 9981|       || a |";
 9982|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
 9983|       |        let tables: Vec<_> = elements
 9984|       |            .iter()
 9985|       |            .enumerate()
 9986|       |            .filter_map(|(idx, el)| match el {
 9987|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
 9988|       |                    headers,
 9989|       |                    rows,
 9990|       |                    alignments,
 9991|       |                } => Some(renderer.compute_table_id(headers, rows, alignments, idx as u64)),
 9992|       |                _ => None,
 9993|       |            })
 9994|       |            .collect();
 9995|       |        assert_eq!(tables.len(), 2);
 9996|       |        assert_ne!(tables[0], tables[1]);
 9997|       |    }

 9999|       |    #[test]
10000|       |    fn test_render_to_ui_rich_document_populates_rects_and_table_stats() {
10001|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10002|       |        let md = "\
10003|       |# Coverage Demo
10004|       |
10005|       |Paragraph with :tada: emoji and **bold** text and a [link](#target).
10006|       |
10007|       |> Quote line one
10008|       |> Quote line two
10009|       |
10010|       |- item one
10011|       |- item two
10012|       |
10013|       |---
10014|       |
10015|       |```rust
10016|       |fn main() {}
10017|       |```
10018|       |
10019|       || Col A | Col B |
10020|       || --- | --- |
10021|       || A1 | B1 |
10022|       || A2 | B2 |
10023|       |
10024|       |![Logo](assets/samples/logo.svg \"Logo\")
10025|       |![Sample](assets/samples/webp_sample.webp \"Sample\")
10026|       |![Missing](missing_test_image.png \"Missing\")
10027|       |![Remote](https://example.com/image.png \"Remote\")
10028|       |";
10029|       |
10030|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
10031|       |        renderer.set_highlight_phrase(Some("item"));
10032|       |
10033|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10034|       |            renderer.render_to_ui(ui, &elements);
10035|       |        });
10036|       |
10037|       |        assert!(renderer.element_rect_at(0).is_some());
10038|       |        assert!(renderer.header_rect_for("coverage-demo").is_some());
10039|       |        let (rendered, total) = renderer.table_render_stats();
10040|       |        assert!(total > 0);
10041|       |        assert!(rendered > 0);
10042|       |        let (_hits, misses) = renderer.table_layout_cache_stats();
10043|       |        assert!(misses > 0);
10044|       |    }
10045|       |
10046|       |    #[test]
10047|       |    fn test_table_layout_cache_records_hits() {
10048|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10049|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("alpha".to_string())];
10050|       |        let style = egui::Style::default();
10051|       |
10052|       |        let _ = renderer.cached_layout_job(&style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);
10053|       |        let _ = renderer.cached_layout_job(&style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);
10054|       |
10055|       |        let (hits, misses) = renderer.table_layout_cache_stats();
10056|       |        assert!(hits >= 1);
10057|       |        assert!(misses >= 1);
10058|       |
10059|       |        renderer.clear_table_layout_cache();
10060|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.table_layout_cache_stats(), (0, 0));
10061|       |    }
10062|       |
10063|       |    #[test]
10064|       |    fn test_cell_layout_cache_eviction() {
10065|       |        let mut cache = CellLayoutCache::new(2);
10066|       |        let build = LayoutJobBuild {
10067|       |            job: LayoutJob::default(),
10068|       |            plain_text: String::new(),
10069|       |            link_ranges: Vec::new(),
10070|       |        };
10071|       |        let key = |row, col| CellLayoutKey {
10072|       |            row: Some(row),
10073|       |            col,
10074|       |            width: 120,
10075|       |            align: 0,
10076|       |            strong: false,
10077|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10078|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10079|       |            content_hash: row as u64,
10080|       |        };
10081|       |
10082|       |        let key_a = key(0, 0);
10083|       |        let key_b = key(1, 0);
10084|       |        let key_c = key(2, 0);
10085|       |
10086|       |        cache.insert(key_a.clone(), build.clone());
10087|       |        cache.insert(key_b.clone(), build.clone());
10088|       |        cache.insert(key_c.clone(), build);
10089|       |
10090|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_a).is_none());
10091|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_b).is_some());
10092|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_c).is_some());
10093|       |    }
10094|       |
10095|       |    #[test]
10096|       |    fn test_cell_layout_cache_handles_empty_order() {
10097|       |        let mut cache = CellLayoutCache::new(1);
10098|       |        let build = LayoutJobBuild {
10099|       |            job: LayoutJob::default(),
10100|       |            plain_text: String::new(),
10101|       |            link_ranges: Vec::new(),
10102|       |        };
10103|       |        let key_a = CellLayoutKey {
10104|       |            row: Some(0),
10105|       |            col: 0,
10106|       |            width: 120,
10107|       |            align: 0,
10108|       |            strong: false,
10109|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10110|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10111|       |            content_hash: 1,
10112|       |        };
10113|       |        let key_b = CellLayoutKey {
10114|       |            row: Some(1),
10115|       |            col: 0,
10116|       |            width: 120,
10117|       |            align: 0,
10118|       |            strong: false,
10119|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10120|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10121|       |            content_hash: 2,
10122|       |        };
10123|       |
10124|       |        cache.insert(key_a, build.clone());
10125|       |        cache.order.clear();
10126|       |        cache.insert(key_b.clone(), build);
10127|       |
10128|       |        assert!(cache.get(&key_b).is_some());
10129|       |    }

10135|       |            job: LayoutJob::default(),

10137|       |            link_ranges: Vec::new(),
10138|       |        };

10140|       |            row: Some(0),
10141|       |            col: 0,
10142|       |            width: 120,
10143|       |            align: 0,
10144|       |            strong: false,
10145|       |            text_color: [0, 0, 0, 255],
10146|       |            highlight_hash: 0,
10147|       |            content_hash: 42,
10148|       |        };
10149|       |        cache.insert(key.clone(), build.clone());
10150|       |        cache.insert(key.clone(), build);

10158|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Text("alpha".to_string())];
10159|       |        let mut light_style = egui::Style::default();

10161|       |        light_style.visuals.override_text_color = Some(Color32::WHITE);

10163|       |

10165|       |            renderer.cached_layout_job(&light_style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);
10166|       |        let _ =
10167|       |            renderer.cached_layout_job(&dark_style, Some(0), 0, &spans, 120.0, false, Align::LEFT);
10168|       |
10169|       |        let (hits, misses) = renderer.table_layout_cache_stats();

10172|       |    }

10178|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md)?;
10179|       |        let alignments = elements
10180|       |            .iter()
10181|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {

10197|       |        let (headers, rows) = elements
10198|       |            .iter()
10199|       |            .find_map(|el| match el {
10200|       |                MarkdownElement::Table { headers, rows, .. } => Some((headers, rows)),
10201|       |                _ => None,
10202|       |            })
10203|       |            .expect("table element");

10209|       |    #[test]
10210|       |    fn test_table_first_column_alignment_parses_three_columns() -> Result<()> {
10211|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10212|       |        let md = fs::read_to_string("tests/regression/cases/026-table-first-column-align.md")?;
10213|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(&md)?;
10214|       |        let tables: Vec<_> = elements
10215|       |            .iter()
10216|       |            .filter_map(|el| match el {
10217|       |                MarkdownElement::Table {
10218|       |                    headers,
10219|       |                    rows,
10220|       |                    alignments,
10221|       |                } => Some((headers, rows, alignments)),
10222|       |                _ => None,
10223|       |            })
10224|       |            .collect();
10225|       |        assert!(tables.len() >= 3);
10226|       |        let (first_headers, first_rows, first_alignments) = tables[0];
10227|       |        for (headers, rows, alignments) in tables.iter() {
10228|       |            assert_eq!(headers.len(), 3);
10229|       |            assert_eq!(alignments.len(), 3);
10230|       |            assert!(rows.iter().all(|row| row.len() == 3));
10231|       |        }
10232|       |        let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(first_headers, first_rows, first_alignments, 0);
10233|       |        let available = Cell::new(0.0f32);
10234|       |        let spacing = Cell::new(0.0f32);
10235|       |        let _estimated_before = {
10236|       |            let column_stats = renderer.column_stats_for_table(
10237|       |                table_id,
10238|       |                first_headers,
10239|       |                first_rows,
10240|       |                first_alignments,
10241|       |            );
10242|       |            let ctx = TableColumnContext::new(
10243|       |                first_headers,
10244|       |                first_rows,
10245|       |                &column_stats,
10246|       |                renderer.font_sizes.body,
10247|       |                table_id,
10248|       |            );
10249|       |            let column_specs = derive_column_specs(&ctx);
10250|       |            let column_spacing = 6.0f32.max(egui::Style::default().spacing.item_spacing.x);
10251|       |            renderer.estimate_table_total_width(table_id, &column_specs, column_spacing)
10252|       |        };
10253|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10254|       |            available.set(ui.available_width());
10255|       |            spacing.set(ui.spacing().item_spacing.x.max(6.0));
10256|       |            renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(
10257|       |                ui,
10258|       |                first_headers,
10259|       |                first_rows,
10260|       |                first_alignments,
10261|       |                table_id,
10262|       |            );
10263|       |        });
10264|       |        let widths = renderer
10265|       |            .table_metrics
10266|       |            .borrow()
10267|       |            .entry(table_id)
10268|       |            .map(|entry| entry.current_widths().to_vec())
10269|       |            .unwrap_or_default();
10270|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 3);
10271|       |        assert!(widths.iter().all(|w| *w > 8.0));
10272|       |        Ok(())
10273|       |    }

10276|       |    fn test_trigger_link_handles_fragments_and_schemes() {
10277|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10278|       |
10279|       |        renderer.trigger_link("#Section-One");
10280|       |        assert_eq!(
10281|       |            renderer.take_pending_anchor(),
10282|       |            Some("section-one".to_string())
10283|       |        );
10284|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10285|       |
10286|       |        renderer.trigger_link("ftp://example.com");
10287|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10288|       |
10289|       |        renderer.trigger_link("https://example.com");
10290|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10291|       |
10292|       |        renderer.trigger_link("mailto:hello@example.com");
10293|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
10294|       |    }

10297|       |    fn test_resolve_image_path_with_base_dir() {
10298|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10299|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
10300|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(Some(temp.path()));
10301|       |
10302|       |        let resolved = renderer.resolve_image_path("sample.png");
10303|       |        assert!(resolved.contains("sample.png"));
10304|       |
10305|       |        let abs = temp.path().join("abs.png");
10306|       |        let abs_str = abs.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
10307|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.resolve_image_path(&abs_str), abs_str);
10308|       |
10309|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(None);
10310|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.resolve_image_path("relative.png"), "relative.png");
10311|       |    }

10314|       |    fn test_disk_image_loads_and_caches() {
10315|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10316|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
10317|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("disk.png");
10318|       |
10319|       |        let mut img = image::RgbaImage::new(2, 2);
10320|       |        for pixel in img.pixels_mut() {
10321|       |            *pixel = image::Rgba([10, 20, 30, 255]);
10322|       |        }
10323|       |        img.save(&image_path).expect("save png");
10324|       |
10325|       |        renderer.set_base_dir(Some(temp.path()));
10326|       |        let resolved = renderer.resolve_image_path("disk.png");
10327|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
10328|       |            // Use wait_for_image to handle async image loading
10329|       |            let loaded = wait_for_image(&renderer, ctx, ui, &resolved);
10330|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some());
10331|       |        });
10332|       |
10333|       |        assert!(renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&resolved));
10334|       |    }

10375|       |            resizable_spec(0, "A", 100.0, 100.0),
10376|       |            resizable_spec(1, "B", 100.0, 100.0),

10385|       |    fn test_estimate_table_row_widths_distributes_remainder() {
10386|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10387|       |        let min_floor = renderer.table_min_column_width();
10388|       |        let specs = vec![
10389|       |            fixed_spec(0, "Ord", 80.0),
10390|       |            ColumnSpec::new(1, "Params", ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: false }, None),
10391|       |        ];
10392|       |        let resolved = vec![80.0, min_floor];
10393|       |        let adjusted = resolved.clone();
10394|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_table_row_widths(
10395|       |            &specs, &resolved, &adjusted, 320.0, min_floor, false, false,
10396|       |        );
10397|       |        assert!((widths[0] - 80.0).abs() < 0.1);
10398|       |        assert!(widths[1] > min_floor + 40.0);
10399|       |    }

10544|       |    fn test_has_pending_renders_with_mermaid_pending() {
10545|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10546|       |        // Give worker threads time to start up under heavy parallel test execution.
10547|       |        // Workers need to initialize QuickJS runtime which can be slow under load.
10548|       |        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(200));
10549|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10550|       |            renderer.mermaid.begin_frame();
10551|       |            renderer
10552|       |                .mermaid
10553|       |                .render_block(ui, "graph TD; A-->B;", 1.0, 14.0, MermaidTheme::Default);
10554|       |        });
10555|       |        assert!(renderer.has_pending_renders());
10556|       |    }

10558|       |    #[test]
10559|       |    fn test_parse_lists_and_blockquotes() {
10560|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10561|       |        let md = "\
10562|       |- Item 1
10563|       |- Item 2
10564|       |  - Nested
10565|       |
10566|       |1. First
10567|       |2. Second
10568|       |
10569|       |> Quote line
10570|       |> > Nested quote
10571|       |
10572|       |---";
10573|       |
10574|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
10575|       |        assert!(elements
10576|       |            .iter()
10577|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::List { ordered: false, .. })));
10578|       |        assert!(elements
10579|       |            .iter()
10580|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::List { ordered: true, .. })));
10581|       |        assert!(elements
10582|       |            .iter()
10583|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::Quote { .. })));
10584|       |        assert!(elements
10585|       |            .iter()
10586|       |            .any(|el| matches!(el, MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule)));
10587|       |    }

10685|       |        ];

10699|       |    fn test_measure_inline_spans_respects_line_breaks() {
10700|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10701|       |        let single_line = vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10702|       |            "Short This is a much longer line".to_string(),
10703|       |        )];
10704|       |        let multi_line = vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10705|       |            "Short\nThis is a much longer line".to_string(),
10706|       |        )];
10707|       |
10708|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10709|       |            let single = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &single_line);
10710|       |            let multi = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &multi_line);
10711|       |            assert!(multi < single);
10712|       |        });
10713|       |    }

10715|       |    #[test]
10716|       |    fn test_measure_inline_spans_normalizes_unicode() {
10717|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10718|       |        let unicode = vec![InlineSpan::Text("A\u{2192}B".to_string())];
10719|       |        let ascii = vec![InlineSpan::Text("A->B".to_string())];
10720|       |
10721|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
10722|       |            let unicode_width = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &unicode);
10723|       |            let ascii_width = renderer.measure_inline_spans(ui, &ascii);
10724|       |            assert!((unicode_width - ascii_width).abs() < 0.5);
10725|       |        });
10726|       |    }

10729|       |    fn test_to_superscript_full_mapping() {
10730|       |        let input = "0123456789+-=()abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ#";
10731|       |        let out = MarkdownRenderer::to_superscript(input);
10732|       |        assert_eq!(out.chars().count(), input.chars().count());
10733|       |        assert!(out.contains('\u{2070}'));
10734|       |        assert!(out.contains('\u{00b9}'));
10735|       |        assert!(out.contains('\u{1d43}'));
10736|       |        assert!(out.contains('#'));
10737|       |    }

10740|       |    fn test_element_plain_text_variants() {
10741|       |        let list = MarkdownElement::List {
10742|       |            ordered: false,
10743|       |            items: vec![ListItem {
10744|       |                blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10745|       |                    "Item".to_string(),
10746|       |                )])],
10747|       |            }],
10748|       |        };

10750|       |            depth: 1,
10751|       |            blocks: vec![MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text(
10752|       |                "Quote".to_string(),
10753|       |            )])],
10754|       |        };
10755|       |        let table = MarkdownElement::Table {
10756|       |            headers: vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Header".to_string())]],
10757|       |            rows: vec![vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Cell".to_string())]]],
10758|       |            alignments: Vec::new(),
10759|       |        };
10760|       |

10766|       |        );

10768|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::element_plain_text(&table).contains("Cell"));
10769|       |    }

10773|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10774|       |        let events = vec![
10775|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
10776|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10777|       |                "img.png".into(),
10778|       |                "Title".into(),
10779|       |            )),
10780|       |            Event::Text("Alt".into()),
10781|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10782|       |        ];
10783|       |        let mut elements = Vec::new();
10784|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
10785|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&events, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
10786|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10787|       |        assert!(matches!(
10788|       |            elements.first(),
10789|       |            Some(MarkdownElement::Paragraph(_))
10790|       |        ));
10791|       |        let spans = paragraph_spans(elements.first().expect("element")).expect("paragraph spans");
10792|       |        let (_src, _alt, title) = image_span_fields(&spans[0]).expect("image span");
10793|       |        assert_eq!(title, Some("Title"));
10794|       |        assert!(paragraph_spans(&MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule).is_none());
10795|       |    }

10824|       |    #[test]
10825|       |    fn test_parse_list_event_variants() -> Result<()> {
10826|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10827|       |        let events = vec![
10828|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
10829|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
10830|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
10831|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
10832|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
10833|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
10834|       |            Event::Text("strong".into()),
10835|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
10836|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
10837|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
10838|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
10839|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
10840|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10841|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
10842|       |                "".into(),
10843|       |            )),
10844|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
10845|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10846|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
10847|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10848|       |                "img.png".into(),
10849|       |                "Title".into(),
10850|       |            )),
10851|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
10852|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10853|       |            Event::Code("code".into()),
10854|       |            Event::Text("text".into()),
10855|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
10856|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(Some(1))),
10857|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
10858|       |            Event::Text("nested".into()),
10859|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
10860|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(Some(1))),
10861|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
10862|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
10863|       |        ];
10864|       |
10865|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
10866|       |        let (items, next) = renderer.parse_list(&events, 1, &mut slugs);
10867|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10868|       |        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
10869|       |        let item = &items[0];
10870|       |        let mut blocks = Vec::new();
10871|       |        blocks.push(MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule);
10872|       |        blocks.extend(item.blocks.clone());
10873|       |        let spans = blocks
10874|       |            .iter()
10875|       |            .find_map(|block| match block {
10876|       |                MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) => Some(spans),
10877|       |                _ => None,
10878|       |            })
10879|       |            .expect("paragraph block");
10880|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Emphasis(_))));
10881|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Strong(_))));
10882|       |        assert!(spans
10883|       |            .iter()
10884|       |            .any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_))));
10885|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
10886|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
10887|       |        assert!(spans.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Code(_))));
10888|       |        assert!(item
10889|       |            .blocks
10890|       |            .iter()
10891|       |            .any(|block| matches!(block, MarkdownElement::List { ordered: true, .. })));
10892|       |        Ok(())
10893|       |    }

10895|       |    #[test]
10896|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_with_breaks_variants() {
10897|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10898|       |        let events = vec![
10899|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10900|       |            Event::Text("Hello".into()),
10901|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
10902|       |            Event::Code("code".into()),
10903|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
10904|       |            Event::Text("bold".into()),
10905|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
10906|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
10907|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
10908|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
10909|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
10910|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
10911|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
10912|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
10913|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10914|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
10915|       |                "".into(),
10916|       |            )),
10917|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
10918|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10919|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
10920|       |                LinkType::Inline,
10921|       |                "img.png".into(),
10922|       |                "Title".into(),
10923|       |            )),
10924|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
10925|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
10926|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10927|       |        ];
10928|       |
10929|       |        let (spans, next) =
10930|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10931|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10932|       |        let mut saw_code = false;
10933|       |        let mut saw_strong = false;
10934|       |        let mut saw_emphasis = false;
10935|       |        let mut saw_strike = false;
10936|       |        let mut saw_link = false;
10937|       |        let mut saw_image = false;
10938|       |        let mut saw_break_text = false;
10939|       |        for span in &spans {
10940|       |            match span {
10941|       |                InlineSpan::Code(_) => saw_code = true,
10942|       |                InlineSpan::Strong(_) => saw_strong = true,
10943|       |                InlineSpan::Emphasis(_) => saw_emphasis = true,
10944|       |                InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_) => saw_strike = true,
10945|       |                InlineSpan::Link { .. } => saw_link = true,
10946|       |                InlineSpan::Image { .. } => saw_image = true,
10947|       |                InlineSpan::Text(t) if t.contains('\n') => saw_break_text = true,
10948|       |                _ => {}
10949|       |            }
10950|       |        }
10951|       |        assert!(saw_code);
10952|       |        assert!(saw_strong);
10953|       |        assert!(saw_emphasis);
10954|       |        assert!(saw_strike);
10955|       |        assert!(saw_link);
10956|       |        assert!(saw_image);
10957|       |        assert!(saw_break_text);
10958|       |    }
10959|       |
10960|       |    #[test]
10961|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_html_breaks() {
10962|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
10963|       |        let events = vec![
10964|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10965|       |            Event::Text("one".into()),
10966|       |            Event::Html("<br>".into()),
10967|       |            Event::Text("two".into()),
10968|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10969|       |        ];
10970|       |
10971|       |        let (spans, next) =
10972|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10973|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
10974|       |        assert!(spans
10975|       |            .iter()
10976|       |            .any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Text(t) if t == "\n")));
10977|       |
10978|       |        let (spans_no_break, _) = renderer.parse_inline_spans(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph);
10979|       |        let text = MarkdownRenderer::spans_plain_text(&spans_no_break);
10980|       |        assert!(text.contains("one two"));
10981|       |
10982|       |        let attr_events = vec![
10983|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10984|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),
10985|       |            Event::Html("<br class=\"tight\">".into()),
10986|       |            Event::Text("beta".into()),
10987|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
10988|       |        ];
10989|       |        let (spans, next) =
10990|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&attr_events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
10991|       |        assert_eq!(next, attr_events.len());
10992|       |        assert!(spans
10993|       |            .iter()
10994|       |            .any(|s| matches!(s, InlineSpan::Text(t) if t == "\n")));
10995|       |
10996|       |        let strong_events = vec![
10997|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
10998|       |            Event::Text("lead ".into()),
10999|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
11000|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),
11001|       |            Event::Html("<br>".into()),
11002|       |            Event::Text("beta".into()),
11003|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
11004|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11005|       |        ];
11006|       |        let (_spans, next) =
11007|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&strong_events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
11008|       |        assert_eq!(next, strong_events.len());
11009|       |
11010|       |        let (spans_no_break, _) = renderer.parse_inline_spans(&strong_events, 1, Tag::Paragraph);
11011|       |        let mut saw_non_strong = false;
11012|       |        let mut strong_text = None;
11013|       |        for span in &spans_no_break {
11014|       |            if let InlineSpan::Strong(text) = span {
11015|       |                strong_text = Some(text.as_str());
11016|       |            } else {
11017|       |                saw_non_strong = true;
11018|       |            }
11019|       |        }
11020|       |        assert!(saw_non_strong);
11021|       |        let mut saw_match = false;
11022|       |        let mut saw_miss = false;
11023|       |        let mut saw_none = false;
11024|       |        for candidate in [strong_text, Some("nope"), None] {
11025|       |            if let Some(text) = candidate {
11026|       |                if text.contains("alpha beta") {
11027|       |                    saw_match = true;
11028|       |                } else {
11029|       |                    saw_miss = true;
11030|       |                }
11031|       |            } else {
11032|       |                saw_none = true;
11033|       |            }
11034|       |        }
11035|       |        assert!(saw_match);
11036|       |        assert!(saw_miss);
11037|       |        assert!(saw_none);
11038|       |    }
11039|       |
11040|       |    #[test]
11041|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_soft_break_without_keep() {

11045|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),

11048|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),

11054|       |    }

11056|       |    #[test]
11057|       |    fn test_collect_blockquotes_nested_and_breaks() {
11058|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11059|       |        let events = vec![
11060|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
11061|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
11062|       |            Event::Text("Line1\nLine2".into()),
11063|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11064|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
11065|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
11066|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
11067|       |            Event::Text("Nested".into()),
11068|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11069|       |            Event::End(Tag::BlockQuote),
11070|       |            Event::End(Tag::BlockQuote),
11071|       |        ];
11072|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
11073|       |        let (quotes, next) = renderer.collect_blockquotes(&events, 1, 1, &mut slugs);
11074|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
11075|       |        assert!(quotes.iter().any(|(depth, _)| *depth == 1));
11076|       |        assert!(quotes.iter().any(|(depth, _)| *depth == 2));
11077|       |    }

11239|       |    fn test_parse_element_variants() {
11240|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11241|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
11242|       |        let mut elements = Vec::new();
11243|       |
11244|       |        let paragraph = vec![
11245|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
11246|       |            Event::Text("para".into()),
11247|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11248|       |        ];
11249|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&paragraph, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11250|       |        assert_eq!(next, paragraph.len());
11251|       |
11252|       |        let heading = vec![
11253|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
11254|       |            Event::Text("Heading".into()),
11255|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
11256|       |        ];
11257|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&heading, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11258|       |        assert_eq!(next, heading.len());
11259|       |

11261|       |            Event::Start(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
11262|       |                "rust".into(),
11263|       |            ))),
11264|       |            Event::Text("fn main() {}\n".into()),
11265|       |            Event::End(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
11266|       |                "rust".into(),
11267|       |            ))),
11268|       |        ];
11269|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&code_block, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11270|       |        assert_eq!(next, code_block.len());
11271|       |

11273|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
11274|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
11275|       |            Event::Text("item".into()),
11276|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
11277|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
11278|       |        ];

11280|       |        assert_eq!(next, list.len());
11281|       |

11283|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
11284|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),

11286|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
11287|       |            Event::End(Tag::BlockQuote),
11288|       |        ];
11289|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&quote, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
11290|       |        assert_eq!(next, quote.len());
11291|       |

11293|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Table(vec![Alignment::Left])),

11306|       |            Event::End(Tag::Table(vec![Alignment::Left])),
11307|       |        ];

11310|       |

11313|       |        assert_eq!(next, rule.len());
11314|       |    }

11317|       |    fn test_parse_code_block_with_language() -> Result<()> {
11318|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11319|       |        let elements = renderer.parse("Intro\n\n```rust\nfn main() {}\n```")?;
11320|       |        let mut rust_lang = None;
11321|       |        for element in &elements {
11322|       |            if let MarkdownElement::CodeBlock { language, .. } = element {
11323|       |                rust_lang = language.as_deref();
11324|       |            }
11325|       |        }
11326|       |        assert_eq!(rust_lang, Some("rust"));
11327|       |        Ok(())
11328|       |    }

11375|       |        let spans = vec![
11376|       |            InlineSpan::Text("t".to_string()),
11377|       |            InlineSpan::Code("c".to_string()),
11378|       |            InlineSpan::Strong("s".to_string()),
11379|       |            InlineSpan::Emphasis("e".to_string()),
11380|       |            InlineSpan::Strikethrough("x".to_string()),
11381|       |            InlineSpan::Link {
11382|       |                text: "link".to_string(),
11383|       |                url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
11384|       |            },
11385|       |            InlineSpan::Image {
11386|       |                src: "img.png".to_string(),
11387|       |                alt: "alt".to_string(),
11388|       |                title: Some("title".to_string()),
11389|       |            },
11390|       |        ];
11391|       |        let hash = MarkdownRenderer::hash_inline_spans(&spans);
11392|       |        let hash2 = MarkdownRenderer::hash_inline_spans(&[InlineSpan::Text("other".to_string())]);
11393|       |        assert_ne!(hash, hash2);
11394|       |    }

11397|       |    fn test_highlight_segments_edge_cases() {
11398|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11399|       |        assert!(renderer.highlight_segments("", Some("a")).is_empty());
11400|       |        assert_eq!(
11401|       |            renderer.highlight_segments("abc", None),
11402|       |            vec![(0..3, false)]
11403|       |        );
11404|       |        assert_eq!(
11405|       |            renderer.highlight_segments("abc", Some("")),
11406|       |            vec![(0..3, false)]
11407|       |        );
11408|       |        let segments = renderer.highlight_segments("Hello", Some("ell"));
11409|       |        assert!(segments.iter().any(|(_, highlighted)| *highlighted));
11410|       |    }

11412|       |    #[test]
11413|       |    fn test_resolve_table_widths_branches() {
11414|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(50.0, &[], &[]).is_empty());
11415|       |        assert_eq!(
11416|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(100.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[20.0, 30.0]),
11417|       |            vec![20.0, 30.0]
11418|       |        );
11419|       |
11420|       |        assert_eq!(
11421|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(10.0, &[0.0, 0.0], &[0.0, 0.0]),
11422|       |            vec![5.0, 5.0]
11423|       |        );
11424|       |
11425|       |        assert_eq!(
11426|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(10.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[20.0, 20.0]),
11427|       |            vec![5.0, 5.0]
11428|       |        );
11429|       |
11430|       |        assert_eq!(
11431|       |            MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(30.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[10.0, 10.0]),
11432|       |            vec![15.0, 15.0]
11433|       |        );
11434|       |
11435|       |        let widths = MarkdownRenderer::resolve_table_widths(30.0, &[10.0, 10.0], &[20.0, 30.0]);
11436|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 2);
11437|       |        assert!((widths[0] + widths[1] - 30.0).abs() < 0.1);
11438|       |    }

11478|       |            MarkdownElement::Table {

11480|       |                rows,
11481|       |                alignments,
11482|       |            } => Some((headers, rows, alignments)),
11483|       |            _ => None,

11527|       |    #[test]
11528|       |    fn test_render_list_paragraph_handles_blank_parts_and_indent() {
11529|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11530|       |        let spans = vec![
11531|       |            InlineSpan::Text("Line 1\n\n  indented".to_string()),
11532|       |            InlineSpan::Image {
11533|       |                src: "assets/emoji/1f600.png".to_string(),
11534|       |                alt: "img".to_string(),
11535|       |                title: None,
11536|       |            },
11537|       |        ];

11604|       |                None,

11619|       |            assert!(embedded.is_some());
11620|       |            let remote = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "https://example.com/img.png");

11626|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_remote_allowed_loads() {
11627|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11628|       |        renderer.set_allow_remote_images(true);
11629|       |        let (url, handle) =
11630|       |            spawn_http_response_server("HTTP/1.1 200 OK", "image/png", tiny_png_bytes());
11631|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
11632|       |            let loaded = wait_for_image(&renderer, ctx, ui, &url);
11633|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some());
11634|       |        });
11635|       |        handle.join().expect("server join");
11636|       |        assert!(renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&url));
11637|       |    }

11750|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(

11757|       |            );

11762|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
11763|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),

11766|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 30.0, false, Align::LEFT);

11773|       |    #[test]
11774|       |    fn test_aligned_text_origin_anchors_to_rect() {
11775|       |        let rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(10.0, 20.0), egui::vec2(100.0, 20.0));
11776|       |        let galley = Arc::new(Galley {
11777|       |            job: Arc::new(LayoutJob::default()),
11778|       |            rows: Vec::new(),
11779|       |            elided: false,
11780|       |            rect: egui::Rect::from_min_max(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::pos2(20.0, 10.0)),
11781|       |            mesh_bounds: egui::Rect::NOTHING,
11782|       |            num_vertices: 0,
11783|       |            num_indices: 0,
11784|       |            pixels_per_point: 1.0,
11785|       |        });
11786|       |        let left_origin = MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, Align::LEFT);
11787|       |        let center_origin = MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, Align::Center);
11788|       |        let right_origin = MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, Align::RIGHT);
11789|       |
11790|       |        assert_eq!(left_origin.x, rect.left());
11791|       |        assert_eq!(center_origin.x, rect.center().x - galley.rect.center().x);
11792|       |        assert_eq!(right_origin.x, rect.right() - galley.rect.right());
11793|       |        assert_eq!(left_origin.y, rect.top());
11794|       |    }
11795|       |

11797|       |    fn test_layout_job_center_rect_is_origin() {
11798|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();

11802|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));

11804|       |        });

11855|       |    #[test]
11856|       |    fn test_find_syntax_for_language_direct_name_match() -> Result<()> {
11857|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
11858|       |        let syntax = SyntaxDefinition::load_from_str(
11859|       |            r#"
11860|       |name: testlang
11861|       |scope: source.testlang
11862|       |file_extensions: [tst]
11863|       |contexts:
11864|       |  main:
11865|       |    - match: '.'
11866|       |      scope: source.testlang
11867|       |"#,
11868|       |            true,
11869|       |            None,
11870|       |        )
11871|       |        .expect("syntax");
11872|       |        let mut builder = renderer.syntax_set.clone().into_builder();
11873|       |        builder.add(syntax);
11874|       |        renderer.syntax_set = builder.build();
11875|       |        assert!(renderer.find_syntax_for_language("testlang").is_some());
11876|       |        Ok(())
11877|       |    }

12000|       |    #[test]
12001|       |    fn test_parse_blockquote_with_nested_blocks() {
12002|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12003|       |        let md = r#"> # Quote Heading
12004|       |> Paragraph **bold** *em* ~~strike~~ [link](https://example.com)
12005|       |> ![alt](assets/samples/logo.svg "Logo")
12006|       |> - Item one
12007|       |>   - Nested item
12008|       |> 
12009|       |> ```rust
12010|       |> let x = 1;
12011|       |> ```
12012|       |> 
12013|       |> | H1 | H2 |
12014|       |> | --- | --- |
12015|       |> | ?? | ![a](assets/samples/logo.svg "Img") |
12016|       |"#;
12017|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse");
12018|       |        assert!(elements
12019|       |            .iter()
12020|       |            .any(|e| matches!(e, MarkdownElement::Quote { .. })));
12021|       |    }

12164|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_cache_and_remote() -> Result<()> {
12165|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12166|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
12167|       |            assert!(renderer
12168|       |                .get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "https://example.com/logo.png")
12169|       |                .is_none());
12170|       |        });
12171|       |
12172|       |        let temp = tempdir()?;
12173|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("image.png");
12174|       |        std::fs::write(&image_path, tiny_png_bytes())?;
12175|       |        let resolved = image_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
12176|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
12177|       |            let first = wait_for_image(&renderer, ctx, ui, &resolved);
12178|       |            assert!(first.is_some());
12179|       |            let second = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &resolved);
12180|       |            assert!(second.is_some());
12181|       |        });
12182|       |        Ok(())
12183|       |    }

12185|       |    #[test]
12186|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_stale_cache_evicted() -> Result<()> {
12187|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12188|       |        let temp = tempdir()?;
12189|       |        let image_path = temp.path().join("image.png");
12190|       |        std::fs::write(&image_path, tiny_png_bytes())?;
12191|       |        let resolved = image_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
12192|       |
12193|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
12194|       |            let tex = ctx.load_texture(
12195|       |                "stale-image",
12196|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
12197|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
12198|       |            );
12199|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
12200|       |                resolved.clone(),
12201|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
12202|       |                    texture: tex,
12203|       |                    size: [2, 2],
12204|       |                    modified: Some(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH),
12205|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([2, 2]),
12206|       |                    degraded: false,
12207|       |                },
12208|       |            );
12209|       |
12210|       |            let result = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &resolved);
12211|       |            assert!(result.is_none());
12212|       |            assert!(!renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&resolved));
12213|       |            assert!(renderer.image_pending.borrow().contains(&resolved));
12214|       |        });
12215|       |
12216|       |        Ok(())
12217|       |    }

12241|       |    fn test_enqueue_degraded_reload_remote_enqueues_request() {
12242|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12243|       |        let (tx, rx) = bounded(1);
12244|       |        renderer.image_job_tx = tx;
12245|       |
12246|       |        assert!(renderer.enqueue_degraded_reload(
12247|       |            "http://127.0.0.1/test.png",
12248|       |            "remote-cache",
12249|       |            None,
12250|       |            true,
12251|       |            Path::new("ignored"),
12252|       |        ));
12253|       |
12254|       |        let request = rx.try_recv().expect("request");
12255|       |        assert_eq!(request.key, "remote-cache");
12256|       |        assert!(matches!(
12257|       |            request.source,
12258|       |            ImageLoadSource::Remote(ref url) if url == "http://127.0.0.1/test.png"
12259|       |        ));
12260|       |        assert!(renderer.image_pending.borrow().contains("remote-cache"));
12261|       |    }

12277|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12278|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded::<ImageLoadResult>();
12279|       |        renderer.image_result_rx = result_rx;

12281|       |            .image_pending

12284|       |

12286|       |            .send(ImageLoadResult::Failed {
12287|       |                key: "failed.png".to_string(),
12288|       |                generation: 0,
12289|       |            })
12290|       |            .expect("send");
12291|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
12292|       |        renderer.poll_image_results(&ctx);
12293|       |        assert!(renderer
12294|       |            .image_failures
12295|       |            .borrow()
12296|       |            .contains_key(&"failed.png".to_string()));

12301|       |    fn test_enqueue_image_job_full_returns_err() {
12302|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12303|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(1);
12304|       |        renderer.image_job_tx = job_tx;

12306|       |            key: "full".to_string(),
12307|       |            source: ImageLoadSource::Embedded(b"png"),
12308|       |            generation: 0,
12309|       |        };
12310|       |        renderer.image_job_tx.send(request).expect("fill queue");
12311|       |        let request = ImageLoadRequest {
12312|       |            key: "full-2".to_string(),
12313|       |            source: ImageLoadSource::Embedded(b"png"),
12314|       |            generation: 0,

12319|       |
12320|       |    /// Integration test: solid-color PNGs that are tiny on disk but large when
12321|       |    /// decoded.  Verifies that (a) insert() does not evict by bytes, and
12322|       |    /// (b) enforce_budget() degrades far entries to bring memory under budget.
12323|       |    #[test]
12324|       |    fn test_cache_pressure_with_large_decoded_images() {
12325|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12326|       |
12327|       |        // Each 200x200 image decodes to 200*200*4 = 160,000 bytes.
12328|       |        // Set cache limit to hold exactly 2 images worth (320,000 bytes).
12329|       |        renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().max_bytes = 160_000 * 2;
12330|       |
12331|       |        let temp = tempdir().expect("temp dir");
12332|       |        let mut keys = Vec::new();
12333|       |        for i in 0..4 {
12334|       |            let name = format!("img_{i}.png");
12335|       |            let path = temp.path().join(&name);
12336|       |            std::fs::write(&path, solid_png_bytes(200, 200)).expect("write png");
12337|       |            keys.push(path.to_string_lossy().to_string());
12338|       |        }
12339|       |
12340|       |        // Verify the PNGs are tiny on disk
12341|       |        let disk_size = std::fs::metadata(temp.path().join("img_0.png"))
12342|       |            .unwrap()
12343|       |            .len();
12344|       |        assert!(
12345|       |            disk_size < 2000,
12346|       |            "solid PNG should be tiny on disk, got {disk_size} bytes"
12347|       |        );
12348|       |
12349|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
12350|       |            // Simulate the background loader completing all 4 images.
12351|       |            // insert() no longer evicts by bytes — all 4 should be present.
12352|       |            for key in keys.iter() {
12353|       |                let image = egui::ColorImage::new([200, 200], Color32::WHITE);
12354|       |                let tex =
12355|       |                    ctx.load_texture(format!("img:{key}"), image, egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR);
12356|       |                renderer.store_image_texture(key, tex, [200, 200], None);
12357|       |            }
12358|       |
12359|       |            {
12360|       |                let cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow();
12361|       |                assert_eq!(
12362|       |                    cache.entries.len(),
12363|       |                    4,
12364|       |                    "all 4 images should be present (no byte eviction on insert)"
12365|       |                );
12366|       |                assert_eq!(cache.current_bytes(), 160_000 * 4);
12367|       |            }
12368|       |
12369|       |            // Set viewport distances: keys[0] near, keys[3] far
12370|       |            {
12371|       |                let mut cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut();
12372|       |                let mut distances = HashMap::new();
12373|       |                for (i, key) in keys.iter().enumerate() {
12374|       |                    distances.insert(key.clone(), (i as f32 + 1.0) * 1000.0);
12375|       |                }
12376|       |                cache.update_viewport_distances(distances);
12377|       |            }
12378|       |
12379|       |            // enforce_budget should degrade the furthest entries
12380|       |            {
12381|       |                let mut cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut();
12382|       |                cache.enforce_budget(ctx, 0.0);
12383|       |            }
12384|       |
12385|       |            let cache = renderer.image_textures.borrow();
12386|       |            // All entries should still exist (degraded, not evicted)
12387|       |            for key in &keys {
12388|       |                assert!(cache.contains_key(key), "entry should still exist: {key}");
12389|       |            }
12390|       |            // Memory should be at or under budget
12391|       |            assert!(
12392|       |                cache.current_bytes() <= 160_000 * 2,
12393|       |                "total_bytes should be under budget, got {}",
12394|       |                cache.current_bytes()
12395|       |            );
12396|       |            // Near entry should not be degraded
12397|       |            assert!(
12398|       |                !cache.entries.get(&keys[0]).unwrap().degraded,
12399|       |                "nearest image should not be degraded"
12400|       |            );
12401|       |        });
12402|       |    }
12403|       |
12404|       |    /// Verify that solid PNGs produce the expected compression ratio:
12405|       |    /// small on disk, large decoded.
12406|       |    #[test]
12407|       |    fn test_solid_png_compression_ratio() {
12408|       |        let bytes = solid_png_bytes(500, 500);
12409|       |        // 500x500 decodes to 1,000,000 bytes (1 MB), but the PNG should be
12410|       |        // well under 10 KB on disk due to solid-color compression.
12411|       |        assert!(
12412|       |            bytes.len() < 10_000,
12413|       |            "solid 500x500 PNG should compress well, got {} bytes",
12414|       |            bytes.len()
12415|       |        );
12416|       |        // Verify it decodes to the expected dimensions
12417|       |        let (img, w, h) =
12418|       |            image_decode::bytes_to_color_image_guess(&bytes, None).expect("should decode");
12419|       |        assert_eq!(w, 500);
12420|       |        assert_eq!(h, 500);
12421|       |        assert_eq!(img.pixels.len(), 500 * 500);
12422|       |    }
12423|       |

12503|       |    #[test]
12504|       |    fn test_render_table_tablebuilder_forced_policies() {
12505|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12506|       |        let _forced = ForcedTablePolicies::new(vec![
12507|       |            ColumnPolicy::Auto,
12508|       |            ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
12509|       |                width: 120.0,
12510|       |                clip: true,
12511|       |            },
12512|       |            ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
12513|       |                min: 60.0,
12514|       |                preferred: 120.0,
12515|       |                clip: true,
12516|       |            },
12517|       |            ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: true },
12518|       |        ]);
12519|       |        let headers = vec![
12520|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("A".to_string())],
12521|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("B".to_string())],
12522|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("C".to_string())],
12523|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("D".to_string())],
12524|       |        ];

12526|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("1".to_string())],
12527|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("2".to_string())],
12528|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("3".to_string())],
12529|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("4".to_string())],
12530|       |        ]];
12531|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
12532|       |            let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(&headers, &rows, &[], 14);
12533|       |            renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(ui, &headers, &rows, &[], table_id);
12534|       |        });
12535|       |    }

12624|       |    #[test]
12625|       |    fn test_render_table_estimates_wrapped_row_height() {
12626|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12627|       |        let _forced = ForcedTablePolicies::new(vec![ColumnPolicy::Fixed {
12628|       |            width: 80.0,
12629|       |            clip: false,
12630|       |        }]);
12631|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Params".to_string())]];
12632|       |        let rows = vec![vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text(
12633|       |            "word word word word word word word word".to_string(),
12634|       |        )]]];
12635|       |        let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(&headers, &rows, &[], 18);
12636|       |
12637|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
12638|       |            let layout = *ui.layout();
12639|       |            ui.allocate_ui_with_layout(Vec2::new(120.0, 0.0), layout, |ui| {
12640|       |                ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 6.0;
12641|       |                ui.set_width(120.0);
12642|       |                renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(ui, &headers, &rows, &[], table_id);
12643|       |            });
12644|       |        });
12645|       |
12646|       |        // Check stored row height in metrics
12647|       |        let height = renderer
12648|       |            .table_metrics
12649|       |            .borrow()
12650|       |            .entry(table_id)
12651|       |            .and_then(|e| e.row(0))
12652|       |            .map(|r| r.max_height)
12653|       |            .unwrap_or(0.0);
12654|       |        assert!(height > renderer.row_height_fallback() + 0.5);
12655|       |    }

12661|       |            ListItem {
12662|       |                blocks: vec![
12663|       |                    MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("Paragraph".to_string())]),
12664|       |                    MarkdownElement::Header {
12665|       |                        level: 2,
12666|       |                        spans: vec![InlineSpan::Text("Header".to_string())],
12667|       |                        id: "header".to_string(),
12668|       |                    },
12669|       |                ],
12670|       |            },
12671|       |            ListItem {
12672|       |                blocks: vec![
12673|       |                    MarkdownElement::CodeBlock {
12674|       |                        language: None,
12675|       |                        text: "code".to_string(),
12676|       |                        highlighted: None,
12677|       |                    },
12678|       |                    MarkdownElement::Paragraph(vec![InlineSpan::Text("After".to_string())]),
12679|       |                ],
12680|       |            },
12681|       |            ListItem { blocks: vec![] },
12682|       |        ];
12683|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {

12685|       |            renderer.render_list(ui, true, &items);

12765|       |    #[test]
12766|       |    fn test_list_marker_info_any_indent_variants() {
12767|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("").is_none());
12768|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("   ").is_none());
12769|       |
12770|       |        let (rest, indent, content_indent, leading_cols) =
12771|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent(" 1. item").expect("marker");
12772|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "item");
12773|       |        assert_eq!(indent, 1);
12774|       |        assert_eq!(content_indent, 4);
12775|       |        assert_eq!(leading_cols, 1);
12776|       |
12777|       |        let (rest, indent, content_indent, leading_cols) =
12778|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("2)\titem").expect("marker");
12779|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "item");
12780|       |        assert_eq!(indent, 2);
12781|       |        assert_eq!(content_indent, 4);
12782|       |        assert_eq!(leading_cols, 0);
12783|       |    }
12784|       |

12786|       |    fn test_list_marker_info_any_indent_requires_trailing_space() {
12787|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("-").is_none());
12788|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("   -").is_none());
12789|       |    }

12792|       |    fn test_list_marker_info_any_indent_rejects_missing_whitespace() {
12793|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("1.item").is_none());
12794|       |        assert!(MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent("1)item").is_none());
