12798|       |    #[test]
12799|       |    fn test_collect_until_tag_end_breaks_and_code() {
12800|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12801|       |        let events = vec![
12802|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
12803|       |            Event::Text("alpha".into()),
12804|       |            Event::Code("beta".into()),
12805|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
12806|       |            Event::Html("<br>".into()),
12807|       |            Event::HardBreak,
12808|       |            Event::Html("<span>".into()),
12809|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
12810|       |        ];
12811|       |        let (text, next) = renderer.collect_until_tag_end(&events, 1, Tag::Emphasis, true);
12812|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12813|       |        assert_eq!(text, "alphabeta\n\n\n");
12814|       |
12815|       |        let (text, next) = renderer.collect_until_tag_end(&events, 1, Tag::Emphasis, false);
12816|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12817|       |        assert_eq!(text, "alphabeta   ");
12818|       |
12819|       |        let unclosed = vec![Event::Text("tail".into()), Event::SoftBreak];
12820|       |        let (text, next) = renderer.collect_until_tag_end(&unclosed, 0, Tag::Emphasis, true);
12821|       |        assert_eq!(next, unclosed.len());
12822|       |        assert_eq!(text, "tail\n");
12823|       |    }

12825|       |    #[test]
12826|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_with_breaks_link_image_and_unclosed() {
12827|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12828|       |        let events = vec![
12829|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
12830|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
12831|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12832|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12833|       |                "".into(),
12834|       |            )),
12835|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
12836|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(
12837|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12838|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12839|       |                "".into(),
12840|       |            )),
12841|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
12842|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12843|       |                "img.png".into(),
12844|       |                "Title".into(),
12845|       |            )),
12846|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
12847|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(
12848|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12849|       |                "img.png".into(),
12850|       |                "Title".into(),
12851|       |            )),
12852|       |            Event::FootnoteReference("note".into()),
12853|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
12854|       |        ];
12855|       |        let (spans, next) =
12856|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
12857|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12858|       |        assert!(spans
12859|       |            .iter()
12860|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
12861|       |        assert!(spans
12862|       |            .iter()
12863|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
12864|       |
12865|       |        let unclosed = vec![Event::Text("tail".into())];
12866|       |        let (spans, next) =
12867|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&unclosed, 0, Tag::Paragraph, true);
12868|       |        assert_eq!(next, unclosed.len());
12869|       |        assert_eq!(MarkdownRenderer::spans_plain_text(&spans), "tail");
12870|       |    }

12901|       |    fn test_parse_inline_spans_image_only_skips_text_buffer() {
12902|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12903|       |        let events = vec![
12904|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
12905|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
12906|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
12907|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
12908|       |        ];
12909|       |        let (spans, next) =
12910|       |            renderer.parse_inline_spans_with_breaks(&events, 1, Tag::Paragraph, true);
12911|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12912|       |        assert_eq!(spans.len(), 1);
12913|       |        assert!(matches!(
12914|       |            spans[0],
12915|       |            InlineSpan::Image { ref alt, .. } if alt.is_empty()
12916|       |        ));
12917|       |    }

12920|       |    fn test_parse_list_inline_variants_unclosed() -> Result<()> {
12921|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12922|       |        let events = vec![
12923|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(Some(1))),
12924|       |            Event::Text("skip".into()),
12925|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
12926|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12927|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
12928|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12929|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12930|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
12931|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12932|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
12933|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
12934|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
12935|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strong),
12936|       |            Event::Text("strong".into()),
12937|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strong),
12938|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
12939|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
12940|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
12941|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
12942|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12943|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12944|       |                "".into(),
12945|       |            )),
12946|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
12947|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(
12948|       |                LinkType::Inline,
12949|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
12950|       |                "".into(),
12951|       |            )),
12952|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
12953|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
12954|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
12955|       |            Event::Text("tail".into()),
12956|       |            Event::SoftBreak,
12957|       |            Event::FootnoteReference("note".into()),
12958|       |        ];
12959|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
12960|       |        let (items, next) = renderer.parse_list(&events, 1, &mut slugs);

12962|       |        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
12963|       |        Ok(())
12964|       |    }

12966|       |    #[test]
12967|       |    fn test_parse_list_empty_paragraph_skips_block() -> Result<()> {
12968|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12969|       |        let events = vec![
12970|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(Some(1))),
12971|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
12972|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
12973|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
12974|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
12975|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(Some(1))),
12976|       |        ];
12977|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
12978|       |        let (items, next) = renderer.parse_list(&events, 1, &mut slugs);
12979|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
12980|       |        assert_eq!(items.len(), 1);
12981|       |        assert!(items[0].blocks.is_empty());
12982|       |        Ok(())
12983|       |    }

12986|       |    fn test_collect_blockquotes_unclosed_variants() -> Result<()> {
12987|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
12988|       |        let events = vec![
12989|       |            Event::Start(Tag::BlockQuote),
12990|       |            Event::Text("plain".into()),
12991|       |            Event::Code("code".into()),
12992|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12993|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
12994|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12995|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12996|       |            Event::Text("Dup".into()),
12997|       |            Event::End(Tag::Heading(pulldown_cmark::HeadingLevel::H2, None, vec![])),
12998|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
12999|       |                LinkType::Inline,
13000|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
13001|       |                "".into(),
13002|       |            )),
13003|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
13004|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(
13005|       |                LinkType::Inline,
13006|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
13007|       |                "".into(),
13008|       |            )),
13009|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
13010|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
13011|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
13012|       |            Event::FootnoteReference("note".into()),
13013|       |        ];
13014|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
13015|       |        let (quotes, next) = renderer.collect_blockquotes(&events, 1, 1, &mut slugs);
13016|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
13017|       |        let mut ids = Vec::new();
13018|       |        for (_, blocks) in &quotes {
13019|       |            for block in blocks {
13020|       |                if let MarkdownElement::Header { id, .. } = block {
13021|       |                    ids.push(id.clone());
13022|       |                }
13023|       |            }
13024|       |        }
13025|       |        assert_eq!(ids.len(), 2);
13026|       |        assert_ne!(ids[0], ids[1]);
13027|       |        Ok(())
13028|       |    }

13088|       |    #[test]
13089|       |    fn test_paint_table_text_job_triggers_anchor_on_click() {
13090|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13091|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
13092|       |            text: "Jump".to_string(),
13093|       |            url: "#Section-One".to_string(),
13094|       |        }];
13095|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
13096|       |        let click_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
13097|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
13098|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
13099|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
13100|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
13101|       |                |ui| {
13102|       |                    let build =
13103|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 60.0, false, Align::LEFT);
13104|       |                    let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
13105|       |                    let (rect, _response) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
13106|       |                        egui::vec2(120.0, galley.size().y),
13107|       |                        egui::Sense::click(),
13108|       |                    );
13109|       |                    let text_origin =
13110|       |                        MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
13111|       |                    click_pos.set(text_origin + egui::vec2(2.0, 2.0));
13112|       |                },
13113|       |            );
13114|       |        });
13115|       |
13116|       |        let input = input_with_click(click_pos.get(), egui::PointerButton::Primary);
13117|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
13118|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
13119|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
13120|       |                |ui| {
13121|       |                    let build =
13122|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 60.0, false, Align::LEFT);
13123|       |                    renderer.paint_table_text_job(ui, 120.0, build);
13124|       |                },
13125|       |            );
13126|       |        });
13127|       |
13128|       |        assert_eq!(
13129|       |            renderer.take_pending_anchor(),
13130|       |            Some("section-one".to_string())
13131|       |        );
13132|       |    }

13135|       |    fn test_paint_table_text_job_hover_link_does_not_trigger() {
13136|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13137|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
13138|       |            text: "Jump".to_string(),
13139|       |            url: "#Section-One".to_string(),
13140|       |        }];
13141|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
13142|       |        let hover_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
13143|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
13144|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
13145|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
13146|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
13147|       |                |ui| {
13148|       |                    let build =
13149|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 60.0, false, Align::LEFT);
13150|       |                    let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
13151|       |                    let (rect, _response) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
13152|       |                        egui::vec2(120.0, galley.size().y),
13153|       |                        egui::Sense::click(),
13154|       |                    );
13155|       |                    let text_origin =
13156|       |                        MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
13157|       |                    hover_pos.set(text_origin + egui::vec2(2.0, 2.0));
13158|       |                },
13159|       |            );
13160|       |        });
13161|       |
13162|       |        let mut input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
13163|       |        input
13164|       |            .events
13165|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(hover_pos.get()));
13166|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
13167|       |            ui.allocate_ui_at_rect(
13168|       |                egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(200.0, 80.0)),
13169|       |                |ui| {
13170|       |                    let build =
13171|       |                        renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 60.0, false, Align::LEFT);
13172|       |                    renderer.paint_table_text_job(ui, 120.0, build);
13173|       |                },
13174|       |            );
13175|       |        });
13176|       |
13177|       |        assert!(renderer.take_pending_anchor().is_none());
13178|       |    }

13266|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();

13268|       |        assert_eq!(empty, vec![120.0]);
13269|       |
13270|       |        let specs = vec![fixed_spec(0, "A", 100.0), fixed_spec(1, "B", 100.0)];
13271|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_table_column_widths(&specs, 100.0, 10.0);
13272|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 2);

13285|       |    // -- Stage 1: content-driven natural width tests ----------------------
13286|       |
13287|       |    #[test]
13288|       |    fn test_natural_widths_two_short_columns() {
13289|       |        // Two columns with content wider than policy ? content drives widths
13290|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13291|       |        let headers = vec![
13292|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Name".to_string())],
13293|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Value".to_string())],
13294|       |        ];
13295|       |        let stats = vec![
13296|       |            ColumnStat {
13297|       |                max_graphemes: 20,
13298|       |                ..Default::default()
13299|       |            },
13300|       |            ColumnStat {
13301|       |                max_graphemes: 18,
13302|       |                ..Default::default()
13303|       |            },
13304|       |        ];
13305|       |        // Small resolved widths so content should dominate
13306|       |        let resolved = vec![60.0, 60.0];
13307|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 2);
13308|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 2);
13309|       |        // body=14.0, char_width_factor=14*0.62=8.68, cell_padding=14*1.8=25.2
13310|       |        // col0: max(4,20)=20 ? 20*8.68+25.2 = 198.8 ? clamp(198.8, 56, 490) = 198.8
13311|       |        // col1: max(5,18)=18 ? 18*8.68+25.2 = 181.44 ? clamp(181.44, 56, 490) = 181.44
13312|       |        assert!(widths[0] > 150.0, "col 0 = {} should be > 150", widths[0]);
13313|       |        assert!(widths[1] > 150.0, "col 1 = {} should be > 150", widths[1]);
13314|       |    }
13315|       |
13316|       |    #[test]
13317|       |    fn test_natural_widths_uses_max_of_content_and_policy() {
13318|       |        // Short content (3 graphemes), policy width 98 should win
13319|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13320|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("ID".to_string())]];
13321|       |        let stats = vec![ColumnStat {
13322|       |            max_graphemes: 3,
13323|       |            ..Default::default()
13324|       |        }];
13325|       |        // body=14.0, char_width_factor=8.68, cell_padding=25.2
13326|       |        // header "ID" ? width 2; max(2,3)=3 ? 3*8.68+25.2 = 51.24
13327|       |        // policy = 98.0 ? max(51.24, 98.0) = 98.0
13328|       |        let resolved = vec![98.0];
13329|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 1);
13330|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 1);
13331|       |        assert!(
13332|       |            (widths[0] - 98.0).abs() < 0.1,
13333|       |            "policy should win: got {}",
13334|       |            widths[0]
13335|       |        );
13336|       |    }
13337|       |
13338|       |    #[test]
13339|       |    fn test_natural_widths_clamped_to_bounds() {
13340|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13341|       |        // body=14.0 ? min_col_width=56.0, max_col_width=490.0
13342|       |        let min_col = 14.0 * 4.0; // 56
13343|       |        let max_col = 14.0 * 35.0; // 490
13344|       |
13345|       |        // Tiny content: 1 grapheme ? 1*8.68+25.2 = 33.88, policy=10 ? clamp to min
13346|       |        let headers_tiny = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("X".to_string())]];
13347|       |        let stats_tiny = vec![ColumnStat {
13348|       |            max_graphemes: 1,
13349|       |            ..Default::default()
13350|       |        }];
13351|       |        let resolved_tiny = vec![10.0];
13352|       |        let widths_tiny =
13353|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats_tiny, &headers_tiny, &resolved_tiny, 1);
13354|       |        assert!(
13355|       |            (widths_tiny[0] - min_col).abs() < 0.1,
13356|       |            "tiny should clamp to min {}: got {}",
13357|       |            min_col,
13358|       |            widths_tiny[0]
13359|       |        );
13360|       |
13361|       |        // Huge content: 200 graphemes ? 200*8.68+25.2 = 1761.2 ? clamp to max
13362|       |        let headers_huge = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Description".to_string())]];
13363|       |        let stats_huge = vec![ColumnStat {
13364|       |            max_graphemes: 200,
13365|       |            ..Default::default()
13366|       |        }];
13367|       |        let resolved_huge = vec![10.0];
13368|       |        let widths_huge =
13369|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats_huge, &headers_huge, &resolved_huge, 1);
13370|       |        assert!(
13371|       |            (widths_huge[0] - max_col).abs() < 0.1,
13372|       |            "huge should clamp to max {}: got {}",
13373|       |            max_col,
13374|       |            widths_huge[0]
13375|       |        );
13376|       |    }
13377|       |
13378|       |    #[test]
13379|       |    fn test_natural_table_width_sums_correctly() {
13380|       |        // Empty input
13381|       |        let empty = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&[], 10.0);
13382|       |        assert!((empty - 0.0).abs() < f32::EPSILON);
13383|       |
13384|       |        // Single column ? no spacing
13385|       |        let single = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&[120.0], 10.0);
13386|       |        assert!((single - 120.0).abs() < f32::EPSILON);
13387|       |
13388|       |        // Three columns: 100+200+150 = 450, spacing = 10*2 = 20, total = 470
13389|       |        let three = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&[100.0, 200.0, 150.0], 10.0);
13390|       |        assert!((three - 470.0).abs() < 0.1, "expected 470, got {}", three);
13391|       |    }
13392|       |
13393|       |    #[test]
13394|       |    fn test_content_fit_mode_activated_for_small_table() {
13395|       |        // 2-column table with short content ? natural_total should be < 1100
13396|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13397|       |        let headers = vec![
13398|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Key".to_string())],
13399|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("Value".to_string())],
13400|       |        ];
13401|       |        let stats = vec![
13402|       |            ColumnStat {
13403|       |                max_graphemes: 10,
13404|       |                ..Default::default()
13405|       |            },
13406|       |            ColumnStat {
13407|       |                max_graphemes: 12,
13408|       |                ..Default::default()
13409|       |            },
13410|       |        ];
13411|       |        let resolved = vec![60.0, 60.0];
13412|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 2);
13413|       |        let total = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&widths, 6.0);
13414|       |        assert!(
13415|       |            total < 1100.0,
13416|       |            "small 2-col table total {} should be < 1100",
13417|       |            total
13418|       |        );
13419|       |    }
13420|       |
13421|       |    #[test]
13422|       |    fn test_viewport_fill_mode_for_wide_table() {
13423|       |        // 9-column table with moderate content ? natural_total should be > 1100
13424|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13425|       |        let headers: Vec<Vec<InlineSpan>> = (0..9)
13426|       |            .map(|i| vec![InlineSpan::Text(format!("Column{i}"))])
13427|       |            .collect();
13428|       |        let stats: Vec<ColumnStat> = (0..9)
13429|       |            .map(|_| ColumnStat {
13430|       |                max_graphemes: 15,
13431|       |                ..Default::default()
13432|       |            })
13433|       |            .collect();
13434|       |        let resolved: Vec<f32> = vec![80.0; 9];
13435|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 9);
13436|       |        let total = MarkdownRenderer::natural_table_width(&widths, 6.0);
13437|       |        assert!(
13438|       |            total > 1100.0,
13439|       |            "9-col table total {} should be > 1100",
13440|       |            total
13441|       |        );
13442|       |    }
13443|       |
13444|       |    // Test: column_count exceeds headers/stats/resolved_widths length
13445|       |    #[test]
13446|       |    fn test_natural_widths_handles_mismatched_lengths() {
13447|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13448|       |        let headers = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Name".into())]]; // only 1 header
13449|       |        let stats = vec![ColumnStat {
13450|       |            max_graphemes: 10,
13451|       |            longest_word: 5,
13452|       |            ..Default::default()
13453|       |        }]; // only 1 stat
13454|       |        let resolved = vec![98.0]; // only 1 width
13455|       |                                   // column_count = 3, exceeds all inputs
13456|       |        let widths = renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers, &resolved, 3);
13457|       |        assert_eq!(widths.len(), 3);
13458|       |        // First column should be content/policy-driven
13459|       |        assert!(widths[0] > 50.0);
13460|       |        // Remaining columns should be clamped to MIN (body * 4.0 = 56.0)
13461|       |        let min_col = renderer.font_sizes.body * 4.0;
13462|       |        assert!(
13463|       |            (widths[1] - min_col).abs() < 1.0,
13464|       |            "extra col should be MIN, got {}",
13465|       |            widths[1]
13466|       |        );
13467|       |        assert!(
13468|       |            (widths[2] - min_col).abs() < 1.0,
13469|       |            "extra col should be MIN, got {}",
13470|       |            widths[2]
13471|       |        );
13472|       |    }
13473|       |
13474|       |    // Test: CJK characters in header get double-width estimate
13475|       |    #[test]
13476|       |    fn test_natural_widths_cjk_header_gets_double_width() {
13477|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13478|       |        let headers_ascii = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text("Name".into())]]; // 4 display width
13479|       |        let headers_cjk = vec![vec![InlineSpan::Text(
13480|       |            "\u{540D}\u{524D}\u{5217}\u{5E45}".into(),
13481|       |        )]]; // 4 CJK chars = 8 display width
13482|       |        let stats = vec![ColumnStat::default()]; // no content
13483|       |        let resolved = vec![0.0]; // no policy
13484|       |        let widths_ascii =
13485|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers_ascii, &resolved, 1);
13486|       |        let widths_cjk =
13487|       |            renderer.estimate_natural_column_widths(&stats, &headers_cjk, &resolved, 1);
13488|       |        // CJK header should produce a wider natural width than ASCII header
13489|       |        assert!(
13490|       |            widths_cjk[0] > widths_ascii[0],
13491|       |            "CJK width {} should exceed ASCII width {}",
13492|       |            widths_cjk[0],
13493|       |            widths_ascii[0]
13494|       |        );
13495|       |    }

13501|       |   | Col | Notes |
13502|       |  | --- | --- |
13503|       |  | code | `a|b` |
13504|       |";
13505|       |        let escaped_parent = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md_parent);
13506|       |        assert!(escaped_parent.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13507|       |

13509|       |- Parent
13510|       |    - | Col | Notes |
13511|       |      | --- | --- |
13512|       |      | code | `a|b` |
13513|       |";
13514|       |        let escaped_nested = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md_nested);
13515|       |        assert!(escaped_nested.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13516|       |    }

13542|       |    fn test_escape_table_pipes_parent_indent_marker_within_range() {
13543|       |        let md = "\
13544|       |- Parent
13545|       |    - | H | I |
13546|       |      | --- | --- |
13547|       |      | `a|b` | c |
13548|       |";
13549|       |        let lines: Vec<&str> = md.split_inclusive('\n').collect();
13550|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(lines[1]);
13551|       |        let _parent_indent =
13552|       |            MarkdownRenderer::parent_list_indent_for_line(&lines, 1, level, rest).unwrap();
13553|       |        let _leading_spaces =
13554|       |            MarkdownRenderer::list_marker_info_any_indent(rest).map(|(_, _, _, spaces)| spaces);
13555|       |        let escaped = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md);
13556|       |        assert!(escaped.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13557|       |    }

13559|       |    #[test]
13560|       |    fn test_escape_table_pipes_parent_indent_strip_without_code_indent() {
13561|       |        let md = "\
13562|       |- Parent
13563|       |  continuation
13564|       |  | H | I |
13565|       |  | --- | --- |
13566|       |  | `a|b` | c |
13567|       |";
13568|       |        let lines: Vec<&str> = md.split_inclusive('\n').collect();
13569|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(lines[1]);
13570|       |        let parent_indent =
13571|       |            MarkdownRenderer::parent_list_indent_for_line(&lines, 1, level, rest).unwrap();
13572|       |        let strip = MarkdownRenderer::strip_indent_columns(rest, parent_indent);
13573|       |        let code_strip = MarkdownRenderer::strip_indent_columns(rest, parent_indent + 4);
13574|       |        assert!(strip.is_some());
13575|       |        assert!(code_strip.is_none());
13576|       |        let escaped = MarkdownRenderer::escape_table_pipes_in_inline_code(md);
13577|       |        assert!(escaped.contains(PIPE_SENTINEL));
13578|       |    }

13698|       |    #[test]
13699|       |    fn test_parse_table_unclosed_and_noise() -> Result<()> {
13700|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13701|       |        let events = vec![
13702|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Table(vec![Alignment::Left])),
13703|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableHead),
13704|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
13705|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
13706|       |            Event::Text("H".into()),
13707|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
13708|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
13709|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableHead),
13710|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableRow),
13711|       |            Event::Text("noise".into()),
13712|       |            Event::Start(Tag::TableCell),
13713|       |            Event::Text("R".into()),
13714|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),
13715|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableRow),
13716|       |            Event::Text("after".into()),
13717|       |        ];
13718|       |        let (headers, rows, next) = renderer.parse_table(&events, 1);
13719|       |        assert_eq!(headers.len(), 1);
13720|       |        assert_eq!(rows.len(), 1);
13721|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
13722|       |        Ok(())
13723|       |    }

13798|       |    fn test_parse_code_block_unclosed() -> Result<()> {
13799|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13800|       |        let events = vec![
13801|       |            Event::Start(Tag::CodeBlock(pulldown_cmark::CodeBlockKind::Fenced(
13802|       |                "rust".into(),
13803|       |            ))),
13804|       |            Event::Text("fn main() {}".into()),
13805|       |            Event::Html("<span>".into()),
13806|       |        ];
13807|       |        let (code, language, next) = renderer.parse_code_block(&events, 0);
13808|       |        assert_eq!(language.as_deref(), Some("rust"));
13809|       |        assert_eq!(code, "fn main() {}");
13810|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
13811|       |        Ok(())
13812|       |    }

13844|       |    #[test]
13845|       |    fn test_render_table_tablebuilder_scaled_down_adjustments() {
13846|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13847|       |        let headers = vec![
13848|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("A".to_string())],
13849|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("B".to_string())],
13850|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("C".to_string())],
13851|       |        ];
13852|       |        let rows = vec![vec![
13853|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("row1".to_string())],
13854|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("row2".to_string())],
13855|       |            vec![InlineSpan::Text("row3".to_string())],
13856|       |        ]];
13857|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
13858|       |        let widths = [220.0, 240.0];
13859|       |        for (idx, width) in widths.iter().enumerate() {
13860|       |            let _forced = ForcedTablePolicies::new(vec![
13861|       |                ColumnPolicy::Resizable {
13862|       |                    min: 40.0,
13863|       |                    preferred: 200.0,
13864|       |                    clip: false,
13865|       |                },
13866|       |                ColumnPolicy::Auto,
13867|       |                ColumnPolicy::Remainder { clip: false },
13868|       |            ]);
13869|       |            let input = screen_input(*width, 120.0);
13870|       |            run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
13871|       |                ui.spacing_mut().item_spacing.x = 2.0;
13872|       |                let table_id = renderer.compute_table_id(&headers, &rows, &[], 90 + idx as u64);
13873|       |                renderer.render_table_tablebuilder(ui, &headers, &rows, &[], table_id);
13874|       |            });
13875|       |        }
13876|       |    }

13878|       |    #[test]
13879|       |    fn test_render_code_block_mermaid_and_context_menu() {
13880|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
13881|       |        renderer.set_mermaid_theme(MermaidTheme::Default);
13882|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
13883|       |            renderer.render_code_block(ui, Some("mermaid"), "graph TD; A-->B;", None);
13884|       |        });
13885|       |
13886|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
13887|       |        let click = input_with_click(egui::pos2(12.0, 12.0), egui::PointerButton::Secondary);
13888|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, click, |_, ui| {
13889|       |            renderer.render_code_block(ui, Some("rust"), "fn main() {}", None);
13890|       |        });
13891|       |        let input = screen_input(320.0, 240.0);
13892|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
13893|       |            renderer.render_code_block(ui, Some("rust"), "fn main() {}", None);
13894|       |        });

13969|       |        let events = vec![
13970|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
13971|       |            Event::Text("alt".into()),
13972|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "img.png".into(), "".into())),
13973|       |        ];
13974|       |        let mut elements = Vec::new();
13975|       |        let mut slugs = HashMap::new();
13976|       |        let next = renderer.parse_element(&events, 0, &mut elements, &mut slugs);
13977|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
13978|       |        let spans = paragraph_spans(&elements[0]).expect("paragraph spans");
13979|       |        let (_src, _alt, title) = image_span_fields(&spans[0]).expect("image span");
13980|       |        assert!(title.is_none());
13981|       |    }

14044|       |    #[test]
14045|       |    fn test_table_width_helpers_empty_inputs() {
14046|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14047|       |        let table_id = 100u64;
14048|       |        let mut specs: Vec<ColumnSpec> = Vec::new();
14049|       |        renderer.apply_persisted_widths(table_id, &mut specs);
14050|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &[], &[]);
14051|       |        let change = renderer.record_resolved_widths(table_id, 0, &[]);
14052|       |        assert!(matches!(change, WidthChange::None));
14053|       |        assert!(renderer
14054|       |            .resolve_table_column_widths(table_id, &[], 10.0)
14055|       |            .is_empty());
14056|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.estimate_table_total_width(table_id, &[], 6.0), 0.0);
14057|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14058|       |            let style = ui.style().clone();
14059|       |            let row = vec![vec![]];
14060|       |            let height =
14061|       |                renderer.estimate_table_row_height(ui, &style, &row, &[Align::LEFT], &[40.0], 12.0);
14062|       |            assert_eq!(height, 12.0);
14063|       |            let row = vec![
14064|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("a".to_string())],
14065|       |                vec![InlineSpan::Text("b".to_string())],
14066|       |            ];
14067|       |            let height =
14068|       |                renderer.estimate_table_row_height(ui, &style, &row, &[Align::LEFT], &[40.0], 12.0);
14069|       |            assert!(height >= 12.0);
14070|       |            let rect = egui::Rect::from_min_size(egui::pos2(0.0, 0.0), egui::vec2(10.0, 10.0));
14071|       |            renderer.paint_table_dividers(
14072|       |                ui.painter(),
14073|       |                ui.visuals(),
14074|       |                rect,
14075|       |                rect,
14076|       |                &[],
14077|       |                0.0,
14078|       |                0.0,
14079|       |                0.0,
14080|       |                true,
14081|       |            );
14082|       |        });
14083|       |    }

14086|       |    fn test_resolve_table_column_widths_ignores_invalid_stored_widths() {
14087|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14088|       |        let table_id = 101u64;
14089|       |        {
14090|       |            let mut metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow_mut();
14091|       |            let entry = metrics.entry_mut(table_id);
14092|       |            entry.resolved_widths = vec![f32::NAN, -5.0];
14093|       |        }
14094|       |        let specs = vec![
14095|       |            resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 40.0),
14096|       |            ColumnSpec::new(1, "B", ColumnPolicy::Auto, None),
14097|       |        ];
14098|       |        let widths = renderer.resolve_table_column_widths(table_id, &specs, 30.0);
14099|       |        assert_eq!(widths, vec![40.0, 30.0]);
14100|       |    }

14102|       |    #[test]
14103|       |    fn test_persist_resizable_widths_stores_new_width() {
14104|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14105|       |        let table_id = 200u64;
14106|       |        let specs = vec![resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0)];
14107|       |        let widths = vec![140.0f32];
14108|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &widths);
14109|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
14110|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
14111|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(specs[0].policy_hash), Some(140.0));
14112|       |    }
14113|       |
14114|       |    #[test]
14115|       |    fn test_persist_resizable_widths_skips_small_delta() {
14116|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14117|       |        let table_id = 201u64;
14118|       |        let spec = resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0);
14119|       |        let hash = spec.policy_hash;
14120|       |        let specs = vec![spec];
14121|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[120.0]);
14122|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[120.2]);
14123|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
14124|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
14125|       |        let stored = entry.persisted_width(hash).unwrap_or_default();
14126|       |        assert!((stored - 120.0).abs() < 0.01);
14127|       |    }
14128|       |

14130|       |    fn test_persist_resizable_widths_updates_on_large_delta() {
14131|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14132|       |        let table_id = 202u64;
14133|       |        let spec = resizable_spec(0, "A", 20.0, 100.0);
14134|       |        let hash = spec.policy_hash;
14135|       |        let specs = vec![spec];
14136|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[120.0]);
14137|       |        renderer.persist_resizable_widths(table_id, &specs, &[130.0]);
14138|       |        let metrics = renderer.table_metrics.borrow();
14139|       |        let entry = metrics.entry(table_id).expect("entry created");
14140|       |        assert_eq!(entry.persisted_width(hash), Some(130.0));
14141|       |    }

14291|       |    #[test]
14292|       |    fn test_link_at_pointer_outside_text_rect() {
14293|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14294|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
14295|       |            text: "link".to_string(),
14296|       |            url: "https://example.com".to_string(),
14297|       |        }];
14298|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
14299|       |        let outside_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
14300|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14301|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
14302|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14303|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14304|       |            let (rect, _response) =
14305|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14306|       |            let text_origin =
14307|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14308|       |            let text_rect = galley.rect.translate(text_origin.to_vec2());
14309|       |            let pos = egui::pos2(rect.right() - 2.0, text_rect.center().y);
14310|       |            assert!(pos.x > text_rect.right());
14311|       |            outside_pos.set(pos);
14312|       |        });
14313|       |
14314|       |        let mut input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14315|       |        input
14316|       |            .events
14317|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(outside_pos.get()));
14318|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
14319|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14320|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14321|       |            let (rect, response) =
14322|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14323|       |            let text_origin =
14324|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14325|       |            let link = renderer.link_at_pointer(&response, &galley, &build, text_origin);
14326|       |            assert!(link.is_none());
14327|       |        });
14328|       |    }
14329|       |
14330|       |    #[test]
14331|       |    fn test_link_at_pointer_inside_text_rect_returns_link() {
14332|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14333|       |        let spans = vec![InlineSpan::Link {
14334|       |            text: "link".to_string(),
14335|       |            url: "#anchor".to_string(),
14336|       |        }];
14337|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
14338|       |        let click_pos = std::cell::Cell::new(egui::pos2(1.0, 1.0));
14339|       |        let layout_input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14340|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, layout_input, |_, ui| {
14341|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14342|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14343|       |            let (rect, _response) =
14344|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14345|       |            let text_origin =
14346|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14347|       |            click_pos.set(text_origin + egui::vec2(2.0, 2.0));
14348|       |        });
14349|       |
14350|       |        let mut input = screen_input(220.0, 120.0);
14351|       |        input
14352|       |            .events
14353|       |            .push(egui::Event::PointerMoved(click_pos.get()));
14354|       |        run_frame_with_input(&ctx, input, |_, ui| {
14355|       |            let build = renderer.build_layout_job(ui.style(), &spans, 40.0, false, Align::LEFT);
14356|       |            let galley = ui.fonts(|f| f.layout_job(build.job.clone()));
14357|       |            let (rect, response) =
14358|       |                ui.allocate_exact_size(egui::vec2(200.0, galley.size().y), egui::Sense::hover());
14359|       |            let text_origin =
14360|       |                MarkdownRenderer::aligned_text_origin(rect, &galley, build.job.halign);
14361|       |            let link = renderer.link_at_pointer(&response, &galley, &build, text_origin);
14362|       |            assert!(link.is_some());
14363|       |        });
14364|       |    }

14405|       |    #[test]
14406|       |    fn test_resolve_image_path_data_uri_decodes_and_remote_rejects() {
14407|       |        use base64::Engine;
14408|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14409|       |
14410|       |        // resolve_image_path still passes data URIs through unchanged
14411|       |        let data_uri_raw = "data:image/png;base64,AAAA";
14412|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.resolve_image_path(data_uri_raw), data_uri_raw);
14413|       |
14414|       |        // Build a valid 1x1 PNG data URI for the texture-loading test
14415|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
14416|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
14417|       |        let valid_data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
14418|       |
14419|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14420|       |            // A valid data URI should now decode and return a texture
14421|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &valid_data_uri);
14422|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some(), "valid data URI should produce a texture");
14423|       |            let (_, w, h) = loaded.unwrap();
14424|       |            assert_eq!((w, h), (1, 1), "expected 1x1 pixel image");
14425|       |
14426|       |            // Remote images without allow_remote should still be rejected
14427|       |            assert!(renderer
14428|       |                .get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "https://example.com/image.png")
14429|       |                .is_none());
14430|       |            assert!(renderer
14431|       |                .get_or_load_image_texture(ui, "http://example.com/image.png")
14432|       |                .is_none());
14433|       |        });
14434|       |    }

14445|       |        assert_eq!(level, 0);
14446|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "text");
14447|       |

14449|       |        assert_eq!(level, 0);
14450|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "   text");
14451|       |
14452|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(">abc");
14453|       |        assert_eq!(level, 1);
14454|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "abc");
14455|       |
14456|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(" >\tabc");
14457|       |        assert_eq!(level, 1);
14458|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "abc");
14459|       |
14460|       |        let (level, rest) = MarkdownRenderer::table_line_info(">");
14461|       |        assert_eq!(level, 1);
14462|       |        assert_eq!(rest, "");
14463|       |    }

14581|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Paragraph),
14582|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(

14585|       |                "".into(),
14586|       |            )),

14590|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14591|       |                "img.png".into(),
14592|       |                "Title".into(),
14593|       |            )),
14594|       |            Event::Text("Alt".into()),

14596|       |            Event::End(Tag::Paragraph),
14597|       |        ];

14601|       |            .iter()
14602|       |            .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));

14659|       |    #[test]
14660|       |    fn test_parse_list_inline_formats_without_paragraph() -> Result<()> {
14661|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14662|       |        let events = vec![
14663|       |            Event::Start(Tag::List(None)),
14664|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Item),
14665|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Emphasis),
14666|       |            Event::Text("em".into()),
14667|       |            Event::End(Tag::Emphasis),
14668|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Strikethrough),
14669|       |            Event::Text("strike".into()),
14670|       |            Event::End(Tag::Strikethrough),
14671|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Link(
14672|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14673|       |                "https://example.com".into(),
14674|       |                "".into(),
14675|       |            )),
14676|       |            Event::Text("link".into()),
14677|       |            Event::End(Tag::Link(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14678|       |            Event::Start(Tag::Image(
14679|       |                LinkType::Inline,
14680|       |                "img.png".into(),
14681|       |                "Title".into(),
14682|       |            )),
14683|       |            Event::Text("Alt".into()),
14684|       |            Event::End(Tag::Image(LinkType::Inline, "".into(), "".into())),
14685|       |            Event::End(Tag::Item),
14686|       |            Event::End(Tag::List(None)),
14687|       |        ];
14688|       |        let mut slugs = std::collections::HashMap::new();
14689|       |        let (items, next) = renderer.parse_list(&events, 1, &mut slugs);
14690|       |        assert_eq!(next, events.len());
14691|       |        assert!(matches!(items[0].blocks[0], MarkdownElement::Paragraph(_)));
14692|       |        let dummy = MarkdownElement::HorizontalRule;
14693|       |        let mut saw_paragraph = false;
14694|       |        for element in [&items[0].blocks[0], &dummy] {
14695|       |            if let MarkdownElement::Paragraph(spans) = element {
14696|       |                assert!(spans
14697|       |                    .iter()
14698|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Emphasis(_))));
14699|       |                assert!(spans
14700|       |                    .iter()
14701|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Strikethrough(_))));
14702|       |                assert!(spans
14703|       |                    .iter()
14704|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Link { .. })));
14705|       |                assert!(spans
14706|       |                    .iter()
14707|       |                    .any(|span| matches!(span, InlineSpan::Image { .. })));
14708|       |                saw_paragraph = true;
14709|       |            }
14710|       |        }
14711|       |        assert!(saw_paragraph);
14712|       |        Ok(())
14713|       |    }

14730|       |            Event::Text("A".into()),
14731|       |            Event::End(Tag::TableCell),

14733|       |            Event::End(Tag::Table(alignments)),
14734|       |        ];

14753|       |    fn test_clear_image_failure_cache_and_texture_cache_stats() {
14754|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14755|       |        renderer.note_image_failure("failed.png");
14756|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, ui| {
14757|       |            let _ = renderer.get_or_make_emoji_texture(ui, "\u{1f600}");
14758|       |            let image = ctx.load_texture(
14759|       |                "image-cache-stats",
14760|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::WHITE),
14761|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
14762|       |            );
14763|       |            renderer.store_image_texture("stats.png", image, [2, 2], None);
14764|       |        });
14765|       |
14766|       |        let (emoji_entries, image_entries) = renderer.texture_cache_stats();
14767|       |        assert_eq!(emoji_entries, 1);
14768|       |        assert_eq!(image_entries, 1);
14769|       |
14770|       |        renderer.clear_image_failure_cache();
14771|       |        assert!(renderer.image_failures.borrow().entries.is_empty());
14772|       |    }

14794|       |    #[test]
14795|       |    fn test_render_link_with_emoji_characters() {
14796|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14797|       |        // Create a link whose text contains actual emoji characters (triggers emoji link path)
14798|       |        let md = "[?? Launch](https://example.com)\n";
14799|       |        let elements = renderer.parse(md).expect("parse ok");
14800|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14801|       |            renderer.render_to_ui(ui, &elements);
14802|       |        });
14803|       |    }

14846|       |
14847|       |    /// Verify that U+FE0F (Variation Selector 16) is stripped from non-catalog
14848|       |    /// emoji before they reach the text buffer. U+1F6E0 (HAMMER AND WRENCH) +
14849|       |    /// U+FE0F is NOT in the 80-emoji Twemoji catalog, so it falls through to
14850|       |    /// text rendering. Without the fix, FE0F would appear as tofu in egui.
14851|       |    #[test]
14852|       |    fn test_render_text_with_emojis_strips_vs16() {
14853|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14854|       |        // U+1F6E0 U+FE0F = ???  — not in catalog, should strip FE0F
14855|       |        let input = "before \u{1F6E0}\u{FE0F} after";
14856|       |
14857|       |        // Confirm the emoji is NOT in our catalog (prerequisite for the fix)
14858|       |        assert!(
14859|       |            renderer
14860|       |                .emoji_key_for_grapheme("\u{1F6E0}\u{FE0F}")
14861|       |                .is_none(),
14862|       |            "test assumes U+1F6E0+FE0F is not in emoji catalog"
14863|       |        );
14864|       |
14865|       |        // The stripping logic is inline in render_text_with_emojis, so we
14866|       |        // verify it directly: for a non-catalog grapheme containing FE0F,
14867|       |        // the chars().filter() path should remove it.
14868|       |        let g = "\u{1F6E0}\u{FE0F}";
14869|       |        assert!(g.contains('\u{FE0F}'));
14870|       |        let clean: String = g.chars().filter(|&c| c != '\u{FE0F}').collect();
14871|       |        assert!(!clean.contains('\u{FE0F}'), "FE0F should be stripped");
14872|       |        assert!(clean.contains('\u{1F6E0}'), "base emoji should remain");
14873|       |
14874|       |        // Smoke test: render_text_with_emojis should not panic with VS16 input
14875|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
14876|       |            renderer.render_text_with_emojis(ui, input, 14.0, InlineStyle::default());
14877|       |        });
14878|       |    }
14879|       |

14900|       |    #[test]
14901|       |    fn test_highlight_code_light_theme() {
14902|       |        let mut renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14903|       |
14904|       |        // Parse with dark theme
14905|       |        let code = "let x = 42;";
14906|       |        let dark_tokens = renderer
14907|       |            .highlight_code(Some("rust"), code)
14908|       |            .expect("dark highlight");
14909|       |
14910|       |        // Switch to light theme and re-highlight
14911|       |        renderer.set_dark_mode(false);
14912|       |        let light_tokens = renderer
14913|       |            .highlight_code(Some("rust"), code)
14914|       |            .expect("light highlight");
14915|       |
14916|       |        // The two themes should produce different token colors
14917|       |        assert!(!dark_tokens.is_empty());
14918|       |        assert!(!light_tokens.is_empty());
14919|       |        // At least one token color should differ between themes
14920|       |        let dark_first_color = dark_tokens[0][0].color;
14921|       |        let light_first_color = light_tokens[0][0].color;
14922|       |        assert_ne!(dark_first_color, light_first_color);
14923|       |    }

14925|       |    #[test]
14926|       |    fn test_release_gpu_textures_clears_all_caches() {
14927|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
14928|       |        with_test_ui(|ctx, _ui| {
14929|       |            // Populate emoji_textures cache
14930|       |            let emoji_tex = ctx.load_texture(
14931|       |                "test-emoji",
14932|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::RED),
14933|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
14934|       |            );
14935|       |            renderer
14936|       |                .emoji_textures
14937|       |                .borrow_mut()
14938|       |                .insert("rocket".to_string(), emoji_tex);
14939|       |
14940|       |            // Populate image_textures cache
14941|       |            let img_tex = ctx.load_texture(
14942|       |                "test-image",
14943|       |                egui::ColorImage::new([2, 2], Color32::BLUE),
14944|       |                egui::TextureOptions::LINEAR,
14945|       |            );
14946|       |            renderer.image_textures.borrow_mut().insert(
14947|       |                "test.png".to_string(),
14948|       |                ImageCacheEntry {
14949|       |                    texture: img_tex,
14950|       |                    size: [64, 32],
14951|       |                    modified: None,
14952|       |                    byte_size: ImageCacheEntry::estimate_bytes([64, 32]),
14953|       |                    degraded: false,
14954|       |                },
14955|       |            );
14956|       |
14957|       |            // Verify caches are populated
14958|       |            assert!(!renderer.emoji_textures.borrow().entries.is_empty());
14959|       |            assert!(!renderer.image_textures.borrow().entries.is_empty());
14960|       |
14961|       |            // Call release_gpu_textures
14962|       |            renderer.release_gpu_textures();
14963|       |
14964|       |            // Assert all texture caches are empty
14965|       |            assert_eq!(renderer.emoji_textures.borrow().len(), 0);
14966|       |            assert_eq!(renderer.image_textures.borrow().entries.len(), 0);
14967|       |        });
14968|       |    }

14970|       |    // ---------------------------------------------------------------
14971|       |    // decode_data_uri() tests
14972|       |    // ---------------------------------------------------------------
14973|       |
14974|       |    /// Helper: create a minimal 1x1 PNG in memory and return the raw bytes.
14975|       |    fn make_1x1_png() -> Vec<u8> {
14976|       |        let mut buf = Vec::new();
14977|       |        let encoder = PngEncoder::new(&mut buf);
14978|       |        // 1x1 white pixel, RGBA
14979|       |        encoder
14980|       |            .write_image(&[255, 255, 255, 255], 1, 1, ColorType::Rgba8)
14981|       |            .expect("encode 1x1 PNG");
14982|       |        buf
14983|       |    }
14984|       |
14985|       |    /// Helper: create a minimal JPEG in memory using the `image` crate.
14986|       |    fn make_1x1_jpeg() -> Vec<u8> {
14987|       |        use image::codecs::jpeg::JpegEncoder;
14988|       |        let mut buf = Vec::new();
14989|       |        let encoder = JpegEncoder::new(&mut buf);
14990|       |        // 1x1 white pixel, RGB (JPEG doesn't support alpha)
14991|       |        encoder
14992|       |            .write_image(&[255, 255, 255], 1, 1, ColorType::Rgb8)
14993|       |            .expect("encode 1x1 JPEG");
14994|       |        buf
14995|       |    }
14996|       |
14997|       |    #[test]
14998|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_valid_png() {
14999|       |        use base64::Engine;
15000|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15001|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15002|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
15003|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
15004|       |        assert!(decoded.is_some(), "valid PNG data URI should decode");
15005|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
15006|       |    }
15007|       |

15087|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_charset_parameter_present() {
15088|       |        use base64::Engine;
15089|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15090|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15091|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;charset=utf-8;base64,{b64}");
15092|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
15093|       |        assert!(
15094|       |            decoded.is_some(),
15095|       |            "charset parameter before ;base64 should still decode"
15096|       |        );

15100|       |    #[test]
15101|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_whitespace_in_payload() {
15102|       |        use base64::Engine;
15103|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15104|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15105|       |        // Inject newlines into the base64 payload
15106|       |        let b64_with_newlines: String = b64
15107|       |            .chars()
15108|       |            .enumerate()
15109|       |            .flat_map(|(i, c)| {
15110|       |                if i > 0 && i % 10 == 0 {
15111|       |                    vec!['\n', c]
15112|       |                } else {
15113|       |                    vec![c]
15114|       |                }
15115|       |            })
15116|       |            .collect();
15117|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64_with_newlines}");
15118|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
15119|       |        assert!(
15120|       |            decoded.is_some(),
15121|       |            "whitespace in payload should be stripped before decoding"
15122|       |        );
15123|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
15124|       |    }
15125|       |
15126|       |    #[test]
15127|       |    fn test_decode_data_uri_tabs_in_payload() {
15128|       |        use base64::Engine;
15129|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15130|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15131|       |        // Inject tabs and spaces into the base64 payload
15132|       |        let b64_with_tabs: String = b64
15133|       |            .chars()
15134|       |            .enumerate()
15135|       |            .flat_map(|(i, c)| {
15136|       |                if i > 0 && i % 8 == 0 {
15137|       |                    vec!['\t', ' ', c]
15138|       |                } else {
15139|       |                    vec![c]
15140|       |                }
15141|       |            })
15142|       |            .collect();
15143|       |        let uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64_with_tabs}");
15144|       |        let decoded = decode_data_uri(&uri);
15145|       |        assert!(
15146|       |            decoded.is_some(),
15147|       |            "tabs and spaces in payload should be stripped before decoding"
15148|       |        );
15149|       |        assert_eq!(decoded.unwrap(), png_bytes);
15150|       |    }

15156|       |    #[test]
15157|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_produces_texture() {
15158|       |        use base64::Engine;
15159|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15160|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15161|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15162|       |        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
15163|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15164|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15165|       |            assert!(loaded.is_some(), "valid data URI should produce a texture");
15166|       |            let (_, w, h) = loaded.unwrap();
15167|       |            assert_eq!((w, h), (1, 1), "expected 1x1 pixel image");
15168|       |        });
15169|       |    }
15170|       |
15171|       |    #[test]
15172|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_invalid_returns_none() {
15173|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15174|       |        // "dGVzdA==" decodes to "test" — valid base64 but not a valid image
15175|       |        let data_uri = "data:image/png;base64,dGVzdA==";
15176|       |        let expected_key = data_uri_cache_key(data_uri);
15177|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15178|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, data_uri);
15179|       |            assert!(loaded.is_none(), "garbage image bytes should return None");
15180|       |            // Failure should be cached under the hash key
15181|       |            assert!(
15182|       |                renderer.image_failures.borrow().contains_key(&expected_key),
15183|       |                "failure should be cached under the hash key"
15184|       |            );
15185|       |        });
15186|       |    }
15187|       |
15188|       |    #[test]
15189|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_cached_on_second_call() {
15190|       |        use base64::Engine;
15191|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15192|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15193|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15194|       |        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
15195|       |        let expected_key = data_uri_cache_key(&data_uri);
15196|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15197|       |            // First call: decodes and caches
15198|       |            let first = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15199|       |            assert!(first.is_some(), "first call should produce a texture");
15200|       |
15201|       |            // Verify the hash key exists in image_textures
15202|       |            assert!(
15203|       |                renderer.image_textures.borrow().contains_key(&expected_key),
15204|       |                "hash key should exist in image_textures after first call"
15205|       |            );
15206|       |
15207|       |            // Second call: should hit cache and still return Some
15208|       |            let second = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15209|       |            assert!(second.is_some(), "second call should return cached texture");
15210|       |            let (_, w1, h1) = first.unwrap();
15211|       |            let (_, w2, h2) = second.unwrap();
15212|       |            assert_eq!((w1, h1), (w2, h2), "dimensions should match between calls");
15213|       |        });
15214|       |    }
15215|       |
15216|       |    #[test]
15217|       |    fn test_get_or_load_image_texture_data_uri_non_image_mime_rejected() {
15218|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15219|       |        // text/html is not an image MIME type — decode_data_uri returns None
15220|       |        let data_uri = "data:text/html;base64,dGVzdA==";
15221|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15222|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, data_uri);
15223|       |            assert!(loaded.is_none(), "non-image MIME type should return None");
15224|       |        });
15225|       |    }
15226|       |
15227|       |    #[test]
15228|       |    fn test_data_uri_not_stale_after_cache() {
15229|       |        use base64::Engine;
15230|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15231|       |        let png_bytes = make_1x1_png();
15232|       |        let b64 = base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(&png_bytes);
15233|       |        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{b64}");
15234|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15235|       |            // Load into cache
15236|       |            let first = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15237|       |            assert!(first.is_some(), "initial load should succeed");
15238|       |            let (_, w1, h1) = first.unwrap();
15239|       |
15240|       |            // Second call: served from cache (not re-decoded), same dimensions
15241|       |            let second = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &data_uri);
15242|       |            assert!(second.is_some(), "cached lookup should succeed");
15243|       |            let (_, w2, h2) = second.unwrap();
15244|       |            assert_eq!(
15245|       |                (w1, h1),
15246|       |                (w2, h2),
15247|       |                "cached image should have same dimensions"
15248|       |            );
15249|       |        });
15250|       |    }
15251|       |
15252|       |    #[test]
15253|       |    fn test_placeholder_label_data_uri_truncated() {
15254|       |        let renderer = MarkdownRenderer::new();
15255|       |        // Use a data URI with invalid image bytes so the placeholder path is taken
15256|       |        let long_data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{}", "A".repeat(1000));
15257|       |        let span = InlineSpan::Image {
15258|       |            src: long_data_uri.clone(),
15259|       |            alt: "".to_string(),
15260|       |            title: None,
15261|       |        };
15262|       |        with_test_ui(|_, ui| {
15263|       |            // Ensure the data URI fails to load (garbage bytes)
15264|       |            let loaded = renderer.get_or_load_image_texture(ui, &long_data_uri);
15265|       |            assert!(
15266|       |                loaded.is_none(),
15267|       |                "garbage data URI should not produce a texture"
15268|       |            );
15269|       |            // Render the span — this exercises the placeholder path
15270|       |            // The placeholder should show "Embedded image (data URI)" instead of the raw base64
15271|       |            renderer.render_inline_span(ui, &span, None, None);
15272|       |            // If we got here without hanging or crashing, the truncation is working.
15273|       |            // The placeholder text is rendered internally via ui.label() — we verify
15274|       |            // the code path is exercised rather than inspecting the rendered text.
15275|       |        });
15276|       |    }

15343|       |    #[test]
15344|       |    fn test_data_uri_cache_key_fixed_length() {
15345|       |        let short_uri = "data:image/png;base64,AA==";
15346|       |        let long_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{}", "A".repeat(1_000_000));
15347|       |        let short_key = data_uri_cache_key(short_uri);
15348|       |        let long_key = data_uri_cache_key(&long_uri);
15349|       |        assert_eq!(
15350|       |            short_key.len(),
15351|       |            long_key.len(),
15352|       |            "cache key length must be fixed regardless of input size (short={}, long={})",
15353|       |            short_key.len(),
15354|       |            long_key.len()
15355|       |        );
15356|       |        // "data-uri:" (9 chars) + 16 hex digits = 25 chars
15357|       |        assert_eq!(
15358|       |            short_key.len(),
15359|       |            25,
15360|       |            "expected 25-char cache key, got {}",
15361|       |            short_key.len()
15362|       |        );
15363|       |    }

  419|       |/// LRU cache for Mermaid textures with both entry count and size limits.
  420|       |/// Prevents excessive GPU memory usage from large rendered diagrams.
  421|       |#[cfg(feature = "mermaid-quickjs")]

  423|       |    entries: HashMap<String, MermaidTextureEntry>,
  424|       |    order: VecDeque<String>,

  434|    487|            entries: HashMap::new(),
  435|    487|            order: VecDeque::new(),
  436|    487|            capacity: capacity.max(1),
  437|    487|            max_bytes,
  438|    487|            current_bytes: 0,
  439|    487|        }
  440|    487|    }
  441|       |
  442|       |    /// Calculate memory size of a texture entry in bytes (width * height * 4 for RGBA)
  443|     28|    fn texture_bytes(entry: &MermaidTextureEntry) -> usize {
  444|     28|        entry.size[0] as usize * entry.size[1] as usize * 4
  445|     28|    }
  446|       |
  447|     47|    fn get(&mut self, key: &String) -> Option<MermaidTextureEntry> {
  448|     47|        let value = self.entries.get(key).cloned();

  457|       |

                                  ^2

  466|      2|            return;

  472|      1|        }
