 9059|       |    #[test]
 9060|       |    fn test_debounce_trailing_edge_timer_reset() {
 9061|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = bounded(16);
 9062|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = bounded(16);
 9063|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 9064|       |
 9065|       |        let theme = MermaidTheme::Default;
 9066|       |        let code = "graph TD; G-->H;";
 9067|       |        let svg_key = hash_str(&format!("{}:{}", theme.theme_name(), code));
 9068|       |
 9069|       |        // First render at width 400
 9070|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 9071|       |        let input = test_raw_input(400.0, 300.0);
 9072|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 9073|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 9074|       |                renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0, theme);
 9075|       |            });
 9076|       |        });
 9077|       |
 9078|       |        // Width change to 500
 9079|       |        let input2 = test_raw_input(500.0, 300.0);
 9080|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input2, |ctx| {
 9081|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 9082|       |                renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0, theme);
 9083|       |            });
 9084|       |        });
 9085|       |
 9086|       |        // Backdate timer so it's almost expired but not quite
 9087|       |        {
 9088|       |            let mut debounce = renderer.mermaid_width_debounce.borrow_mut();
 9089|       |            let entry = debounce.get_mut(&svg_key).unwrap();
 9090|       |            entry.bucket_changed_at = Some(
 9091|       |                Instant::now() - std::time::Duration::from_millis(MERMAID_RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS - 20),
 9092|       |            );
 9093|       |        }
 9094|       |
 9095|       |        // Width change to 600 — should reset the timer (trailing edge)
 9096|       |        let input3 = test_raw_input(600.0, 300.0);
 9097|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input3, |ctx| {
 9098|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 9099|       |                renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0, theme);
 9100|       |            });
 9101|       |        });
 9102|       |
 9103|       |        // Timer should have been freshly reset (close to now)
 9104|       |        let changed_at = renderer
 9105|       |            .mermaid_width_debounce
 9106|       |            .borrow()
 9107|       |            .get(&svg_key)
 9108|       |            .unwrap()
 9109|       |            .bucket_changed_at
 9110|       |            .unwrap();
 9111|       |        assert!(changed_at.elapsed().as_millis() < 50);
 9112|       |
 9113|       |        // Still only 1 enqueue (original)
 9114|       |        assert_eq!(job_rx.len(), 1);
 9115|       |
 9116|       |        drop(job_rx);
 9117|       |    }

 9120|       |    #[test]
 9121|       |    fn test_theme_change_clears_debounce_state() {
 9122|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = bounded(1);
 9123|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 9124|       |
 9125|       |        // Populate debounce maps
 9126|       |        renderer.mermaid_width_debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 9127|       |            1,
 9128|       |            WidthDebounce {
 9129|       |                last_enqueued_bucket: 320,
 9130|       |                last_seen_bucket: 320,
 9131|       |                bucket_changed_at: None,
 9132|       |            },
 9133|       |        );
 9134|       |        renderer
 9135|       |            .mermaid_latest_texture
 9136|       |            .borrow_mut()
 9137|       |            .insert(1, "some_key".to_string());
 9138|       |        renderer.mermaid_wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(1, 320);
 9139|       |
 9140|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.mermaid_width_debounce.borrow().len(), 1);
 9141|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.mermaid_latest_texture.borrow().len(), 1);
 9142|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.mermaid_wanted_bucket.borrow().len(), 1);
 9143|       |
 9144|       |        renderer.clear_mermaid_cache();
 9145|       |
 9146|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.mermaid_width_debounce.borrow().len(), 0);
 9147|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.mermaid_latest_texture.borrow().len(), 0);
 9148|       |        assert_eq!(renderer.mermaid_wanted_bucket.borrow().len(), 0);
 9149|       |    }

C:\language\mdmdview\src\pikchr_renderer.rs:
    1|       |//! Pikchr diagram renderer.
    2|       |//!
    3|       |//! Renders Pikchr markup to SVG using the embedded pikchr C library,
    4|       |//! then rasterizes SVG to egui textures via usvg/resvg. Heavy rendering
    5|       |//! work (Pikchr C call + SVG rasterization) runs on a dedicated worker
    6|       |//! thread; the main thread only handles the cheap GPU texture upload.
    7|       |//!
    8|       |//! Key components:
    9|       |//! - `PikchrRenderer` struct: holds font database, caches, and worker channels
   10|       |//! - `LruCache<K, V>`: generic LRU cache (shared module in lru_cache.rs)
   11|       |//! - `render_pikchr_to_svg()`: static method wrapping the pikchr C library
   12|       |//! - `rasterize_svg()`: pure function: SVG string -> RGBA bytes (runs on worker)
   13|       |//! - `poll_results()`: drains worker results, uploads textures to GPU (main thread)
   14|       |//!
   15|       |//! This module is conditionally compiled behind `#[cfg(feature = "pikchr")]`.
   16|       |//! It integrates with `MarkdownRenderer` following the same public API pattern
   17|       |//! as `MermaidRenderer` (render_block, begin_frame, has_pending, release_gpu_textures).
   18|       |
   19|       |use std::cell::RefCell;
   20|       |use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
   21|       |use std::sync::Arc;
   22|       |use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
   23|       |
   24|       |use crossbeam_channel::{Receiver, Sender, TrySendError};
   25|       |
   26|       |use crate::lru_cache::{hash_str, LruCache};
   27|       |use crate::ThemeColors;
   28|       |
   29|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   30|       |// Cache capacity constants
   31|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   32|       |
   33|       |const PIKCHR_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
   34|       |const PIKCHR_SVG_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 64;
   35|       |const PIKCHR_ERROR_CACHE_CAPACITY: usize = 32;
   36|       |/// Trailing-edge debounce cooldown for resize. During window resize, suppress
   37|       |/// rasterization enqueue and show the last rendered texture scaled to fit.
   38|       |/// Only enqueue a new rasterization job once the width stabilizes for this
   39|       |/// duration.
   40|       |const RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS: u64 = 150;
   41|       |/// Maximum rasterized dimension (width or height) in pixels.
   42|       |/// Matches the cap used by mermaid_renderer.
   43|       |const MAX_RASTER_SIDE: u32 = 4096;
   44|       |/// Rasterization supersample factor. Pikchr SVGs have compact viewBox
   45|       |/// dimensions (a single box is ~67x35 units), so rasterizing at 1x
   46|       |/// produces textures with insufficient pixel density for crisp text.
   47|       |/// 2x gives 4x the pixels, making anti-aliased text and thin lines
   48|       |/// look sharp -- the standard "retina rendering" approach.
   49|       |const PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE: f32 = 2.0;
   50|       |/// Bounded channel capacity for job and result channels. With debounce
   51|       |/// suppressing enqueue during resize, at most one job per diagram is
   52|       |/// enqueued after the debounce expires. A document with ~6-8 diagrams
   53|       |/// fits within 8 slots. The bounded channel provides natural backpressure
   54|       |/// if the worker falls behind.
   55|       |const CHANNEL_CAPACITY: usize = 8;
   56|       |
   57|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   58|       |// Channel type aliases
   59|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   60|       |
   61|       |type JobSender = Sender<RasterJob>;
   62|       |type JobReceiver = Receiver<RasterJob>;
   63|       |type ResultSender = Sender<RasterResult>;
   64|       |type ResultReceiver = Receiver<RasterResult>;
   65|       |
   66|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   67|       |// RasterJob / RasterResult
   68|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   69|       |
   70|       |/// Job sent from main thread to worker.
   71|       |struct RasterJob {
   72|       |    /// Hash of the source code (for cache keying).
   73|       |    code_hash: u64,
   74|       |    /// Composite texture cache key (includes width, scale, theme).
   75|       |    texture_key: String,
   76|       |    /// Source code to render. None if SVG is provided directly.
   77|       |    code: Option<String>,
   78|       |    /// Pre-cached SVG string. If present, skip source->SVG step.
   79|       |    svg: Option<String>,
   80|       |    /// Dark mode flag (affects source->SVG for Pikchr).
   81|       |    dark_mode: bool,
   82|       |    /// Width bucket (32px quantized) for rasterization scaling.
   83|       |    width_bucket: u32,
   84|       |    /// Scale bucket (integer %, e.g., 100 = 1.0x zoom).
   85|       |    scale_bucket: u32,
   86|       |}
   87|       |
   88|       |/// Result sent from worker back to main thread.
   89|       |struct RasterResult {
   90|       |    /// Hash of the source code (for cache keying).
   91|       |    code_hash: u64,
   92|       |    /// Composite texture cache key.
   93|       |    texture_key: String,
   94|       |    /// Dark mode flag (copied from job, for SVG cache key computation).
   95|       |    dark_mode: bool,
   96|       |    /// SVG string (always set on success, for caching).
   97|       |    svg: Option<String>,
   98|       |    /// RGBA pixel data from rasterization.
   99|       |    rgba: Option<Vec<u8>>,
  100|       |    /// Raster dimensions in actual pixels (for GPU upload).
  101|       |    raster_size: Option<[usize; 2]>,
  102|       |    /// Display dimensions in logical pixels (for UI layout).
  103|       |    display_size: Option<[u32; 2]>,
  104|       |    /// Error message (set on failure, mutually exclusive with rgba).
  105|       |    error: Option<String>,
  106|       |}
  107|       |
  108|       |/// Intermediate output from `rasterize_svg`, before GPU upload.
  109|       |struct RasterOutput {
  110|       |    rgba: Vec<u8>,
  111|       |    raster_size: [usize; 2],
  112|       |    display_size: [u32; 2],
  113|       |}
  114|       |
  115|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  116|       |// PikchrTextureEntry
  117|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  118|       |
  119|       |/// A cached rasterized Pikchr diagram texture with its display dimensions.
  120|       |#[derive(Clone)]
  121|       |struct PikchrTextureEntry {
  122|       |    /// GPU texture handle (Clone wraps Arc internally).
  123|       |    texture: egui::TextureHandle,
  124|       |    /// Display dimensions in logical pixels [width, height].
  125|       |    /// The actual texture is larger by `PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE` for crisp rendering.
  126|       |    display_size: [u32; 2],
  127|       |}
  128|       |
  129|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  130|       |// Helper functions
  131|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  132|       |
  133|       |/// Composite SVG cache key: embeds both the source code hash and dark_mode flag.
  134|       |/// Dark mode produces different SVG output for the same source code.
  135|     25|fn svg_cache_key(code_hash: u64, dark_mode: bool) -> u128 {
  136|     25|    ((code_hash as u128) << 1) | (dark_mode as u128)
  137|     25|}
  138|       |
  139|       |/// Quantize a width value to 32-pixel buckets to avoid cache thrashing
  140|       |/// when the panel width changes by a few pixels (e.g., during resize).
  141|       |/// Returns the bucket midpoint as u32.
  142|     22|fn bucket_width(width: f32) -> u32 {
  143|     22|    let bucket = (width / 32.0).round() as u32;
  144|     22|    bucket * 32
  145|     22|}
  146|       |
  147|       |/// Quantize a UI scale factor to integer percentage buckets.
  148|       |/// e.g., 1.0 -> 100, 1.25 -> 125, 1.5 -> 150.
  149|       |/// Matches the bucketing strategy in mermaid_renderer.rs.
  150|     18|fn bucket_scale(scale: f32) -> u32 {
  151|     18|    (scale * 100.0).round() as u32
  152|     18|}
  153|       |
  154|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  155|       |// PikchrRenderer
  156|       |// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  157|       |
  158|       |/// Per-diagram resize debounce state.
  159|       |struct DiagramDebounce {
  160|       |    /// Width bucket at which we last rasterized (and cached a texture).
  161|       |    last_rasterized_bucket: u32,
  162|       |    /// Width bucket seen on the most recent frame (for trailing-edge reset).
  163|       |    last_seen_bucket: u32,
  164|       |    /// When `last_seen_bucket` last changed. `None` when no resize is active.
  165|       |    bucket_changed_at: Option<Instant>,
  166|       |}
  167|       |
  168|       |/// Pikchr diagram renderer.
  169|       |///
  170|       |/// Renders Pikchr markup to SVG using the embedded pikchr C library,
  171|       |/// then rasterizes SVG to egui textures via usvg/resvg. Heavy rendering
  172|       |/// runs on a dedicated worker thread; the main thread only uploads the
  173|       |/// resulting RGBA data to the GPU (~0.1-1ms).
  174|       |pub(crate) struct PikchrRenderer {
  175|       |    /// LRU cache: texture_key (String) -> (TextureHandle, [width, height])
  176|       |    textures: RefCell<LruCache<String, PikchrTextureEntry>>,
  177|       |    /// SVG cache: svg_cache_key (u128: code_hash << 1 | dark_mode) -> SVG string
  178|       |    svg_cache: RefCell<LruCache<u128, String>>,
  179|       |    /// Error cache: code_hash (u64) -> error message.
  180|       |    /// Errors don't depend on dark_mode, only on source syntax.
  181|       |    errors: RefCell<LruCache<u64, String>>,
  182|       |    /// Per-diagram debounce state for resize suppression.
  183|       |    debounce: RefCell<HashMap<u64, DiagramDebounce>>,
  184|       |    /// Maps code_hash -> texture_key of the most recently rasterized texture.
  185|       |    /// Used to display a stale texture scaled to fit during debounce cooldown.
  186|       |    latest_texture_key: RefCell<HashMap<u64, String>>,
  187|       |    /// Job sender. Wrapped in Option so Drop can close the channel
  188|       |    /// before joining the worker thread (same pattern as Mermaid).
  189|       |    job_tx: Option<JobSender>,
  190|       |    /// Result receiver. Polled each frame in poll_results().
  191|       |    result_rx: ResultReceiver,
  192|       |    /// Worker thread handle. Joined on drop for clean shutdown.
  193|       |    worker_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
  194|       |    /// Set of code_hashes with in-flight jobs. Keyed by code_hash (not
  195|       |    /// texture_key) to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes
  196|       |    /// while a job is already pending for the same diagram.
  197|       |    pending: RefCell<HashSet<u64>>,
  198|       |    /// Tracks the currently desired width bucket per diagram (for stale discard).
  199|       |    wanted_bucket: RefCell<HashMap<u64, u32>>,
  200|       |}
  201|       |
  202|       |impl PikchrRenderer {
  203|       |    /// Create a new PikchrRenderer, loading system fonts once at startup.
  204|       |    /// Without system fonts, usvg's default empty fontdb silently drops all
  205|       |    /// text labels in rasterized SVGs. Takes ~50-200ms (one-time cost).
  206|       |    /// Spawns a dedicated worker thread for background rasterization.
  207|       |    /// Same pattern as mermaid_renderer.rs and image_decode.rs.
  208|    460|    pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
  209|    460|        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
  210|    460|        db.load_system_fonts();
  211|    460|        let fontdb = Arc::new(db);
  212|       |
  213|    460|        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);
  214|    460|        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);
  215|       |
  216|       |        // Clone fontdb for the worker thread
  217|    460|        let worker_fontdb = Arc::clone(&fontdb);
  218|    460|        let worker_handle = std::thread::Builder::new()
  219|    460|            .name("pikchr-raster".into())
  220|    460|            .spawn(move || {
  221|    460|                Self::worker_loop(worker_fontdb, job_rx, result_tx);
  222|    460|            })
  223|    460|            .expect("failed to spawn pikchr raster worker");
  224|       |
  225|    460|        Self {
  226|    460|            textures: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(PIKCHR_TEXTURE_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  227|    460|            svg_cache: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(PIKCHR_SVG_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  228|    460|            errors: RefCell::new(LruCache::new(PIKCHR_ERROR_CACHE_CAPACITY)),
  229|    460|            debounce: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  230|    460|            latest_texture_key: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  231|    460|            job_tx: Some(job_tx),
  232|    460|            result_rx,
  233|    460|            worker_handle: Some(worker_handle),
  234|    460|            pending: RefCell::new(HashSet::new()),
  235|    460|            wanted_bucket: RefCell::new(HashMap::new()),
  236|    460|        }
  237|    460|    }
  238|       |
  239|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  240|       |    // Worker thread
  241|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  242|       |
  243|       |    /// Main loop for the worker thread. Receives jobs, processes them, and
  244|       |    /// sends results back. Uses `try_send` to avoid blocking if the result
  245|       |    /// channel is full (prevents shutdown deadlock). Same pattern as Mermaid
  246|       |    /// (mermaid_renderer.rs line 1264-1269).
  247|    464|    fn worker_loop(
  248|    464|        fontdb: Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  249|    464|        job_rx: JobReceiver,
  250|    464|        result_tx: ResultSender,
  251|    464|    ) {
  252|    470|        while let Ok(job) = job_rx.recv() {
                                   ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (252:19): [True: 6, False: 464]
  ------------------
  253|      6|            let result = Self::process_job(&fontdb, job);
  254|      6|            // Use try_send to avoid blocking if result channel is full.
  255|      6|            // This prevents deadlock during shutdown: if Drop joins workers
  256|      6|            // while they're blocked on send (because result_rx isn't being
  257|      6|            // polled), we'd hang forever. Dropping results is acceptable --
  258|      6|            // the main thread still has the code_hash in the pending set, so
  259|      6|            // render_block will show the stale texture or placeholder and the
  260|      6|            // pending entry will be leaked (harmless, cleared on next
  261|      6|            // release_gpu_textures or app restart).
  262|      6|            let _ = result_tx.try_send(result);
  263|      6|        }
  264|       |        // job_rx disconnected (main thread dropped job_tx) -- exit
  265|    464|    }
  266|       |
  267|       |    /// Process a single rasterization job: source->SVG (if needed), then
  268|       |    /// SVG->RGBA. Returns a result with either RGBA data or an error.
  269|      8|    fn process_job(fontdb: &Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>, job: RasterJob) -> RasterResult {
  270|       |        // Step 1: Get SVG (from cache or render fresh)

  272|      2|            Some(cached_svg) => Ok(cached_svg),

  277|       |        };

  279|      8|        let svg = match svg {
                          ^7

  304|      6|                display_size: Some(output.display_size),

  319|       |
  320|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  321|       |    // Rasterization (runs on worker thread)
  322|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  323|       |
  324|       |    /// Rasterize an SVG string to RGBA pixels. Pure function -- no GPU access,
  325|       |    /// no egui context needed. Runs on the worker thread.
  326|       |    ///
  327|       |    /// Injects font-family into Pikchr SVGs, parses via usvg (using the shared
  328|       |    /// fontdb with system fonts), and rasterizes via resvg/tiny-skia.
  329|       |    /// Rasterizes at `PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE` x the logical display resolution
  330|       |    /// for crisp text and lines. Returns display dimensions in logical pixels
  331|       |    /// and raster dimensions in actual pixels.
  332|      9|    fn rasterize_svg(
  333|      9|        fontdb: &Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database>,
  334|      9|        svg: &str,
  335|      9|        width_bucket: u32,
  336|      9|        scale_bucket: u32,
  337|      9|    ) -> Result<RasterOutput, String> {
  338|       |        // Inject a font-family into the SVG so usvg picks a known good font.
  339|       |        // Pikchr outputs <svg style='font-size:initial;'> with no font-family
  340|       |        // on <text> elements, so usvg falls back to whatever thin default it finds.
  341|      9|        let svg = Self::inject_svg_font_family(svg);
  342|       |
  343|       |        // Parse SVG using the shared fontdb with system fonts loaded.
  344|      9|        let opt = usvg::Options {
  345|      9|            fontdb: Arc::clone(fontdb),
  346|      9|            ..Default::default()
  347|      9|        };
  348|      9|        let tree = usvg::Tree::from_data(svg.as_bytes(), &opt)
                          ^7
  349|      9|            .map_err(|e| format!("SVG parse error: {}", e))?;
                                       ^2                                ^2
  350|       |
  351|       |        // Compute dimensions from the parsed SVG tree
  352|      7|        let size = tree.size().to_int_size();
  353|      7|        let (w, h) = (size.width().max(1), size.height().max(1));
  354|       |
  355|       |        // Compute logical scale factor (user zoom, constrained by available width)
  356|      7|        let base_scale = scale_bucket as f32 / 100.0;
  357|      7|        let width_scale = if width_bucket > 0 {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (357:30): [True: 6, False: 1]
  ------------------
  358|      6|            width_bucket as f32 / w.max(1) as f32
  359|       |        } else {
  360|      1|            base_scale
  361|       |        };
  362|      7|        let scale = base_scale.min(width_scale).clamp(0.1, 4.0);
  363|       |
  364|       |        // Display dimensions in logical pixels (what render_texture uses)
  365|      7|        let display_w = (w as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  366|      7|        let display_h = (h as f32 * scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  367|       |
  368|       |        // Raster dimensions: supersample for crisp text and thin lines
  369|      7|        let raster_scale = scale * PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE;
  370|      7|        let mut raster_w = (w as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  371|      7|        let mut raster_h = (h as f32 * raster_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  372|       |
  373|       |        // Clamp to reasonable maximum
  374|      7|        if raster_w > MAX_RASTER_SIDE || raster_h > MAX_RASTER_SIDE {
                                                       ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (374:12): [True: 1, False: 6]
  |  Branch (374:42): [True: 0, False: 6]
  ------------------
  375|      1|            let clamp_scale = (MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_w as f32)
  376|      1|                .min(MAX_RASTER_SIDE as f32 / raster_h as f32);
  377|      1|            raster_w = (raster_w as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  378|      1|            raster_h = (raster_h as f32 * clamp_scale).round().max(1.0) as u32;
  379|      6|        }
  380|       |
  381|       |        // Rasterize: Pikchr SVGs have no background fill, so the pixmap starts
  382|       |        // transparent and the diagram draws on top.
  383|      7|        let mut pixmap = tiny_skia::Pixmap::new(raster_w, raster_h)
  384|      7|            .ok_or_else(|| "Pixmap allocation failed".to_string())?;
                                         ^0                         ^0          ^0
  385|      7|        let transform = tiny_skia::Transform::from_scale(raster_scale, raster_scale);
  386|       |        // Bind PixmapMut to a local -- can't take &mut of a temporary rvalue.
  387|      7|        let mut pmut = pixmap.as_mut();
  388|      7|        resvg::render(&tree, transform, &mut pmut);
  389|       |
  390|      7|        Ok(RasterOutput {
  391|      7|            rgba: pixmap.data().to_vec(),
  392|      7|            raster_size: [raster_w as usize, raster_h as usize],
  393|      7|            display_size: [display_w, display_h],
  394|      7|        })
  395|      9|    }
  396|       |
  397|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  398|       |    // Main-thread polling
  399|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  400|       |
  401|       |    /// Drain completed results from the worker thread. Called once per frame
  402|       |    /// from `begin_frame`, NOT from `render_block`. This is critical:
  403|       |    /// `render_block` only runs for visible diagrams, so if diagrams scroll
  404|       |    /// off-screen while jobs are in-flight, results would pile up in the
  405|       |    /// bounded channel and eventually block the worker thread (deadlock).
  406|       |    /// Polling from `begin_frame` ensures results are always drained.
  407|     42|    fn poll_results(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) -> bool {

  490|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  491|       |    // Public API
  492|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  493|       |
  494|       |    /// Called once at the start of each egui frame. Drains completed results
  495|       |    /// from the worker thread and requests repaint if jobs are still pending.
  496|     42|    pub(crate) fn begin_frame(&self, ctx: &egui::Context) {
  497|       |        // Drain completed results from worker thread. Must happen here (not in
  498|       |        // render_block) because render_block only runs for visible diagrams --
  499|       |        // off-screen results would pile up and block the worker.
  500|     42|        self.poll_results(ctx);
  501|       |
  502|       |        // If jobs are still in-flight, ensure we get another frame to poll again.
  503|     42|        if self.has_pending() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (503:12): [True: 1, False: 41]
  ------------------
  504|      1|            ctx.request_repaint_after(Duration::from_millis(16));
  505|     41|        }
  506|     42|    }
  507|       |
  508|       |    /// Returns true if any rasterization jobs are in-flight on the worker thread.
  509|     59|    pub(crate) fn has_pending(&self) -> bool {
  510|     59|        !self.pending.borrow().is_empty()
  511|     59|    }
  512|       |
  513|       |    /// Release GPU textures to reduce idle GPU usage (e.g., when minimized).
  514|       |    /// SVG and error caches are retained for fast texture rebuilds on next render.
  515|       |    /// Also clears pending, wanted_bucket, and debounce to allow clean re-enqueue
  516|       |    /// on restore. In-flight results will be detected as stale (wanted_bucket is
  517|       |    /// empty) and discarded in poll_results.
  518|      3|    pub(crate) fn release_gpu_textures(&self) {
  519|      3|        self.textures.borrow_mut().clear();
  520|      3|        self.debounce.borrow_mut().clear();
  521|      3|        self.latest_texture_key.borrow_mut().clear();
  522|      3|        self.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().clear();
  523|      3|        self.pending.borrow_mut().clear();
  524|      3|    }
  525|       |
  526|       |    /// Main entry point for rendering a Pikchr code block.
  527|       |    ///
  528|       |    /// Checks caches (error, texture, SVG) before enqueuing. On cache miss,
  529|       |    /// enqueues a job to the worker thread and shows a stale texture or
  530|       |    /// placeholder while waiting.
  531|       |    ///
  532|       |    /// Returns `true` when the block has produced UI output (always true).
  533|     10|    pub(crate) fn render_block(
  534|     10|        &self,
  535|     10|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  536|     10|        code: &str,
  537|     10|        ui_scale: f32,
  538|     10|        code_font_size: f32,
  539|     10|    ) -> bool {
  540|     10|        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
  541|     10|        let dark_mode = ui.visuals().dark_mode;
  542|     10|        let svg_key = svg_cache_key(code_hash, dark_mode);
  543|       |
  544|       |        // 1. Check error cache (don't re-render known failures).
  545|       |        //    Errors depend only on syntax, not dark_mode, so keyed by code_hash.
  546|       |        //    Clone the error and drop the RefMut before calling render_error_block,
  547|       |        //    to avoid holding a borrow on self.errors across a &self method call.
  548|     10|        let cached_err = self.errors.borrow_mut().get(&code_hash);
  549|     10|        if let Some(err) = cached_err {
                                  ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (549:16): [True: 1, False: 9]
  ------------------
  550|      1|            self.render_error_block(ui, &err, code, code_font_size);
  551|      1|            return true;
  552|      9|        }
  553|       |
  554|       |        // 2. Compute texture cache key (incorporates width bucket + scale + theme)
  555|      9|        let available_width = ui.available_width().max(1.0);
  556|      9|        let width_bucket = bucket_width(available_width);
  557|      9|        let scale_bucket = bucket_scale(ui_scale);
  558|      9|        let texture_key = format!(
  559|       |            "pikchr:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
  560|      9|            code_hash, width_bucket, scale_bucket, dark_mode as u8
  561|       |        );
  562|       |
  563|       |        // 2b. Update wanted_bucket (for stale discard in poll_results)
  564|      9|        self.wanted_bucket
  565|      9|            .borrow_mut()
  566|      9|            .insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
  567|       |
  568|       |        // 3. Check texture cache.
  569|       |        {
  570|      9|            let mut cache = self.textures.borrow_mut();
  571|      9|            if let Some(entry) = cache.get(&texture_key) {
                                      ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (571:20): [True: 1, False: 8]
  ------------------
  572|      1|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  573|      1|                return true;
  574|      8|            }
  575|       |        }
  576|       |
  577|       |        // 3b. Debounce gate: suppress rasterization enqueue during resize.
  578|       |        //     Show the last rendered texture scaled to fit. Only enqueue
  579|       |        //     once the width stabilizes for RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS.
  580|      8|        let db_entry = self.debounce.borrow().get(&code_hash).map(|d| {
                                                                                    ^2
  581|      2|            (
  582|      2|                d.last_rasterized_bucket,
  583|      2|                d.last_seen_bucket,
  584|      2|                d.bucket_changed_at,
  585|      2|            )
  586|      2|        });
  587|      8|        if let Some((last_rasterized, last_seen, _changed_at)) = db_entry {
                                   ^2               ^2         ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (587:16): [True: 2, False: 6]
  ------------------
  588|      2|            if width_bucket != last_rasterized {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (588:16): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  589|       |                // Width changed since last rasterize -- apply trailing-edge debounce
  590|      2|                if width_bucket != last_seen {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (590:20): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  591|       |                    // New bucket since last frame -- reset timer
  592|      2|                    if let Some(d) = self.debounce.borrow_mut().get_mut(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (592:28): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  593|      2|                        d.last_seen_bucket = width_bucket;
  594|      2|                        d.bucket_changed_at = Some(Instant::now());
  595|      2|                    }
                                  ^0
  596|      0|                }
  597|      2|                let changed_at = self
  598|      2|                    .debounce
  599|      2|                    .borrow()
  600|      2|                    .get(&code_hash)
  601|      2|                    .and_then(|d| d.bucket_changed_at)
  602|      2|                    .unwrap_or_else(Instant::now);
  603|      2|                let elapsed = changed_at.elapsed();
  604|      2|                let debounce_dur = Duration::from_millis(RESIZE_DEBOUNCE_MS);
  605|      2|                if elapsed < debounce_dur {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (605:20): [True: 2, False: 0]
  ------------------
  606|       |                    // Still in cooldown -- show stale texture scaled to fit
  607|      1|                    if let Some(stale_key) =
  ------------------
  |  Branch (607:28): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  608|      2|                        self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned()
  609|       |                    {
  610|      1|                        if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (610:32): [True: 1, False: 0]
  ------------------
  611|      1|                            Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  612|      1|                            ui.ctx().request_repaint_after(debounce_dur - elapsed);
  613|      1|                            return true;
  614|      0|                        }
  615|      1|                    }
  616|       |                    // No stale texture available (first render) -- fall through to enqueue
  617|      0|                }
  618|       |                // Debounce expired -- fall through to enqueue
  619|      0|            }
  620|       |            // width == last_rasterized: texture was evicted -- fall through to enqueue
  621|      6|        }
  622|       |        // No debounce entry: first render -- fall through to enqueue
  623|       |
  624|       |        // 4. Check if already pending for this diagram (by code_hash, not texture_key,
  625|       |        //    to prevent redundant enqueues when the width changes mid-flight)
  626|      7|        if self.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash) {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (626:12): [True: 1, False: 6]
  ------------------
  627|      1|            return self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width);
  628|      6|        }
  629|       |
  630|       |        // 5. Enqueue job
  631|      6|        let cached_svg = self.svg_cache.borrow_mut().get(&svg_key);
  632|      6|        let job = RasterJob {
  633|      6|            code_hash,
  634|      6|            texture_key: texture_key.clone(),
  635|      6|            code: if cached_svg.is_none() {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (635:22): [True: 5, False: 1]
  ------------------
  636|      5|                Some(code.to_string())
  637|       |            } else {
  638|      1|                None
  639|       |            },
  640|      6|            svg: cached_svg,
  641|      6|            dark_mode,
  642|      6|            width_bucket,
  643|      6|            scale_bucket,
  644|       |        };
  645|       |
  646|      6|        match self.job_tx.as_ref().unwrap().try_send(job) {
  647|      4|            Ok(()) => {
  648|      4|                self.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
  649|      4|            }
  650|      1|            Err(TrySendError::Full(_)) => {
  651|      1|                // Queue full -- will retry next frame
  652|      1|            }
  653|       |            Err(TrySendError::Disconnected(_)) => {
  654|       |                // Worker died -- show error
  655|      1|                self.errors
  656|      1|                    .borrow_mut()
  657|      1|                    .insert(code_hash, "Raster worker unavailable".to_string());
  658|      1|                self.render_error_block(ui, "Raster worker unavailable", code, code_font_size);
  659|      1|                return true;
  660|       |            }
  661|       |        }
  662|       |
  663|       |        // 6. Show stale texture or placeholder while waiting
  664|      5|        self.show_stale_or_placeholder(ui, code_hash, available_width)
  665|     10|    }
  666|       |
  667|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  668|       |    // Helpers
  669|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  670|       |
  671|       |    /// Show a stale texture (from a previous width) scaled to fit, or a
  672|       |    /// minimal-height placeholder if no stale texture is available.
  673|       |    /// Returns true (block always produces UI output).
  674|      8|    fn show_stale_or_placeholder(
  675|      8|        &self,
  676|      8|        ui: &mut egui::Ui,
  677|      8|        code_hash: u64,
  678|      8|        available_width: f32,
  679|      8|    ) -> bool {
  680|       |        // Try stale texture first (gives a good visual approximation)
  681|      8|        if let Some(stale_key) = self.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash).cloned() {
                                  ^2
  ------------------
  |  Branch (681:16): [True: 2, False: 6]
  ------------------
  682|      2|            if let Some(entry) = self.textures.borrow_mut().get(&stale_key) {
                                      ^1
  ------------------
  |  Branch (682:20): [True: 1, False: 1]
  ------------------
  683|      1|                Self::render_texture(ui, &entry, available_width);
  684|      1|                return true;
  685|      1|            }
  686|      6|        }
  687|       |        // No stale texture -- show minimal placeholder (first render).
  688|       |        // Use a small fixed height to reduce layout shift when the real texture arrives.
  689|      7|        let placeholder_height = 60.0;
  690|      7|        let (rect, _) = ui.allocate_exact_size(
  691|      7|            egui::vec2(available_width, placeholder_height),
  692|      7|            egui::Sense::hover(),
  693|      7|        );
  694|      7|        ui.painter().text(
  695|      7|            rect.center(),
  696|       |            egui::Align2::CENTER_CENTER,
  697|       |            "Rendering...",
  698|      7|            egui::FontId::proportional(12.0),
  699|      7|            ui.visuals().weak_text_color(),
  700|       |        );
  701|      7|        true
  702|      8|    }
  703|       |
  704|       |    /// Parse the width bucket value from a texture cache key.
  705|       |    /// Key format: `pikchr:<hex>:w<width>:s<scale>:dm<0|1>`
  706|     13|    fn parse_width_from_texture_key(key: &str) -> Option<u32> {
  707|     13|        let w_start = key.find(":w")? + 2;
                          ^9                      ^4
  708|      9|        let w_end = key[w_start..].find(':')? + w_start;
                                                          ^0
  709|      9|        key[w_start..w_end].parse().ok()
  710|     13|    }
  711|       |
  712|       |    /// Check whether a result's width bucket no longer matches what the main
  713|       |    /// thread currently wants for this diagram. Stale results have their SVG
  714|       |    /// cached but skip the expensive GPU upload.
  715|      7|    fn is_stale_result(&self, texture_key: &str, code_hash: u64) -> bool {
  716|      7|        let result_bucket = Self::parse_width_from_texture_key(texture_key);
  717|      7|        let wanted = self.wanted_bucket.borrow().get(&code_hash).copied();
  718|      7|        match (result_bucket, wanted) {
  719|      4|            (Some(rb), Some(wb)) => rb != wb, // Width mismatch -> stale
  720|      1|            (Some(_), None) => true,          // No wanted bucket (e.g. after
  721|       |            // release_gpu_textures) -> stale
  722|      2|            _ => false, // Can't parse -> assume fresh
  723|       |        }
  724|      7|    }
  725|       |
  726|       |    /// Render Pikchr source code to an SVG string.
  727|       |    ///
  728|       |    /// Static associated function (no `&self`) -- pure input/output.
  729|       |    /// Uses the pikchr crate's builder-pattern API for flags.
  730|     47|    fn render_pikchr_to_svg(code: &str, dark_mode: bool) -> Result<String, String> {
  731|       |        use pikchr::{Pikchr, PikchrFlags};
  732|       |
  733|     47|        let mut flags = PikchrFlags::default();
  734|     47|        flags.generate_plain_errors();
  735|     47|        if dark_mode {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (735:12): [True: 6, False: 41]
  ------------------
  736|      6|            flags.use_dark_mode();
  737|     41|        }
  738|       |
  739|     47|        Pikchr::render(code, None, flags).map(|svg| svg.to_string())
                                                                  ^40 ^40
  740|       |        // Pikchr::render already returns Err(String); no map_err needed.
  741|     47|    }
  742|       |
  743|       |    /// Inject a `font-family` declaration into a Pikchr SVG string.
  744|       |    ///
  745|       |    /// Pikchr outputs `<text>` elements with no `font-family` attribute,
  746|       |    /// causing usvg to fall back to a potentially thin default font.
  747|       |    /// We prepend a `font-family` to the existing `<svg style='...'>`
  748|       |    /// so all text inherits a known good sans-serif font.
  749|     11|    fn inject_svg_font_family(svg: &str) -> String {
  750|       |        // Pikchr outputs: <svg ... style='font-size:initial;' ...>
  751|       |        // Inject font-family before the existing font-size declaration.
  752|     11|        if let Some(pos) = svg.find("style='font-size:") {
                                  ^6
  ------------------
  |  Branch (752:16): [True: 6, False: 5]
  ------------------
  753|      6|            let insert_at = pos + "style='".len();
  754|      6|            let mut result = String::with_capacity(svg.len() + 60);
  755|      6|            result.push_str(&svg[..insert_at]);
  756|      6|            result.push_str("font-family:\"Segoe UI\",\"Helvetica Neue\",Arial,sans-serif;");
  757|      6|            result.push_str(&svg[insert_at..]);
  758|      6|            result
  759|       |        } else {
  760|      5|            svg.to_string()
  761|       |        }
  762|     11|    }
  763|       |
  764|       |    /// Display a cached texture in the UI, scaling down if it exceeds available width.
  765|      3|    fn render_texture(ui: &mut egui::Ui, entry: &PikchrTextureEntry, available_width: f32) {
  766|      3|        let (tw, th) = (entry.display_size[0] as f32, entry.display_size[1] as f32);
  767|      3|        let scale = if tw > available_width {
  ------------------
  |  Branch (767:24): [True: 2, False: 1]
  ------------------
  768|      2|            (available_width / tw).clamp(0.01, 4.0)
  769|       |        } else {
  770|      1|            1.0
  771|       |        };
  772|      3|        let size = egui::vec2((tw * scale).round(), (th * scale).round());
  773|      3|        ui.add(egui::Image::new(&entry.texture).fit_to_exact_size(size));
  774|      3|    }
  775|       |
  776|       |    /// Display a Pikchr error block with the error message and original source code.
  777|      2|    fn render_error_block(&self, ui: &mut egui::Ui, error: &str, code: &str, code_font_size: f32) {
  778|      2|        let tc = ThemeColors::current(ui.visuals().dark_mode);
  779|      2|        egui::Frame::none()
  780|      2|            .fill(tc.box_bg)
  781|      2|            .stroke(egui::Stroke::new(1.0, egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80)))
  782|      2|            .inner_margin(8.0)
  783|      2|            .show(ui, |ui| {
  784|      2|                ui.label(
  785|      2|                    egui::RichText::new("Pikchr rendering error:")
  786|      2|                        .color(egui::Color32::from_rgb(200, 80, 80))
  787|      2|                        .size(code_font_size),
  788|       |                );
  789|      2|                ui.label(
  790|      2|                    egui::RichText::new(error)
  791|      2|                        .color(egui::Color32::LIGHT_GRAY)
  792|      2|                        .size(code_font_size)
  793|      2|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  794|       |                );
  795|      2|                ui.add_space(4.0);
  796|      2|                ui.label(
  797|      2|                    egui::RichText::new(code)
  798|      2|                        .size(code_font_size)
  799|      2|                        .color(tc.code_fallback_text)
  800|      2|                        .family(egui::FontFamily::Monospace),
  801|       |                );
  802|      2|            });
  803|      2|    }
  804|       |}
  805|       |
  806|       |impl Drop for PikchrRenderer {
  807|    474|    fn drop(&mut self) {
  808|       |        // Close job channel -- worker will see Disconnected on recv() and exit.
  809|       |        // The worker uses try_send for results (never blocks), so it will
  810|       |        // drain remaining jobs and exit promptly once job_rx disconnects.
  811|    474|        self.job_tx.take();
  812|       |        // Join worker thread for clean shutdown
  813|    474|        if let Some(handle) = self.worker_handle.take() {
                                  ^460
  ------------------
  |  Branch (813:16): [True: 460, False: 14]
  ------------------
  814|    460|            let _ = handle.join();
  815|    460|        }
                      ^14
  816|    474|    }
  817|       |}

  878|       |        assert!(svg.is_ok());

  887|       |        let dark = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"Test\" fit", true).unwrap();
  888|       |        assert_ne!(light, dark);

  919|       |    #[test]
  920|       |    fn test_texture_key_includes_all_components() {
  921|       |        let key = format!("pikchr:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}", 12345u64, 640, 100, 0);
  922|       |        assert!(key.contains("pikchr:"));
  923|       |        assert!(key.contains(":w640:"));
  924|       |        assert!(key.contains(":s100:"));
  925|       |        assert!(key.contains(":dm0"));
  926|       |    }
  927|       |

  939|       |            arrow right 200% "SVG" "Output"
  940|       |            box rad 10px "Rasterizer" fit

  945|       |
  946|       |    #[test]
  947|       |    fn test_width_bucketing() {
  948|       |        // Same bucket for nearby widths (round() gives +/-16px half-buckets)
  949|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(641.0));
  950|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(655.0)); // 655/32=20.47 -> rounds to 20
  951|       |                                                              // Different buckets once we cross the half-bucket boundary
  952|       |        assert_ne!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(656.0)); // 656/32=20.5 -> rounds to 21
  953|       |        assert_ne!(bucket_width(640.0), bucket_width(672.0)); // clearly different bucket
  954|       |                                                              // Verify actual bucket values
  955|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(640.0), 640); // 640/32=20.0 -> 20*32=640
  956|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_width(672.0), 672); // 672/32=21.0 -> 21*32=672
  957|       |    }
  958|       |
  959|       |    #[test]
  960|       |    fn test_renderer_new_creates_empty_caches() {
  961|       |        let renderer = PikchrRenderer::new();
  962|       |        // No panics, caches start empty
  963|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
  964|       |    }

  995|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(code, false);

 1002|       |
 1003|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1004|       |    // bucket_scale coverage
 1005|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1006|       |
 1007|       |    #[test]
 1008|       |    fn test_scale_bucketing() {
 1009|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.0), 100);
 1010|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.25), 125);
 1011|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.5), 150);
 1012|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(2.0), 200);
 1013|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(0.5), 50);
 1014|       |        // Rounding: 1.124 * 100 = 112.4 -> rounds to 112
 1015|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.124), 112);
 1016|       |        // Rounding: 1.126 * 100 = 112.6 -> rounds to 113
 1017|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.126), 113);
 1018|       |        // Values within rounding distance bucket together
 1019|       |        assert_eq!(bucket_scale(1.001), bucket_scale(1.004)); // both round to 100
 1020|       |    }

 1130|       |    #[test]
 1131|       |    fn test_svg_has_proper_structure() {
 1132|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"X\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1133|       |        assert!(svg.contains("<svg"), "SVG must have opening <svg tag");
 1134|       |        assert!(svg.contains("</svg>"), "SVG must have closing </svg> tag");
 1135|       |        assert!(
 1136|       |            svg.contains("viewBox") || (svg.contains("width") && svg.contains("height")),
 1137|       |            "SVG must have viewBox or width/height attributes"
 1138|       |        );
 1139|       |    }
 1140|       |
 1141|       |    #[test]
 1142|       |    fn test_svg_dimensions_are_reasonable() {
 1143|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"Test\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1144|       |        // Pikchr uses viewBox="x y width height" for dimensions
 1145|       |        let (w, h) =
 1146|       |            extract_viewbox_size(&svg).expect("SVG should have a parseable viewBox attribute");
 1147|       |        assert!(w > 0.0, "viewBox width must be > 0, got {}", w);
 1148|       |        assert!(h > 0.0, "viewBox height must be > 0, got {}", h);
 1149|       |        assert!(w < 10000.0, "viewBox width must be < 10000, got {}", w);
 1150|       |        assert!(h < 10000.0, "viewBox height must be < 10000, got {}", h);
 1151|       |    }
 1152|       |
 1153|       |    #[test]
 1154|       |    fn test_different_diagrams_produce_different_dimensions() {
 1155|       |        let small = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box", false).unwrap();
 1156|       |        let large = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(
 1157|       |            "box; arrow right; box; arrow right; box; arrow right; box; arrow right; box",
 1158|       |            false,
 1159|       |        )
 1160|       |        .unwrap();
 1161|       |        let (w_small, _) = extract_viewbox_size(&small).expect("small SVG should have viewBox");
 1162|       |        let (w_large, _) = extract_viewbox_size(&large).expect("large SVG should have viewBox");
 1163|       |        assert!(
 1164|       |            w_large > w_small,
 1165|       |            "Chain of boxes should be wider than single box: {} vs {}",
 1166|       |            w_large,
 1167|       |            w_small
 1168|       |        );
 1169|       |    }
 1170|       |
 1171|       |    /// Helper: extract width and height from an SVG `viewBox="x y w h"` attribute.
 1172|       |    fn extract_viewbox_size(svg: &str) -> Option<(f64, f64)> {
 1173|       |        let marker = "viewBox=\"";
 1174|       |        let start = svg.find(marker)? + marker.len();
 1175|       |        let rest = &svg[start..];
 1176|       |        let end = rest.find('"')?;
 1177|       |        let parts: Vec<f64> = rest[..end]
 1178|       |            .split_whitespace()
 1179|       |            .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
 1180|       |            .collect();
 1181|       |        if parts.len() >= 4 {
 1182|       |            Some((parts[2], parts[3]))
 1183|       |        } else {
 1184|       |            None
 1185|       |        }
 1186|       |    }

 1211|       |    fn test_special_characters_in_labels() {

 1213|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"x < y\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1214|       |        // Pikchr XML-escapes < to &lt; in text content

 1216|       |            svg.contains("&lt;"),
 1217|       |            "SVG should contain XML-escaped '<':\n{}",
 1218|       |            svg
 1219|       |        );
 1220|       |
 1221|       |        // Test ampersand: Pikchr XML-escapes & to &amp;
 1222|       |        let svg2 = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"A & B\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1223|       |        assert!(
 1224|       |            svg2.contains("&amp;"),

 1234|       |    #[test]
 1235|       |    fn test_error_message_has_content() {
 1236|       |        let err = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("this is not valid pikchr }{}{", false)
 1237|       |            .unwrap_err();
 1238|       |        assert!(
 1239|       |            !err.is_empty(),
 1240|       |            "Error message should not be empty for invalid syntax"
 1241|       |        );
 1242|       |        // Error should contain some useful diagnostic info
 1243|       |        assert!(
 1244|       |            err.len() > 5,
 1245|       |            "Error message should be descriptive, got: '{}'",
 1246|       |            err
 1247|       |        );
 1248|       |    }
 1249|       |
 1250|       |    #[test]
 1251|       |    fn test_error_cache_theme_independent() {
 1252|       |        // Errors are keyed by code_hash, not theme -- same syntax error in
 1253|       |        // light and dark mode should produce the same error message.
 1254|       |        let invalid = "totally broken pikchr syntax }{}{";
 1255|       |        let err_light = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(invalid, false).unwrap_err();
 1256|       |        let err_dark = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(invalid, true).unwrap_err();
 1257|       |        assert_eq!(
 1258|       |            err_light, err_dark,
 1259|       |            "Same syntax error should produce same message regardless of theme"
 1260|       |        );
 1261|       |    }
 1262|       |
 1263|       |    #[test]
 1264|       |    fn test_multiple_errors_are_distinct() {
 1265|       |        let err1 =
 1266|       |            PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("invalid syntax aaa }{}{", false).unwrap_err();
 1267|       |        let err2 =
 1268|       |            PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("different invalid bbb }{}{", false).unwrap_err();
 1269|       |        // Different invalid inputs may produce different error messages
 1270|       |        // (at minimum they should both be errors, which we've already asserted
 1271|       |        // by unwrap_err). If they happen to be the same generic error, that's
 1272|       |        // still valid -- but let's verify they're both non-empty.
 1273|       |        assert!(!err1.is_empty(), "Error 1 should not be empty");
 1274|       |        assert!(!err2.is_empty(), "Error 2 should not be empty");
 1275|       |    }
 1276|       |
 1277|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1278|       |    // Dark mode output inspection
 1279|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1280|       |
 1281|       |    #[test]
 1282|       |    fn test_dark_mode_inverts_stroke_colors() {
 1283|       |        let light = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"X\" fit", false).unwrap();
 1284|       |        let dark = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("box \"X\" fit", true).unwrap();
 1285|       |
 1286|       |        // Pikchr uses PIKCHR_DARK_MODE flag to invert colors.
 1287|       |        // Light mode should contain dark strokes (e.g., "black" or rgb(0,0,0))
 1288|       |        // Dark mode should contain light strokes (e.g., "white" or rgb(255,255,255))
 1289|       |        let light_has_black =
 1290|       |            light.contains("black") || light.contains("rgb(0,0,0)") || light.contains("#000");
 1291|       |        let dark_has_white =
 1292|       |            dark.contains("white") || dark.contains("rgb(255,255,255)") || dark.contains("#fff");
 1293|       |
 1294|       |        assert!(
 1295|       |            light_has_black,
 1296|       |            "Light mode SVG should reference dark colors:\n{}",
 1297|       |            light
 1298|       |        );
 1299|       |        assert!(
 1300|       |            dark_has_white,
 1301|       |            "Dark mode SVG should reference light colors:\n{}",
 1302|       |            dark
 1303|       |        );
 1304|       |    }
 1305|       |
 1306|       |    #[test]
 1307|       |    fn test_dark_mode_preserves_labels() {
 1308|       |        let label = "DarkTest";
 1309|       |        let code = format!("box \"{}\" fit", label);
 1310|       |        let light = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(&code, false).unwrap();
 1311|       |        let dark = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(&code, true).unwrap();
 1312|       |        assert!(
 1313|       |            light.contains(label),
 1314|       |            "Light mode should contain label '{}'",
 1315|       |            label
 1316|       |        );
 1317|       |        assert!(
 1318|       |            dark.contains(label),
 1319|       |            "Dark mode should contain label '{}'",
 1320|       |            label
 1321|       |        );
 1322|       |    }

 1328|       |    #[test]
 1329|       |    fn test_large_diagram_renders() {
 1330|       |        // Build a chain of 50+ boxes connected by arrows
 1331|       |        let mut code = String::from("box \"B0\" fit\n");
 1332|       |        for i in 1..=50 {
 1333|       |            code.push_str(&format!("arrow right\nbox \"B{}\" fit\n", i));
 1334|       |        }
 1335|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(&code, false);
 1336|       |        assert!(
 1337|       |            result.is_ok(),
 1338|       |            "Large diagram (50+ objects) should render: {:?}",
 1339|       |            result.err()
 1340|       |        );
 1341|       |        let svg = result.unwrap();
 1342|       |        assert!(svg.contains("B0"), "First box label missing");
 1343|       |        assert!(svg.contains("B50"), "Last box label missing");
 1344|       |    }
 1345|       |
 1346|       |    #[test]
 1347|       |    fn test_whitespace_only_input() {
 1348|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg("   \n  \n  ", false);
 1349|       |        // Whitespace-only should behave like empty input (OK, not error)
 1350|       |        assert!(
 1351|       |            result.is_ok(),
 1352|       |            "Whitespace-only input should not error: {:?}",
 1353|       |            result.err()
 1354|       |        );
 1355|       |    }
 1356|       |
 1357|       |    #[test]
 1358|       |    fn test_comments_in_pikchr() {
 1359|       |        let code = "# This is a comment\nbox \"Visible\" fit\n# Another comment";
 1360|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(code, false);
 1361|       |        assert!(
 1362|       |            result.is_ok(),
 1363|       |            "Comments should not cause errors: {:?}",
 1364|       |            result.err()
 1365|       |        );
 1366|       |        let svg = result.unwrap();
 1367|       |        assert!(
 1368|       |            svg.contains("Visible"),
 1369|       |            "Label should appear despite comments"
 1370|       |        );
 1371|       |        // Comments should NOT appear in SVG output
 1372|       |        assert!(
 1373|       |            !svg.contains("This is a comment"),
 1374|       |            "Comment text should not leak into SVG output"
 1375|       |        );
 1376|       |    }
 1377|       |
 1378|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1379|       |    // Output snapshot / characterization test
 1380|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1381|       |
 1382|       |    #[test]
 1383|       |    fn test_svg_output_snapshot() {
 1384|       |        // Render a known diagram and validate specific SVG structural fragments.
 1385|       |        // This acts as a characterization test documenting what the output looks like.
 1386|       |        let code = r#"arrow right 200% "Input" above
 1387|       |box "Process" fit rad 10px
 1388|       |arrow right 200% "Output" above"#;
 1389|       |        let svg = PikchrRenderer::render_pikchr_to_svg(code, false).unwrap();
 1390|       |
 1391|       |        // Must have proper SVG wrapper
 1392|       |        assert!(svg.starts_with("<svg"), "SVG must start with <svg tag");
 1393|       |        assert!(
 1394|       |            svg.trim_end().ends_with("</svg>"),
 1395|       |            "SVG must end with </svg>"
 1396|       |        );
 1397|       |
 1398|       |        // Must have viewBox for proper scaling
 1399|       |        assert!(svg.contains("viewBox"), "SVG should have viewBox attribute");
 1400|       |
 1401|       |        // Arrow produces polygon elements (arrowheads)
 1402|       |        assert!(
 1403|       |            svg.contains("<polygon"),
 1404|       |            "Arrows should produce <polygon> arrowheads"
 1405|       |        );
 1406|       |
 1407|       |        // Labels appear as text elements
 1408|       |        assert!(
 1409|       |            svg.contains("<text"),
 1410|       |            "Labels should produce <text> elements"
 1411|       |        );
 1412|       |        assert!(svg.contains("Input"), "Label 'Input' must be in SVG");
 1413|       |        assert!(svg.contains("Process"), "Label 'Process' must be in SVG");
 1414|       |        assert!(svg.contains("Output"), "Label 'Output' must be in SVG");
 1415|       |
 1416|       |        // Lines appear as path or line elements
 1417|       |        assert!(
 1418|       |            svg.contains("<line") || svg.contains("<path"),
 1419|       |            "Connectors should produce <line> or <path> elements"
 1420|       |        );
 1421|       |    }
 1422|       |
 1423|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1424|       |    // Font injection and supersample tests
 1425|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1426|       |
 1427|       |    #[test]
 1428|       |    fn test_inject_svg_font_family_adds_declaration() {
 1429|       |        let svg = "<svg style='font-size:initial;' viewBox='0 0 100 50'></svg>";
 1430|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::inject_svg_font_family(svg);
 1431|       |        assert!(
 1432|       |            result.contains("font-family:"),
 1433|       |            "Should inject font-family declaration"
 1434|       |        );
 1435|       |        assert!(
 1436|       |            result.contains("sans-serif"),
 1437|       |            "Should include sans-serif fallback"
 1438|       |        );
 1439|       |        // Original content preserved
 1440|       |        assert!(
 1441|       |            result.contains("font-size:initial"),
 1442|       |            "Original style should be preserved"
 1443|       |        );
 1444|       |        assert!(
 1445|       |            result.contains("viewBox"),
 1446|       |            "Other attributes should be preserved"
 1447|       |        );
 1448|       |    }
 1449|       |
 1450|       |    #[test]
 1451|       |    fn test_inject_svg_font_family_passthrough_without_style() {
 1452|       |        // SVG without Pikchr's style pattern should pass through unchanged
 1453|       |        let svg = "<svg viewBox='0 0 100 50'><text>Hello</text></svg>";
 1454|       |        let result = PikchrRenderer::inject_svg_font_family(svg);
 1455|       |        assert_eq!(
 1456|       |            result, svg,
 1457|       |            "SVG without matching style should be unchanged"
 1458|       |        );
 1459|       |    }
 1460|       |
 1461|       |    #[test]
 1462|       |    fn test_supersample_constant_is_reasonable() {
 1463|       |        const { assert!(PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE >= 1.0, "Supersample must be >= 1.0") };
 1464|       |        const {
 1465|       |            assert!(
 1466|       |                PIKCHR_SUPERSAMPLE <= 4.0,
 1467|       |                "Supersample > 4.0 would waste memory"
 1468|       |            )
 1469|       |        };
 1470|       |    }
 1471|       |
 1472|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1473|       |    // Stale result and texture key parsing tests
 1474|       |    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
 1475|       |
 1476|       |    #[test]
 1477|       |    fn test_parse_width_from_texture_key() {
 1478|       |        assert_eq!(
 1479|       |            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:0000000000003039:w640:s100:dm0"),
 1480|       |            Some(640)
 1481|       |        );
 1482|       |        assert_eq!(
 1483|       |            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:abc:w1024:s150:dm1"),
 1484|       |            Some(1024)
 1485|       |        );
 1486|       |        assert_eq!(
 1487|       |            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:abc:w0:s100:dm0"),
 1488|       |            Some(0)
 1489|       |        );
 1490|       |        // No :w prefix
 1491|       |        assert_eq!(
 1492|       |            PikchrRenderer::parse_width_from_texture_key("pikchr:abc:s100:dm0"),
 1493|       |            None
 1494|       |        );
 1495|       |    }

 1501|       |    /// Helper: create a fontdb with system fonts loaded (shared across worker tests).
 1502|       |    fn make_fontdb() -> Arc<usvg::fontdb::Database> {
 1503|       |        let mut db = usvg::fontdb::Database::new();
 1504|       |        db.load_system_fonts();
 1505|       |        Arc::new(db)
 1506|       |    }
 1507|       |
 1508|       |    #[test]
 1509|       |    fn test_render_block_basic_and_pending_placeholder() {
 1510|       |        let renderer = PikchrRenderer::new();
 1511|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1512|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1513|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1514|       |        let mut rendered_first = false;
 1515|       |        let mut rendered_second = false;
 1516|       |
 1517|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1518|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1519|       |                rendered_first = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1520|       |                rendered_second = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1521|       |            });
 1522|       |        });
 1523|       |
 1524|       |        assert!(rendered_first);
 1525|       |        assert!(rendered_second);
 1526|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&hash_str(code)));
 1527|       |    }

 1529|       |    #[test]
 1530|       |    fn test_render_block_reports_cached_error() {
 1531|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1532|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(None, result_rx);
 1533|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1534|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1535|       |        renderer
 1536|       |            .errors
 1537|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1538|       |            .insert(code_hash, "boom".to_string());
 1539|       |

 1542|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1543|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1544|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1545|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1546|       |            });
 1547|       |        });

 1553|       |    #[test]
 1554|       |    fn test_render_block_enqueues_cached_svg() {
 1555|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1556|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1557|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1558|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1559|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1560|       |        renderer.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(
 1561|       |            svg_cache_key(code_hash, false),
 1562|       |            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"></svg>".to_string(),
 1563|       |        );
 1564|       |        renderer.svg_cache.borrow_mut().insert(
 1565|       |            svg_cache_key(code_hash, true),
 1566|       |            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"></svg>".to_string(),
 1567|       |        );
 1568|       |
 1569|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1570|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1571|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1572|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1573|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1574|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1575|       |            });
 1576|       |        });
 1577|       |
 1578|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1579|       |        let request = job_rx
 1580|       |            .recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(1))
 1581|       |            .expect("request");
 1582|       |        assert!(request.svg.is_some());
 1583|       |        assert!(request.code.is_none());
 1584|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1585|       |    }

 1588|       |    fn test_render_block_queue_full_keeps_rendering() {
 1589|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1590|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);

 1592|       |        job_tx
 1593|       |            .send(RasterJob {
 1594|       |                code_hash: 1,
 1595|       |                texture_key: "pikchr:full:w224:s100:dm0".to_string(),

 1597|       |                svg: None,
 1598|       |                dark_mode: false,
 1599|       |                width_bucket: 224,
 1600|       |                scale_bucket: 100,
 1601|       |            })
 1602|       |            .expect("fill queue");
 1603|       |

 1605|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1606|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1607|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1608|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {

 1611|       |        });

 1614|       |        assert!(!renderer.has_pending());
 1615|       |        drop(job_rx);
 1616|       |    }

 1640|       |    #[test]
 1641|       |    fn test_begin_frame_processes_result_and_uses_cached_texture() {
 1642|       |        let (result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(2);
 1643|       |        let (job_tx, _job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1644|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1645|       |        let code = "box \"Hello\" fit";
 1646|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1647|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash);
 1648|       |        renderer.pending.borrow_mut().insert(999);
 1649|       |        let result_tx = RefCell::new(Some(result_tx));
 1650|       |        let texture_key = RefCell::new(String::new());
 1651|       |
 1652|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1653|       |        let input = test_raw_input(240.0, 160.0);
 1654|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1655|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1656|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1657|       |                let width_bucket = bucket_width(ui.available_width().max(1.0));
 1658|       |                let key = format!(
 1659|       |                    "pikchr:{:016x}:w{}:s{}:dm{}",
 1660|       |                    code_hash,
 1661|       |                    width_bucket,
 1662|       |                    100,
 1663|       |                    ui.visuals().dark_mode as u8
 1664|       |                );
 1665|       |                *texture_key.borrow_mut() = key.clone();
 1666|       |                renderer.wanted_bucket.borrow_mut().insert(code_hash, width_bucket);
 1667|       |                if let Some(tx) = result_tx.borrow_mut().take() {
 1668|       |                    tx.send(RasterResult {
 1669|       |                        code_hash,
 1670|       |                        texture_key: key,
 1671|       |                        dark_mode: ui.visuals().dark_mode,
 1672|       |                        svg: Some(
 1673|       |                            "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 1 1\"><rect width=\"1\" height=\"1\" fill=\"red\"/></svg>"
 1674|       |                                .to_string(),
 1675|       |                        ),
 1676|       |                        rgba: Some(vec![255, 0, 0, 255]),
 1677|       |                        raster_size: Some([1, 1]),
 1678|       |                        display_size: Some([64, 32]),
 1679|       |                        error: None,
 1680|       |                    })
 1681|       |                    .expect("send result");
 1682|       |                }
 1683|       |                renderer.begin_frame(ctx);
 1684|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1685|       |            });
 1686|       |        });
 1687|       |
 1688|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1689|       |        let texture_key = texture_key.into_inner();
 1690|       |        assert!(renderer.textures.borrow_mut().get(&texture_key).is_some());
 1691|       |        assert!(renderer
 1692|       |            .svg_cache
 1693|       |            .borrow_mut()
 1694|       |            .get(&svg_cache_key(code_hash, ctx.style().visuals.dark_mode))
 1695|       |            .is_some());
 1696|       |        assert_eq!(
 1697|       |            renderer.latest_texture_key.borrow().get(&code_hash),
 1698|       |            Some(&texture_key)
 1699|       |        );
 1700|       |        assert!(!renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1701|       |    }
 1702|       |

 1850|       |    #[test]
 1851|       |    fn test_render_block_debounce_without_stale_texture_enqueues_job() {
 1852|       |        let (job_tx, job_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1853|       |        let (_result_tx, result_rx) = crossbeam_channel::bounded(1);
 1854|       |        let renderer = test_renderer_with_channels(Some(job_tx), result_rx);
 1855|       |        let code = "box \"A\"\narrow right 50\nbox \"B\"";
 1856|       |        let code_hash = hash_str(code);
 1857|       |        renderer.debounce.borrow_mut().insert(
 1858|       |            code_hash,
 1859|       |            DiagramDebounce {
 1860|       |                last_rasterized_bucket: 224,
 1861|       |                last_seen_bucket: 224,
 1862|       |                bucket_changed_at: Some(Instant::now()),
 1863|       |            },
 1864|       |        );
 1865|       |
 1866|       |        let ctx = egui::Context::default();
 1867|       |        let input = test_raw_input(96.0, 160.0);
 1868|       |        let mut rendered = false;
 1869|       |        let _ = ctx.run(input, |ctx| {
 1870|       |            egui::CentralPanel::default().show(ctx, |ui| {
 1871|       |                rendered = renderer.render_block(ui, code, 1.0, 14.0);
 1872|       |            });
 1873|       |        });
 1874|       |
 1875|       |        assert!(rendered);
 1876|       |        assert_eq!(job_rx.len(), 1);
 1877|       |        assert!(renderer.pending.borrow().contains(&code_hash));
 1878|       |        assert_eq!(
 1879|       |            renderer
 1880|       |                .debounce
 1881|       |                .borrow()
 1882|       |                .get(&code_hash)
 1883|       |                .map(|d| d.last_seen_bucket),
 1884|       |            Some(bucket_width(96.0))
 1885|       |        );
 1886|       |    }
